We show that the RDM of a 2D topologically ordered system, expressed at the entanglement cut, realizes a 1D mixed-state phase. For the $\mathbb{Z}_2$ toric code phase, it is a 1D $\mathbb{Z}_2$ strong-to-weak spontaneous symmetry breaking (SW-SSB) phase, where deconfinement of anyons manifests as the short-range correlation of both $\mathbb{Z}_2$ charge and $\mathbb{Z}_2$ domain-wall in the RDM. The bulk $e$-$m$ duality translates into a Kramers--Wannier self-duality of the SW-SSB phase. Extending the framework to gapped $\mathbb{Z}_2$ spin liquids, the global spin-rotation symmetry manifests as an additional weak symmetry for the 1D RDM. Spin-$\frac{1}{2}$ spinons result in a cusp on the disorder parameter of spin-rotation at $θ=π$, providing a direct, ground-state signature of symmetry fractionalization. We verify this prediction analytically using the matrix product density operator formalism and numerically for the kagome-lattice resonating valence bond state. The proposed observable requires only a single ground-state wavefunction, making it amenable to quantum simulation platforms.

title: Analysis of Power Iteration Algorithm with Partially Observed Matrix-vector Products
authors: Soumyadip Ghosh, Lior Horesh, Vassilis Kalantzis, Yingdong Lu, Tomasz Nowicki, Shashanka Ubaru
abstract: We consider the problem of computing the dominant eigenvector of a symmetric matrix via the power iteration algorithm subject to constraints in the computation of matrix-vector pr ucts. In particular, we focus on scenarios where the entries of matrix-vector products with the input matrix are only partially observed. Such constraints frequently arise on cloud architectures implemented via the controller-worker model where the matrix-vector products are distributed across workers on remote servers. Instead of a prolonged delay incurred by waiting for the slowest workers to return their output to the controller, a phenomenon known as straggling, a set of pre-determined values can replace the values of the delayed workers and allow the power iteration to proceed to the next iteration. In this paper, we develop two algorithms whose expected approximation converges to the true dominant eigenvector. The first algorithm relies on a probabilistic switch between two different approaches to set the omitted entries: either set them to zero or to their previous recorded value. The second algorithm relies on averaging previously generated partial power iteration approximations obtained by ignoring a set of columns of the iteration matrix. several theoretical details are discussed while numerical experiments verify the effectiveness of the two proposed schemes and demonstrate their comparative performance advantage over current state-of-the-art.

title: Accelerated Implicit GDA Schemes: Theoretical Guarantees and Application to Proximal Augmented Lagrangian Methods
authors: Jiaqi Liu, Bin Shi
abstract: Convex optimization problems with linear equality constraints arise ubiquitously in scientific computing, machine learning, and control theory. Classical Krylov methods are effective but rely on problem-specific preconditioners and high memory. Conversely, gradient-based methods like the augmented Lagrangian method (ALM) avoid these issues yet suffer from slow outer iterations. Developing accelerated outer-iteration schemes, therefore, remains a critical research objective. In this study, we demonstrate that incorporating a proximal operation into the augmented Lagrangian framework yields the proximal ALM, where the outer iteration is equivalent to an implicit gradient descent-ascent (GDA) scheme. We further establish that this equivalence extends naturally to the setting of variable step sizes. Through Lyapunov analysis, we show that the underlying potential function must be shifted from the conventional objective gap to a variational inequality measure, signaling a shift in perspective from pure convex optimization to minimax optimization. Motivated by these observations, we first develop an implicit GDA scheme with variable step sizes based on a continuous-time ODE framework, which achieves an $o(1/k)$ last-iterate convergence rate for both the primal-dual objective gap and the gradient norm. Building upon a second-order ODE framework, we then propose a family of Nesterov-type implicit GDA schemes parameterized by $r \geq 0$, which achieves an $o(1/k^{r+1})$ last-iterate convergence rate for the primal-dual objective gap. Furthermore, specializing the second-order ODE formulation to the case $r=0$, we derive a corresponding explicit GDA scheme and prove an $o(1/k)$ last-iterate convergence rate for the primal-dual objective gap. Finally, we present several numerical experiments to validate these theoretical results and demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed methods.

title: Polarization-Selective Near-Perfect Absorption via Mie-Type Resonance in van der Waals Anisotropic ReS$_2$/$α$-MoO$_3$/Au Heterostructure
authors: Shoumik Debnath, Sudipta Saha
abstract: We investigate polarization-selective absorption in a visible-wavelength heterostructure consisting of a ReS$_2$ stripe grating, an $α$-MoO$_3$ spacer, and an Au back-reflector using finite-difference time-domain simulations. For an optimized geometry with a grating period of 500 nm, stripe width of 250 nm, and ReS$_2$ thickness of 80 nm, the structure exhibits near-unity absorption of 99.99\% at 650.5 nm under TE-polarized illumination. The resonant field is concentrated near the outer edges of the ReS$_2$ stripe, while absorption power density is localized in the same region, consistent with a localized edge mode. The absorption response depends strongly on polarization, producing a TE--TM resonance separation of 16.2 nm. Replacing either the biaxial ReS$_2$ layer or the anisotropic $α$-MoO$_3$ spacer with isotropic equivalents substantially modifies the spectral response and reduces the polarization-dependent wavelength separation. In addition, rotating the crystal orientation of the ReS$_2$/$α$-MoO$_3$ stack shifts both the resonance wavelength and peak absorption without changing the device geometry. The results show that the combination of anisotropic resonator and spacer layers provides an effective means of controlling resonant absorption and polarization selectivity in van der Waals photonic structures.

title: What is the Long-Term Value of Reliability?
authors: Chenyu Qiu, Xu Kuang, Inessa Liskovich, Ali Rauh, Stefan Wager
abstract: We describe Chronos LTV, a system to measure the long-term impact of delays and other service defects on key business metrics. We use Markov decision processes to model customer interactions over time, and formalize our target estimand as the marginal policy effect with respect to moving the average delay rate. Given this setup, we show that we can identify long-term effects under a sequential unconfoundedness assumption where delays are as good as random given observed order characteristics; and can estimate these effects using a simple covariate-balancing algorithm.

title: LAM-MD: A Large Action Model Integrated Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning Framework for NOMA Networks
authors: Yuhong He, Wenjing Xiao, Miaojiang Chen, Anfeng Liu, Min Chen, Ahmed Farouk, Houbing Herbert Song, Athanasios V. Vasilakos
publish: ACM Transactions on Multimedia Computing, Communications, and Applications
abstract: None

title: Combining Design Thinking and Product Development Tools to Promote Innovation in the Classroom
authors: Rosalino Rodríguez-Calderón, Salvador González-García, Ricardo García-Martínez, J. Jordi Zavala-Morales
abstract: This paper describes an approach to addressing a common issue in engineering education: students often do not follow structured methodologies when tackling projects, either due to a lack of awareness or because they perceive these methodologies as impractical. As a result, their solutions frequently fail to fully meet customer needs, leading to inefficiencies in problem-solving and product development. This misalignment negatively impacts the university-industry relationship, resulting in poor communication and ineffective collaboration. Companies struggle to find graduates with the necessary skills, while students face difficulties applying academic knowledge to real-world contexts. In response to this situation, it is proposed to integrate Design Thinking with structured Product Development Tools, exemplified through a technological project developed for a solar energy company. This combination enables students' creativity to connect with methodologies relevant to industry, offering a more effective framework for engineering education. The implementation of this approach led to the development of a minimum viable product that met the company's stated needs, thus validating its effectiveness in fostering students' capacity for innovation and product development.

title: Random Forest-Based Identification of Defect-Causing Steps in Semiconductor WET Processes
authors: Chung-Yuan Yu, Ming-Lang Kuo, Chin-Che Hsu
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: Defect source identification in semiconductor manufacturing remains a persistent challenge due to being a complex multi-step process. WET cleaning processes are often shared across wafers from different upstream steps, making the root cause of defects difficult to trace. This study introduces a Random Forest (RF)-based machine learning framework to systematically identify defect-causing steps in single type WET cleaning processes. The proposed method encodes the three most recent upstream processes as input features and performs binary wafer classification based on MQC inspection outcomes. Experimental results demonstrate that the RF model achieved high classification accuracy (97%) with a ROC-AUC score of 0.99. Feature importance consistently identified Step A as the dominant contributor to defect generation, which was validated by control wafer experiments. These results demonstrate the potential of AI-driven approaches to accelerate root cause analysis, enhance yield improvement, and provide a scalable framework for defect source identification in semiconductor manufacturing.

title: Multi-Operator Image Resizing Using Saliency-Aware Seam Carving
authors: Abolfazl Rouhizahir, Sana Ghoreyshi, Peyman Ayubi
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Seam carving is a widely used content-aware image retargeting method for resizing digital images. However, the lack of a high-level importance map to guide the seam-selection stage often leads to perceptual content loss or severe distortions. Here, we propose a multi-stage image retargeting pipeline that improves perceptual preservation during image resizing by combining low-level and high-level importance cues. Specifically, three energy maps are computed: gradient, saliency (via BiRefNet), and a parameterized center-prior. Following this, we normalize and linearly fuse them, then drive a classical dynamic-programming seam-selection scheme for content-aware resizing. On the RetargetMe dataset, the proposed pipeline yields consistent qualitative improvements over baseline methods, and relatively high quantitative gains in Aspect Ratio Similarity (ARS).

title: Effect of Microfocused Ultrasound on the Rheological Properties of Crosslinked Hyaluronic Acid: An In Vitro Study
authors: Fernando Lopes Pinheiro, Thais Nogueira Barradas, Aline Raybolt Dos Santos, Wagner Coelho De Albuquerque Pereira
publish: 2026 Global Medical Engineering Physics Exchanges/ Pan American Health Care Exchanges (GMEPE/PAHCE)
abstract: The increasing adoption of minimally invasive facial procedures underscores the need for evidence-based protocols to ensure treatment safety and efficacy. Among energy-based technologies, microfocused ultrasound (MFU) is widely used in aesthetic practice; however, its interaction with previously administered dermal fillers remains insufficiently elucidated. This in vitro study aimed to evaluate the effects of MFU exposure on cross-linked hyaluronic acid fillers through rheological analysis. Samples were subjected to MFU, and changes in viscoelastic behavior were assessed using the storage modulus (G′), loss modulus (G′′), and loss tangent (tan δ). The results revealed measurable alterations in the rheological properties of the filler following MFU exposure, characterized by an increased viscous contribution and a reduced elastic response, suggesting structural rearrangements within the polymeric network induced by mechanical and thermal effects. No complete degradation of the gel structure was observed. These findings indicate that MFU may modify the viscoelastic properties of cross-linked hyaluronic acid, potentially influencing its clinical performance, particularly regarding tissue support and material longevity. This study contributes to a deeper understanding of MFU–filler interactions and supports the need for caution and evidence-based clinical protocols when combining these treatments.

title: Bi-level Capacity Planning and Scheduling of Energy Storage Systems via Multi-Fidelity Quantile-Robust Bayesian Optimization
authors: Ziyang Xiao, Liang Huang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: As the proportion of renewable energy increases, the capacity planning of energy storage is becoming necessary to operate a power system economically. This plan makes up for the complicated two level optimization problem that costs more to compute and has an inborn uncertainty itself. A Multi-Fidelity Quantile Robust Bayesian Optimization-based Method is proposed for Energy Storage Capacity Planning. At the level of searching for the size, full year simulation is merged with typical daily simulation data dual layered sampling. upper quantile confidence interval collecting function accelerates convergence while keeping robustness. Operation Dispatching Level: 2 stage Dynamic Programming - greedy algorithm that splits scheduling into discharging and charging stages to achieve global optimal scheduling. Case studies have demonstrated that this method reduces a lot of computational burden compared with the traditional methods and greatly improves the annual net revenue of the system.

title: Consumable Qualification and Process Optimization in Oxide CMP via RPM-Driven Recipe Engineering
authors: Duraisamy Logamanya Rukmangathan, Jovi Ong, Chee Wing Chang, Chung-Shou Chen, Yew Siew Chong
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: This paper presents a case study describing how operational robustness and device integrity are simultaneously improved by the implementation of a comprehensive alternate-source, 5-zone contour membrane qualification, paired with a rotational velocity-engineered (RPM: rotation per minute Engineered) recipe for oxide chemical planarization (CMP). The strategy enables significant cost reduction while mitigating charging-related anomalies that threaten advanced device performance, particularly in memory operations. The results demonstrate >35% annual savings, improved contact resistance and read & write current stability by linking head rotation torque to device-level electrical performance, and the elimination of all head torque-related alarms and unscheduled tool breakdowns (a 100% reduction).

title: STI Silicon Etch Process Wafer Uniformity Improvement Using Out-of-Phase Coil Current and Motorized Radial Assembly Dial
authors: Pete Pusamlidlert, Edward Ibinson, Ganquan Song, Thien Lai
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: A method for STI (Shallow Trench Isolation) Si etch uniformity improvement has been explored which involves the use of out-of-phase coil current ratio (reverse RF mode) to control inner/outer etch rate and the use of Motorized radial assembly dial (MRAD) to tilt the coil assembly to control etch rate depending on tilt direction. In this paper, the reverse RF mode current ratio has been demonstrated to improve inline critical dimension (CD) uniformity and a combination of MRAD/reverse RF mode current ratio was used to resolve wafer underetch issue. The results indicated that CD sigma can achieve 65.5% reduction from experimental chamber with reverse RF mode current ratio adjustment. As for MRAD, an etch rate skew test showed 44.1% reduction in sigma and combination of MRAD/reverse RF mode current ratio can eliminate wafer edge underetch issue.

title: Provincial-Level Short-Term Load Forecasting Using TimeXer with TCN and Gated Cross-Attention
authors: Yu Gao, Qipei Zhang, Hui Sun, Bo Yang, Wenteng Kuang, Weichao Jiang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Provincial load forecasting is influenced not only by aggregated city-level demand but also by diverse external factors such as weather conditions, which increases modeling complexity. To address this issue, we propose an enhanced TimeXer-based framework for short-term provincial load forecasting by integrating temporal convolutional networks and a gated cross-attention mechanism. This design enables effective modeling of key dependencies between external variables and load dynamics while reducing noise interference. In addition, variational mode decomposition is employed to separate load signals into low-frequency trends and high-frequency fluctuations, allowing joint modeling of long-term patterns and short-term variations. Experiments on real load data from Hunan Province verify that the proposed method achieves superior forecasting accuracy compared with mainstream time-series models.

title: Improving Reading Fluency in Elementary Students: The Role of ICT-Based Learning Tools
authors: Ruth Palacios-Aguilera, Jack Bravo-Torres, Romel Soto-Sarango, Julio Montesdeoca
abstract: Reading fluency is a critical skill for academic and social development, particularly in early elementary education. Yet, many students struggle with speed and accuracy, hindering comprehension and motivation. This study implements an educational intervention using Information and Communication Technologies (ICT) to improve reading fluency among third-grade students. Adopting a qualitative design, the research evaluates how digital tools influence students' reading pace, motivation, and confidence. Results reveal significant fluency improvements and high levels of student engagement with ICT, highlighting their potential as effective pedagogical tools in primary education.

title: Transdisciplinarity Between Leadership and Design in a Computer Science Undergraduate PjBL Course
authors: Filipe Gonçalves de Souza Nogueira da Silva, Guilherme Henrique de Oliveira Cestari
abstract: The education of technology professionals increasingly depends on the integration of diverse fields and bodies of knowledge. In higher education, the adoption of project-based methodologies for training these professionals has become a growing trend. However, this approach requires effective coordination across different domains, whether from an interdisciplinary or transdisciplinary perspective. This paper discusses qualitatively how such integration was achieved between the Design and Leadership axes to support students in developing a sensory mat using the Project-Based Learning (PjBL) model. It concludes by emphasizing the importance of establishing deliberate routines to foster collaboration across knowledge areas and provides suggestions for their implementation, drawing on a specific case study in Brazil. If this kind of initiative is institutionally supported-rather than relying solely on spontaneous collaboration-it has the potential to scale up, leading to pedagogical experiences that are more closely aligned with the educational principles of inter- and transdisciplinarity.

title: A Novel Framework to Convert Public Source URLs to Cryptographic-Enabled API Key
authors: Simar Singh Rayat, Sujal Thapa, Susheela Dahiya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Air Defense and Security (ICADS)
abstract: The development of digital applications has provided ease in human tasks and diverse capabilities. Industrialists and certain organizations have provided Application Programming Interfaces (abbreviated as APIs). This terminology helps developers in adaptive learning and the development of their tools and applications. APIs are generally a package given by various organizations to authorize private or public users to access specific services; they behave like a two-way medium where the user sends a request and the receiver accepts the request, provides the service, and manages the number of requests. This paper demonstrates the working of an API to understand the proposed framework. Today, digital advancement provides various tools to speed up tasks. Looking at this issue, the proposed framework provides a novel inventive application which allows publicly open-access application Uniform Resource Locators (abbreviated as URL) to be converted into a Cryptographic-enabled API key that works normally like a general public API. The detailed methodology is discussed throughout the paper; many applications provide their API keys that work perfectly and without any obstruction, but during model training, they fetch unrelated data that affects the false positive rate. To address this emerging limitation, our proposed framework allows the user to input a specific URL, which is converted into an API key equipped with a cryptography mechanism to limit data tampering and injection, or other related miscellaneous activities. The proposed framework shows that there has been good advancement in mechanically computing and also offers an adaptive and scalable answer to present-day developer infrastructure.

title: Comprehensive DC Fault Characterization and Fast-Stabilizing Control Strategy for Bipolar Hybrid Cascaded MTDC Systems
authors: Muhammad Asim Mond, Zhou Li, Wenwen Mei, Muhammad Akhtar Nawaz, Muhammad Ehab, Muhammad Aurangzeb
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Bipolar hybrid cascaded MTDC system shows a key advancement in long-distance power transmission, integrating the bulk-power efficiency of LCCs with the fast and flexible controllability of MMCs. An example case study is the Baihetan–Jiangsu project in China, which highlights the suitability of this hybrid configuration for large-scale renewable energy delivery. In this study, a detailed characterization of pole-to-pole DC faults is conducted in two critical regions of the bipolar system: the hybrid section between the LCCs and MMCs, and the series LCC section. The dynamic behavior of current, voltage, active power, and reactive power is analyzed to identify the dominant mechanisms influencing instability during these severe fault events. Building upon this initial characterization, an improved coordinated control strategy is developed and re-evaluated through a new fault characterization process. Comparative results reveal that the enhanced control scheme significantly accelerates the system’s stabilization both during startup and following pole-to-pole DC faults. The refined control approach effectively suppresses transient oscillations, restores DC voltage and power balance more rapidly, and improves the post-fault dynamic behavior of both LCCs and MMCs. The new characterization demonstrates that the system achieves stable operation in substantially shorter time, confirming the effectiveness of the improved control framework in enhancing the dynamic stability of bipolar hybrid cascaded MTDC systems under pole-to-pole DC fault conditions.

title: A Multimodal Vision–Language Framework for Yield-Oriented and Explainable Wafer Defect Reasoning
authors: Shih-Chih Lin, Jenq-Neng Hwang, Shang-Hong Lai
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: Wafer defect diagnosis is critical for semiconductor yield management, yet most artificial intelligence (AI) systems treat wafer-map analysis as a pattern-classification task and provide limited support for structured diagnosis.We study structured multimodal post-training for wafer defect diagnosis and introduce WaferMapVQA, which reformulates wafer-map analysis as a five-stage visual question answering (VQA) task: anomaly detection, defect classification, spatial localization, appearance description, and process-informed analysis. Each stage is framed as a four-option multiple-choice question (MCQ), enabling explicit supervision under a compact and auditable output schema.Our post-training follows a two-stage design: supervised fine-tuning (SFT) stabilizes the answer schema, while Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) refines stage-wise reasoning via format, accuracy, and domain-logic rewards.Experiments on wafer maps derived from the public WM811K dataset show improved question-level accuracy and a strong balance between strict image-level correctness and deployment-oriented errors, including fatal misses and false alarms.These results demonstrate that structured post-training, rather than model scaling, is the key to transforming generic VLMs into reliable and deployable wafer-diagnosis systems.

title: A Non-Invasive Ensemble Learning Approach for the Diagnosis of Pancreatic Cancer
authors: Hanif Aghagoli Kasgari, Sekineh Asadi Amiri
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma is a member of the deadliest gastrointestinal malignant tumors, primarily as a result of late identification and the resulting extremely low survival rate. Early detection can significantly improve treatment outcomes. This study aimed to develop an exact and non-invasive approach to enable the swift identification in pancreatic malignancy using machine-learning models and urinary biomarkers. We analyzed data from 590 urine samples obtained from three groups: healthy individuals, patients with benign pancreatic disorders, and patients with pancreatic cancer. The features included age, sex, the serum biomarker CA19-9, and a panel of urinary biomarkers (LYVE-1, REG1B, TFF1, REG1A, and creatinine). Before modeling, data preprocessing involved one-hot encoding for categorical variables, imputing missing values with the median within each class, and standardizing all numerical features. Two ensemble learning algorithms, XGBoost and CatBoost, were employed for three-class classification of the study groups. To enhance accuracy and ensure model robustness, five-fold and ten-fold cross-validation strategies were implemented. The analysis revealed that both models reached a mean accuracy of approximately 90%, representing a marked improvement over previous methods reporting an accuracy of 85.5%. These findings indicate that combining urinary biomarkers with the serum marker CA19-9, together with advanced ensemble machine-learning techniques, provides a practical non-invasive approach for the rapid identification of pancreatic cancer.

title: Fault Identification of Transmission Equipment Under Strong Convective Weather of Northeast Cold Vortex Based on Stochastic Configuration Networks
authors: Shicong Sun, Xuchen Lu, Yi Lin, Guanhua Li, Quanying Liu, Jingwei Han, Zhenchi Zhang, Bingbing Yu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: The Northeast Cold Vortex often triggers extreme convective weather such as strong winds and heavy rains, leading to line breakage faults, transformer failures, and overheating phenomena, which seriously threaten the stable operation of the power grid. Aiming at the problems of complex nonlinear mapping between multi-dimensional meteorological and equipment state features under extreme weather, as well as the poor generalization ability of single prediction models, this paper proposes a fault classification and operational risk assessment method based on the Maximal Information Coefficient (MIC) and AdaBoost-SCN. Firstly, the MIC method is used to deeply analyze and verify the correlation between multi-dimensional meteorological factors and equipment operating states, realizing dimensionality reduction and screening of the full feature vector and accurately extracting key sensitive features with strong nonlinear correlation. Subsequently, the AdaBoost-SCN algorithm is adopted for risk identification of equipment operating states. Experimental results based on the actual meteorological and fault datasets of the power grid show that the average recognition accuracy of the proposed method on the test set reaches over 93.3%, which is 1%~3% higher than that of traditional baseline models, while effectively reducing the computational load of high-dimensional data.

title: Enhancing Acoustics Education Through Experiential Learning: Implementation of an Impedance Tube for Teaching Sound Absorption in Materials
authors: David I. Ibarra-Zarate, Ngoc Quy Hoang, Jose Manuel Nieto-Jalil, Jorge Alvarez, Adrian Isrrael Tec Chim
abstract: This paper presents the design, construction, and educational implementation of a low-cost impedance tube for teaching sound absorption in an undergraduate Acoustics course. The tube, built from carbon steel and integrated with MATLABbased analysis, follows ASTM E1050 and ISO 10534-2 standards for normal-incidence absorption measurements. Technical validation showed absorption curves consistent with literature and manufacturer data, with uncertainties below 1% in amplitude and 0.6° in phase. Tests with foams, carpets, and fibrous panels confirmed accuracy and repeatability, demonstrating suitability for instructional use. Educational evaluation involved 52 students divided into control and experimental groups. The experimental group, which conducted hands-on measurements, achieved higher learning gains (normalized gain 0.56 vs. 0.27), reported greater motivation (90% vs. 55%), and demonstrated stronger analytical skills in laboratory reports. The results indicate that the impedance tube functions not only as a reliable measurement device but also as a pedagogical tool that improves conceptual understanding, engagement, and skill development. This approach offers a scalable pathway to strengthen acoustics education in contexts with limited access to specialized laboratories.

title: Enhancing Industrial Engineering Education through LEGO®-Based Simulation: A Hands-On Approach to Teaching Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE)
authors: Juan Carlos Cisneros Ortega, Astrid Xiomara Rodríguez Castelblanco, Andrés Alberto García-León
abstract: This study proposes a hands-on pedagogical approach for teaching Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) within Industrial Engineering courses through the assembly of a chair using LEGO® pieces. The activity simulates a production environment where students collect operational data on availability, performance, and quality to calculate OEE and identify improvement opportunities. Conducted with undergraduate students organized into teams, the exercise included eight assembly rounds: an initial baseline and second after implementing improvement actions. Statistical analysis using paired t-tests showed a significant increase in OEE mean of 39.52 % mainly due to reduced downtime and defects. The activity enhanced student engagement and understanding of Operational Excellence principles. These results confirm that LEGO-based simulations are an effective, low-cost tool for teaching process efficiency, data analysis, and continuous improvement in industrial engineering education.

title: Selection of Meteorological Key Factors and an Interpretable Identification Method for Transmission Tower Collapse Risk under Severe Convective Weather
authors: Shicong Sun, Xuchen Lu, Guanhua Li, Quanying Liu, Jingwei Han, Sen Gong
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Under extreme hazardous weather such as severe convection, transmission line tower collapse events are characterized by strong abruptness, complex disaster mechanisms, and scarce samples. Traditional defensive standards based on a single absolute wind speed threshold are insufficient for the power grid’s dynamic disaster-prevention needs. To address the issues of few real collapse samples, overfitting of complex models under small-sample conditions, and insufficient interpretability, this study proposes a transmission tower collapse risk identification method based on meteorological key-factor selection and interpretable machine learning. First, short-term sequence meteorological evolution data along towers were extracted from real collapse data samples, and features such as maximum wind speed, maximum U wind, maximum V wind, mean humidity, wind speed range, cumulative precipitation, and wind–rain coupling were constructed. Second, random forest was used to screen key meteorological factors. On this basis, collapse risk identification models based on logistic regression, random forest, and XGBoost were developed. Finally, SHAP was introduced to analyze the contributions of key meteorological factors to model results. Results based on 52 real tower samples indicate that maximum wind speed, maximum U wind, maximum V wind, mean humidity, and wind speed range are the primary meteorological factors affecting collapse risk identification. The random forest model performed best with an AUC of 0.969, XGBoost followed with an AUC of 0.922, while logistic regression performed poorly. SHAP analysis shows that wind-field intensity, wind-direction components, humidity conditions, and wind-speed fluctuations make more significant contributions to the model outputs. The study indicates that transmission tower collapse risk is driven by multi-factor nonlinear characteristics, and the proposed method can effectively improve collapse risk identification under severe convective weather, providing support for proactive disaster prevention and operational decision-making in power grids.

title: A Prediction model of wind and photovoltaic joint power considering multiple meteorological characteristics
authors: Jingyan Zhang, Xi Zhu, Yuxuan Lu, Mengdi Yuan, Qun Chen, Shunjiang Wang, Shida She
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the increasing development of renewable energy resources, the output fluctuation of wind and solar power generation has an increasing impact on the stable operation of the system. It is important to improve the prediction accuracy of renewable energy power generation. Based on this, the key meteorological factors affecting wind and solar power output are analyzed via Spearman’s rank and Pearson correlation coefficients. A hybrid prediction model is developed based on a temporal convolutional network (TCN), a bidirectional long short-term memory (BiLSTM) network, and a gated recurrent unit (GRU) network, which could extract weather features, model temporal dependencies, and capture short-term variations and the complementary nature of wind and solar output. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model achieves better performance than other models in predicting the total output of photovoltaic and wind turbines.

title: TransDot: An Area-efficient Reconfigurable Floating-Point Unit for Trans-Precision Dot-Product Accumulation for FPGA AI Engines
authors: Jiayi Wang, Maohua Nie, Sin-Chen Lin, C.-J. Richard Shi, Ang Li
publish: 2026 IEEE 34th Annual International Symposium on Field-Programmable Custom Computing Machines (FCCM)
abstract: Commercial FPGAs, such as AMD Versal devices, increasingly incorporate AI engines that exploit low-precision packed-SIMD fused multiply–accumulate (FMA) to achieve proportional throughput gains. However, trans-precision FMA (e.g., multiplying two FP16 numbers and adding their result to an FP32 accumulator), which preserves numerical stability by accumulating in higher precision, remains bottlenecked by the highest-precision, lowest-throughput operation. Dot-product accumulation (DPA) (e.g., performing a dot-product on two 4-element FP8 vectors and adding its result to an FP32 accumulator) can fully utilize the input/output bandwidth and computational resources. Existing flexible open-source FPUs, such as FPnew, do not support DPA and implement SIMD FMA on low-precision formats by replicating independent FMA lanes, which increases area, underutilizes shared arithmetic resources, and complicates the integration of DPA operations.This paper presents TransDot, a reconfigurable FPU that unifies multi-precision SIMD FMA and trans-precision DPA within a shared, reconfigurable datapath. TransDot extends the baseline design with 2-term FP16, 4-term FP8, and 8-term FP4 dot-product accumulation into FP32 using reconfigurable subcomponents. Evaluation shows that TransDot delivers 2× FP16, 4× FP8, and 8× FP4 throughput via DPA with FP32 accumulation, and 1.46× area efficiency in FP16 DPA and 2.92× area efficiency in FP8 DPA, at the cost of 37.3% larger area on average and an additional pipeline stage in dot-product mode compared to the FPnew baseline. These results demonstrate that TransDot’s area-efficient design enables scalable deployment in next-generation AMD Versal AI engines.

title: Reconfigurable Computing Challenge: Deploying SmolVLA Model on an AMD XDNA NPU
authors: Xiaoyu Liang, Elaine Cao, Chun-Ning Kao, David Lin, Jiajie Li, Zhiru Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 34th Annual International Symposium on Field-Programmable Custom Computing Machines (FCCM)
abstract: Vision–Language–Action (VLA) models have demonstrated strong generalization across robotic tasks and embodiments. However, current deployments rely on GPU-based inference that is power inefficient for edge platforms, motivating the exploration of more efficient hardware targets. We present an initial exploration of the deployment of a VLA model on an AMD XDNA1 NPU, targeting SmolVLA and using Allo, a Python-based dataflow DSL. Operational intensity profiling identifies GEMM as the dominant kernel and the primary NPU target. To support SmolVLA’s wide dynamic shape variation, we develop a pre-cached profiling database augmented by an LLM-assisted tiling agent. Normalization and activation kernels are further optimized through vectorization and approximations. This work results in a functionally correct end-to-end prototype. As an initial exploration, we also discuss current limitations and optimization opportunities toward practical VLA deployment on NPUs.

title: Optimal Configuration Design of DC Collection and Transmission System of Large-Scale Offshore Wind-Solar Power Plant
authors: Qian Wu, Yuchao Zheng, Yang Wang, Linyuan Wang, Huiqun Wang, Zhichao Yang, Yu Wang, Bingtuan Gao
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Considering limited land resources and continuously increasing power demand, offshore renewable energy, namely wind and photovoltaic (PV) solar, have emerged as important targets for development. All direct current (DC) integration scheme for large-scale offshore wind-solar power plants becomes a promising solution over conventional alternating current (AC) based integration schemes suffering from inherent limitations such as reactive power losses. This paper comparatively investigates three typical topologies of DC collecting and transmission systems for offshore wind-solar power plant, namely the two-stage topology, the centralized topology, and the generator-side topology, along with their representative DC transformer structures. Meanwhile, a quantitative techno-economic evaluation of the three topology configurations is performed according to a planning capacity of 2 gigawatt (2GW), with a voltage level of ±500 kilovolt (±500 kV) offshore wind-solar power plant project in Jiangsu Province, China, which yields the optimal selection of topology configuration including the key equipment of DC transformer. The results demonstrate the technological and engineering feasibility of all-DC integration schemes for future large-scale offshore wind-solar power plants.

title: AI-Driven Psychoeducational Analytics for Early Detection of Academic Risk in Colombian Schools
authors: Ruth Y. Zuleta Torres, Dixon Salcedo Morillo, Raúl Ramírez Velarde, Pedro López Pérez
abstract: This article presents the development of an academic management model integrating artificial intelligence, aimed at the early prediction of school failure risk. The study employed scientifically validated psychometric instruments, from which multi-stage databases were constructed, incorporating variables related to reading comprehension, mental health, spelling, and mathematical performance. Regression and clustering algorithms were compared with traditional linear approaches, showing a substantial enhancement in predictive performance. The proposed model is designed to support institutional data-driven decision-making and to guide timely pedagogical interventions. The findings confirm the high potential of artificial intelligence as a tool to strengthen educational inclusion, optimize academic management processes, and improve school performance in public education contexts marked by socioeconomic vulnerability. These findings lay the groundwork for scalable, ethical AI systems supporting inclusive education.

title: Promoting Research Skills in EDUNINE 2026 Doctoral Symposium
authors: María Isabel Pozzo
abstract: Doctoral education is a challenge that encompasses individual, institutional, national, and international efforts to ensure the development of research skills. In this context, EDUNINE Conference 2026 offered a specific event for those professionals who are engaging in a research career at the highest level of education. The Doctoral Symposium was planned as a workshop devoted to reflecting on the process of building a research project at doctoral level while systematizing its core concepts in a practical way withing the current scenario. This paper states the theoretical foundation based on Willison et al, systematizes the proposal and features, and concludes on the potential and limitations of a doctoral symposium within the International Conference, based on EDUNINE 2026. By these objectives, this paper aims to contribute to spreading Doctoral Symposiums as a promising teaching strategy for doctoral students and a helping hand for doctoral supervisors.

title: Unsupervised Deep Autoencoder-Based Clustering for mining the Metabolic Syndrome
authors: Hanieh Palouj, Mojtaba Hajihasani
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Metabolic syndrome (MetS) encompasses a set of interrelated metabolic disturbances such as impaired insulin sensitivity, excess abdominal fat accumulation, abnormal lipid profiles, and high blood pressure, leading to an increased risk of cardiovascular complications and type 2 diabetes. Despite the widespread use of the ATP III criteria for MetS diagnosis, these rule-based thresholds may not fully capture transitional metabolic states. This study aims to identify hidden metabolic subgroups beyond the traditional ATP III definition using a deep autoencoder-based unsupervised clustering approach. A hospital-based cross-sectional dataset of 2,555 subjects aged 20–69 years was analyzed. A stacked deep autoencoder was developed to learn latent feature representations from 23 clinical and biochemical variables. The encoded features were clustered using the K-Means algorithm. The trained autoencoder obtained a minimum reconstruction error (MSE = 0.018), and clustering within the latent space revealed three substantial distinct groups: healthy, transitional, and MetS. The transitional group displayed mild abnormalities in glucose, triglycerides, and BMI, indicating an early metabolic deviation that is not captured by the ATP III rule-based thresholds. The proposed framework effectively captured nonlinear relationships among metabolic variables among metabolic variables, enabling the identification of either an intermediate metabolic cluster or subtype of MetS. This finding supports early detection and highlights the potential need to revisit the current MetS definitional boundaries.

title: Imputation of Missing Data in IoT Using an MLP Neural Network Optimized by the GOA
authors: Elham Bideh, Samaneh Emami
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Missing data is one of the inevitable challenges in Internet of Things (IoT) systems, particularly in real-time environmental monitoring applications. Traditional imputation methods often fail to achieve an effective trade-off between prediction accuracy and computational efficiency. To overcome this limitation, this paper proposes a novel hybrid approach that integrates a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) neural network with the Grasshopper Optimization Algorithm (GOA) for predicting missing values in time-series sensor data. In the proposed model, the MLP hyperparameters, including the number of neurons in the hidden layer and the learning rate, are optimized by the GOA to enhance prediction performance. Experimental validation is carried out using an air quality dataset, and the results are compared with those of a General Regression Neural Network (GRNN) and a simple, non-optimized MLP model. The results demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly reduces imputation errors and achieves a substantial improvement in the Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) compared with the GRNN and simple MLP models. Therefore, the proposed framework provides a reliable solution for accurate data reconstruction in IoT-based environments.

title: Modeling analysis of thermal and stress behavior of energy storage cabin with sodium ion battery
authors: Ying Meng, Qinqin Liang, Fangyuan Han, Min Yu, Bin Tang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the rapid global energy transition, energy storage technology is crucial for supporting large-scale renewable energy integration. Sodium-ion battery energy storage systems show significant promise due to their abundant resources, low cost, and high safety. However, thermal stress induced during constant power cycles, especially under high temperatures, poses a critical challenge to the safety and stability of containerized energy storage system. This study focuses on the prediction of strength, stiffness, and fatigue life for a square sodium-ion battery energy storage container. A multi-physics coupling model integrating electro, thermal and structural interactions was developed. Simulations under various constant power rates were conducted to analyze temperature distribution, stress fields, and displacement fields. Results indicate that maximum temperature increases significantly with power, reaching 45.8°C at 1P. Structural stress enters 134-141 MPa at 0.5P, concentrating at frame joints and corner posts. Displacement increases with power, peaking at 0.664 mm, primarily at the top and ends of the container. Using carbon steel offers limited safety margins under medium-high power cycles, suggesting high strength steel for enhanced reliability. Design recommendations include incorporating forced ventilation for high power scenarios and maintaining assembly gaps over 1 mm to accommodate thermal displacement. The established methodology provides a scientific basis for structural optimization, material selection, and fatigue life assessment of grid scale energy storage system containers.

title: Growgreen: A Machine Learning-Based Crop Recommendation and Whatsapp-Controlled Smart IoT Greenhouse
authors: Rakib Hasan, Fardin Ahmed Ashan, Fahmida Ahmed Antara, Jahedul Islam
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: In this paper, GrowGreen is introduced as a low-cost and affordable IoT-enabled smart greenhouse that incorporates the real-time monitoring capabilities, WhatsApp-based actuation, and machine learning-based crop recommendation. The system integrates ESP32 and Arduino UNO microcontrollers and environmental sensors and actuators to allow automatic regulation of climate. The system utilizes a solar panel primarily for charging the lithium-ion battery to ensure sustainable power delivery. In this system, a piezoelectric sensor is incorporated to detect vibrations and convert them into usable energy, thereby enhancing the system with a reusable energy feature. An Express.js backend supporting WebSockets and MongoDB can be used to synchronize real-time data and a React-based dashboard can be used to provide constant visualization and ML inference. A trained Random Forest classifier on augmented agricultural data sets attains the accuracy of 95.91% in crop recommendations. Experiments exhibit a 1-3 second command latency, and a strong environmental control. GrowGreen is a user environmentallyfriendly, scalable solution to the automation of greenhouses, particularly where connectivity and power resources are limited and intermittent.

title: A Hybrid Taxonomy-Aware Efficient Framework for Protein Function Prediction
authors: Md. Neamul Haque, Asma Akter, Md. Mahbubur Rahman, Md Istiak Tanvir, AL-Amran Mozumder, Asma Joshita Trisha, Tanni Dhoom
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The exponential growth of genomic data has widened the annotation gap leaving millions of protein sequences without experimentally verified functions. While Protein Language Models (PLMs) like ESM-2 have revolutionized automated function prediction by capturing deep evolutionary semantics, they face two critical limitations which one is prohibitive computational costs and another is taxonomic bias where models often hallucinate biologically impossible functions for non-model organisms. In this study, we propose a Taxonomy-Aware Efficient Protein Predictor, a hybrid architecture designed to democratize high-performance functional annotation. Our approach integrates a frozen, lightweight ESM-2 backbone (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 M}$</tex> parameters) with a learnable taxonomic embedding layer, explicitly conditioning predictions on the organism's evolutionary context. Evaluation on the CAFA-6 dataset demonstrates that this resource-efficient model outperforms both homology-based baselines (BLAST) and sequence-only deep learning models, particularly in the Molecular Function (MF) ontology. Crucially, the inclusion of taxonomic metadata significantly reduces false positives in the twilight zone of low sequence identity, such as preventing the erroneous assignment of eukaryotic cellular components to prokaryotic proteins. The <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F}$</tex> _max comparison and qualitative analysis proved that our approach offers a resource-efficient alternative to massive foundation models, demonstrating that integrating biological context allows lightweight architectures to achieve state-of-the-art performance in protein function prediction.

title: A Hybrid YOLO-EfficientNet Framework for Fine-Grained Vegetable Recognition in Dense Multi-Object Images
authors: Ariful Islam, Sajeeb Kumar Ray, Sadia Afroz Oishi, Md. Anwar Hossain, Md Akash Miya, Shubail Haque Turza, Moumita Haque Mohona, Muntasir Mamun
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Fine-grained vegetable recognition in cluttered realworld environments poses great challenges for the automation in agriculture due to occlusion, scale variation and high visual similarity among species. In response to this, this research proposes a novel hybrid framework, which integrates the strengths of YOLOv8m for robust object localization and EfficientNetB0 for accurate fine-grained classification. Our key contribution is the design and comprehensive evaluation of this two-stage pipeline on a novel, densely annotated dataset of 3,534 images from Bangladesh markets, featuring over 86,000 instances across 22 imbalanced vegetable classes. The model reports a perfect precision (1.00) of classification for all the classes, confirming its reliability from a classification point of view. But an overall recall of 0.50 indicates that the existing model still suffers from a long-standing detection bottleneck for small or occluded objects, achieving a micro-averaged F1-score of 0.66. This work gives a highly accurate baseline for automation in sorting systems and crucially highlights increasing detection recall as the most important challenge in the direction of future research on realworld agricultural AI.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Based Hybrid Control Approach for a Class of Underactuated Mechanical Systems
authors: Tengfei Zhang, Yana Yang, Xiaoshuang Zhou, Ying Zhang, Changchun Hua
abstract: Underactuated mechanical systems are pivotal in industrial applications due to their lightweight design and flexibility. Most control methods designed for such systems are either tailored to specific types or feature complex continuous controller structures, whose discretization in practical implementations may lead to performance degradation due to sampling and quantization effects. To this end, an advanced hybrid control approach is developed directly based on a discrete-time model, ensuring consistency and improved performance in digital control environments. Unlike single-method control techniques, the proposed design synergistically integrates feedforward and feedback control, combining the accuracy and disturbance rejection capability of feedback control with the timeliness of feedforward control. Moreover, a reinforcement learning (RL) optimization strategy is then employed to achieve synergistic adaptive adjustment of both the feedforward shaping parameters and the feedback control gains. Notably, a composite signal is designed to incorporate information related to the underactuated state into the feedback controller, enhancing coupling so that the underactuated variables achieve the desired response. Finally, the experimental results on double-pendulum crane and tower crane systems verify the effectiveness and advantages of the proposed approach.

title: Low-Power Ultra-Broadband Supercontinuum Generation in a Compact AlGaAs Rib Waveguide with Three Zero-Dispersion Wavelengths
authors: Md. Zia Uddin Azad Sifat, Mohammad Sakib Raihan, Sagar Mutsuddi, Tanha Zaman, Mohammad Istiaque Reja
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: A highly compact 0.9 mm long rib waveguide has been designed with core of AlGaAs and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{MgF}_{2}$</tex> as cladding, enhancing nonlinear parameter. Optimizing the core height, width and slab thickness, three zero dispersion wavelengths has been yielded at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.06,5.97$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7.72 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$</tex> in our optimized design. An ultra-broadband supercontinuum generation from 1.7-13 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>, spanning nearly 2.7 octaves, has been generated at just 1.6 kW peak power using a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.1 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$</tex> pump with 50 fs pulse. To the best of the author's knowledge, this is the broadest supercontinuum reported to date using AlGaAs with such a low pump power. The spectrum has been further extended to 2-16 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu$</tex> m (3 octaves) with increasing power up to 10 kW. This low-power octavespanning supercontinuum generation, achieved with minimal loss in a compact and cost-effective waveguide, demonstrates strong potential for MIR applications, such as spectroscopy, metrology, and gas detection.

title: Enhancing Stability and Transient Response of LLC Resonant Converters Using a Reduced-Order Model with Pole-Zero Cancellation
authors: Dohong Lee, Bonggook Kim, Younghoon Cho
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: LLC resonant converters are widely adopted for their high efficiency, but their control design poses significant challenges due to the complex beat-frequency double pole dynamics that shift with operating conditions. Conventional control methods often restrict bandwidth to ensure stability, resulting in poor transient responses. This paper proposes a robust pole-canceling compensator to achieve both high control bandwidth and fast transient response. First, a reduced-order second-order small-signal model is derived. Based on this model, a pole-canceling compensator is synthesized to effectively eliminate the resonant peak, enabling a potentially higher crossover frequency with sufficient stability margins. The proposed control strategy offers a systematic design methodology that yields a practical low-order controller, facilitating straightforward implementation without computational burdens. The proposed method is compared with a conventional design, demonstrating superior dynamic performance and robustness against the parameter variations. Experimental results on a 500-W prototype confirm that the proposed scheme significantly reduces settling time and eliminates high-frequency ringing compared to conventional designs.

title: A Bayesian game framework for service function chaining in NFV with incomplete information
authors: Shuting Le, Yuhu Wu, Carmen Del Vecchio, Zhengtian Wu, Benlian Xu
abstract: Service function chain (SFC) routing in Network Function Virtualization (NFV) is challenging when users share physical servers but keep their traffic profiles private. This letter formulates SFC routing as a Bayesian game where each traffic load is a private type. We prove that the game is a weighted Bayesian Potential Game and derive, via the semi-tensor product, a closed-form Bayesian Potential Function, ensuring the existence of a pure Bayesian Nash Equilibrium (BNE). The ex-ante transformation reduces BNE computation to finite potential minimisation, enabling distributed strict best-response convergence without disclosure of realised traffic types. In a 2-user, 8-function NFV example, the proposed BNE attains a cost within 1.3% of the full-information oracle, reduces expected total cost by 3.9% over a mean-load baseline and by 26.6% over congestion-blind greedy routing.

title: Spectral State Fusion Tree Mamba for Hyperspectral Image Classification
authors: Bing Tu, Zhenghao Hu, Bo Liu, Yan He
abstract: Hyperspectral image (HSI) data possess complex spatial structures and high-dimensional spectral information. Mamba has been applied to address the limitations of general methods in HSI classification, including restricted receptive fields and high computational complexity. However, the scan mechanism of traditional Mamba unreasonably constructs spatial distance relationships between neighboring row pixels and fails to adaptively construct the optimal scanning path based on the spectral similarity of pixels. Additionally, the characteristic of traditional Mamba scanning each channel independently overlooks the feature extraction from high-dimensional spectral information. This work proposes a Spectral State Fusion Tree Mamba (SSFTM) architecture to resolve these limitations. The Tree Scan (TS) mechanism computes cosine distances among spatial neighboring pixels and spectral channels to construct adaptive minimum spanning trees in both the spatial and spectral domains, thereby establishing reasonable spatial–spectral relationships and enabling efficient joint feature extraction. The Spectral State Fusion (SSF) mechanism applies multi-layer one-dimensional dilated convolutions along the spectral dimension to the state space vectors, enabling inter-channel interaction and promoting multi-scale spectral feature extraction. The proposed SSFTM demonstrates superior classification accuracy across multiple datasets compared to SOTA methods and exhibits acceptable computational complexity. The code is available at https://github.com/copawloroous/SSFTM.

title: PIVOT: A Framework for High-Fidelity Facade PV Layouts via Generative Seeding and Differentiable Optimization_supp1-3701997.pdf
authors: Ruyu Liu
abstract: The automation of Building-Integrated Photovoltaic (BIPV) design is essential for the digital transformation of urban renewable energy, yet current methods struggle to bridge the gap between semantic facade assessment and engineering-grade installation plans. Existing AI-based approaches typically yield raw segmentation masks that lack the geometric precision and physical validity required for real-world deployment. This paper presents PIVOT, a novel end-to-end framework for the automated synthesis of high-fidelity, collision-free PV layouts from single 2D facade images. We formulate the layout generation as a two-stage neuro-symbolic and differentiable optimization process. First, an LLM-based programmatic seeding stage uses structured facade information and elementary spatial-relation cues to generate initial layout hypotheses. Second, a differentiable layout optimization engine refines these proposals by treating panel placement as a continuous constrained optimization problem, effectively resolving spatial overlaps while maximizing geometric regularity. Validated on a diverse dataset of 80 building facades, experimental results demonstrate that PIVOT significantly outperforms heuristic (MaxRects) and evolutionary (MOGA) baselines. Our framework reduces the layout collision rate from 9.7% to 0.7% and improves geometric regularity by over 10%, while requiring only approximately 80 seconds of processing time per building. This work advances BIPV design automation by converting semantically parsed facade information into geometrically constrained, constructibility-oriented PV layouts for downstream performance evaluation.

title: Cache-Aware Generative NLP: Reducing Latency in Large-Scale Multilingual Systems
authors: Arnob Aich Anurag, Md. Nasif Rafidi, Shamiul Islam, Sultanul Arifeen Hamim, Muhammad Firoz Mridha
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Large-scale multilingual generative models face significant computational bottlenecks due to repetitive linguistic computations across languages. While existing caching mechanisms focus on token-level optimization, they overlook the computational redundancy in morphological and syntactic processing that occurs during multilingual text generation. We introduce Adaptive Linguistic Computation Caching (ALCC), a novel framework that identifies and caches expensive linguistic transformation computations during the generation process. ALCC employs a three-tier caching architecture: (1) Morphological Transformation Cache for expensive inflection computations, (2) Syntactic Pattern Cache for grammatical structure generation, and (3) Cross-Language Transfer Cache that leverages typological similarities to share computational patterns between related languages. Our approach introduces a Linguistic Complexity Predictor that dynamically identifies high-cost linguistic operations and prioritizes them for caching. Experiments on morphologically rich languages (Finnish, Turkish, Arabic) show an average of 75.8% latency reduction across 12 models on curated settings; gains vary with typological similarity and warm-up. in generation tasks while maintaining identical output quality. This work represents the first systematic approach to caching linguistic computations in generative multilingual systems.

title: A Real-Time Multi-Modal Conversational Agentic AI Framework for Automated Demographic Data Collection and Analytical Visualization
authors: Abdullah Al Zubaer, Md. Faishal Ahmed, Abdul Hasib, Bakhtiar Muiz, Md. Ashiqussalehin, Saurav Chandra Das
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Traditional methods of demographic data collection such as structured surveys are facing significant challenges such as poor engagement of participants, high dropout rates and lengthy processing delays. This study a new real-time and multimodal conversational Agentic AI system that can overcome these constraints by means of natural dialogue interface. Our system uses Large Language Models (LLMs) for advanced natural language processing (NLP) capabilities, combined with WebSocketbased communication that seamlessly extract structured demographic data from unstructured responses. The architecture relies on React for the interface and FastAPI for the backend. We also integrated speech recognition and synthesis to ensure the user experience is fully multi-modal and accessible. We used a statemanagement algorithm to handle the dialogue flow, which allows the system to collect and verify demographic data as the user speaks. Extensive experimental analysis shows that our method has a 94.5 % F1-score on participant name extraction, it is 40 60 % faster than conventional form-based methods in terms of data collection period, and it increases participant engagement rates by 60 %. The system includes real-time analytics for instant data visualization and export. This framework bridges the gap between traditional surveys and modern AI.

title: Research on Medical Text Information Fusion and Cerebral Infarction Auxiliary Diagnosis Based on Large Language Models
authors: Desheng Liu, Zilong Chen, Sen Ji, Xuyang Zheng, Xiaobing Zhang, Zihan Zhou, Yunxiang Zhang, Guangyao Sun
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Statistics, Modeling and Advanced Algorithms (ASMA)
abstract: Cerebral infarction is a neurological disorder with high incidence and disability rates, where early recognition and diagnosis are crucial for reducing mortality. Traditional prediction models primarily rely on imaging analysis or single diagnostic indicators, struggling to fully leverage semantic information within medical records and imaging reports. This paper proposes TextFusion-QwenStroke, a text-based multi-source fusion framework. This model achieves auxiliary diagnosis of cerebral infarction through fine-tuning on Qwen2.5-VL-3B. By integrating structured diagnostic data, unstructured medical records, and imaging report texts, the model enables risk prediction without relying on raw imaging data. A multi-layer Transformer architecture achieves text feature fusion, while a lightweight semantic alignment loss function ensures multi-source data consistency. Using the ‘stroke-risk-prediction-dataset’ from Kaggle and hospital-collected datasets, the model achieved an AUC of 0.901 and an F1 score of 0.872, significantly outperforming both logistic regression and unimodal language models. These results demonstrate the broad potential of large language models for disease prediction in scenarios involving medical text semantic understanding and where imaging examinations are unavailable.

title: Hate Speech Classification using Hybrid Deep Learning Framework and Explainable AI
authors: Ashfia Jannat Keya, Nourin Khandaker, Muhammad Nazrul Islam
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Social media hate speech has increased significantly, necessitating robust and automated detection systems. Traditional natural language processing methods often struggle to capture complex semantic dependencies, sarcasm, and contextual cues inherent in hateful content. Therefore, we present a hybrid deep-learning architecture that integrates BERT-based contextual embeddings with Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Residual Networks (ResNet) to enhance the accuracy of hate speech classification. To mitigate the effects of class imbalance, data balancing techniques are applied prior to model training, enabling more reliable learning for minority classes. In addition to predictive performance, model interpretability is addressed using LIME and SHAP, which provide local explanations by highlighting word-level contributions that influence classification decisions. Experimental results show that the proposed model consistently performs better than several baseline and hybrid deep-learning approaches with 94% accuracy and 94% F1-score, while the explainability analysis confirms that the model's predictions are driven by meaningful and contextually relevant linguistic cues.

title: Research on Hub Location Problem Based on the Integration of Deep Ranking and Genetic Algorithms
authors: Cong Wu, Ruofan Dai, Aoying Shen, Congcong Tian
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Statistics, Modeling and Advanced Algorithms (ASMA)
abstract: The Single Allocation Hub Location Problem is a classical combinatorial optimization problem in transportation and logistics network design. Its NP-hard nature leads to significant computational challenges for large-scale instances. To address this, we introduce a genetic algorithm enhanced by deep learning–based structural priors. Pre-learned node rankings from an existing deep ranking model are incorporated into both the initialization and evolutionary stages, providing node-level hub potential based on network topology and demand patterns. These structural priors guide the search toward promising regions of the solution space, improving convergence speed and solution quality. Experiments on standard Australian Post and Civil Aeronautics Board datasets demonstrates that the proposed method achieves lower total cost, faster convergence, and higher stability than conventional heuristics, indicating strong practical value and potential for wider application.

title: Uncertainty-Aware and Explainable Feature-Level Ensembles for CT-Based Kidney Stone Detection
authors: Abdullah Khondoker, Faria Ahmed Richi, Sarah Binte Islam, Kazi Tanvir, Dipta Justin Gomes, Mahfujur Rahman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Kidney stone condition happens often and comes back again, so it is important to find stones early with Computed Tomography(CT) scans to prevent serious problems and help guide treatment.. This work proposes a CT-specific preprocessing pipeline and a feature-level ensemble model for slice-wise classification on a publicly available axial CT dataset. An image preprocessing and augmentation pipeline was used to enhance stone visibility and improve data quality before training the classification models. An ensemble model(ResNet18+DenseNet121) is constructed by concatenating 512- and 1,024-dimensional features and passing them through a multilayer perceptron fusion head. Trained with AdamW and early stopping (100 -epoch budget, best at epoch 23), the ensemble achieves a test accuracy of 0.9944, precision of 1.0000, recall of 0.9888, F1-score of 0.9944, Matthews correlation coefficient of 0.9889, and ROC-AUC of 0.9993, outperforming all single backbones. Three-fold crossvalidation yields a mean F1-score of 0.9663 with low variance, indicating robust generalization. Monte Carlo dropout was used to estimate how confident the model is in its predictions, allowing us to identify uncertain cases and support safer, more selective clinical decision-making, while LIME, SHAP, and Grad-CAM highlight clinically plausible stone regions. These results demonstrate that a carefully designed feature-level ensemble, combined with rigorous evaluation, uncertainty estimation, and explainable AI, can deliver reliable and interpretable support for CT-based kidney stone triage.

title: Diff-AS: Diffusion-Augmented Strategy for Chrysanthemum Leaf Classification
authors: Sonjoy Prosad Shaha, Abir Ahmed, Md Hasan Ahmad, Amatul Bushra Akhi, Mahmudul Hasan Badhan, Md. Atiqur Rahman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The Chrysanthemum (Chrysanthemum morifolium), an economically and aesthetically valuable ornamental plant, is highly vulnerable to leaf diseases that reduce yield and quality. Traditional management approaches often fall short due to unpredictable disease prevalence and limited data availability. While deep learning (DL) models show promise for automated disease detection, their effectiveness is constrained by scarce and imbalanced datasets. To address this challenge, our proposed Diff-AS, a novel diffusion-augmented strategy that leverages Stable Diffusion models fine-tuned with DreamBooth for generating realistic synthetic chrysanthemum leaf images. Our pipeline integrates generative augmentation with an EfficientNetB0-based classification framework, supported by additional loss functions (prior, instance, and L1) to enhance fidelity during denoising. Experimental results demonstrate that incorporating synthetic images with original samples significantly improves performance, achieving 94.88% accuracy, 96 % precision, 91 % recall, and 93% F1-score. These findings highlight the potential of diffusion-driven augmentation to overcome data scarcity and imbalance, offering a scalable and robust solution for plant disease classification in real-world, resource-constrained environments.

title: Explainable Multi-Model Prediction of Suicide Mortality Rates using TabNet, SVM, and HistGradientBoosting with Demographic Stratification
authors: Fatema Tuj Tarannom Esty, Tahmina Islam
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Suicide remains a major global public health concern, influenced by a complex interplay of mental, social, and demographic factors. An explainable multi-model framework is developed in this work for the prediction of suicide mortality rates by integrating TabNet and Support Vector Machine classifiers after demographic stratification to further the explanation and prediction. Data preprocessing steps are given special emphasis for achieving high predictive performance, e.g., encoding categorical variables, scaling features, and imputing missing values. TabNet confers feature importance through its attention mechanism, whereas the SVM acts as a powerful competing modeling technique optimized through GridSearchCV. This ensemble is later joined by HistGradientBoosting as the third competing classifier. The dataset used was extracted from Kaggle— “Death Rates by Suicide (2000’2018)”, which was originally published on the official U.S. government database website. Experiments on public health data consisting of age, gender, race, and yearly suicide death rates show high accuracy for classification-rate results: TabNet (91%), SVM (87%), and HistGradientBoosting (98%). Model performance, decision transparency, and interpretability are later evaluated through ROC-AUC and SHAP analysis. This displays the importance of explainable AI in public health and emphasizes the delicate balance between predictability and interpretability when assessing suicide risk and implementing preventive measures.

title: Embodied Teaching for Powered Exoskeletons via Variance-Decomposed Impedance Learning
authors: Jeongsu Park, Hyojun An, Hyeongjun Kim, Kyeongsu Shi, Jimin Youn, Gunhee Lee, Jinsu Park, Jongwon Kim, Abhiraj Singh, Kyoungchul Kong
abstract: This article proposes an intuitive motion generation framework for powered exoskeletons designed to assist individuals with complete paraplegia. While learning from demonstration is traditionally applied to collaborative robots, “Embodied Teaching” is adopted in this study, where the demonstrator physically interacts with the exoskeleton while wearing it. The core contribution of this work is the simultaneous extraction of kinematic motion trajectories and phase-dependent impedance profiles, which capture the empirically proven stability characteristics from the demonstration. A critical aspect of the proposed approach is the methodological decoupling of position variance associated with purposeful compliance from variance arising from spatiotemporal human inconsistency (execution noise). By isolating the variance dominated by impedance modulation, a variable impedance controller is designed to emulate functional impedance. Experimental validation indicates that the proposed method improves gait robustness under unmodeled load variations. Compared to conventional high-gain position control and variable impedance control without variance isolation, the proposed framework effectively reduces state-space dispersion and impact shocks, highlighting the practical utility of the embodied teaching approach in powered exoskeletons.

title: Novel Self-aligned Flat Source Contact Architecture for Low On-resistance SiC Trench MOSFETs
authors: Akifumi Iijima, Takaaki Tominaga, Shiro Hino, Yutaka Fukui, Kohei Adachi, Hideyuki Hatta, Yasuhiro Kagawa, Tatsuro Watahiki
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: Silicon carbide (SiC)-based trench MOSFETs are increasingly adopted in next-generation power devices due to their superior physical properties. In prior studies, the channel and JFET resistances have not been reduced effectively, and a wide trench pitch has been observed, which degrades the breakdown voltage and oxide reliability. In this study, we present a novel Flat Source Contact (FSC) architecture for SiC trench MOSFETs, in which the interlayer dielectric (ILD) is embedded within the trench. This configuration mitigates the requirement for alignment along the direction perpendicular to the trench, thereby reducing the trench pitch and increasing channel density. Moreover, it suppresses electric-field peaks at the bottom p-well (BPW) by reducing the trench pitch. Fabrication demonstrates negligible gate leakage and a reduction of 29% in the specific on-resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on,sp</inf>) at a breakdown voltage of approximately 1500 Fig. 1. Schematic diagrams of (a) a conventional MOSFET and (b) a V at room temperature. Thus, the FSC design achieves lower novel MOSFET named Flat Source Contact (FSC) structure. R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on,sp</inf> with enhanced manufacturability. Fully self-aligned device process enables continued trench-pitch scaling, thereby reducing on-resistance.

title: OvFusionX-Net: An Explainable Deep Learning Model with Attention Mechanism for Early-Stage Ovarian Cancer Classification
authors: Neyamul Islam, Shimanto Kumar, Md Sadi Al Huda, Md. Shamsul Alam, Md. Asraf Ali, Touhid Bhuiyan
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
