abstract: Ovarian cancer diagnosis at the early stages remains one of the most difficult tasks in the medical field because of the minute histopathological variations and non-specific symptoms. In this paper, the authors have proposed a deep learning-based approach called OvFusionX-Net, which can automatically classify ovarian cancer from histopathological images. OvFusionX-Net uses a convolutional neural network to automatically discover morphological patterns between malignant and non-malignant ovarian cells. To verify the integrity and quality assessment of the results, the authors have eliminated duplicate images by pixel-level MD5 hashing. Stratified data partitioning and data augmentation are also performed to verify the accuracy and consistency of the results. The proposed approach uses accuracy, precision, and F1-measure as the performance metrics to show the superior performance and accuracy in classifying ovarian cancer. To provide visual explanations, the authors have utilized Grad-CAM, a technique in artificial intelligence. Results show that the proposed approach is really effective and provides interpretable explanations.

title: Federated Learning Enhancing Fraud Detection Across Multiple Financial Institutions
authors: Sushree Sangeeta Mukhi, P. Jayapriya, Syed Hassan Imam Gardezi, Charu Dutta, V. Manimegalai, Srijib Shankar Jha, R. G. Nirmala
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: In this paper, Research discuss how Federated Learning can be used to improve fraud detection across various financial institutions using a new model that is built on Federated Reinforcement Learning (RL) and PrivacyPreserving Federated Averaging (FedAvg). With the help of TensorFlow Federated (TFF), we present a scalable and secure system in which institutions can work together in detecting fraud without compromising sensitive financial information. Federated RL component is adjusted to recent patterns of fraud, whereas FedAvg guarantees the effectiveness of updating the model and maintains privacy. According to our experiments, the proposed approach is much more effective than conventional centralized models, in terms of bettering the accuracy of fraud detection (by 25 percent) and decreasing the risk of data leakage (by 30 percent). The findings show that federated learning can be enhanced with reinforcement learning and privacy protection to offer a powerful solution to the real-time and collaborative fraud detection in different financial institutions, which can make financial ecosystems safer.

title: Exploring Learner Perceptions in Job-Oriented Online Courses Using Review Sentiment Analysis
authors: Utsha Roy, Abdus Sattar, Md. Sabbir Khan, Md. Mahedi Hassan, Masuma Parvin, Shanjida Hossain Shefa
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Sentiment analysis is crucial for understanding learner perceptions in online education. Existing approaches often rely on single sentiment labels, limiting their ability to capture nuanced feedback. This study introduces a hybrid framework that integrates rating-based, text-based, and combined sentiment labels, along with feature extraction using TF-IDF, GloVe, and FastText. Multiple machine learning and ensemble models were optimized through GridSearchCV and RandomizedSearchCV. Findings reveal that TF-IDF features consistently outperformed embeddings, while ensembles such as Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, and XGBoost delivered the best results. The hybrid label configuration achieved 84.62% accuracy with balanced precision, recall, and F1 scores. Interpretability was enhanced using confusion matrices, ROC curves, and LIME visualizations, which clarified feature contributions to sentiment predictions. Overall, the proposed framework improves accuracy, transparency, and scalability, with potential applications in e-learning, e-commerce, and social media monitoring.

title: Design and Comparison of a 180° Differential Phase Shifter Using Direct and Impedance-Transformed 100Ω Microstrip Lines
authors: Pratik Sarkar, Tasnia Rahman Prome, Md Asaduzzaman Chunnu, Pranti Debnath
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This paper presents the design and comparison of a 180° differential microstrip phase shifter implemented using two different approaches: a direct <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$100-\Omega$</tex> microstrip line and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$100-\Omega$</tex> line realised through a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$50-100 \Omega$</tex> impedance transformer. The design is implemented on a Teflon (PTFE) substrate with a dielectric constant of 2.15 and a loss tangent of 0.001. Both designs are simulated to evaluate their phase shift, S-parameters, and differential output performance. Results show that the proposed designs achieve an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$180 \pm 5^{\circ}$</tex> phase shift over the targeted frequency range, with the direct line offering improved matching and reduced insertion loss compared to the impedance-transformed line. A comparative analysis demonstrates the efficacy of direct microstrip line in improving phase shifter performance for microwave and RF applications.

title: Comparative Study of Nanobots in Nano medicines
authors: Aneesa Shoaib, Eva Shamaoon, Tanzeela Gohar, Nabeela Noor, Zaki Uddin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robotics and Automation in Industry (ICRAI)
abstract: In the recent years combining nanotechnology and robotics, nano-scaled machines are invented, called nanobots (or nanorobots). These are designed to perform a particular job like sensing, signaling and information processing. Nanobots are made from organic materials e.g. DNA, proteins, and molecular cells and also from inorganic materials such as metal, gold, and diamond. They are set to revolutionize nanomedicine by delivering drugs precisely and diagnosing diseases. Apart from medicines, they play an active role in oil industries removing oil spills. Moreover, water decontamination and environmental cleaning can be done using nanobots. Using special sensors and processors, nanobots give in-vivo information. These nanobots can be operated from outside. In our work a comprehensive review of nanobots is presented. Working and processing of nanobots is studied in detail. This review article will elaborate applications of nanobots in compact form. Furthermore, we will review major application of nanobots in nano-medicines that will help in curing diseases like cancer, diabetes, kidney stones etc. without any side effects.

title: Comprehensive Study of Distinct TID Mechanisms in Hardened SGT MOSFET under On /Off State and Annealing Recovery
authors: Junfeng Yu, Jihong Ding, Ruihan Gao, Haonan Liu, Jun Ye, Xuan Xiao, Junyan Zhu, Chunlei Wu, D.W. Zhang, Pengcheng Yu, Hao Gu, Hongping Ma, Qingchun Zhang, Haiming Xu, Tao Wang, Qingdong Zhang, Liang Li, Haiou Li, Hong Zhou, Jincheng Zhang, Xiaofeng Gu, Linna Zhao, Wei Huang
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: This study investigates Total-Ionizing-Dose effects mechanisms and annealing recovery in hardened split-gate trench (SGT) MOSFETs. Under on-state irradiation, charges accumulated in the gate oxide (Q<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">GOX</inf>) and inter-poly oxide (Q<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">IPOX</inf>) distort the channel electrostatics, enhancing Drain-Induced Barrier Lowering (DIBL) and degrading leakage current, breakdown voltage (BV), and capacitance, while off-state irradiation induces a severe trench-region charge imbalance dominated by Q<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">TROX</inf>, leading to BV degradation. Post-irradiation annealing restores performance by partially neutralizing oxide-trapped charges. Extraction results reveal that ΔQ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">GOX</inf> and ΔQ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">IPOX</inf> are reduced from 3.23 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</sup>cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-2</sup> and 9.2× 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">12</sup> cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-2</sup> to 2.23 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</sup> cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-2</sup> and 5.7 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">12</sup> cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-2</sup>, respectively, enabling the recovery of V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</inf> from 0.66 V to 0.95 V and suppression of DIBL and leakage current. More importantly, annealing mitigates switching degradation by reducing the Miller-plateau Q<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">GD</inf> from 87 nC to 55 nC, and notably eliminates the abnormal pre-Miller gate-current diversion caused by excessive C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">gd</inf>, leading to faster switching and reduced switching energy losses. These results provide comprehensive insights into both degradation and recovery mechanisms in hardened SGT MOSFETs, offering practical guidance for hardening design and reliable operation.

title: Trustworthiness in Connected Autonomous Vehicles: Standards, Tradeoffs With Efficiency and Comfort, and Architectural Choices
authors: Lei Zhang, Zongyao Li, Zhongxu Dong, Yixuan Fan, David Flynn, Xianbin Wang, Muhammad Imran
abstract: Connected autonomous vehicles (CAVs) are envisioned to transform future mobility by enhancing safety, efficiency, and passenger experience through communication. However, the integration of networking into autonomous driving also introduces new vulnerabilities and complex tradeoffs across multiple performance dimensions. This article positions trustworthiness as the cornerstone of CAV design and explores its interdependence with efficiency and comfort—the three core performance metrics (CPMs) for evaluating system performance and public acceptance. We review the elements of trustworthiness, including safety, security, privacy, reliability, resilience, fairness, and accountability, and analyze their inherent synergies and conflicts with efficiency and comfort. Furthermore, we present frameworks for building trustworthy CAVs under both centralized decision-making (CDM) and distributed decision-making (DDM) paradigms, supported by simulation-based evaluations of reliability and latency across different network topologies. The findings demonstrate that centralized architectures generally favor efficiency and comfort, whereas distributed approaches improve resilience and accountability at the cost of higher latency. By highlighting standards, tradeoffs, and architectural choices, this article offers practical insights into designing CAV systems that balance trust, performance, and user experience in real-world deployments.

title: Deep Learning Driven Respiratory Sound Analysis for Early Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease Detection
authors: Shahran Rahman Alve, Muhammad Zawad Mahmud, Md Asiful Islam, Samiha Islam, Sunehera Rahman, Mohammad Monirujjaman Khan
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) and deep learning (DL) have been paid greater attention in the clinical field within recent years, as they provide an efficient tool for early diagnosis and accurate prediction of diseases based on medical images or sounds. In an environment where qualified medical staff are in short supply, many diagnostic systems that can assist physicians in dealing with the increasing number of patients have become popular. Pulmonary diseases, such as Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease (COPD), cancer, and diabetes-related complications, still account for major global health challenges, highlighting the need for early diagnosis and intervention. Historically, auscultation has been a critical diagnostic tool for lung sounds, and it is used in combination with chest X-rays as a part of the examination performed in diagnosing respiratory illness. However, traditional diagnostic methods are typically expensive and subject to human bias. To overcome these limitations, this paper aims to develop a Deep CNN-based framework for the automatic analysis of respiratory sound data, with a particular focus on COPD detection. For feature extraction, acoustic representations were generated using the Librosa package, including MFCCs, Mel-Spectrogram, Chroma CQT, and Chroma CENS. The proposed model not only differentiates COPD from non-COPD but also categorizes disease severity into mild, moderate, and severe as well. Experimental results on the publicly released ICBHI database also showed that our approach gave excellent performance and accuracy with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{9 6 \%} / \text{1 0}$</tex>-fold cross-validation and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{9 5 \%}$</tex> without using any cross-validation. These findings suggest that the CNN framework is superior to state-of-the-art and has significant potential as a reliable noninvasive diagnostic assist tool. The use of this tool in routine clinical practice could potentially support clinicians in minimizing diagnostic delays, enhancing patient relief, and cost-effective resource utilization.

title: GRU: A Robust Sequential Learning Model for Appliance Energy Forecasting
authors: Rokon Mia, Rayhan, Mohammad Sakib Mahmood, Nasrin Akter, Arafat Hossain, Tareq Hasan
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Electric energy forecasting is crucial for optimizing energy utilization and resource management due to the rapidly increasing use of electric energy. Conventional machine learning models, such as Gradient Boosting Regression (GBR), Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs), and Support Vector Machines (SVMs), are unable to accurately represent the non-linear relationships and temporal dependencies included in energy consumption data. To address this limitation, this study experimented with four temporally dependent models named CNN-LSTM, CNNGRU, StackLSTM, and StackGRU. The higher performance of StackGRU is demonstrated by experimental findings on popular Appliance Energy Prediction (AEP) dataset. It significantly outperforms state-of-the-art models, achieving the lowest Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 0.15 and Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 0.11. These findings confirm that the proposed framework enhances both prediction accuracy and generalizability for energy forecasting.

title: AirMOS: A Global Optical-SAR Multimodal Benchmark for Airport Detection
authors: Xuyang Zhao, Lijun Zhao, Keli Shi
abstract: Multimodal remote sensing object detection demonstrates superior potential over single-modality approaches by leveraging complementary observations from diverse sensors, particularly under complex backgrounds and degraded observation conditions. Despite these advantages, the lack of standardized benchmarks for optical-Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) fusion continues to impede the rigorous evaluation and development of advanced detection algorithms. In this paper, a comprehensive global benchmark, namely AirMOS, is presented, which is specifically designed for multimodal airport detection. AirMOS integrates Sentinel-2 optical imagery with Sentinel-1 dual-polarization SAR data, establishing a unified three-modality representation consisting of Optical, SAR-VV, and SAR-VH. A standardized pipeline is implemented encompassing automated data acquisition, preprocessing and standardization, registration verification and quality control, and annotation. Extensive benchmarks with multiple single-modal and multimodal detectors demonstrate that AirMOS effectively differentiates model performance and fusion strategies. The experimental analysis reveals that while multimodal integration significantly enhances detection performance at mAP@50, achieving high-precision localization remains a critical challenge. Overall, AirMOS provides a reproducible, scalable, and challenging platform to advance research in optical-SAR multimodal remote sensing interpretation. The AirMOS dataset is publicly available for download at: https://www.scidb.cn/s/j2aaUb.

title: Path-HGT: A Hierarchical Graph Transformer Framework for Gigapixel Pathology Image Analysis
authors: Sazzad Hossen Chowdhury, Mohammad Iftikar Hasan, Mohammad Tarek Hossen, Muhammad Sayeed Sadman Saad, Adnan Shamim, Abdullah Al Abrar Chowdhury, Rashedul Arefin Ifty
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The analysis of gigapixel Whole-Slide Images (WSIs) in digital pathology is the gold standard for cancer diagnosis, but it presents a formidable challenge for AI models due to extreme data scale. Current state-of-the-art methods rely on Multiple Instance Learning (MIL), treating WSIs as an unordered “bag-of-patches.” This approach critically discards the spatial microarchitecture of tissue, which is a primary source of diagnostic information for pathologists. To overcome this limitation, we propose Path-HGT, a novel Pathology Hierarchical Graph Transformer framework. Path-HGT first models a WSI as a spatially-explicit graph of patches. It then employs a hierarchical architecture composed of stacked Graph Transformer (GATv2) layers and self-attention graph pooling (SAGPool) modules. This design enables the model to learn multi-scale representations, capturing both local cellular interactions at the patch level and global tissue-region architectures at coarser graph levels. We validate Path-HGT on the Camelyon16 and TCGA-RCC benchmarks. Our framework achieves state-of-the-art performance, attaining an AUC of 0.965 on Camelyon16 and an accuracy of 0.946 on TCGA-RCC, significantly outperforming existing MIL, Transformer, and “flat” GNN baselines. Our work demonstrates that modeling explicit spatial hierarchy is crucial for robust and interpretable gigapixel WSI analysis.

title: Design and Application of Deep Learning-Based Massive Text Classification Algorithms
authors: Shue Ma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Statistics, Modeling and Advanced Algorithms (ASMA)
abstract: To enhance semantic recognition and classification accuracy for massive text, a deep learning algorithm integrating Transformer and BiLSTM structures was employed for feature modeling and optimization experiments across multi-domain corpora. Using multiple datasets as validation subjects, the model's performance in semantic modeling, calibration capabilities, and inference efficiency was investigated. Results demonstrate that this algorithm outperforms traditional models across multiple performance metrics, exhibiting high accuracy and stability, and is suitable for text classification tasks in complex semantic scenarios.

title: Bridging the accuracy-latency gap in PCB inspection via Response Knowledge Distillation
authors: Tooba Ahmed Alvi, Rizwan Ahmed Khan, Virda Akhtar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robotics and Automation in Industry (ICRAI)
abstract: The quality assurance of Printed Circuit Boards (PCBs) comes under first principles in electronics manufacturing, where defects can affect device reliability and lead to major financial losses. As components reduce in size and their densities increase, manual inspection becomes inefficient and unscalable due to human variability rates up to 30%. Traditional rulebased expert systems, though consistent, struggle with sudden alterations in lighting, reflections, and defect morphologies. This project addresses the accuracy-latency problem: advanced deep learning models like MAS-YOLO and HSA-RTDETR provide exceptional $\mathbf{m A P}$ ($\gt99 \%$ at $\mathbf{I o U}=\mathbf{0. 5}$) but require GPU resources, giving < 100 FPS unsuitable for edge deployment. In today’s fast-paced world, the industry demands real-time processing ($\gt200$ FPS) on low-power hardware to sustain throughput without any blockages. Our work proposes a Response Knowledge Distillation approach, which is thereby motivated by the need to improve manufacturing efficiency, reduce cost, and maximize defect accuracy. By distilling “dark knowledge” via softened logits from a teacher to a student model using temperature-scaled KL divergence (T = 4 and $\alpha=0.7$), our proposed solution targets $\leq 5 \% \mathrm{mAP} \text{@} 0.5: 0.95$ drop while meeting edge constraints. Study objectives include implementing Response Knowledge Distillation, quantifying efficiency ($\leq 5 M$ parameters, >200FPS), achieving $m A P \text{@} 0.5 \geq 99 \%$, and benchmarking accuracy retention for industrial object detection.

title: Computer Aided Design Tool for Multiple-Impedance Matching Network
authors: Ahmad Eldahshan, Hesham Nabil, Ayman Eltager
abstract: This paper introduces an automatic and systematic method to design a generic multiple-impedance matching-network using a novel proposed computer aided design (CAD) tool. The task of this tool is to achieve conjugate matching for multiple source-load impedances at arbitrary frequencies. Maximum gain within the acceptable user-defined design parameters is achieved. Design automation feature provided by the tool relies on the built-in multi-objective optimization algorithms using MATLAB software. This tool provides the designer with the ability to dictate various design parameters such as source/load complex impedances, frequencies of operation, bandwidths, and VSWR to be achieved simultaneously. Starting with suggesting applicable circuit topologies, the proposed CAD tool provides the acceptable operating ranges for each component, and the corresponding design parameters. The interactive features included give the designer extra degree of freedom to study the impact of each component on the whole network performance. To demonstrate the interesting features of the proposed CAD tool, a wide-band antenna is introduced as a practical case study to be matched with 50-Ω load. Based on the predefined constraints, the tool provides many solutions to solve the matching problem with the user constraints.

title: Early Prediction of Autism Spectrum Disorder Using an Ensemble Voting Classifier
authors: Hrithik Ravi, Gurucharan, Niranjan Chennakrishnasrinivasan, Sandhya B R
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is a neurodevelopmental disorder that affects individuals from young as toddlers to adults. Early screening has often been difficult. The research work proposed a machine learning prediction model based on combined dataset of toddlers, children, adolescents, and adults. After standard cleaning and normalization of the data, several classification models are developed including logistic regression, decision tree, random forest, support vector machine, k-nearest neighbors, gradient boosting, weighted voting ensemble classifier and neural network. Later, the model performances were compared, weighted voting ensemble classifier proves the best performing model for overall prediction having 99.64% of accuracy. Testing on the combined dataset shows consistent performance across all age groups. The framework also incorporates straightforward explainability analysis to clarify feature influence and evaluate model reliability. The results indicate that this approach can aid in automated and consistent ASD screening in real-world applications.

title: ⊓-ideals and ⊔-filters in double Boolean algebras : To the memory of Prof. Tonga Marcel, 11.04.1951 – 15.09.2025
authors: Gael Tenkeu Kembang, Michel Krebs, Leonard Kwuida, Etienne Romuald Temgoua Alomo, Yannick Léa Tenkeu Jeufack
publish: 2026 IEEE 56th International Symposium on Multiple-Valued Logic (ISMVL)
abstract: In this work we introduce a new class of ideals and filters in double Boolean algebras and call it ⊓-ideal (resp. ⊔-filter). We show that ⊓-ideals (resp. ⊔-filters) generalize ideals (resp. filters), and that the set of ⊓-ideals (resp. ⊔-filters) forms an algebraic, and residuated lattice that is actually a Heyting algebra.

title: A Gate Protection Interface Enabling Robust and User-Friendly Gate Driving of GaN/SiC HyFET
authors: Ji Shu, Mian Tao, Yat Hon Ng, Jiaheng He, Ziying Li, Qingyuan Tang, Shi-Wei Ricky Lee, Kevin J. Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: The GaN/SiC HyFET integrates the advantages of GaN and SiC into a single power device, achieving performance that surpasses either technology alone. Similar to a conventional GaN HEMT, the p-GaN gate of the HyFET requires stringent control of the gate driving voltage to prevent overvoltage stress and the associated gate reliability issues. In this work, a gate protection interface is designed for the HyFET, which provides effective overvoltage protection and also elevates the threshold voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">TH</inf>) by two times. Board-level experiments demonstrate that the HyFET equipped with the proposed interface can be safely operated even under a 15-V gate driving voltage. More importantly, the interface substantially suppresses switching oscillations without degrading the switching speed.

title: Impact of Integrated Substrate Potential Control (SPC) Circuits and Dual Field-Plates on Dynamic ON-Resistance in GaN Monolithic Bidirectional Switches
authors: Junsong Jiang, Gang Liu, Zhiwen Dong, Yao Chen, Hongfeng Chen, Xin Chu, Fengping Lin, Xingang Ren, Gaofei Tang, Xi Tang
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: This work systematically investigates the dynamic ON-resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf>) characteristics of normally-off p-GaN monolithic bidirectional switches (MBDS). A double pulse test (DPT) circuit and a MBDS-based 500-kHz AC-AC buck converter are employed to evaluate the impact of integrated substrate potential control (SPC) circuits on dynamic R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf> with different substrate terminal connections. In the 400-V DPT characteristics, the MBDS exhibits a dynamic/static R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf> ratio of 1.92 with the substrate connected to SPC, whereas the ratio increases to 5.2 with a floating substrate. Meanwhile, the AC-AC buck converter with MBDS connected to SPC exhibits a higher efficiency compared to that with MBDS of floating substrate. In addition, compared to unidirectional HEMTs, the MBDS using dual field-plates suffers from a larger dynamic R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf> increase due to the carriers trapping effects in the passivation/AlGaN interface between the field-plate and the substrate. The mechanisms underlying the impact of SPC circuits and dual field-plates on the R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf> increase in MBDS are revealed. These results are crucial for optimizing SPC circuits and buffer/substrate designs to effectively suppress dynamic R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf> increase, maximizing the advantages of GaN MBDS for next-generation high-performance power conversion systems.

title: Towards Wearable Sensor-based Human Activity Recognition: A Survey
authors: Hailin Zou, Zijie Chen, Yuanyuan Pan, Jianqing Li
abstract: Human Activity Recognition (HAR), which aims to identify and classify human behaviors through multi-source sensing data, has become a long-standing research hotspot in ubiquitous computing, enabling a wide range of real-world applications. Focusing on Wearable HAR (WHAR), this survey provides a system-level synthesis organized around the life cycle of WHAR systems, collating fundamental theories, key technologies, and implementation details across data acquisition, model construction, and system deployment. Rather than treating sensing configuration, learning paradigm selection, evaluation protocol design, and deployment constraints as isolated topics, we emphasize their inherent interdependencies, demonstrating how early design choices propagate through the entire development pipeline to shape final system performance. We further establish a unified taxonomy of HAR paradigms based on data collection devices, clarifying the boundaries between vision-based, ambient sensor-based, and wearable sensor-based approaches to eliminate long-standing classification ambiguities. Additionally, we systematically review mainstream supervised WHAR models, emerging learning paradigms, deployment-oriented evaluation criteria, and cutting-edge directions including foundation models and Large Language Model (LLM)-based activity understanding. By integrating all aspects into a coherent life cycle framework, this survey aims to serve as a design-oriented reference for researchers and practitioners building accurate, efficient, and deployable WHAR systems.

title: Deep Learning-Driven Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis of Bangladeshi Hotel Reviews
authors: Sanzida Afrin Promi, Nurul Absar, Md Sadman Hafiz
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: With the growing reliance on online reviews in the hospitality industry, understanding customer feedback at an aspect level has become essential for enhancing service quality and guest satisfaction. This study proposes a deep learning-based multi-label aspect-based sentiment analysis (ABSA) framework for effectively analysing unstructured hotel review texts, in which multiple sentiments toward different aspects may be expressed simultaneously. A newly constructed dataset of 7567 Bangladeshi hotel reviews was used to evaluate sentiments across nine predefined aspects, including food, cleanliness, staff behaviour and service, amenities, cost, room quality, location, parking, and overall experience. Machine learning, deep learning, and transformer-based models were employed for aspect-wise sentiment classification to capture both statistical patterns and contextual semantic relationships in the text. Experimental results demonstrate that the BERT-base-uncased Transformer model outperforms the other models, achieving an average accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 1 \%}$</tex> in binary classification and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 0 \%}$</tex> in multi-label classification. The proposed multi-label approach enables simultaneous and accurate sentiment prediction across multiple aspects within a single review. This represents a scalable, and domain-specific ABSA framework tailored to the Bangladeshi hotel industry, addressing cultural and linguistic nuances in localised feedback and offering actionable insights for service improvement and decision-making.

title: Rotor-Angle Stability Enhancement Control for MMC-MTDC Systems Without Sending-End Power Reserves
authors: Haobo Zhang, Wang Xiang, Jun Liang, Jinyu Wen
abstract: High-voltage direct current (HVDC) systems are increasingly integrated into AC grids, making rotor-angle stability more critical. Improper HVDC dynamics during disturbances may weaken synchronizing torque and damping, thereby aggravating oscillations. This paper investigates the offshore wind farm (OWF)-based multi-terminal DC (MTDC) system and analyzes the impact of its power injections on grid rotor-angle stability. On this basis, a frequency-based power control logic is first proposed to enhance rotor-angle stability. Building on this logic, Lyapunov-based control laws are derived to coordinate active and reactive power both among multiple terminals (inter-terminal level) and within each converter under capacity constraints (intra-terminal level). To further optimize the coordinated control, an optimization framework is then designed, ensuring effective and secure support. Finally, considering the absence of power reserves at the sending-end OWF, a capacitor energy-based control strategy is proposed for MTDC systems to utilize all MMCs' capacitor energy at the same time for stability support (device level), thereby expanding the available support energy and further improving rotor-angle stability. The effectiveness and robustness of the proposed control are validated on the IEEE 39-bus system via PSCAD/EMTDC and MATLAB co-simulation.

title: A Dual-branch Network with Cross-scale Feature Interaction and Alignment for Weakly Supervised Whole Slide Image Analysis
authors: Jianan Zhang, Hangbei Cheng, Xueyu Liu, Feixue Shao, Junxin Chen, Guanghui Yue, Yongfei Wu, Weihua Yang
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Whole slide images (WSIs) analysis plays a critical role in computer-aided diagnosis. Recently, weakly supervised multiple instance learning (MIL) has become a widely adopted approach for WSI processing, as it enables effective learning from slide-level labels without the need for exhaustive pixel-level annotations. However, many existing MIL methods do not fully leverage the inherent pyramidal structure of WSIs and struggle to capture dependencies among instances and local contextual information, which may restrict their ability to capture rich hierarchical information. To address these issues, this paper proposes FIA-MIL, a dual-branch network designed for weakly supervised WSIs analysis that incorporates cross-scale feature interaction and alignment. Specifically, a dual-scale feature interaction module models semantic relationships across magnifications using a pyramid-aligned two-branch architecture, with Transformer encoders capturing instance-level dependencies within each scale. Furthermore, a dual-scale feature aggregation module integrates multi-scale features and introduces alignment constraints at both the bag and instance levels to ensure semantic consistency across scales. Classification and survival analysis experiments conducted on publicly available WSIs datasets demonstrate that the proposed method exhibits promising performance.

title: The Primary-Side Regulation of Active-Clamp Forward Converter with On-the-Fly Output Voltage Variation
authors: Junho Shin, Yuqi Wei, Jong-Won Shin
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: Primary-side regulation (PSR) methods have limited applicability in DC-DC converters with output inductors, such as forward converters, due to low regulation accuracy and the inability to adjust the output voltage during operation. This paper proposes a novel PSR method that accurately estimates the output voltage of an active-clamp forward converter (ACFW) to overcome these limitations. The estimator models the output voltage as a polynomial function of input power, input voltage, and clamp capacitor voltage, which are all sensed at the primary side. The polynomial is derived from high-resolution simulations and its order is optimized to minimize the computational burden on the microcontroller. The error of the output voltage regulated by the polynomial is <1% under parameter variations of ±20%. The proposed method is also capable of varying output voltage during converter operation, i.e., on-the-fly output voltage adjustment. A small-signal model is presented and validated by simulation in order to support controller design. Experimental results with a 100-kHz, 200-W ACFW hardware prototype confirmed that the proposed PSR method has 99.8% accuracy during the steady-state operation.

title: An IoT-Based Qr-Code-Enabled Smart Medicine Box for Reducing Prescription Errors and Improving Medication Adherence
authors: Abdullah Al Galib Tonmoy, Tanjimul Islam Tamim, Moniruzzaman Monir, Jahid Hasan, Saad Ahmed
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Handwritten medical prescriptions remain widely used in many healthcare systems, often leading to misinterpretation by pharmacists and patients due to illegible handwriting. Such misinterpretations can result in medication dispensing errors, posing significant risks to patient safety. Furthermore, medication non-adherence particularly among elderly patients who may forget to take medicines at prescribed times-continues to be a major challenge, reducing treatment effectiveness. To address these issues, this paper proposes a QR-code-based smart medicine box integrated with a web-based prescription management system. In the proposed framework, physicians generate digital prescriptions that are encoded into QR codes. Pharmacies scan these QR codes to accurately identify prescribed medications and ensure correct dispensing. Patients subsequently scan the same QR code through a web interface connected to the smart medicine box, enabling automatic import of prescription data. Based on the imported prescription information, the system autonomously configures medication schedules and drawer assignments using an ESP32 microcontroller and a realtime clock module. The proposed system effectively eliminates ambiguities associated with handwritten prescriptions, minimizes human intervention and related errors, and enhances medication adherence without requiring a dedicated mobile application. The feasibility and effectiveness of the system were validated through the development and testing of a functional hardware prototype under real-world operating conditions.

title: Estimating Technical Debt with Machine Learning: Leveraging Code Complexity and Developer Interactions
authors: Md Abu Sufian, Estiyak Rubaiat, Md Daud Hossain Supto, Nabajit Dey, Tajmun Nahar Tisha, Shafiqul Islam Nayem
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Estimating technical debt (TD) is crucial in managing large-scale software projects. This study introduces a machine learning-based framework for TD estimation in opensource Java projects, integrating code complexity metrics and developer interaction features. Using a dataset of 55 million Java files from 100,000 GitHub repositories, we apply XGBoost, LSTM, and Informer models for multi-horizon TD forecasting. SHAP (SHapley Additive Explanations) is utilized for interpretability, revealing key TD drivers such as cyclomatic complexity, commit frequency, and review cycle time. The results demonstrate that the Informer model outperforms others, achieving an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> of 0.9250 and an MAE of 3.60, particularly excelling in long-term forecasting. This model's ability to predict TD with high accuracy makes it suitable for integration into CI/CD pipelines, ensuring proactive TD management. The proposed framework provides actionable insights for developers, enabling them to prioritize remediation based on the most significant factors contributing to TD. This research offers a scalable, data-driven solution for software maintenance, contributing to improved software quality and sustainability.

title: Steganographic Optical Camera Communication: A Framework for Secure Wireless Data Transfer
authors: Shivani Rajendra Teli, Stanislav Vitek, Zabih Ghassemlooy, Stanislav Zvanovec
abstract: This paper introduces a novel framework for secure data transmission in optical camera communications (OCC), termed the Stega-OCC system, which integrates steganography with OCC. The proposed framework is structured into three main components: (i) the reference OCC link (OCCRL); (ii) image acquisition and watermarking; and (iii) image recovery and data processing. A dedicated experimental testbed is developed to evaluate a 1 m long OCCRL link, with performance analyzed in terms of the reception success rate. The data images (DIs) obtained from the OCCRL link are embedded into cover images (CIs) using a least significant bit watermarking technique to ensure secure data embedding. In the final stage, CIs and DIs are separated, and data recovery is performed. Image quality is assessed using the peak signal-to-noise ratio and the compression quality factor with respect to the image threshold, as well as the structural similarity index measure. Further analysis of the Stega-OCC framework is conducted for various modulation frequencies of OCC. The experimental results demonstrate that the performance of the proposed system remains robust and independent of variations in modulation frequency. In summary, the developed testbed and results confirm that the Stega-OCC framework provides an effective and versatile solution for secure data transmission in OCC systems.

title: Monitoring Agricultural Transformation and Its Impacts on Water Resources in the Gharb Plain, Morocco, Using Machine Learning and Remote Sensing
authors: Tarik Bahouq, Adil Moumane, Abdessamad Elmotawakkil, Nadia Touhami, Suraj Kumar Bhagat
abstract: Driven by national initiatives like the Green Morocco Plan, Morocco’s Gharb Plain has shifted from traditional rain-fed farming to intensive, high-value commercial cultivation. Previous regional research has documented land-use changes and groundwater stress separately.Acritical research gap remains in capturing the direct, seasonal interactions between long-term surface greening and subsurface aquifer degradation, where apparent vegetation expansion often masks underlying resource depletion. This study aims to bridge this gap by quantifying the direct relationship between long-term agricultural intensification and localized hydrological stress, within a representative study area of the Gharb Plain.We adopted an integrated, multi-scale approach combining four decades (1984–2024) of Landsat satellite imagery processed via Google Earth Engine (GEE) with seasonal field observations. A Random Forest machine-learning algorithm and spectral indices tracked land use/land cover (LULC) changes. These findings were cross-validated with in situ seasonal hydrochemical monitoring across 68 wells spanning four distinct soil-agricultural zones. Long-term data confirmed robust vegetation densification, with EVI increasing by 1.24 km2/year due to perennial avocado expansion. Seasonal LULC mapping showed the agricultural footprint peaks in winter (166.5 km2) and contracts to its minimum in summer (113.7 km2). This summer contraction marks peak hydrological stress: intensive irrigation pumping caused electrical conductivity (EC) to spike to 2,909 μS/cm in the sandy loam zone and 2,888 μS/cm in the sandy-clay zone, whilewater table depths dropped to seasonal lows of 6.75 m and 7 m, respectively. This work establishes a vital baseline reference. Practically, these insights enable regional water management authorities and policy planners to deploy targeted multi-year monitoring, implement water-efficient irrigation technologies, and restrict water-intensive crops to protect the plain’s long-term agricultural viability.

title: How much warmer are floating Sargassum mats than surrounding waters? A systematic assessment using Landsat observations
authors: Junnan Jiao, Chuanmin Hu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: While the emergent Great Atlantic Sargassum Belt has caught extensive attention on its initiation, maintenance, and environmental and socioeconomic impacts, much less attention has been given to how Sargassum mats may change the local thermal environments. Here, using 9243 pairs of Landsat OLI and TIRS images collected over the Wider Caribbean during 2025, a record year of Sargassum proliferation, we assess temperature anomalies of Sargassum pixels referenced against nearby waters. Temperature anomalies of Sargassum pixels are found to increase with Sargassum pixel-wise density due primarily to the mixing effect. For the same density, temperature anomalies decrease with increasing winds until about 6 m/s. After accounting for the mixing effects (fractional cover within a 30-m pixel and mismatch between 30-m Sargassum pixel and 100-m thermal pixel), this study estimates that 25% of individual Sargassum mats from the 100%-density pixels would have positive temperature anomalies of  1 °C, and a smaller percentage can have positive temperature anomalies reaching 2 °C. Such temperature anomalies are also found to change seasonally due to changes in solar insolation and winds. These findings have profound ecological and biological implications.

title: RF Link Budget Sensitivity Analysis of Bangladesh Satellite-1 for Reliable Coastal and Maritime Communication
authors: Mohammad Mashhunur Rahman, Afia Saiyara Suchana
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This research presents a comprehensive RF link budget analysis for satellite-based non-terrestrial networks involving terrestrial, coastal, and maritime earth stations in tropical regions. The novelty and motivation of this work lie in the integrated evaluation of seasonal atmospheric attenuation, pointing mismatch effects, and terminal diversity to realistically quantify link margin degradation under practical operating conditions. The methodology combined ITU-R-based models for atmospheric attenuation with detailed uplink and downlink carrier-to-noise (C/N) calculations, followed by end-to-end C/N aggregation and modulation-dependent link margin assessment. Multiple communication scenarios were analyzed using QPSK, 8-PSK, 16-APSK, and 32-APSK under both vertical and horizontal polarizations. The results show that link margin decreased by approximately 14 dB when transitioning from QPSK to 32-APSK, with monsoon conditions causing an additional 0.01-5 dB degradation. The findings demonstrate that uplink limitations dominate system performance in maritime scenarios, highlighting the necessity of adaptive modulation and worst-case seasonal dimensioning to ensure high-availability satellite connectivity for next-generation 5G/6G networks in tropical climates.

title: Multi-Modal Machine Learning for Sentiment and Emotion Recognition in Social Media Data
authors: Mayiri Sravani, K.Naga Rajeswari
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This paper examines a new multi-mode model of sentiment and emotion recognition in social media, which combines text, visual and social-contextual data to understand affective states. In contrast to traditional methods, which ignore cross-modal interactions or involve post-level classification, the work in question presents a Cross-Modal Emotion Trajectory Network (CMETNet), which trains the temporal dynamics of emotion development in post series and in series of conversation posts. It uses multi-modal fusion with attention-based text, image, and audio feature integration with a trajectory module to increase the accuracy and consistency of predictions. The paper is set to elaborate on interrelationship between modalities with emphasis on how the semantics of text, visual image and social context interact to shape the inferences toward emotion. Based on this, it investigates a variety of advanced extensions such as affective contrastive adapters, conditional multimodal augmentation of rare-emotion enhancement, and meta-learning to cross-domain generalization. The literature discloses that the addition of community-conscious embeddings and search path modelling enhances the degree of robustness and coherence of estimations to a great extent. Additionally, the explainable AI aspects emphasized in the study have to produce interpretable explanations so that human beings can understand what the model is doing. This study helps improve sentiment analysis by overcoming shortcomings of current models, providing a scalable, trajectory-based, ethically-conscious framework to multimodal emotion recognition applicable to the social media analytics, human-computer interaction and behavioral scientists.

title: Multimodal Deep Learning Framework for Blood Test Diagnosis Integrating Medical Imaging and Clinical Parameters
authors: Tohedul Islam, Umme Jannatul Fariha, Asir Foysal Al Mukit, Ajmayeen Chisty, Nafisa Sharmin Sadia
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Conventional diagnostic techniques usually rely on biochemical test results or microscopic images, which limits the scope of diagnosis interpretation. In order to increase diagnostic accuracy, this study proposes a Multimodal Deep Learning Framework that combines organized clinical data with microscopic blood cell images. The framework employs a multilayer perceptron (MLP) to assess structured clinical data, including red blood cell (RBC) count, white blood cell (WBC), platelets, hemoglobin, and glucose levels, and convolutional neural networks (CNNs) to extract features from medical images. The learnt representations from both modalities are combined in a fusion layer to produce a single output for disease categorization. Experimental evaluations on a properly selected multimodal dataset outperformed unimodal baselines with a macro F1-score of 0.95 and a validation accuracy of 94.6%. By combining both visual and numerical health parameters, the results show the potential of multimodal fusion in medical AI, especially for early and precise blood disease diagnosis.

title:
Cross-Tracer Synthesis Model of
                    <sup>11</sup>
                    C-CFT and
                    <sup>18</sup>
                    F-DOPA PET Images from
                    <sup>18</sup>
                    F-FDG for Parkinson's Disease
authors: Wenxiang Yi, Xiaolin Sun, Hao Sun, Yuan Chang, Zanting Ye, Xiaolong Niu, Lei Jiang, Lijun Lu
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Positron emission tomography (PET) with various tracers plays a critical role in Parkinson's disease (PD) studies. However, the clinical application of PET with novel or less-available tracers is limited by factors such as high cost and short half-life. To address this limitation, we developed a cross-tracer PET synthesis model based on diffusion model to directly synthesize cross-tracer PET images from widely used <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">18</sup>F-FDG PET images for PD studies. The network was initially trained on <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">18</sup>F-FDG and <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</sup>C-CFT datasets, and subsequently fine-tuned on <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">18</sup>F-FDG and <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">18</sup>F-DOPA datasets. After anonymization, synthetic images were mixed with real ones and underwent visual assessment by radiologists. Quantitative analyses were further performed through voxel-wise comparisons and regional error analysis between synthetic and real images. Visual assessment indicated that the synthetic <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</sup>C-CFT and <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">18</sup>F-DOPA PET images were comparable to real images in terms of quality, noise, and striatal conspicuity, with no significant differences observed in the error maps. Synthetic <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</sup>C-CFT images achieved an average PSNR of 36.22 and an SSIM of 0.96, whereas <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">18</sup>F-DOPA images yielded values of 29.79 and 0.95, respectively. Bland-Altman analysis indicated high consistency between synthetic and real images. The average standardized uptake value (SUV) bias was 0.013 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm$</tex-math></inline-formula> 0.032 SUV for <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</sup>C-CFT and -0.175 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm$</tex-math></inline-formula> 0.149 SUV for <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">18</sup>F-DOPA. The proposed cross-tracer PET synthesis model achieves comparable performance with <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</sup>C-CFT or <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">18</sup>F-DOPA PET imaging for PD studies.

title: Small-Sample Cropland Extraction From Multi-Temporal SAR Based on Temporal Difference
authors: Yuhuan Huang, Hang Li, Linpeng Wang, Dongdong Lu, Hanlin Zhang, Zhe Zhang
abstract: Accurate cropland extraction from synthetic aperture radar (SAR) imagery is essential for agricultural monitoring and land resource management. However, the strong speckle noise of SAR images and the scarcity of labeled samples limit the performance and generalization of conventional deep learning methods. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a small-sample cropland extraction method based on temporal-difference-enhanced representation using multi-temporal SAR imagery. Unlike existing methods that directly stack raw temporal observations, the proposed method explicitly constructs temporal difference images to enhance crop-related temporal scattering responses while suppressing stable background interference. These difference-enhanced features are integrated with multi-temporal SAR inputs and fed into segmentation networks to improve cropland discrimination under limited training samples. Experiments on multi-temporal Lutan-1 SAR data show that the proposed strategy consistently improves extraction accuracy, completeness, and robustness compared with conventional multi-temporal inputs and baseline methods. Moreover, the proposed method demonstrates strong generalization capability across different segmentation architectures.

title: Satellite Optical Remote Sensing of Clouds and Aerosols: From Particle Single-Scattering and Gaseous Absorption Through Radiative Transfer to Retrieval Products
authors: Ping Yang, Kerry Meyer, Robert Levy, Dongchen Li, Feng Xu, Anita D. Rapp, Zhibo Zhang
abstract: Clouds and aerosols are fundamental regulators of Earth’s radiation budget and climate system, influencing both solar and terrestrial radiation through scattering, absorption, and emission processes. Accurate characterization of their physical and radiative properties from space requires a rigorous understanding of particle single-scattering, gaseous absorption, and radiative transfer in the atmosphere, as well as reliable inversion methods. This review synthesizes the physical foundations and algorithmic implementations of satellite-based passive optical remote sensing of clouds and aerosols, spanning the ultraviolet to thermal infrared (IR) spectral range. Beginning with electromagnetic scattering theory and state-of-the-art methods for computing single-scattering by nonspherical particles and computationally efficient methods for accounting for atmospheric absorption, we discuss the radiative transfer framework underpinning cloud and aerosol retrievals. In particular, the connection between single-scattering and multiple-scattering is rigorously formulated. We then summarize operational and research-grade retrieval techniques, including cloud masking and thermodynamic phase determination, CO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> slicing for cloud-top pressure, the Nakajima–King shortwave bi-spectral and IR split-window approaches for cloud optical thickness and effective particle size, inversion algorithms for determining aerosol properties from multispectral and/or multiangle radiometric and polarimetric measurements, and active-passive remote sensing synergy. Examples of the global cloud and aerosol climatologies are illustrated using observations from the Moderate Resolution Imaging Spectroradiometer (MODIS) and the Multi-Angle Imaging SpectroRadiometer (MISR). Furthermore, the unique capabilities of active remote sensing techniques using spaceborne lidar observations are briefly discussed in the context of investigating ice clouds composed of randomly and horizontally oriented ice crystals, which remain a significant challenge for conventional passive remote sensing techniques. By connecting physical theory to practical retrievals, this review highlights both the maturity of current methodologies and the remaining challenges in reducing uncertainties in particle morphology, vertical structure, absorption, and aerosol–cloud interactions. Finally, the impact of artificial intelligence (AI) on atmospheric remote sensing is briefly addressed.

title: Extremely Low On-Resistance AlN-based SBDs with Distributed Polarization Doping Drift Layer
authors: Issei Sasaki, Masanobu Hiroki, Kazuaki Ebata, Kazuyuki Hirama, Yoshitaka Taniyasu, Takuya Maeda
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: AlN-based quasi-vertical Schottky barrier diodes (SBDs) with a graded AlGaN drift layer featuring a distributed polarization doping (DPD), a Si-doped graded AlGaN interlayer (IL) and an AlN/AlGaN superlattices (SLs) current spreading layer (CSL) on an AlN bulk substrate were demonstrated. The space charge profiles in the samples were carefully characterized by SIMS and C–V measurements. A free electron concentration of n ~ 1.2×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">17</sup> cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−3</sup> was measured, which was induced by DPD without any impurity doping in the graded AlGaN drift layer. Forward I–V measurement exhibited an extremely low on-resistance of 0.34 mΩcm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and nearly ideal thermionic emission with the ideality factor of 1.5. For the reverse I–V measurements, breakdown occurred around 400 V. The maximum electric field at the breakdown reached 8 MV/cm, even though the devices have no edge termination. These results showcase the strong potential of AlN-based electronic devices utilizing a DPD drift layer toward high-voltage and low-loss power device applications.

title:
High Thermal Stability of Threshold Voltage in p-Cr
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    Gated AlGaN/GaN HEMTs
authors: Jingang Li, Hao Tian, Maoqing Ling, Jiachen Duan, Zifeng Qu, Ye Liang, Harm van Zalinge, Ivona Z. Mitrovic, Ping Zhang, Kain Lu Low, Sen Huang, Wen Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: This work presents the first integration of Cr<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> as a gate stack into AlGaN/GaN HEMTs and demonstrates stable device operation at elevated temperatures, highlighting the potential of Cr<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> for high-temperature GaN HEMTs applications. X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) was employed to construct the band alignment at the Cr<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>/AlGaN heterointerface. Benefiting from the large band offsets at the Cr<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>/AlGaN interface, with valence-band and conduction-band offsets of 2.65 eV and 1.96 eV, respectively, the devices exhibit excellent threshold voltage stability during bias temperature instability (BTI) measurements. At 300 °C, the threshold voltage shift of only 0.12 V is observed after a positive gate-bias stress of 10 V for 10000 s, while a nearly negligible 0.06 V threshold voltage shift is obtained under a negative gate-bias stress of -10 V for the same stress duration. These results indicate that the p-Cr<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> gate stack effectively suppresses carrier injection under electrical stress and significantly enhances gate stability at elevated temperatures.

title: AI-Driven Cognitive Framework for Predictive Social Behaviour Dynamics Analysis
authors: Pradhiba SPM, Saroj Sangeetha G, Lakshminarayana K, Roopadarshini S, Binoy Mathew, Kanchan Khatreja, Abijith G R
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Predictive analysis of social behavior remains a challenging task due to the dynamic, heterogeneous, and interconnected nature of social systems. In this paper, the author introduces an AI-based cognitive framework to predictive dynamics of social behavior analysis that incorporates the feature optimization, periodic modeling, and graph-based learning. The framework uses time-windowed event sequencing and normalization to effectively track the changing interaction patterns and minimize noise and sparsity of data. Minimum Redundancy Maximum Relevance is applied in order to select the most informative social and behavioral features to make the model more efficient and interpretable. The TGNN is then used to fit time-dependent social relationships and long-term dependencies among agents. PyTorch Geometric Temporal is used to implement the proposed approach and learn dynamically presented graph data effectively and at scale. Experimental analysis proves that the framework is more accurate and stable in predictions compared to traditional machine learning and other traditional graphbased methods. The findings demonstrate the possibility of the framework to be used in analyzing social behavior in real-time and on a large scale.

title: Hybrid LSTM and SVM Framework for Uncertainty Reduction in Renewable Energy Forecasting
authors: Hima Bindu Eluri, D. Krishna, S. Venkateshwarlu, Karnam Rajeev, D. Chandra Sekhar
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Electricity supply is becoming less reliable due to the fact that renewable energy sources like wind, solar only function when the weather is ideal. Renewable energy forecasts can benefit from the suggested methodology of this paper, which integrates Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) with Support Vector Machine (SVM). Locating time-dependent relationships in time-series data is a strong suit of long short-term memory (LSTM) models. Generalization and nonlinear regression are two areas where support vector machines (SVMs) truly shine. With the help of SVM for more accurate predictions with less uncertainty and LSTM for feature selection and learning of order, this approach achieves both objectives. When training and testing our models,it consult databases that contain information regarding renewable energy sources, such as the amount of sunlight and the speed of wind. There are a number of popular metrics used to evaluate performance, including RMSE, MAE, and the ability to forecast uncertainty. The simulation findings demonstrate that compared to the individual LSTM and SVM models, the hybrid LSTMSVM architecture outperforms them both in terms of accuracy and uncertainty. A fantastic approach to reliably use renewable energy sources is the one proposed. A long-lasting power system can be more easily constructed with its aid.

title: ISAR Neural 3D Reconstruction of Space Target with Cross-Frame Attention
authors: Huiting Fang, Yifan Chen, Feng Wang
abstract: With the unprecedented growth of commercial and military space activities, orbital resources are increasingly scarce, and the development of space situational awareness technologies is urgently required. Compared with 2D Inverse Synthetic Aperture Radar (ISAR) images, 3D models of space targets provide more comprehensive information and are regarded as the key to accurate space situational awareness. However, structural flickering and inherent persistent artifacts in 2D ISAR sequences make precise reconstruction unattainable for most existing ISAR 3D reconstruction methods, and detailed structural features of targets cannot be fully preserved. A neural 3D reconstruction framework based on cross-frame attention mechanism is proposed to address this issue. In the overall framework, implicit modeling of the target geometric structure is implemented via a coordinate-based multi-layer perceptron (MLP). A feature-based encoder is adopted to capture semantic consistency under Adjacent viewing angles. Regularization constraints are imposed on the reconstruction process through cross-frame attention weights. Stable scattering centers of the target are retained during optimization, and transient artifacts are suppressed. ISAR simulation images closer to real scenarios are employed in experiments compared with baseline methods. Improved quality of novel view synthesis and accuracy of 3D reconstruction are verified by experimental results.
