title:
On the SiC/SiO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Interface and SiO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Gate Reliability of Planar and Trench SiC MOSFETs
authors: P. Moens, A. Feng, M. Avramenko, G. Gomez, J. Seo, H. Kim, D. An, S. Maslougkas, T. Nigam, E. Van Brunt
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: The intrinsic SiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> gate reliability of both planar and trench MOSFETs is compared and found to be at par with Si devices. Trap creation (and filling) near the semiconductor/SiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> interface causes E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ox</inf> to drop linearly with log<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">10</inf>(time), an effect that takes place in all devices studied. For over 7 decades of stress time, Si, SiC trench and SiC planar behave the same in terms of SiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> reliability. Under Gate Switching Instability (GSI) stress, two "phonon-kicks" are observed in planar and trench MOSFETs The first phonon kick is stronger in trench devices but saturates at around 1x10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">12</sup> cycles whereas the second phonon kick is only visible as from ~5x10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">12</sup> cycles but shows no saturation in both planar and trench, at least up to 3x10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">13</sup> cycles (~1.5 years of testing). Ron degradation is less than 4%, both for planar and trench.

title: Performance and Fault Analysis of LCC Versus VSC Back-to-Back HVDC Systems for India-Bangladesh Interconnection
authors: Sajit Ghising Tamang, Pitom Chakraborty, Md. Shahiduzzaman, Mostafa Galib Jami, Mehedi Hassan, Imran Khan
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The present India-Bangladesh power exchange is a 1000 MW (two units of 500 MW) Line Commutated Converter (LCC) back-to-back (B2B) HVDC border crossing (between Behrampur in India and Bheramara in Bangladesh). Although this system has managed to offer trusted bilateral power transfer, it experiences primary operational difficulties, such as commutation failure, the absence of reactive power control, and poor functionality under weak-grid or fault conditions. The paper will provide a comparative technical analysis of the technical performance of each system and a fault examination of the current LCC-based B2B HVDC system as opposed to a proposed Voltage Source Converter (VSC)-based B2B HVDC setup. The systems are modeled under the same grid and load parameters, which are similar to those of the India-Bangladesh interconnection. DigSILENT PowerFactory software is used to design the two configurations. The test evaluates the system's performance under steady state conditions, dynamic voltage stability, reactive power performance, harmonic distortion, and transient responsiveness during AC/DC fault conditions. Simulations under the proposed VSC system show that the power can be actively and reactively controlled, quick fault recovery is possible, minimal harmonics can be produced, and excellent voltage can be offered in the event of a contingency.

title: Detecting Stress Levels using Physiological Signals
authors: Akash Mojumder, Nasmin Suraya, Nazifa Tabassum Tandra, Mohaimen-Bin-Noor
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Stress has become a critical issue in modern life and severely impacts mental, physical and emotional health. Chronic stress is associated with cardiovascular disorders, immune dysfunction and depression making early and accurate detection essential. Traditional self-report methods, such as questionnaires, lack subjective and continuous monitoring capabilities. With the advancement of wearable sensing and machine learning, it is now possible to objectively detect stress through physiological signals. This study investigates supervised machine learning algorithms for stress level classification using the WESAD dataset, which includes multimodal signals such as electrodermal activity, blood volume pulse, heart rate, temperature and accelerometer data collected under different mental states. The data were preprocessed by outlier removal, normalization and dimensionality reduction using principal component analysis. Multiple models were evaluated using precision, accuracy, recall, and F1-score metrics. Experimental results show that the stacking ensemble learning model achieved the highest accuracy of 91.45 percent, outperforming other classifiers such as random forest and logistic regression. These results demonstrate that multimodal physiological features are effective indicators of stress and that ensemble learning significantly increases the reliability of detection, paving the way for real-time, wearable-based stress monitoring applications.

title: Frequency-Aware Domain Generalization_supp1-3689419.pdf
authors: Xiang Xiang
abstract: Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) exhibit surprising zero-shot generalization and emergent phenomena across various tasks. However, the underlying mechanisms behind these behaviors remain unclear. By analyzing the perception of image frequencies by DNNs, we establish the association between the generalization behavior and frequency-aware regions. DNNs with stronger generalization exhibit wider frequency-aware regions. Therefore, we improve the generalization performance by broadening the frequency awareness. Specifically, we enable DNNs to learn the relations between high-frequency components and semantic labels through frequency decomposition and mixup. Based on hierarchical feature alignment, we allow larger submodels to guide the frequency awareness of smaller submodels. Beyond training, we ensemble submodels to extract features from different frequency bands to enrich DNNs’ frequency awareness during inference. We validate the effectiveness of our proposed method in image classification and object detection tasks in single and multi-source domain generalization scenarios. We also demonstrate the plug-and-play scalability of our method across existing approaches and different DNNs.

title: Comprehensive Review of Advancements in Near-Infrared Imaging Techniques for Vein Detection
authors: Shrabani Das, Tapan Chandra Ghos, Md Asif Khan, Papel Chandra, J. Bino, Venkateswara U, K. Immanuel, Md Tabil Ahammed
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Near-Infrared (NIR) vein imaging and detection have become critical in medical diagnosis, biometric identification, and clinical procedures such as venipuncture. Advances in non-invasive vein visualization systems have been discussed extensively in this paper, focusing on imaging devices, image processing methods, and machine learning algorithms for vein detection. The review consolidates key contributions from twenty-two new publications, emphasizing portable, low-budget, highresolution systems capable of real-time vein detection under varying skin colors and illumination conditions. In addition, existing multiview and fullview finger vein authentication modalities are discussed. A comparative system specification table, image processing techniques, data sets, and performance metrics summarize the discussions. The paper outlines current challenges and future research directions for efficient, accurate, and cost-effective vein detection systems.

title: FeatherTrust-EM: An Ensemble Deep Learning Framework for Continuous Zero-Trust Authentication via Keystroke Dynamics
authors: Hasin Almas Sifat, Koushik Biswas Arko, Raiyan Yusuf, Md. Mortuza Ahmmed
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: As cybercrime has increased and the incidents of identity theft and data breaches have continued to rise, the inability to provide ongoing authentication using static passwords or PIN numbers demonstrates its failure as a secure means of online identity verification. Continuous Authentication is a new method of determining user verification based upon behavioral biometrics in real time and dynamically. We propose the solution termed FeatherTrust-EM, which is a continuous system of authentication that applies keystroke dynamics and utilizes the Zero-Trust Architecture (ZTA) principles to provide user verification in real time. FeatherTrust-EM utilizes both fixed and free typing data to process, develop features, and increase robustness of input representation. The system utilizes an ensemble approach to deep learning by employing CNN1D (1D Convolutional Neural Network) to learn local temporal patterns of keystroke dynamics and Keystroke BERT (KBERT) to learn information that relates long-range dependencies on typing. The fusion of these two neural networks and the different characteristics of input representations enhance overall performance far beyond either network as stand-alone. Experimental results show that FeatherTrust-EM can provide an accuracy rate of 87.3% and a macro F1 score of 87.25%, while the Top-2 accuracy is 95.7% and the Top-3 accuracy is 97.9%. In addition, FeatherTrust-EM provides the ability to incorporate ZTA to allow for user verification continuously, by implementing adaptive confidence thresholds to maintain an acceptable level of False Acceptance Rate (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 0 1 \%}$</tex>) and False Rejection Rate (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 7. 5 \%}$</tex>). The analysis of confusion matrices, PR/ROCs (AUC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.949 / 0.999$</tex>), and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\tau$</tex> reliability curves shows that the FeatherTrust-EM system can be operated at scale and reliably in real-time continuous authentication environments. FeatherTrust-EM has set the new standard for secure and seamless user verification and has created a foundation to be used in modern, high-risk environments where trust is something to be continually earned.

title: Lightweight Coprime Array Virtualization Method Based on Polynomial Interpolation
authors: Xincheng Tang, Xiaokang Qi, Qiangli Xi, Kuiwen Xu, Yezhou Wu, Chunyi Song, Zhiwei Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Multi-target sensing and detection is an important scientific proposition for radar signal processing, and direction-of-arrival (DOA) estimation is a key subsequent step after target detection. In order to reduce hardware complexity, aperture virtualization is usually used to improve the performance of the sparse MIMO radar which has less physical channels. However, the existence of holes in the virtualization of the sparse array will lead to the loss of some array element information, degrading radar's detection performance. Existing interpolation strategies commonly recover these missing lags through convex optimization, improving estimation capability at the expense of substantial computational cost. In this paper, we propose an array virtualization method based on polynomial interpolation to optimize radar's multi-target DOA estimation performance with reduced computational complexity. In this method, the received signals will be pre-processed under the idea of power equalization. Subsequently, the proposed polynomial interpolation algorithm is introduced to fill the holes in coprime array, a typical kind of sparse radar array. The outputs are second-order statistics (SOS), which are related to the virtual measurements under the Hermitian positive semi-definite Toeplitz condition. Consequently, the virtual interpolation signals covariance matrix is yielded by the Toeplitz covariance matrix reconstruction approach, making it suitable for all the mainstream radar DOA estimation method. Meanwhile, a lightweight coprime sensing radar array virtualization method is proposed, which can further enhance DOF performance with the proposed array configuration. Both simulation and hardware validation results demonstrate the improvement on both of multi-target DOA estimation performance and computational complexity, making it a practical solution for diverse lightweight radar platforms.

title: Model Predictive Control for an Integrated Power-Pulsation Buffer Four-Cell Flying-Capacitor PFC Converter
authors: Parth Patel, Ambrish Chandra
abstract: Single-phase power factor correction (PFC) front-ends must buffer a twice-line-frequency power pulsation, which is typically handled with bulky electrolytic DC-link capacitors or by adding decoupling components for active buffering. This article integrates the power-pulsation buffer (PPB) into the existing flying-capacitor stack of a totem-pole four-cell flying-capacitor (TP-4CFC) rectifier, eliminating auxiliary PPB hardware and reducing dependence on large, lifetime-limited electrolytic capacitors. Control is realized by a lexicographic multiobjective model predictive strategy that requires no weight tuning and is implemented for fast execution on a dual-core microcontroller with a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathbf{6.66}\,\bm{\mu}{\mathbf{s}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> sampling period. The proposed flying-capacitor voltage-reference generation method maintains current-tracking performance while increasing the usable energy-buffering capability of the existing converter. A 2.5 kW, 400 V experimental prototype validates the approach over a wide operating range, demonstrating up to 54% reduction in DC-link ripple, 3.7% grid-current total harmonic distortion (THD), and half-cycle settling for rated load-step disturbances. Whereas auxiliary active PPB stages often reduce efficiency, the proposed approach yields a modest efficiency gain over standard operation, corroborated by a loss-and-stress analysis. The results indicate a practical path to higher power density for compact single-phase front-end rectifier applications.

title: Mamba-Based Time and Frequency Fusion Network
authors: Yongdong Zhu, Lei Zou, Na Li, Chenxi Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Statistics, Modeling and Advanced Algorithms (ASMA)
abstract: Multivariate time series forecasting plays an indispensable role in a wide range of fields. Feature extraction in current mainstream neural network methods is typically conducted only in a single dimension, either the time domain or the frequency domain. However, this single-domain extraction paradigm not only leads to limited feature representation capability but also overlooks the inherent complex dependencies between time-domain and frequency-domain features. In recent years, existing studies have sought to mitigate this limitation by fusing the time and frequency domains, yet there remains room for further improvement. To address these issues more effectively, a novel model architecture, the Mamba-Based Time and Frequency Fusion Network, is proposed in this study. Specifically, a frequency-domain mamba extractor is designed, which leverages the Mamba module to efficiently model dependencies in the frequency domain. Meanwhile, a time-domain feature extraction path is incorporated to enhance the capture of temporal dependencies. Furthermore, a time-frequency fusion mechanism is designed, which enables the adaptive integration of information from both domains and further optimizes the model representation performance. Extensive experiments are conducted on seven widely used multivariate time series datasets. Experimental results show that the proposed model demonstrates consistently superior performance in the experiments, while simultaneously possessing strong feature extraction capabilities, lower computational overhead, and higher forecasting accuracy.

title: Multi-Domain Feature-based Analysis EMG framework for Neuropathy vs Myopathy Characterization and Rehabilitation Assessment
authors: Farhana Mehmood, Amen Ali, Ahsan Naveed, Zia Mohy Ud Din
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robotics and Automation in Industry (ICRAI)
abstract: Myopathy and Neuropathy are two major neuromuscular diseases that significantly affect normal muscle function through muscle fiber degeneration and defective neural transmission, which results in irregularity in muscle activity patterns. This irregular change in muscle activity can be assessed considerably through the analysis of Electromyogram (EMG) signal. Several recent studies on neuromuscular disorders, such as myopathy and neuropathy, remain constrained as most approaches are limited to either condition and consist of single domain analysis, either time, frequency or complex domain. Many of these studies lack comprehensive characterization and analysis of neuromuscular behaviors and limit effective comparative analysis across different neuromuscular conditions. Moreover, neither study includes assessment of therapy-induced tremor reduction or functional rehabilitation. These limitations highlight the need for a work that provides a comprehensive framework capable of efficiently analyzing neuromuscular disorders in an integrated way. To overcome these limitations, this paper presents a unified framework that integrates the preprocessing of normal, myopathic, and neuropathic EMG signals using a publicly available dataset with multidomain analysis of time and frequency domain features. Additionally, another publicly available EMG rehabilitation dataset is utilized to evaluate therapy-induced changes in tremor characteristics and other neuromuscular activity patterns. The results successfully represent a progressive increase in the RMS values (0.06135 to 0.33516), which are highest in neuropathy, followed by myopathy and healthy. This clearly distinguishes muscle activation patterns across these conditions. In the future, this framework could be further improved by incorporating machine learning techniques and larger datasets to obtain automation in feature extraction and classification.

title: An Explainable Stacking Ensemble Model Combining Advanced DL Techniques for Depression Detection in Social Media Posts
authors: Kamrul Golder, Majharul Alam Rana, S.M. Jubaer, A F M Fahim Sadique, Anupam Tarafder, Shahanaj Akter
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Social media platforms often mirror people's emotional states, making depression one of the most urgent mental health issues in the globe. In order to determine the best representation for text-based depression diagnosis, we examine the efficacy of three feature extraction techniques in this study: Word2Vec, Hashing, and Doc2Vec. Expanding upon this, we suggest a stacking ensemble framework that incorporates DL architectures such as Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Unit (BiGRU), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM), with Random Forest (RF) acting as the meta-classifier. Smoothing methods were utilized to improve model stability across the various feature extraction procedures, and training and assessment were conducted on a balanced and publicly accessible Reddit dataset. With an impressive 99% accuracy, the proposed RF_Stacking model outperformed alternatives while maintaining exceptionally low error rates. The findings highlight how reliable the suggested framework is for detecting depression in real time and how it may be incorporated into digital mental health platforms for widespread monitoring and early intervention.

title: Influence of p-GaN Cap Layer Engineering and Thermal Effects on the Electrical Performance of AlGaN/GaN HEMTs
authors: Hrithik Mohanto Himel, Md. Rafiqul Islam, Arefin Ahamed Shuvo, Partha Jyoti Roy, Durjoy Sarkar Dhrubo
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This study analyzes the effects of temperature and p-GaN cap thickness on device performance using Silvaco TCAD simulations. Both parameters play an important role in improving the device's electrical properties. The addition of a pGaN cap layer changes the threshold voltage to the positive area, resulting in normally-off functioning. The maximum transconductance of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 0 0 m S} / \mathbf{m m}$</tex> is achieved for cap thicknesses of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \text{nm}, 10 \text{nm}, 20 \text{nm}, 30 \text{nm}$</tex>, and 50 nm. As the device temperature rises from 300 K to 600 K, power dissipation and subthreshold swing increase, but on-state current falls from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$565.71 \text{mA} / \text{mm}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$251.01 \text{mA} / \text{mm}$</tex>. However, the threshold voltage remains virtually constant throughout the temperature range. The drain-induced barrier lowering (DIBL) increases as the temperature rises from 300 K to 600 K, as the temperature rises, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V_{\text{th}}$</tex> and gate control decrease while carrier energy increases, increasing DIBL. Collectively, these findings indicate that an optimized p-GaN/AlGaN/GaN HEMT structure can greatly enhance the device's electrical performance and thermal stability.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)

title: Selectively Generating High-Purity Vortex Beams by a Planar Filtenna with a Compact Integration of Spiral SSPP and HMSIPW
authors: Si-Wen Zhang, Jun Hu, Yue-Fei Hu, Huang-Yan Li, Xiang Wang, Wen Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: Orbital angular momentum (OAM) waves exhibit orthogonal phase characteristics and have attracted considerable interest in wireless communications, radar imaging, and electromagnetic sensing applications. This paper presents a compact planar OAM filtenna that integrates an incomplete uniform circular array (UCA), a spiral spoof surface plasmon polaritons (SSPPs) waveguide, and a half-mode substrate integrated plasmonic waveguide (HMSIPW) filter. The SSPPs feed excites sequentially rotated circularly polarized elements to generate vortex waves, while the HMSIPW introduces bandpass filtering without increasing the size. Theoretical analysis establishes the mode relation, revealing the OAM generation mechanism. Simulated and measured results confirm frequency-selective radiation from 4.12 to 5.82 GHz, with six modes and mode purities up to 0.96. The proposed filtenna achieves compact, high-purity, and frequency-reconfigurable OAM radiation, showing great potential for wireless communication and radar imaging systems.

title: Towards Sustainable Smart Cities with AGI-Enabled Cyber-Physical-Social-Thinking Systems: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Jifar Wakuma Ayana, Zita Lifelo, Huansheng Ning, Jianguo Ding
abstract: Sustainable smart cities require cross-domain, adaptive, and anticipatory intelligence. Current Internet of Things and Artificial Intelligence systems are largely siloed, limiting their ability to support comprehensive urban management. This review addresses this gap by examining the prospective integration of Artificial General Intelligence with the Cyber-Physical-Social-Thinking paradigm. We synthesize research published from January 2015 to September 2025 to provide an overview of how Artificial General Intelligence can serve as the cognitive core, facilitating proactive orchestration across Internet of Things-driven urban layers. Specifically, the paper (1) presents a conceptual analysis of reasoning and meta-cognitive capabilities within the Cyber-Physical-Social-Thinking framework, (2) maps enabling technologies, including digital twins, neuro-symbolic Artificial Intelligence, and the edge-cloud continuum, that operationalize this vision, and (3) reviews applications in mobility, energy management, and climate resilience, highlighting potential gains in efficiency, adaptability, and sustainability. Key technical, ethical, and governance challenges, such as scalability, security, and algorithmic fairness, are discussed. By integrating these insights, the review provides a unified perspective and a roadmap for future research on human-centric, resilient, and sustainable urban intelligent systems.

title: Congratulations on 10 Years of Power Electronics

title: Knowledge Graph-based Debiasing for Trustworthy Recommendation Systems
authors: Youru Li, Xuying Ning, Zhenfeng Zhu, Hanqiu Wang, Zhi Cai, Minnan Luo, Qinghua Zheng, Yao Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: These years have witnessed remarkable progress in modeling user behaviour from personalized online services, especially knowledge graph-based recommendation systems. Meanwhile, more studies are focusing on aspects beyond recommendation performance, since such an observational data-driven paradigm is posing threats to both users and society in terms of trustworthiness. In fact, existing problem-oriented solutions still face significant challenges, as almost all of them suffer from the generality limitations to improve their trustworthiness in a uniform fashion. To address these issues, we propose a plug-and-play <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">D</u>eb<underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</u>asing framework for <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">K</u>nowledge <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">G</u>raph-based <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">R</u>ecommendation <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">S</u>ystems, also known as DiKGRS. Specifically, the Knowledge-augmented Pseudo-Samples Generation (KPSG) method, a novel data augmentation perspective, is proposed to explore more auxiliary information beyond observational user behaviors. Furthermore, the Debiasing Value Networks (DVN), is also developed to evaluate the reliability of generated pseudo-samples by modeling both the item popularity and user demographic bias in the platform. Moreover, an adaptive weighting coordination module is performed to coordinate the proposed DiKGRS framework and its backbones. Experimental results on four real-world datasets from different online service personalization scenarios have illustrated that the proposed framework can significantly improve the trustworthiness of existing knowledge graph-based recommendation systems. The code has been released public available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/alipay/A-Knowledge-augmented-Method-DiKGRS</uri>.

title:
Extremum Seeking With Double Integrators in the Presence of Local Extrema
                    <sup>*</sup>
authors: Raik Suttner, Christian Ebenbauer, Sergey Dashkovskiy
abstract: We study the problem of global extremum seeking in the presence of local extrema. We investigate two different perturbation-based methods: 1) a well-known classical extremum seeking scheme for steady-state output optimization, and 2) a source seeking scheme for a two-dimensional point mass. In each of these two scenarios, the closed-loop system involves a damped double integrator subject to an oscillatory force. An averaging analysis reveals that the respective averaged system is again a damped double integrator, but now subject to a potential force. The potential force is given by the gradient of a locally averaged objective function. Such a function is less prone to have undesired local extrema and is therefore better suited for global optimization. We provide sufficient conditions for semi-global practical uniform asymptotic stability of the closed-loop system. The sufficient conditions only involve assumptions on the averaged objective function but not the original one.

title: A Computationally Efficient Hierarchical MPC with Adaptive Horizon for Real-Time Electro-Thermal Control of IGBTs
authors: Yi Liu, Hongtao Niu, Yunhui Mei
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: High-fidelity electro-thermal models are essential for accurately predicting and controlling the junction temperature of Insulated Gate Bipolar Transistors (IGBTs). However, integrating these complex models into a Model Predictive Control (MPC) framework imposes a prohibitive computational burden, rendering real-time implementation infeasible. To resolve this conflict between model fidelity and computational tractability, this paper proposes a Hierarchical Adaptive-Horizon MPC (HAH-MPC) framework. The architecture decouples the control problem into a two-level structure: a slow-timescale outer loop for thermal management, which utilizes a high-fidelity coupled model, and a fast-timescale inner loop for current tracking. The key feature is an adaptive-horizon mechanism within the outer loop that dynamically adjusts the prediction length based on system operating conditions, effectively balancing control performance with computational load. The proposed controller is implemented and experimentally validated on a 15-kW test bench. Experimental results demonstrate that the HAH-MPC effectively limits junction temperature overshoot during severe load transients, a significant improvement over conventional methods, while maintaining its worst-case execution time safely within the 1-ms control period. This work presents a viable framework for deploying advanced, model-based thermal control strategies in practical power electronic applications.

title: Intention to Continue Using Information Technology in Accounting Information Systems–The Mediating Role of Information Technology Mindfulness
authors: Thuan Luong Duc, Huong Truong Thi Thu
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Information and Network Technologies (ICINT)
abstract: The study applies the Expectation Confirmation Model (ECM) to investigate the impact of key factors on the intention to continue using the Information Technology (IT) of accountants and users of Accounting Information System (AIS) at public educational institutions in Vietnam. The research employs a quantitative method, surveying 171 participants. The Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modeling (PLS-SEM) technique, utilizing SmartPLS 4.1 software, is employed to analyze the data. The research results clarify the relationships in the proposed model, where all hypotheses are accepted. Notably, IT mindfulness has the strongest influence on the intention to continue using IT. The research results propose theoretical implications and practical management implications to enhance perceived usefulness and IT mindfulness in maintaining the use of IT at public educational institutions in Vietnam.

title: Integrating AI in Food Engineering Education: Raising Student Competencies in Market Research and Consumer-Driven Product Development
authors: Blanca Isabel Maldonado-Guevara, Juan José Carracedo-Navarro, Lucio López-Cavazos
abstract: New product development is vital for the competitiveness and survival of the food industry. Consequently, future Food Engineers must master Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools to manage the high-dimensionality data required to understand evolving consumer needs. This study analyzed seventh-semester students who utilized ATLAS.ti software to investigate product categories through social media, scientific databases, and field research. By leveraging AI-driven sentiment analysis and pattern recognition, students processed complex information more efficiently than the control group. The results indicate that AI integration enables students to organize diverse data sources effectively and establish deeper analytical relationships in less time. Ultimately, this approach fostered clearer, more creative contextual interpretations, allowing students to design food product proposals with superior commercial viability and closer alignment with market demands.

title: An MLP-Based Multi-Frequency Enhancement Long-term Prediction Model for Multivariate Load Forecasting
authors: Huaiyu Xing, Bin Jia, Wenlong Ding, Jinlong Wu, Changlong Li, Jinqiu Song, Xiaobo Chi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the increasing complexity of the power system and the growing global demand for electricity, it is increasingly important to obtain accurate and reliable forecasts of load, price, and key meteorological variables. However, existing methods focus on a single target variable—typically the electricity load— rather than simultaneously predicting multiple indicators such as load, price, and weather-related features. Such single-output forecasting is often inadequate for generation companies and other market participants when making informed operational and economic decisions. While some time series forecasting methods can predict multiple indicators simultaneously, they often lack effective mechanisms to adaptively extract global frequency features and model long-term dependencies. To address these issues, this paper proposes an MLP-based multi-frequency enhancement network called MFE-Net. It first introduces a Multi-level Discrete Wavelet Transform to capture intricate information in both the time and frequency domains. By treating each frequency component as an independent channel, it utilizes an adaptive frequency fusion block to further integrate frequency components via learnable weight adjustment and fuses outputs to enhance forecasting accuracy. Experimental results show that the proposed model outperforms state-of-the-art models in different time periods and sampling frequencies, achieving the best results in forecasts with a maximum lead time of up to 30 days.

title: Research on Space Power Supply Technology Based on GaN Device
authors: Baolei Dong, Zirui Zhu, Xiao Wang, Xu Huang, Lidong Shi, Tao Liu, Suliang Wu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: As the core energy guarantee of spacecraft, space power supply system puts forward strict requirements for power density, efficiency, extreme environmental adaptability and long-term reliability of devices. As the core representative of the third generation wide band gap semiconductor materials, gallium nitride (GaN) has shown revolutionary application potential in the field of space power supply with its high breakdown electric field, high electron mobility, low switching loss and excellent radiation resistance and high temperature resistance. In this paper, the core characteristics of GaN devices and their adaptability to space power demand are systematically summarized, and its application status in typical space power subsystems such as solar array power conversion, energy storage system management, electric propulsion power supply and load point power supply is expounded in detail. The key challenges such as radiation effect, extreme temperature stability, long-term reliability and packaging compatibility faced by GaN devices in space power supply applications are deeply analyzed, and the corresponding solutions are summarized from the aspects of device design, process optimization and system integration. Finally, combined with the development trend of space technology, the application prospect of GaN devices in super-large satellite constellation power network, deep space exploration power system and space solar power station is prospected. This paper aims to provide reference for the research and development and engineering application of GaN devices in the field of space power supply, and promote the development of space power supply system in the direction of high efficiency, miniaturization and long life.

title: Upgrading LSTM-Attention to a Transformer Decoder for Image Captioning with Object and Visual Features
authors: Nima Sharifinia, Hasti Marjaneh, Mohammad Javad Parseh, Pejman Rasti
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Accurately generating natural language descriptions of visual scenes remains a core challenge in vision–language understanding. Existing image captioning models often entangle improvements in architecture, feature extraction, and large-scale pretraining, making it difficult to isolate the contribution of each component. In this work, we present a controlled architectural study that replaces a recurrent LSTM-attention decoder with a Transformer-based decoder while preserving the same visual feature inputs. The proposed framework fuses global context features from Xception and object-level embeddings from YOLO, ordered by detection confidence to emphasize salient visual entities. This design enhances long-range dependency modeling and cross-modal attention without introducing additional semantic or relational supervision. Trained solely on the MS-COCO dataset, the model achieves substantial improvements over a strong baseline—raising CIDEr by more than 100%—and produces captions that are both syntactically fluent and semantically grounded. These findings provide a reproducible and efficient foundation for future transformer-based captioning systems.

title: Interpretable Stacking Ensemble for Photovoltaic Power Forecasting under Extreme Low Irradiance
authors: Hanyang Guo, Weiru Wang, Xiaojuan Han
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Precise forecasting of photovoltaic (PV) power under extreme low-irradiance weather is essential for grid stability yet remains a formidable challenge due to complex nonlinearities. This paper proposes an interpretable Stacking ensemble framework that synergistically integrates prediction and explanation. The model employs a two-stage architecture, where six heterogeneous base learners are strategically combined via a meta-learner trained with cross-validation, effectively enhancing robustness and accuracy under diverse extreme conditions. Beyond achieving superior performance (average R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>>0.95, RMSE reduction>9.5% versus the best standalone model), the framework incorporates SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) to quantitatively interpret predictions. SHAP analysis reveals scenario-specific driving features—such as diffuse irradiance dominance during heavy rainfall—and deciphers nonlinear feature-power interactions. The proposed approach not only delivers a highly accurate forecasting tool but also provides actionable physical insights, offering a reliable and transparent decision-support solution for PV integration under increasingly volatile climates.

title: When Systolic Arrays Meet AI Engines: Architectural Constraints on AMD Versal ACAP
authors: Jason Kimko, Jason Cong
publish: 2026 IEEE 34th Annual International Symposium on Field-Programmable Custom Computing Machines (FCCM)
abstract: The AMD Versal ACAP provides a powerful heterogeneous architecture for accelerating kernels like general matrix multiplication (GEMM). Because the AI Engine (AIE) array mimics the spatial regularity of a systolic array (SA), mapping SA dataflows onto the fabric is highly intuitive. However, maximizing performance requires overcoming specific coordination challenges between data movement and compute cores. In this work, we demonstrate that the gap between theoretical peak and realized performance is the result of a fundamental "Systolic Tax" imposed by the AIE architecture. We provide a systematic study of 2D systolic mapping to quantify the architectural cost incurred when traditional wavefront data forwarding consumes execution cycles otherwise available for processing. This engine-level overhead leads to a simulated 8.2% compute cycle loss. To address this constraint, we implement a multicast-based SA (MCSA) design that leverages hardware multicasting for efficient operand delivery. This design is supported by a Mixed-Integer Non-Linear Programming model used for architectural optimization. Our design achieves a programmable logic frequency that analytically saturates the logic-engine communication bandwidth. While MCSA delivers a 1.28× throughput improvement over prior state-of-the-art systolic mappings, performance is ultimately limited by synchronization overhead, establishing a ceiling for systolic designs on these platforms and revealing the lack of parallel AIE-initiated data movement as a critical architectural bottleneck.

title: A Study on the Impact of Extraction Electrode Axes on Implant Dose in Applied Materials E500 Medium Current Implanter
authors: Yik Han Kwee, King Suen Lee, Ching Chee Chu
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: This study describes how the extraction electrode axes can have an adverse impact on the implant dose in the Applied Materials (AMAT) E500 medium current implanter. The extraction electrode, through its beam steering capability, influences the ion beam trajectory, which in turn affects the ion beam’s parallelism — a critical factor for accurate dose calculations during implantation in this implanter. Experiments were conducted using B⁺ implants at 200 keV under conditions of proper and improper extraction electrode axes tuning, with beam parallelism quantified using delta deflector voltage (DDV). DDV was found to be significantly higher under the improper axes tuning condition which indicated a greater degree of non-parallelism. Despite meeting the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) specifications for beam parallelism at 100 kV, the wafers implanted under improper tuning with B⁺ implants at 200 keV exhibited up to 5% overdose, through sheet resistivity measurements and dynamic secondary ion mass spectrometry (DSIMS) analysis. These findings highlight the importance of monitoring extraction electrode axes positions and DDV to ensure implant accuracy and minimize dose deviations. The findings also suggest that the OEM specifications for beam parallelism may be insufficient for higher energy implants and additional beam parallelism verification at larger energy ranges should be performed to ensure accurate ion implantation in the AMAT E500 medium current implanter.

title: DiffRouter: A Differentiable Routing Framework for UltraScale FPGAs
authors: Xiaohan Gao, Zhili Xiong, Yusu Wang, David Z. Pan
publish: 2026 IEEE 34th Annual International Symposium on Field-Programmable Custom Computing Machines (FCCM)
abstract: Routing has become a major bottleneck in large-scale FPGA design flows, where traditional routers rely on sequential net-by-net processing and rip-up-reroute heuristics. This inherent sequentialism limits parallelism, leads to suboptimal utilization of routing resources, and significantly increases runtime on modern FPGAs. This paper introduces DiffRouter, the first differentiable, gradient-based FPGA routing framework with GPU acceleration. By lifting routing into a continuous optimization space, DiffRouter enables highly concurrent routing and improved global optimality before projecting solutions back onto the discrete fabric. The framework consists of three key stages: (1) Resource-pruned preprocessing, which extracts compact routing variables per net to reduce memory footprint and problem complexity; (2) Differentiable global routing via gradient descent, which formulates routing as a Lagrangian-relaxed optimization that minimizes wirelength while iteratively enforcing connectivity and congestion constraints as relaxed terms; and (3) Postprocessing, which maps the continuous wire distributions to a valid UltraScale routing solution and completes solutions by detailed routing. Experiments on the FPGA 2024 Routing Contest benchmarks show that DiffRouter outperforms state-of-the-art parallel routers, achieving over 5× speedup over RWRoute and 2× over Vivado, and running 18% faster than the state-of-the-art academic FPGA router Potter while delivering competitive critical path wirelength.

title: Bidding Strategies for Multi-Type Charging Operators in Peak-Shaving Markets
authors: Shiyu Wang, Qinyi Xue, Qingyu Wu, Linjun Shi, Yang Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Electric vehicles are a significant controllable load resource, offering potential for grid regulation. EV types exhibit both regularity and uncertainty, so this study proposes separate data structure models for public and private charging stations, which are then aggregated. During day-ahead bidding for charging operators in peak-shaving ancillary services across various station types, a two-tier bidding-allocation model is proposed. This allows operators to participate in electricity ancillary services markets while also building these markets through flexible resource dispatch. Real-world simulation results show that the two-tier bidding-allocation strategy lowers net operating costs for charging operators and improves power mobilisation at public transport charging stations.

title: Research on Electric Field Distribution of Composite Insulators under Different Extreme Weather Conditions
authors: Shicong Sun, Xuchen Lu, Yi Lin, Guanhua Li, Quanying Liu, Jingwei Han, Zhenchi Zhang, Bingbing Yu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Extreme weather associated with the northeast cold vortex tends to alter the surface conditions of composite insulators, thereby distorting their electric field distribution and threatening the safe and stable operation of transmission lines. Taking 500 kV high-voltage composite insulators as the research object, this paper establishes multi-physics coupling simulation models under three typical extreme weather conditions using COMSOL Multiphysics. The influence laws of different weather conditions on the electric field intensity in various directions and the surface electric field distortion of insulators are systematically analyzed. Key regional electric field parameters are extracted through simulation to clarify the influence degree of different extreme weather events, and the correlation between electric field distortion characteristics and operation risks is analyzed. The results show that short-duration heavy precipitation and rainstorm with strong wind exert the most significant distortion effect on the electric field distribution of insulators. The uniformity of electric field distribution is relatively poor under hail weather, and the edges of insulator sheds are always the high-incidence areas of electric field concentration. The research results provide theoretical support and data reference for the insulation design, risk assessment of composite insulators and safety protection of transmission lines under extreme weather, and possess important engineering value for improving the adaptability of high-voltage transmission lines to extreme weather.

title: A Transfer Learning Modeling Framework Fusing Domain Knowledge and Its Application in District Heating Network
authors: Liang Zhang, Hui Zhang, Bo Sun, Jing Chen, Xiangyu Zhou
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: District heating networks (DHNs) represent a critical infrastructure for decarbonizing the heating sector. However, as DHN systems grow in scale and complexity, the computational burden associated with numerical simulations increases significantly. To meet both user demand and energy efficiency objectives under emission reduction policies, efficient and accurate simulation modeling approaches are essential for the optimal operation and scheduling of DHNs. This paper proposes a transfer learning-based modeling framework integrated with domain knowledge to enable high-fidelity network modeling. First, the partial differential equation governing the dynamic temperature evolution of fluid in heating pipelines is incorporated into a Physics-Informed Neural Network (PINN) as embedded domain knowledge. Subsequently, high-resolution data generated by Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) simulations are employed to construct a pre-trained model for an individual heating pipe. Finally, this pre-trained model is fine-tuned according to the specific initial and boundary conditions of various pipelines within the district heating network, enabling accurate knowledge transfer through integration with sparse measurement data. Experimental results show that the proposed framework agrees well with reference numerical solutions in the literature and exhibits superior accuracy compared to conventional neural network–based modeling approaches.

title: Research on Integrated AC/DC Shore Power Supply based on DC Bus
authors: Mengtao Zheng, Yifei Yang, Minjia Tan, Zhenfei Wu, Jianpeng Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: To address the need for coexisting AC and DC power supply for various ships in port shore power, this paper proposes an AC-DC integrated shore power system based on a DC bus, ensuring stable DC bus voltage and high-quality AC voltage and current output. A dual closed-loop control strategy for voltage and current based on a synchronous rotating coordinate system has been designed to achieve stable voltage of the DC bus and high-quality voltage and current output on the AC side. Simulation verifies its steady-state and dynamic performance under load changes. Experiments on a low-voltage prototype test AC and DC outputs, showing good power quality and stability, meeting diverse ships' direct and AC shore power demands.

title: Correlation between particles and induced voids at the bonding interface
authors: Vasco Matias, Hans Billington, Soheila Bagherilimaei, Serena Iacovo, Soon Aik Chew
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: Contamination particles induce voids at the bonding interfaces in wafer-to-wafer (W2W) and die-to-wafer (D2W) hybrid bonding (HB). Such bonding voids can be detrimental to the electrical properties of the devices being fabricated.In this study we have deposited W-particles with e-beam assisted deposition on the surface of SiCN-coated wafers, proceeded to bonding the wafers with a blanket counterpart and inspected the resulting bonding voids with a Scanning Acoustic Microscope.We could obtain a correlation between the sizes of the deposited particles and the respective induced voids – an amplification factor of 50x. Such correlation allows determining the sensitivity of bonding voids to the dimensions of particles at the bonding interface.

title: A novel approach for controlling robotic eye movement using U-Net based model
authors: Reza Javanmard Alitappeh, Saeed Abbasi, Mohammad Reza Jafari, Mahyar Eslamian
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: This paper proposes a simplified vision-based approach to the control of robotic eye systems using deep learning. Instead of complex physical modeling and nonlinear control equations, desired eye movements are encoded as 2D images, and a U-Net encoder predicts corresponding motor control signals. We present a model that has a CNN encoder, multilayer perceptron, and smoothing module that generates six motor signals for a 6-DoF cable-driven robotic eye. Trained on 199 trajectories in simulation, a system based on the proposed approach achieved a validation loss of 0.0075 (MSE) and rendered smooth, biologically plausible movements at reduced computational cost. While there is still some residual noise, our results indicate that trajectory images can be an effective representation of motion dynamics and an efficient alternative to physics-based models. The work described here points out the potential of image-driven control to simplify robotic eye design and lays the foundation for future real-time implementations on physical hardware.

title: Convolutional Estimation of Reactive Betweenness and Dependency from DC Load Flow Data
authors: Mohammad Shahraeini
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: This paper introduces a lightweight U-Net that estimates the full reactive dependency matrix D<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Q</inf> from the DC dependency matrix D<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">dc</inf> with R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> =0.985, RMSE = 0.0198, and MAE = 0.0097 on masked entries of the IEEE 14-bus system. Using a direct input skip-addition and standard MSE loss, the CNN accurately captures reactive flow mediation patterns from computationally inexpensive DC load flow inputs. Post-processing thresholding eliminates small negative artifacts, ensuring physical consistency. Results on 50 unseen scenarios confirm near-perfect reconstruction of D<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Q</inf>, enabling fast reactive power flow analysis without full AC load flow. The approach offers a scalable, interpretable, and real-time AI-driven solution for modern power system analytics.

title: Workshop: Fostering Quality Participation and Critical Thinking in Hybrid Classrooms with the Whytson App
authors: Mexitli Sandoval-Reyes, Maria Ballesteros, Ricardo Ganem
abstract: This interactive online workshop introduces “Whytson” a novel mobile application designed to gamify and enhance student participation in educational settings. Traditional methods of eliciting student questions often fail to engage the entire class, limiting the development of critical thinking and social intelligence. Whytson addresses this by providing a structured platform for students to request turns to speak, with instructors providing real-time, qualitative feedback on their contributions. Originally developed and tested in face-to-face bachelor's degree courses, this workshop will adapt the Whytson methodology for online and hybrid environments. Participants (primarily educators and educational innovators) will experience the app from a student's and instructor's perspective, engage in collaborative discussion on its pedagogical foundations, and brainstorm implementation strategies for their own contexts. The session is grounded in constructivist learning theories, neuroscience of learning, and gamification principles. Furthermore, the workshop will serve as a live research session to validate our instruments (measuring empathy, critical thinking, technology adoption, and engagement) with an expert audience. Attendees will leave with a practical understanding of how technology can be used to foster a culture of respectful, empathetic, and high-quality classroom dialogue.

title: Student Perceptions of Usefulness and Ease of Use of a Gamified Learning Platform in STEM Courses
authors: Guillermo M. Chans, César Merino-Soto, Ana Paula Valle-Arce, Alec Martinez-Gallardo, Angeles Dominguez, Genaro Zavala
abstract: This study explores the implementation of a webbased gamification platform, Fred!, designed to enhance student engagement and motivation in STEM higher education. Grounded in Self-Determination Theory, the platform integrates short-term, medium-term, and long-term achievements, token-based rewards, and peer collaboration. A total of 270 undergraduate students from chemistry courses participated in the study, divided into experimental (gamified) and control (traditional) groups. Two constructs-perceived usefulness and perceived ease of use-were evaluated through adapted Likert-scale items. Results from confirmatory factor analysis supported the two-factor model with high reliability across both groups. A significant difference was found in perceived ease of use, favoring the experimental group. While no global differences emerged for perceived usefulness, local analysis revealed variation at lower score percentiles. Findings suggest that gamification can be implemented effectively and perceived as easy to use when supported by a dedicated platform. Implications for broader applications and future research directions are discussed.

title: Measuring Changes in Sustainability Knowledge: A Case Study of a Civil Engineering Course
authors: Khaled Shaaban
abstract: Introductory transportation courses are commonly offered in any civil engineering program to educate students about the various aspects of transportation systems. It is important to assess whether these courses effectively deliver knowledge and promote sustainable transportation literacy among students. The purpose of this work is to evaluate the effectiveness of an introductory transportation course in developing sustainable transportation literacy among college students. The study assesses the baseline transportation literacy of students at the beginning of the semester, evaluates the impact of the course on students' knowledge of transportation systems, and identifies any areas of improvement. The pre-course survey indicated a baseline of limited awareness across environmental, social, economic, and technological aspects of transportation sustainability. However, the post-course survey shows a different picture. The students showed an increase in their awareness of all areas. The observed changes affirm the effectiveness of the course in increasing students' knowledge of sustainable transportation.

title: Low-Precision POSIT Arithmetic for Spiking Neural Networks with Kahan Summation
authors: Shayan Naghizadeh, Reza Adinepour, Morteza Saheb Zamani
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) are highly suitable for energy-efficient neuromorphic computing due to their event-driven nature. Unlike Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs), where POSIT arithmetic has been used for storage saving and require decoding and encoding during each operation, SNNs process information only when events occur, minimizing the overhead. This makes POSIT arithmetic particularly advantageous for SNNs, as it avoids unnecessary operations. However, at low bit-widths such as 6 bits, accumulation errors can arise, affecting accuracy. To address this problem, we propose integrating Kahan's compensated summation algorithm into the synaptic accumulation process of neurons in SNNs, which restores loss precision and enhances the stability of low-precision SNNs. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed 6-bit POSIT format achieves 93.2% accuracy—close to 8-bit POSIT ( 95.1% ) and 32-bit floating point ( 97.6% )—while reducing LUT usage by about 27% and FF usage by about 55% compared to the 32-bit floating-point implementation, highlighting its efficiency for low-power, resource-constrained neuromorphic hardware and demonstrating its potential for next-generation neuromorphic accelerators.

title: Degradation-Aware Stochastic Sizing of Second-Life Battery Energy Storage System for Microgrids
authors: Yifang Ren, Qiuwei Wu, Guocheng Song, Tianyi Zhao
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: The optimal sizing of second-life battery energy storage systems (SLBESS) in microgrids is influenced not only by operating cost, but also by battery degradation, renewable and load uncertainty, and distribution-network constraints. To address this issue, this paper proposes a degradation-aware stochastic sizing model for SLBESS in a radial microgrid. In the proposed formulation, battery degradation is explicitly modeled by a state-of-charge-weighted throughput method and embedded into the sizing problem through a degradation cost term. On this basis, a two-stage stochastic optimization framework is established to coordinate SLBESS sizing and microgrid operation scenario-based uncertainty. The model jointly considers electricity purchasing cost, degradation cost, and demand charge, while network constraints are represented by a linearized power flow formulation. To validate the effectiveness of the proposed method, a modified five-bus radial microgrid is adopted as the test system. The results show that the proposed method can reduce the annual electricity bill and suppress peak grid import while satisfying the voltage and branch-flow constraints of the microgrid. After accounting for the annualized storage cost, the system still maintains a positive net annual benefit. Overall, the proposed model provides an effective approach for SLBESS sizing in microgrids while explicitly considering battery degradation, uncertainty, and network constraints.

title: Multi-objective Optimization Scheduling for Integrated Energy Systems Considering Exergy Efficiency and Stepped Carbon Trading
authors: Jinlong Wu, Xing Dong, Wenlong Ding, Huaiyu Xing, Xiaobo Chi, Changlong Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Traditional optimization strategies for integrated energy systems (IES) predominantly focus on the balance of energy quantity while overlooking energy quality matching, often resulting in significant exergy destruction due to high-grade energy degradation. Furthermore, exergy efficiency is typically treated as an evaluation metric rather than an active optimization objective. To address these issues, an IES multi-objective optimization scheduling model is proposed, which incorporates exergy efficiency as a key objective and introduces a stepped carbon trading mechanism to achieve low-carbon, cost-effective, and high-quality system operation. The ε-constraint method is adopted to generate the Pareto front, and a Pareto front curvature analysis (PFCA) method is proposed to determine the optimal compromise solution that balances economic performance and exergy efficiency. Case studies validate the comprehensive superiority of the proposed method in achieving high-quality, low-carbon, and economic operation of the IES studied.

title: Intelligent Model for Identifying Defective Aircraft Parts
authors: Zohre Fasihfar, Hamidreza Rokhsati, Hossein Lotfi, Peyman Vafadoost Sabzevar
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: The aviation industry heavily relies on the safety and reliability of aircraft parts. Detecting and addressing defective parts promptly is crucial to ensure the overall safety of aircraft operations. In this paper, we propose an AI-enhanced Extreme Learning Machine (ELM) model for the identification of defective aircraft parts. Our objectives include developing a comprehensive dataset of aircraft parts with labeled defect samples, training the ELM model using this dataset, integrating real-time monitoring for proactive defect detection, and creating a user-friendly interface for aviation professionals to interact with the system. Our methodology involves collecting a diverse dataset of aircraft parts with annotated defect labels, preprocessing the data, training the ELM model, incorporating sensor data for real-time monitoring, and developing an intuitive interface for users to visualize and analyze identified defects. The model achieves an impressive accuracy rate of 94.27% in accurately detecting and classifying defects in aircraft components. The high accuracy achieved by our model demonstrates its effectiveness in identifying defective aircraft parts and contributes to enhanced aviation safety and maintenance efficiency. Ongoing evaluation and validation of the model's performance are essential to ensure its continued accuracy and reliability in real-world scenarios. The successful implementation of our AI-enhanced ELM model has the potential to revolutionize the identification of defective aircraft parts, leading to safer and more reliable aviation operations.

title: Cu Via Resistance Failure Induced by CMP in Advanced Interconnects
authors: Wong Kian Hui, Leong Voon Kiong, Tubati Sricharan, Peck Yan Zheng
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: Elevated electrical via resistance in copper (Cu) top vias is attributed to insufficient CU removal during Platen 2 (P2) Chemical Mechanical Planarization (CMP), which is responsible for soft Cu landing and stops at Ta barrier by endpoint detection. Failure analysis based on Transmission Electron Microscopy (TEM) identified barrier metal cracking, Cu voids, and Cu migration from top via (TV) into the top metal (TM). Simulations confirm that reduced polishing from P2 process results in residual Cu remaining and subsequently induced a longer higher frictional force during barrier polishing on Platen 3 (P3), causing microstructural damage at the Cu/barrier interface. These defects, exacerbated by subsequent high-temperature processes, compromise via integrity and further increase resistance. The study highlights the need for optimized CMP endpoint control and uniform Cu removal to ensure interconnect reliability in advanced semiconductor fabrication.

title: Enhancing Privacy-Preserving Software Development from a Developers' Perspective: A Survey
authors: Tharaka Wijesundara, Shakya R.D.N., Matthew Warren, Nalin Arachchilage
abstract: None

title: Online Advertisements with LLMs: Opportunities and Challenges
authors: Soheil Feizi, Mohammadtaghi Hajiaghayi, Keivan Rezaei, Suho Shin
abstract: None

title: An Intelligent Student Management System Using Temporal Stability-Aware Adaptive Learning
authors: Aihui Nie, Zhixin Yao
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: In modern times, student management systems played a critical role in modern educational institutions by allowing effectively handling of academic records, attendance tracking and student performance monitoring. In existing big data-driven decision tree-based student management platform is used to classify and monitor student performance and behavior. However, this existing approach struggled with overfitting which is caused by frequent learning updates and restricted scalability while assisting continuous real-time data that limited its efficiency in deployment. To overcome these challenges, Temporal Stability-Aware Adaptive Learning Layer (TSALL) is proposed for an improved student management system. Primarily, data is collected from multiple real-world sources and preprocessed to remove inconsistencies and data is normalized for ensuring reliability. Further, TSALL analyzes student data across many time periods for identifying stable behavior and patterns of performance. Here, learning updates are selectively triggered only when consistent patterns persist over time by avoiding unrequired model complexity and reduces computational overhead. Moreover, data is employed to classify student performance and update individual student's profiles in real-time. Finally, proposed TSALL-based student management system efficiently reduces model complexity by supporting real-time student data processing. Additionally, by learning only from stable student behavior patterns, proposed TSALL obtained better generalization and reliable performance. Therefore, proposed TSALL framework achieved better results in accuracy (95.1 %) and precision (97.4 %) compared to existing approach.

title: Automated Machine Learning Trainer: A No-Code Framework for Model Development and Explainability
authors: G. Sivagami Devi, P. Kanagalakshmi, A. Dharani Dharan, E. Harini
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Rapid progress in Machine Learning (ML) has accentuated the necessity for an informed available tool, which strives to cut and reconstruct model development and deployment. Traditional ML workflows tend to be too convoluted, requiring specific interventions in data preprocessing, model selection, hyperparameter tuning, and interpretability. We therefore present the Automated Machine Learning Trainer (AMLT), an easy-to-use no-code framework that automates the whole ML pipeline, allowing access to model development for both beginners and professionals. AMLT houses embedded Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) methods to augment transparency into its predictions, increasing trust in ML applications. Comparison experiments showcased how AMLT proves to be efficacious, cutting down time-to-deploy by 35 % and increasing prediction performance by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{5} \%$</tex> in comparison with existing AutoML frameworks. Our results indicate that AMLT has the potential to democratize the automated application of ML, allowing its wide adoption across various disciplines.

title: Distribution-Agnostic Idle-Age-Aware Joint Channel and Packet-Size Learning in Cognitive Radio Networks
authors: Haythem Bany Salameh, Malik Umar
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Cognitive radio (CR) enables opportunistic spectrum access while protecting primary users (PUs). This opportunistic transmission is uncertain due to time-varying PU activity (unpredictable PU returns) and heterogeneous channel quality. PU idle times differ across channels, in which some have long mean availability, others only short opportunities, and PU returns vary in severity. In multi-user CR networks (CRNs), contending CR users must coordinate over a control channel to reserve an idle PU channel and select a packet size that maximizes throughput while reducing PU-return-induced forced terminations. Most existing designs optimize channel assignment or packet sizing in isolation, assume memoryless PU idle times, and often ignore control contention/reservation overhead that directly reduces the residual transmission time. This paper investigates joint channel assignment and packet-size control under unknown PU availability while explicitly accounting for realistic control overhead time. We model the Residual PU-idle Time with Idle Age using a distribution-agnostic residual-life survival function and incorporate control overhead into the packet probability of success (PoS). We formulate a maximum-profit, discounted-return problem that maximizes long-term network goodput subject to a required PoS, a received signal-to-noise (SNR) threshold, and exclusive channel occupancy. Due to the uncertainty in PU activity and the long-term nature of the optimization problem, which cannot be solved using conventional optimization methods, we model it as a constrained Markov decision process (CMDP). To solve the resulting CMDP, we propose an on-policy State-Action-Reward-State-Action (SARSA) reinforcement learning (RL) algorithm with convergence-based exploration and a quantized/pruned action-space mechanism that filters infeasible channel–packet-size pairs to reduce online search complexity. The proposed RL-based algorithm learns perchannel PU dynamics online and jointly selects the throughput-maximizing channel and packet size. Our method is a hybrid model-assisted/model-free design, in which online survival/quantile estimates are used only for PoS-based feasibility screening, while SARSA learns the long-term policy directly from interaction without requiring a parametric PU model. Simulations under heterogeneous PU availability/severity show up to 32% throughput improvement, with fewer retransmission attempts than reference schemes.

title: Enhancing Security in Multicasting Using Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface and Selection Combining Diversity Technique
authors: Shafi Abid, Md. Zahurul Islam Sarkar, Muhtasib Farhan, Ahshan Habib
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This paper analyzes physical-layer secure multicasting in a wireless network that utilizes reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RIS) utilizing selection combining (SC) diversity receivers. Although RIS enhances signal directionality, it is still vulnerable to deep fading. This work shows that the integration of SC provides a vital diversity advantage that RIS alone cannot attain. Hence it successfully mitigates deep fading in secure multicast scenarios. Analytical formulations in closed form have been established for the Probability Density Function (PDF), Cumulative Distribution Function (CDF), and Ergodic Secrecy Multicast Capacity (ESMC). The results confirm the study and demonstrate that the proposed RIS-assisted SC technique achieves significant enhancements in secrecy compared to the non-diversity system, especially at higher Signal-to-Noise Ratios (SNR). Moreover, the findings suggest that increasing the number of RIS reflecting elements improves secrecy performance, whereas an increased number of multicast users decreases ESMC due to the selection of the lowest SNR value at the destination user.

title: An Emotion Recognition Framework from EEG Signals Using Dynamic Graph Convolutional Neural Networks (DGCNN)
authors: Aniruddha Deb, Bishal Roy Chowdhury, Dip Muhuri
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: In this paper, a high-performance eEG-based emotion recognition is suggested, using a Dynamic Graph Convolutional Neural Network (DGCNN). The model was trained using the SEED-IV dataset which is a publicly available multi-session EEG benchmark dataset at Shanghai Jiao Tong University which contains preprocessed, cleaned, and filtered recordings of four emotional states happy, sad, fear, and neutral. In contrast to other deep learning-based EEG signal models, which tend to represent the processed data as fixed grids or sequence of signals, DGCNN inherently views the dynamic and intertwined nature of the brain by making EEG electrodes nodes of a graph and dynamically changing the edges between these nodes as the training progresses. PyTorch v2.3.0 (cu121), Torch-scatter, Torch-geometric are used as the deep learning environment, with easy loading of EEG features, data augmentation, and easy loading of Python 3.10, scikit-learn, matplotlib and seaborn being used to evaluate and visualize the models. It has been experimentally demonstrated that DGCNN is always high-performing and stable, and it performs even better in emotions state classification in comparison with the classical CNN and RNN-based models. The article sheds light on how neural modeling with graphs can be used to decode the affective brain patterns and issues more neuro-sensitive EEGbased applications in the health care, mental health, and humancomputer interface.

title: RIS-Assisted Atomic MIMO Receiver
authors: Qihao Peng, Jiuyu Liu, Qu Luo, Yi Ma, Pei Xiao, Maged Elkashlan, George K. Karagiannidis
abstract: In this paper, we propose a novel and low-complexity atomic multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) receiver architecture assisted by a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS). By introducing RIS and utilizing pulse amplitude modulation (PAM), the phase of the transmitted signal is effectively aligned with that of the local oscillator (LO), mitigating phase ambiguity and reducing the complexity of the receiver. To tackle the resulting non-convex optimization problem, we reformulate it into a tractable form by minimizing the Frobenius norm of an equivalent matrix, which is solved using an Adam-based gradient descent algorithm. Building upon the optimized equivalent signals, we develop a low-complexity signal detection algorithm and evaluate its performance. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed RIS-assisted atomic MIMO receiver significantly enhances detection performance compared to the conventional atomic MIMO receivers. More importantly, these results underscore the proposed architecture's potential for scalable and high-performance atomic MIMO communication systems.

title: Comparative Study of Cnn Architectures for Automated Detection of Lung Diseases from Chest X-Ray and Ct Scan Images
authors: Misbahul Sheikh, Nafisa Tasnim Neha, Naushin Mamun, Samia Habib, Kazi A Kalpoma
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Different types of respiratory disease are common and have affected many people around the world. However, their detection remains challenging due to overlapping symptoms and complex radiographic patterns. The early detection of lung diseases such as Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis (IPF) and Pneumonia is critical. Although CT scans are commonly used to diagnose IPF and other lung diseases, they are expensive and not always widely available. Some deep learning techniques, including convolutional neural networks (CNN), are used for lung disease detection and prediction. The basic CNN model performs poorly for images with abnormal orientation. This study proposes a Deep Learning (DL) based approach to initially classify IPF and other lung diseases using both chest X-ray and CT scan images. We employed a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architectures like VGG19, DenseNet121, and ResNet50 to perform classification tasks using three different approaches. First, we have collected a real-time dataset of X-ray and CT scan images from across Bangladesh and prepared three different datasets: 3-class X-ray classification (IPF, Pneumonia, Normal) using our own dataset, 15-class X-ray classification using NIH Chest X-ray 14 dataset with customized classes, and 3-class CT scan classification (IPF, Pneumonia, Normal) using our own dataset. Our findings demonstrate that DenseNet121 consistently outperformed the other models across all tasks, achieving the highest accuracy of 96% in 3-class X-ray classification, 88% in 15 -class X-ray classification, and 95% in 3-class CT scan classification.
