As a scalable and budget-friendly alternative for automated lung disease screening, the fusion of deep learning and chest X-rays proves highly promising. Such AI-driven systems could significantly bolster diagnostic workflows in hospitals, particularly within medical infrastructures that face severe resource limitations.

title: LegoBot: Modular, Compact, Device-Agnostic Robot for Endovascular Catheter and Guidewire Manipulation in Stroke Thrombectomy
authors: Weibo Gao, Nitin Srinivasan, Dongming Piao, Qianhe Fan, Miki Guo, Rui Li, Peter Patalano, Hao Su
abstract: State-of-the-art endovascular robots are often limited by coupled translation–rotation actuation, narrow compatibility with off-the-shelf devices, and fixed architectures that are difficult to reconfigure for multidevice procedures. These limitations are especially restrictive in neurovascular intervention, where long working lengths, tortuous anatomy, and coordinated manipulation of multiple catheters and guidewires are frequently required. In this work, we present LegoBot, a modular, compact, device-agnostic robot for neurovascular catheter and guidewire manipulation. First, we design a decoupled two-degree-of-freedom (2-DOF) actuation mechanism together with an instrument-agnostic adjustable clamping mechanism, enabling independent or simultaneous instrument translation and rotation while accommodating off-the-shelf devices with diameters ranging from 0.33 to 3 mm. Second, we present the mechatronic design of a modular catheter robot with interchangeable adapters for different instruments, which support module-level device exchange. We also design a module coupler that connects adjacent robotic modules for multi-instrument actuation and improves rotational torque transmission through long, compliant instruments. Benchtop characterization of the proposed 2-DOF mechanism showed peak insertion force up to 5.3 N and rotational torque up to 8.4 mN<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\cdot$</tex-math></inline-formula>m, exceeding reported clinical requirements. Phantom studies demonstrated a two-module (4-DOF) configuration for diagnostic angiography and a three-module (6-DOF) configuration for aspiration thrombectomy, including successful clot aspiration in a neurovascular phantom.

title: Edge-Based Respiratory Profiling: An Explainable Hierarchical Framework for Automated COPD Screening
authors: Hassan Jubair, Abid Ahasan, Mithela Mehenaz, Nabomita Sarkar, Md. Nurunnabi Mollah, Md. Merajul Islam
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Respiratory diseases such as COPD are a leading cause of global mortality, yet automated screening tools are often hindered by the high computational cost of Deep Learning models and a lack of clinical interpretability. Furthermore, many high-performance benchmarks in the literature are inflated by methodological flaws, specifically data leakage during validation. This study proposes a Hierarchical Explainable Framework that decouples event detection from disease diagnosis to achieve high-precision screening on edge devices. First, a lightweight Random Forest classifier, engineered with 156 statistical spectral features, detects adventitious sounds (wheezes/crackles) with an inference latency of 0.67 ms, reducing computational cost by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 4}$</tex> times compared to CNN baselines. Second, a patient-level statistical profiling module aggregates these discrete events to generate a robust diagnostic score. Our hierarchical approach achieves a competitive cycle-level accuracy of 55.5% and a Diagnostic AUC of 0.90 in distinguishing COPD patients from healthy controls. Explainability analysis identifies harmonic variability (Chroma features) as a superior biomarker for obstruction compared to standard spectral envelopes. These results demonstrate that computationally frugal edge classifiers can yield high-precision clinical insights when aggregated via robust statistical profiling.

title: Integrating Pap Smear Imaging for Enhanced Deep Learning-Based Cervical Cancer Screening
authors: Pradeep Kumar, Santosh Kumar Choudhary, Manoj Anumolu, Kreshnaa Priiyaa, Sharmitha Vasantham, Manoj Kumar
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Cervical cancer is a worldwide health concern. In short, the detection of cervical cancer through the Pap smear test will certainly contribute to saving a large number of lives. But the cumbersome process of checking the results of the Pap smear test by the expert only, based on human intuition, results in biased outcomes. A number of deep learning and Machine learning techniques developed but the efficacy is limited for Pap smear images. In short, a novel automatic system for the detection of abnormal cervical cells by incorporating deep learning with Focused Mask Scoring R-CNN is proposed in this research paper which incorporate a lightweight squeeze-andexcitation (SE) block into the mask head to recalibrate channelwise feature responses. In the experiment, a high accuracy of 97.05 % is achieved by the model. In addition, the average precision, recall and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.97,0.98$</tex>, and 0.97 are obtained through the use of the testing data, respectively. Further, the area under the ROC curve is found to be greater than 0.95 by using the novel automatic system introduced in this paper, the experimental output shows the model is performing well as compare to existing state of art techniques.

title: LEAF-CT: Lightweight Energy-Aware Forwarding with Controlled Transmission for Underwater Acoustic Sensor Networks
authors: Saravanan Subramani, Kiran Kumari Gayam, P. Thiyagarajan, R. Naveenkumar, Shalini Raji A
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Wireless underwater communication incorporating acoustic sensor networks plays a crucial role in various applications that includes ocean monitoring, environmental surveillance, disaster prevention and offshore exploration. However, underwater acoustic channels struggle with issues such as low bandwidth, high propagation delay and other energy constraints that determines reliable and efficient data transmission as a challenging task. Therefore, to resolve these issues, a Lightweight Energy-Aware Forwarding scheme with Controlled Transmission (LEAF-CT) is proposed for underwater acoustic sensor networks. Specifically, the proposed LEAF-CT framework integrates distance-based controlled transmission for short-range communication with a simple rulebased adaptive forwarding mechanism for long-range multi-hop routing. Rather than employing complex metaheuristic optimization, relay nodes are selected through a lightweight scoring function that considers residual energy, progress toward the sink and current forwarding load. Such that this design assists to significantly reduce computational overhead while maintaining energy efficiency and routing reliability. Thus, the simulation-based evaluation using the same network settings as the base model, LEAF-CT demonstrates improved network lifetime, reduced energy consumption, and stable packet delivery performance compared to conventional routing protocols and complex optimization-based schemes.

title: A Comparative Analysis of YOLO Variants with Improved YOLOv12m Model for Students' Classroom Behavior Detection
authors: Souvik Pramanik, Md. Ibrahim Khalil, Ornima Hafizur, Kamruddin Nur
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The automated detection of student classroom behavior is critical for enhancing engagement assessment and providing educators with real-time feedback. However, this task remains challenging owing to the prevalence of small, subtle, and often occluded actions. This study presents a robust framework for the real-time recognition of five key student behaviors: raising hands, using phones, bowing heads, bending, and turning heads. We conducted a comprehensive comparative analysis of state-of-the-art YOLO models, including YOLOv9m, YOLOv10m, YOLOv11m, and YOLOv12m. Our findings identified YOLOv12m as the most promising architecture in this domain. Through systematic fine-tuning and the- incorporation of advanced data augmentation, attention mechanisms, and hyperparameter optimization we significantly enhanced the YOLOv12m model. The resulting system achieved a state-of-the-art test mAP50 of 0.916, precision of 0.881, and recall of 0.861, outperforming all its baseline counterparts. Ablation studies confirmed the efficacy of multi-scale feature aggregation and attention modules in mitigating challenges related to scale and occlusion. The proposed framework demonstrated high robustness in real classroom scenarios, offering a practical and intelligent tool for automating behavioral analysis and supporting interactive teaching methods.

title: Cost Effective Sizing of Off Grid PV Systems and Batteries for Residential Energy Systems
authors: Tanjima Akter Mukta, Md Abu Talha, Sancoy Barua, Tahsin Nawroj
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The rising demand for electricity and the need for sustainable energy solutions have led to increased interest in solar photovoltaic (PV) systems, particularly for residential applications. This paper focuses on optimizing PV panel and battery sizing for residential energy systems in Bangladesh. Using Sequential Quadratic Programming (SQP), the study aims to minimize the total cost of off-grid PV systems while ensuring efficient energy demand. The optimization process was applied to three consumer categories—larger, medium, and smaller—based on their energy consumption profiles. Results demonstrate that the optimized systems provide significant cost reductions, with smaller consumers benefiting from a 90% reduction compared to larger consumers. Additionally, the study shows how PV systems with integrated battery storage can reduce grid dependency and improve energy reliability. The findings highlight the importance of accurate system sizing to balance cost, performance, and energy needs, offering a scalable solution for residential electrification in regions with high solar potential.

title: A Closed-Form Phase Design for RIS-Aided UCA-OAM Systems
authors: Zhongpeng Wang, Hongyang Han
abstract: Reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) phase optimization is essential for enhancing the capacity of RIS-aided uniform circular array (UCA) based orbital angular momentum (OAM) systems. In this letter, we propose a closed-form phase design that aligns the propagation paths of all OAM modes to maximize the total received power. The proposed scheme computes the RIS reflection coefficients without iteration, offering a low-complexity alternative to conventional iterative algorithms. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed design achieves near-optimal sum-rate performance across a wide range of RIS sizes and transmit powers, while iterative algorithms (e.g., DSM) provide marginal gains only under extreme high-SNR conditions. The proposed design also serves as an efficient initialization for more sophisticated optimizers.

title: Physically-Based Polarization Reflection Separation for Laser Stripe Extraction on Highly Reflective Metal Surfaces_supp2-3699730.mp4
authors: Yiting Yu
abstract: Polarization-based laser vision imaging offers high accuracy, noncontact sensing, and strong robustness to interference, making it promising for weld seam localization and intelligent welding. However, for planar filet-weld workpieces with highly reflective metallic surfaces, reliable laser stripe segmentation and precise extraction of centerlines and feature points remain challenging. This is because inter-reflection interference and camera saturation severely degrade measurement accuracy. In this article, we propose a novel physically based polarization reflection separation method using polarization features and spatial structural information for robust laser stripe extraction. The physical principles and polarization properties of direct- and inter-reflections from metallic surfaces are investigated through theoretical modeling and simulation. By combining the Stokes parameter <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$S\!_{2}$ </tex-math></inline-formula> with a saturation region segmentation criterion, the inter-reflection regions of the laser stripe are automatically determined. An adaptive mask dilation approach is then introduced to suppress saturation-induced interference without complex parameter tuning. Finally, the centerlines and feature points are accurately extracted using the gray-gravity method with least-squares fitting. Experiments demonstrate superior segmentation and extraction performance under strong reflective interference, achieving an average feature-point extraction error of 2.36 pixels, a repeatability error within ±1.16 pixels, and an average computational time of 0.2531 s. Moreover, comprehensive evaluations across varying groove angles, laser rotation angles, exposure times, surface conditions, and dynamic robotic platforms further validate the robustness and effectiveness of the proposed method. This study highlights the potential of polarization imaging for addressing optical interference challenges in industrial welding scenarios, providing a solid foundation for intelligent and automated welding.

title: A Collaborative Hybrid CNN–LSTM Framework for Real-Time Multichannel Retail Demand Forecasting Using Edge–Cloud Computing
authors: Aashish K C, Khandakar Rabbi Ahmed, Sunil Kumar Goyal, Md Anisur Rahman Chowdhury, Towfika Salam, Mohammad Tajrian Arafat Alvi
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Demand forecasting in a modern multichannel retail ecosystem is essential to maximising consumer happiness, simplifying the supply chain, and improving inventory levels due to its precision and reliability. The diffusion of boundaries of online and offline shopping channels results in an extremely erratic, nonlinear, and spatiotemporal demand process that is difficult to accurately predict using statistical and machine learning models like CNNs and LSTMs. Particularly, the centralized cloud computing model possesses inherent scalability and latency bottlenecks in real-time conditions in an omnichannel retail environment of today, and is a major constraint in implementing these models in online applications. To address such problems, the proposed paper describes a Collaborative Hybrid CNN-LSTM architecture to forecast the demand in real-time omnichannel retail with the help of a more efficient edge-cloud computing paradigm. The specific implication of our proposed framework to reduce the latency in computations and bandwidth bottlenecks is the notion of utilizing the collaborative learning process to enhance the latency inference and the enhancement of the data confidentiality in a modern edge-cloud computing framework to support big retail data analytics applications and machine learning modeling applications. Experiments using a large-scale retail dataset demonstrate that our proposed model is significantly better than LSTMs, CNN models, RNN models, and LightGBM machine learning models, achieving 94.7% model accuracy and 0.96 ROC AUC value, respectively, thereby confirming their practical effectiveness in a large-scale modern omnichannel retail setting when applied as a big.

title: A Transformer-Based Perspective to Bangla-English Code-Mixed Sentiment Analysis
authors: Sandwip Dash Talukder, Debajyoti Das, Mahmudul Hasan Badhan, Rubel Ahmod, Md Saidul Islam, Islam Md Saiful
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Despite Bangla being the seventh most spoken language worldwide, it remains a low-resource language in Natural Language Processing, particularly concerning Bangla-English (BE) code-mixed text. While Banglish (transliterated Bangla) has been studied, sentiment analysis of text containing native Bangla script alongside English words remains underexplored. This paper addresses this gap by introducing BE-CodeMix-3, a manually annotated sentiment dataset of 8,179 social media comments categorized into three sentiment classes. We provide a comprehensive evaluation across various architectures, including traditional Recurrent Neural Networks (LSTM, BiLSTM, GRU) and transformer-based models (mBERT, XLMRoBERTa, and BanglaBERT). Experimental results demonstrate that language-specific pretraining is critical for capturing the nuances of code-mixed text. The BanglaBERT model significantly outperforms all baselines, achieving a peak accuracy and F1-score of 0.90. Our findings highlight that while multilingual models provide a strong foundation, transformers pretrained specifically on large-scale Bangla corpora offer superior semantic and syntactic encoding for real-world, codemixed applications.

title: Towards Heterogeneous Multimodal Sentiment Analysis with ASR Errors via Multi-Granularity Contrastive Coordination_supp1-3702501.pdf
authors: Zhiqiong Wang
abstract: Multimodal sentiment analysis improves the understanding of human emotions by integrating multiple modalities. However, the language modality accessed by the actually deployed MSA systems is obtained through automatic speech recognition (ASR), which can induce text errors and lead to MSA performance degradation. Besides, multimodal heterogeneity and multimodal information imbalance further aggravate the negative impact of ASR errors. To this end, we propose a multi-granularity contrastive coordination framework (MGCCF) for achieving robust MSA under ASR errors. Specifically, an error sentiment awareness calibration (ESAC) unit is devised to learn cross-modal word-level correlation and contextual information, enabling the detection and calibration of erroneous sentiment word embedding. Furthermore, a multimodal contrastive coordination (MCC) paradigm is presented to achieve cross-modal multi-granularity alignment from both global semantic and local temporal aspects. Meanwhile, MCC ingeniously leverages non-verbal features to reinforce the ability of ESAC to correct anomalous sentiment information and refine the expression of language features. Eventually, a multimodal information fusion (MIF) approach is developed to deal with multimodal information imbalance, which interpretably quantifies the contributions of different modalities and achieves multimodal dynamic fusion. Comprehensive experiments on seven MSA datasets demonstrate that our MGCCF achieves remarkable performance and surpasses the current state-of-the-art MSA methods.

title: An RGB-Event Hierarchical Fusion Enhancement Network for Object Detection
authors: Kaiyi Wang, Xinyu Hou, Siying Liu, Chen Cheng, Lei Deng, Hao Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Object detection in frame-based vision systems often faces the problem of significant performance degradation under challenging conditions due to the inherent sensing limitations of conventional cameras. Event cameras, with their advantages of high temporal resolution and sparse data output, offer a promising solution for addressing the performance degradation problem. However, due to the asynchronous and sparse nature of event data, event camera–based detection methods generally exhibit lower accuracy than their image-based counterparts. Therefore, effectively fusing data from both event and frame-based cameras represents the main challenge. To this end, this study introduces a hierarchical fusion enhancement network (FENET) designed to achieve more robust object detection through complementary multimodal fusion. First, to address the problem of underutilization of event timescales in conventional dense representations, this study designs an innovative, learnable multi-timescale event preprocessing module. This module generates temporal aggregation frames (TAFs) by leveraging event data from a spectrum of intervals, ranging from standard RGB exposure times down to finer timescales. This approach ensures the comprehensive capture and clear delineation of the target's structure, thereby enhancing the integrity of the visual information. Furthermore, a two-branch adaptive enhanced cross-modal aggregation module (ECAM), which guides the adaptive fusion of features from both modalities by first self-enhancing the features of each, is employed. The main goal of the ECAM is to achieve efficient feature aggregation by combining global context with local spatial information, subsequently using the aggregated features to steer the adaptive fusion process. Finally, by recognizing the scarcity of large-scale, high-quality annotated datasets for event-based object detection, the DSEC-D dataset comprising 60 sequences, with a total of 70,739 images and over 250,000 labels, is manually constructed. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that the proposed FENET can successfully overcome the perceptual limitations of conventional cameras. Notably, the proposed FENET surpasses current RGB-event fusion schemes, achieving an 8.6% improvement in the mAP50 metric and a 3.7% improvement in the mAP50:95 metric over the FRN baseline. We have released the code and the dataset at https://github.com/wkydawang/FENET.

title: Transmission Cross Section of Electrically Large Dielectric Interface
authors: Chong Li, Tieying Feng, Yongjiu Zhao, Lei Xing, Qian Xu, Yi Huang
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: The transmission cross section (TCS) of an electrically large dielectric interface is evaluated. By computing the plane wave reflection coefficients from both sides of the aperture, the corresponding TCSs are obtained using numerical integral. It is interesting to find that the average TCS can be derived analytically, and the ratio of the average TCSs from two sides of the interface equals the ratio of the dielectric permittivities. Both integral representations and analytical expressions of the average TCS are derived, and full-wave simulations are conducted in dielectric-filled reverberation chambers (RCs) for validation.

title: Optical Simulation & Image Quality for Automotive Semantic Segmentation
authors: Daniel Jakab, Joel Herrera Vázquez, Mahendar Kumbham, Brian Deegan, Tim Brophy, Reenu Mohandas, Anthony Scanlan, Enda Ward, Fiachra Collins, Ciarán Eising
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Vehicular Technology
abstract: Cameras are often deployed in automated driving systems for multiple perception tasks, including object recognition. In previous work, a novel optical simulation framework, SOLAS, was proposed to simulate wide-angle and fisheye cameras on automotive data for 2D object detection. The main focus of this work is to assess semantic segmentation networks in relation to camera quality, mainly used for collision avoidance, lane marking detection, and enhanced road navigation. Characterising semantic segmentation performance across the full range of camera production tolerances is essential, given that image quality may vary across units. This study further develops the novel optical simulation framework, SOLAS, where a Pythonic ray-tracing library, KrakenOS, is adapted to simulate wide-angle surround-view optical systems across a range of optical defocus, directly in automotive scenes. Optical defocus is achieved through sensor displacement. In this paper, we propose extending SOLAS with ARDÁN, a pipeline for measuring image quality in automotive scenes. Furthermore, we demonstrate that our framework, utilising a diverse set of cameras and dense grids of Point Spread Functions (PSFs), is scalable in semantic segmentation, achieving consistent results across various datasets supported by statistical metrics. The primary metric used for performance evaluation is mean Intersection over Union (mIoU). Based on our results, the Segformer network with 24.73M parameters was the most robust across wide-angle automotive datasets, namely, nuScenes and Virtual KITTI 2.0. In nuScenes, image sharpness (cycles/pixel) dropped by 20.5% from a nominal focus, with a small Segformer performance drop from 55.21% mIoU by 0.37%. In Virtual KITTI 2.0, image sharpness dropped by 44.66% from a nominal focus, with a Segformer performance drop by 37.42%. However, for fisheye, the PIDNet model with 28.76M parameters was the most robust model across the defocus range. In Woodscape, image sharpness dropped by 53.45% from a nominal focus, with a PIDNet performance drop by 8.55%. In Parallel Domain Woodscape, image sharpness dropped by 34.46% from a nominal focus, with a PIDNet performance drop by 7.28%.

title: DeGMix: Efficient Multi-Task Dense Prediction with Deformable and Gating Mixer
authors: Yangyang Xu, Yibo Yang, Bernard Ghanem, Lefei Zhang, Bo Du, Jun Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Convolution neural networks (CNNs) and Transformers offer complementary strengths for multi-task dense prediction: CNNs are effective at modeling local spatial patterns, while Transformers capture long-range dependencies. However, existing methods often rely primarily on one paradigm or do not fully exploit their complementarity for task-aware representation learning. In this work, we propose DeGMix (Deformable and Gating Mixer), a unified encoder–decoder framework for multi-task dense prediction that combines deformable convolution and attention-based task interaction. DeGMix consists of a deformable mixer encoder for task-aware spatial feature extraction and a task-aware gating Transformer decoder for cross-task interaction and task-specific feature selection. In this way, the proposed framework jointly models local deformable cues, global dependencies, and task-aware feature adaptation within a single architecture. Extensive experiments on three benchmark datasets, including NYUD-v2, PASCAL-Context, and Cityscapes, show that DeGMix consistently outperforms competitive CNN-based and Transformer-based multi-task methods across multiple dense prediction tasks. For example, with a Swin-L backbone, DeGMix achieves 57.55 mIoU on semantic segmentation on NYUD-v2, surpassing the previous best result by +5.2 mIoU. In parameterefficient fine-tuning on PASCAL-Context, DeGMix obtains a +1.96 Δm gain with 2.2M trainable parameters, approaching MTL-full fine-tuning (+2.23 Δm) while updating 13.7× fewer parameters. Our code and models are publicly available at https://github.com/yangyangxu0/DeGMix.

title: A Blind Signal Detection Method Based on Constrained Dual-Cluster K-means for FSO Communications with Atmospheric Turbulence
authors: Zhenning Yi, Hao Zhou, Zhiyong Xu, Jianhua Li, Jingyuan Wang, Jiyong Zhao, Yang Su, Cheng Li, Han Zhou, Ying Du
abstract: Existing blind detection schemes for free-space optical (FSO) communications often suffer from bit error rate (BER) floors. These floors fundamentally arise from the misclassification of extreme signal blocks like all-0 and all-1 blocks. This letter proposes a robust blind signal detection method based on constrained dual-cluster K-means. The proposed method incorporates the physical properties of on-off keying modulation. It fixes the cluster center for the “0” symbol and iteratively optimizes only the “1” symbol center. Furthermore, the method integrates a temporal center retention mechanism to explicitly handle all-0 blocks. This combined strategy effectively prevents the center drift issue inherent in conventional unsupervised learning. Consequently, the proposed scheme completely eliminates the extreme-block-induced BER floor without requiring pilot overhead or prior channel statistics. Simulation results using measured intensity data confirm the superiority of this approach. The method outperforms existing blind detection schemes and achieves robust performance approaching ideal maximum likelihood (ML) detection. It also maintains a linear computational complexity suitable for real-time processing.

title: A Comprehensive Hybrid Deep Learning Approach for Bone Fracture Detection in X-Ray Images Using Optimized CNNs and Transfer Learning
authors: Sumaya Hannan Shova, Rinku Islam, Sarwar Hossain, Md Ishtiaq Ahmed, Md. Abdul Malek, Mst. Mohona Akter, Sadekatul Shihab, Abdullah Al Bassam
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Bone fractures, particularly prevalent among elderly populations, demand rapid and precise diagnostic methods to mitigate complications and enable timely intervention. Tradi- tional fracture detection relying on radiologists' manual interpre- tation of X-ray images remains time-consuming and susceptible to human error, especially for subtle or complex fractures. This paper introduces a hybrid deep learning framework that integrates optimized convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with transfer learning to automate and enhance fracture detection in X-ray images. Utilizing the Human Bone Fracture Multi- modal Image Dataset (HBFMID), comprising diverse X-ray and MRI scans, our approach employs pre-trained CNN architectures (ResNet-101, DenseNet-121) fine-tuned via transfer learning to adapt to fracture-specific features, effectively addressing data scarcity challenges. Architectural optimizations including layer pruning, channelwise attention mechanisms, and adaptive pooling reduce computational complexity while improving sensitivity to critical fracture patterns. Augmentation techniques such as rotation, flipping, and noise injection further bolster model generalizability. Experimental results demonstrate state-of-theart performance, with the proposed model achieving the accuracy (ACC) of 95.66%, 99.41%, 97.70%, 99.67%, 99.41% and the value of AUC is 95.54%, 99.98%, 99.78%, 99.99%, 99.98% for Custom Classifier, SVM, RF, LR, XGBoost, respectively.

title: ROBBO: An Efficient Method for Pareto Front Estimation With Guaranteed Accuracy
authors: Roberto Boffadossi, Marco Leonesio, Lorenzo Fagiano
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: A new method to estimate the Pareto front (PF) in bi-objective optimization problems (BOPs) is presented. Assuming a continuous PF, the approach, named ROBBO (RObust and Balanced BOP), needs to sample at most a finite, precomputed number of PF points. Upon termination, it guarantees that the worst case approximation error lies within a desired tolerance range, predefined by the decision maker (DM), for each of the two objective functions. Theoretical results are derived about the worst case number of PF samples required to guarantee the desired accuracy, both in general and for specific sampling methods from the literature. A comparative analysis, both theoretical and numerical, demonstrates the superiority of the proposed method with respect to popular ones. The approach is finally showcased in a constrained path-following problem for a 2-axis positioning system and in a steady-state optimization problem for a continuous-flow stirred tank reactor (CSTR). An open demo implementation of ROBBO is made available online.

title:
High-Performance Mid-Infrared Photodetector Based on WSe
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    /WTe
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    /WS
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    P-Type-Semimetal-N-Type Heterojunction
authors: Preeti Yadav, Anuj K. Sharma, Vandana Devi Wangkheirakpam
abstract: A high- performance PD comprising p-type WSe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>/T<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">d</sub>-WTe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> semi-metal/n-type WS<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> (PSN) configuration is reported by sandwiching 2D semi-metal between two semiconductor layers. In this heterojunction configuration, the top and bottom semiconductor layers generate an internal built-in electric field oriented vertically within the T<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">d</sub>-WTe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>, which serves as an absorption layer for low energy photons leading to reduced dark current and enhanced collection efficiency. Finite difference time domain (FDTD) is used for simulation of proposed PD design. Notably, the simulated device can respond to the wide spectrum of 8-12 μm with the optimal wavelength around 9.68763 μm. In particular, the device demonstrated remarkable photovoltaic performance, characterized by a maximum responsivity of 4.56 A/W, a detectivity of 2.108 ×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">12</sup> Jones, and a low dark current of∼10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−17</sup> A at 9.687 μm. Further, the transient analysis led to rise and fall times of 5.29 μs and 4.48 μs, respectively, indicating a fast-responding PD design. Furthermore, the 3dB bandwidth of the proposed PSN design is calculated to be 66.16 kHz. This work presents a major step towards Weyl semimetal-based PDs on the silicon platform for MIR applications.

title: Non-Orthogonal Multiple Access Based M -Ary CDMA Systems
authors: Ruofei Ma, Huei-Hong Tsai, Hsiao-Hwa Chen
abstract: Code division multiple access (CDMA) is missing in 4G and 5G systems, albeit it possesses many salient technical advantages over OFDMA, such as its unique multipath diversity gain with RAKE receiver. This work aims to propose a way to vitalize the performance of CDMA systems with M-ary code modulation (i.e., M-ary CDMA). In particular, non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) technique is leveraged to augment spectrum efficiency. Built upon the foundation of a high spectrum efficiency of M-ary CDMA systems, NOMA is integrated with M-ary CDMA for enhancing its user capacity further. Specifically, this work showcases the designs of downlink transmitter and receiver structures for a NOMA based M-ary CDMA system, and validates the effectiveness of the proposed scheme in terms of improved user capacity and bit error rate via simulations. Finally, the challenges and limitations faced by the proposed scheme are identified and the directions for future research are given.

title: Manipulation of Deformable Objects with Soft Grippers Based on Adaptive Fuzzy Control and a Self-Supervised Feature Descriptor
authors: Yuexuan Xu, Ning Han, Jinrui Li, Tong Yang, Yun-Hui Liu, Ning Sun, David Navarro-Alarcon
abstract: Although visual servoing control is widely used in practical applications, its application to deformable object manipulation (DOM) remains underexplored. This is due to the significant diversity of material properties and geometric configurations, the high uncertainties of deformation characteristics, and the high-dimensional configuration spaces. To this end, a new data-driven visual servoing control method is proposed for three-dimensional DOM. Specifically, a self-supervised keypoint detection network is developed without manual annotation while preserving spatial geometric information, addressing limitations of traditional dimensionality reduction methods. The network employs weighted feature aggregation modules to effectively balance the fusion of diverse local information while preserving features essential for keypoint localization. Furthermore, a geodesic distance loss function ensures the semantic consistency of detected keypoints across deformation processes. Based on the detected keypoints, an adaptive fuzzy-based Jacobian estimator is developed to establish the online mapping relationship between keypoint velocities and robot joint velocities. To optimize transient performance, a prescribed performance control method is designed to ensure that keypoint errors converge within predefined spatial funnel functions. Additionally, a pneumatic soft gripper based on a rigid origami-inspired mechanism is designed to prevent damage to deformable objects and provide compliant interaction. Finally, detailed stability analysis and a series of experimental results are provided to verify the feasibility of the proposed method.

title: Vehicle-Side Lightweight Conditional Anonymous Double Authentication Framework with Password Robustness in VANETs
authors: Yangfan Liang, Zhiguo Wan, Yiming Chen, Zhiqiang Zhao, Gao Liu, Zhiquan Liu, Hong Sun, Yining Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: The emergence of Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks (VANETs) has revolutionized modern transportation by enabling real-time and cooperative communication between vehicles and infrastructure. However, vehicular interactions operate across two layers: human–vehicle access and vehicle–network communication. This dual-layered nature introduces complex security and privacy challenges. Existing Conditional Privacy-Preserving Authentication (CPPA) schemes mainly focus on message-level anonymity and integrity. However, they often overlook systemic vulnerabilities across the entire trust lifecycle. These limitations include the inability to verify driver legitimacy during vehicle access, high computational overhead due to heavyweight cryptographic primitives, the lack of password update and recovery mechanisms, and insufficient resistance to physical key extraction attacks. To address these issues, we propose VLCADA, a Vehicle-side Lightweight Conditional Anonymous Double Authentication framework. VLCADA establishes a vertically integrated security architecture for intelligent vehicles. It unifies multi-factor driver authentication with efficient and conditionally anonymous message authentication. At the access layer, VLCADA combines identity–password credentials, biometric verification, and Physical Unclonable Functions (PUF). This ensures that only legitimate users can access and control the vehicle. At the communication layer, we design a Signer-side Lightweight Conditional Anonymous Signature (SLCAS) scheme. This scheme shifts computationally intensive operations to an offline phase, leaving only lightweight hash and XOR operations during real-time execution. In addition, we propose a Dynamic Password Mechanism (DPM) based on threshold secret sharing. This mechanism enables secure password update and recovery without trusted authority intervention. Furthermore, by integrating PUF with fuzzy extractors, VLCADA effectively mitigates noise and provides resistance against physical attacks. Comprehensive security analysis shows that VLCADA satisfies essential security requirements. Experimental results demonstrate that VLCADA reduces total computational overhead by up to 48.55% and communication cost by up to 32.61% compared with state-of-the-art schemes.

title: Administrative Committee Information
authors: N/A

title: A Compact 132.5–142-GHz ×6 Frequency Multiplier With 2.4-dBm Output Power and 2.1% DC-RF Efficiency in 65-nm CMOS
authors: Yi Wu, Zhe Yang, Zongyang Zhang, Dujie He, Guangyin Feng, Fanyi Meng, Xiu Yin Zhang
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter presents a compact D-band <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\times 6$</tex-math> </inline-formula> frequency multiplier, which consists of a self-mixing tripler, a driver amplifier (DA), a push–push doubler, and a D-band power amplifier (PA). A self-mixing technique is introduced in the tripler to generate the third harmonic with a harmonic-selective matching network codesigned at its output. This approach consequently reduces the generation of spurious signals near the sixth harmonic frequency. A prototype was implemented in a 65-nm CMOS process with a compact area of only 0.22 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. Measurement results show that it achieves an output frequency range of 132.5–142 GHz, a saturated output power of 2.4 dBm, a maximum DC-RF efficiency of 2.1%, and a harmonic rejection ratio (HRR) of 36 dBc at 137 GHz.

title: Dispatch Strategy Against Typhoon-Induced Wind Farm Cut-Outs Considering Resilience: An Adaptive Robust Stochastic Optimization Approach
authors: Haitao Xu, Haotian Zhao, Hongbin Sun, Qinglai Guo
abstract: Typhoon disasters pose serious threats to the secure operation of wind-integrated power systems by damaging transmission lines and perturbing wind power output. To address the typhoon-induced large-scale unplanned wind farm cut-outs, it is necessary to develop an orderly wind curtailment plan in day-ahead dispatch. However, balancing robustness and wind power utilization in such plans remains challenging. Furthermore, it is difficult for a single uncertain optimization framework to simultaneously account for the distinct uncertainties in both transmission contingency and wind power output, leading to strategies with limited practicality. To tackle these challenges, this paper proposes a day-ahead dispatch model incorporating an orderly wind curtailment strategy, using adaptive robust stochastic optimization (ARSO) to handle the dual uncertainties of line failure and wind generation. The model consists of pre-dispatch and re-dispatch stages. The curtailment plan is determined in the form of curtailment thresholds in pre-dispatch stage and implemented in re-dispatch stage. In addition, this paper designs a wind power sample generation method and develops a solution algorithm for the ARSO model based on the column-and-constraint generation (C&CG) approach. Case studies on modified IEEE 39-bus and 118-bus systems verify the necessity of orderly wind curtailment and the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Wavelet-Domain Masked Image Modeling for Color-Consistent HDR Video Reconstruction
authors: Yang Zhang, Zhangkai Ni, Wenhan Yang, Hanli Wang
abstract: High Dynamic Range (HDR) video reconstruction aims to recover fine brightness, color, and details from Low Dynamic Range (LDR) videos. However, existing methods often suffer from color inaccuracies and temporal inconsistencies. To address these challenges, we propose WMNet, a novel HDR video reconstruction network that leverages Wavelet domain Masked Image Modeling (W-MIM). WMNet adopts a two-phase training strategy: In Phase I, W-MIM performs self-reconstruction pre-training by selectively masking color and detail information in the wavelet domain, enabling the network to develop robust color restoration capabilities. A curriculum learning scheme further refines the reconstruction process. Phase II fine-tunes the model using the pre-trained weights to improve the final reconstruction quality. To improve temporal consistency, we introduce the Temporal Mixture of Experts (T-MoE) module and the Dynamic Memory Module (DMM). T-MoE adaptively fuses adjacent frames to reduce flickering artifacts, while DMM captures long-range dependencies, ensuring smooth motion and preservation of fine details. Additionally, since existing HDR video datasets lack scene-based segmentation, we reorganize HDRTV4K into HDRTV4K-Scene, establishing a new benchmark for HDR video reconstruction. Extensive experiments demonstrate that WMNet achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple evaluation metrics, significantly improving color fidelity, temporal coherence, and perceptual quality.

title: Advanced Deep Learning Framework for Ultrasound Breast Cancer Classification Using CNNs and Vision Transformers with Explainable AI
authors: Nafisa Tasnim, Shahriar Siddique Arjon, Tamanna Yasmin, Nymur Rahaman Antor, Al Arian Ahmad, Mohammad Ali Sakib
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Breast cancer remains a leading cause of mortality among women worldwide, making early and accurate classification crucial. Despite being an essential diagnostic tool, manual interpretation of ultrasound images is time-consuming and prone to error. Leveraging Explainable AI (XAI) techniques, this study presents an advanced deep learning framework for automated breast cancer classification using convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and Vision Transformers (ViTs). Utilizing a publicly available dataset of 9,016 annotated breast ultrasound images, we evaluate several state-of-the-art architectures, including DenseNet201, ResNeSt50, ConvNeXtV2, ViT-B16, ViTB/32, Swin Transformer V2, and RepVGG. Compared with existing approaches, our proposed ViT-B/32 model surpasses them, achieving 99.67% validation accuracy, 0.9999 AUC, and minimal misclassifications. Transparency for medical practitioners is enhanced through attention mechanisms and LIME-based interpretability, providing clinically meaningful insights into model decision-making. Extensive evaluations, including confusion matrix analysis and t-SNE visualization, validate our framework's robustness. The results demonstrate that transformer-based models integrated with XAI offer superior interpretability and accuracy, making them highly suitable for clinical applications in breast cancer diagnosis.

title: Investigation of SVD-Based Speech Emotion Recognition
authors: Divya Sharma, Kushalatha M R, H. Mallika, Garima Joshi
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Over time, research activity in the domain of Speech Emotion Recognition (SER) is steadily pacing, utilizing Matlab and Python interfaces. It involves analyzing input speech to discern the underlying emotional state being conveyed. This process typically undergoes stages like feature extraction, feature matching, classification, and database integration. Emotions recognized by the system encompasses anger, worry, happiness, neutrality, and gloomy. Various models enable the analysis of specific characteristics within the speech signal, allowing the system identification of emotional state expressed. The main objective of this work is to present the implementation of the Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) algorithm in combination with varied classifiers and speech emotion databases for detecting human's emotions and evaluating the performance of SVD-SER with respect to different classifiers. The results show that SVD with CNN SER gives higher accuracy as compared to other classification methods. LSTM also exhibits good precision but gives more number of false negatives.

title: Power MOSFETs With Super Safe-Operating-Area
authors: Mark A. Gajda, Ali Ghasemi, Hamza Patel
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: In this work we present a method of eliminating the undesirable effect of premature thermal runaway in MOSFETs operating in linear-mode and show a fully fabricated 100V-class split-gate trench power-MOSFET that achieves the theoretical safe-operating-area entitlement with minimal impact on R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf>. The mechanism responsible for thermal runaway is described in terms of competition between charge carrier density, device channel mobility and system geometry. A solution is proposed, which involves integrating two MOSFETs onto the same die to increase the average local thermal mass of active cells, thereby reducing the local thermal impedance. This allows for very fast thermal stabilisation of the device during linear-mode operation by inverting the sign of relevant temperature coefficients and quickly transitioning the device into negative feedback, thus preventing thermal runaway. The operating principles are electrothermal, hence universal, and can be transferred to any base technology, material, voltage rating, and package configuration.

title: Asymmetrically Perturbed Dual-Band Patch Antenna for X-Band Applications
authors: Md Faruk Muntasir Banna, Mehedi Hasan Munna, Md Aminul Islam, Fariha Tasnim
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The rapid growth and development of X-band ap-plications-such as satellite communication links and unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) systems has created a strong demand for low-profile, dual-band antennas that ensure stable impedance performance while maintaining simple fabrication. This work presents the design and simulation of a compact microstrip antenna based on a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 2$</tex> array of inset-fed rectangular patches incorporating an asymmetric slot perturbation. Using Rogers RT/Duroid 5880 substrate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\epsilon_{r}=2.2, \tan \delta=0.0009\right.$</tex>, thickness <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=\mathbf{1. 6 ~ m m}$</tex>), the optimized unit cell resonates at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8.235 \mathbf{~ G H z}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$9.06 \mathbf{~ G H z}$</tex>, achieving return losses of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-44 \mathbf{~ d B}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-28 \mathbf{~ d B}$</tex> with corresponding peak gains of 8 dBi and 7.96 dBi. In <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times$</tex> 2 array configuration, resonances occur at 8.07 GHz and 9.48 GHz due to inter-element coupling, with return losses of -23 dB and -22 dB, respectively. The corresponding -10 dB impedance bandwidths are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7.93-8.24 \text{GHz}(310 \text{MHz}, 3.83 \%)$</tex> and 9.37-9.58 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{GHz}(210 \text{MHz}, 2.22 \%)$</tex>. The array achieves peak gains of 9.5 dBi and 8.1 dBi with radiation efficiencies of approximately 96.2 % and 94.5 % at the respective resonant frequencies. The array demonstrates enhanced directivity and efficient radiation performance across both operating bands. With its single-layer design, compact footprint, and strong dual-band characteristics, the proposed antenna is well suited for integration into modern X-band communication systems.

title: A Data-Driven Algorithm for Indoor/Outdoor Detection Based on 3GPP MDT measures
authors: Giuliano Muratore, Davide Micheli, Roberto Verdone
abstract: Using cellular networks outdoors generally leads to terminals experiencing elevated power levels and increased interference from neighboring cells. These conditions are significantly different from those encountered by the same terminals when they are indoors, where absorption caused by building walls reduces the available serving cell received power and mitigates interference from other cells. Conditions where mobile radio services are predominantly used by terminals located outdoors or, conversely, predominantly used within buildings, typically mix depending on the city area, the specific time, or peculiar circumstances. Therefore, the ability to track the evolution of the dominant indoor or outdoor context within a network over time is crucial for automatically adjusting, optimizing, and enhancing radiomobile service quality. Although numerous methods exist for estimating a device's indoor or outdoor status, based on specific positioning strategies, our approach stands apart due to its real-time applicability to a large-scale set of smartphones across any current 4G/5G 3GPP radiomobile network worldwide—something not achieved by other solutions in the literature. The proposed model is validated using an extensive dataset sourced from a live 5G network access.

title: Enhancing Representation Inversion and Alignment for Zero-Shot Composed Image Retrieval
authors: Feifei Zhang, Jia Chen, Haoliang Zhou, Changsheng Xu
abstract: Composed Image Retrieval (CIR) retrieves a target image by combining a reference image with a textual modification. Existing supervised methods have demonstrated strong performance; however, their dependence on costly, manually annotated triplets limits their scalability and generalizability across diverse applications. Zero-Shot Composed Image Retrieval (ZS-CIR) addresses this by mapping visual inputs to textual representations, enabling annotation-free composed image retrieval. However, accurately capturing both fine-grained details and high-level semantics remains a key challenge. To address this, we introduce an enhanced visual representation inversion and alignment model designed to bridge the gap between visual and textual modalities. Specifically, we propose a Multi-Level Visual Prompting (MLVP) module that integrates local image information into global representations through both instance-level and feature-level prompting, ensuring a more comprehensive capture of fundamental visual content. We further introduce a Dynamic Alignment (DA) module that complements contrastive learning, ensuring semantic flow is effectively transferred and aligned across different modalities without adding extra trainable parameters, thereby narrowing the semantic gap between the visual and textual representations. Extensive experiments across multiple datasets validate the superiority of our method over existing state-of-the-art approaches, demonstrating robust generalization and adaptability for zero-shot composed image retrieval.

title: Compliant Magnetic Sensor Arrays Enable Real-Time Force Myogram Pattern Recognition for Dexterous Hand Control by Amputees
authors: Wen-Yu Cheng, Erik D. Engeberg
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: Control of powered prosthetic hands could benefit from improved techniques to infer the amputees’ desired grasp intentions. Force myography (FMG) has recently emerged as a potential alternative to electromyography, which is traditionally used in clinical practice. Thus, we introduce innovative compliant magnetic FMG sensor arrays for forearm muscle pattern recognition that enables ten subjects, including three upper limb amputees, to have real-time control of a dexterous artificial hand. Sensor arrays with 18 or 24 compliant magnetic FMG sensor modules are fabricated, demonstrating the customizability of our 3D scanning—3D printing process to create individualized wearable sensor arrays for varying limb differences. On average, all 10 subjects control 16.20 ± 3.68 classes with mean accuracy of 93.64% ± 2.90% in real-time control experiments. Additionally, principal component analysis (PCA) is used to both select the most impactful sensors and improve classification accuracy in subsequent offline analyses. Seven out of the ten subjects achieve their highest accuracy with a reduced number of compliant magnetic sensors, highlighting the value of the PCA-informed sensor selection approach. Because the proposed compliant magnetic sensor arrays are highly practical and have a signal to noise ratio (SNR) of 34.51 dB ± 1.49 dB, we are providing our open-source dataset of FMG signals from 3 amputees and 7 non-amputees as a resource for the research community. These novel compliant magnetic FMG sensor arrays have the potential to advance the state of the art for prosthetic hand control and could be used broadly in the fields of tactile sensing, haptics, medical robotics, and rehabilitation.

title: Thermo-Mechanical Frequency Stability and Anisotropic Modeling of Carbon Fiber-Reinforced PETG Accelerometers for Additive Manufacturing
authors: Xuan Luc Le, ThuHang Bui, Tuan Vu Quoc
abstract: This research presents a rigorous investigation into the structural optimisation and material-driven performance enhancement of 3D-printed inertial transducers, specifically focusing on the transition from monolithic thermoplastics to carbon fibre-reinforced composites (PETG-CF). Conventional Polyethylene Terephthalate Glycol (PETG) has been demonstrated functional viability under ambient conditions; however, its susceptibility to temperature-induced mechanical fluctuations and viscoelastic degradation remains a barrier to precision applications. The present study employs integrated modal, harmonic, and thermo-mechanical Finite Element Method (FEM) analysis to delineate the relationship between matrix reinforcement and operational stability across an extended thermal gradient from -40 °C to 100 °C. The analytical model yielded resonant frequencies at ambient temperature of 62.38 Hz for PETG and 102.62 Hz for PETG-CF, demonstrating a high correlation with FEM simulation results of 62.94 Hz and 103.71 Hz, respectively. The simulation results indicate that standard PETG-based transducers exhibit high thermal sensitivity, with the fundamental natural frequency decreasing from 67.1 Hz at −40 °C to 47.0 Hz at 75 °C, representing a linear frequency drift of approximately 0.32 Hz/°C. Conversely, the incorporation of carbon reinforcement (PETG-CF) significantly enhances mechanical resonance performance, achieving a fundamental natural frequency of 103.71 Hz at 25 °C, corresponding to a 65% increase over the baseline (62.945 Hz). The PETG-CF variant demonstrates superior thermal robustness, evidenced by a reduced average frequency shift of approximately 0.26 Hz/°C. The experimental validation of these findings was conducted through the utilisation of free-decay vibration measurements, which substantiated the observations. The resonant frequencies observed were found to be approximately 71 Hz for PETG and 106 Hz for PETG-CF. Static calibration yielded a sensitivity of approximately 108 mV/g. The results obtained establish a practical operational temperature limit between -40 °C and 75 °C, thus providing a fundamental framework for developing high-performance, low-cost micro-sensors through advanced Fused Deposition Modeling (FDM) techniques.

title: Message Passing Based Bayesian Detection for Distributed Dual-Channel Passive MIMO Radar
authors: Bin Li, Jun Li, Qinghua Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: This paper investigates Bayesian target detection for distributed dual-channel passive multi-input multi-output (MIMO) radar to systematically exploit prior statistical knowledge of unknown parameters. First, a closed-form Bayesian generalized likelihood ratio test (B-GLRT) detector is derived by marginalizing random channel coefficients while estimating the unknown transmitted signals via maximum likelihood estimation, yielding a computationally efficient detector. Second, to further incorporate the discrete constellation structure of the transmitted symbols, a message passing based maximum-a-posteriori (MP-MAP) ratio test detector is proposed. It is efficiently realized via an iterative MP algorithm to jointly infer the channel coefficients, transformed signals and symbols, without exhaustive searching over all possible symbol sequences. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed detectors consistently outperform existing adaptive detectors across a wide range of signal-to-noise ratios.

title: Unique Ferroxplana Ferrites Impedance Matched to Free Space at HF to VHF Frequencies and Beyond
authors: Van Dao Nguyen, Coenradt Taylor, Katherine A. Kelly, Thi Thuy Pham, Ying Chen, Ogheneyunume Fitchorova, Vincent G. Harris
abstract: new class of RF magnetoceramic materials has been developed for operation across the high-frequency (HF), very-high-frequency (VHF) and lower ultra-high-frequency UHF regime, spanning approximately 1 MHz ≤ f ≤ 1000 MHz. These materials are realized as Ztype ferroxplana ferrites with nominal compositions Ba₁.₅Sr₁.₅Co₂−xMₓFe₂₄O₄₁, where M = Mn or Zn and x = 0, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, and 1.25. Through compositional substitution and microstructural engineering, the electromagnetic constitutive parameters of these materials can be tailored such that the effective magnetic permeability (μᵣ) and dielectric permittivity (εᵣ) are approximately equal over broad frequency ranges. Under these conditions the normalized impedance approaches that of free space (Z₀ ≈ 377 Ω), enabling efficient electromagnetic coupling with minimal reflection at the material interface. Magnetodielectrics satisfying this impedance-matched condition represent an important materials platform for advanced RF technologies, including miniaturized antennas, radomes, electromagnetic absorbers, and broadband RF components. Because electromagnetic waves can enter such media without significant impedance discontinuities, device architectures can be designed with reduced reflection losses while simultaneously exploiting the high refractive index associated with large μᵣεᵣ products. This combination enables substantial reductions in electrical size, device thickness, and system complexity, while also enhancing bandwidth and radiation efficiency. Experimental characterization of these Z-type ferrites demonstrates operational frequency ranges extending from approximately 1 MHz to beyond 800 MHz, with usable bandwidths exceeding 500 MHz. The materials exhibit size-reduction factors (SRF) greater than 20, while maintaining relatively low loss parameters, with magnetic loss tangents on the order of tan δμ ≈ 0.01 and dielectric loss tangents typically below tan δε < 10⁻³. The simultaneous realization of broadband impedance matching, high magnetodielectric loading, and low loss is uncommon among presently available materials. Consequently, these systems represent a promising foundation for a new generation of impedance-engineered magnetodielectrics (ZEMs) capable of enabling compact, broadband RF devices for communications, sensing, and electromagnetic energy management.

title: Dynamic Reinforcement Learning-Driven Event-Aware Adaptive for Education Management System
authors: Rui Guo, Meng Li, Ao Cui, Shulian Zhang, Guangsheng Cao, Qi Zhang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: In recent years, the rapid growth of online and blended learning environment has increased the need for intelligent Education Management Systems (EMS) capable of adapting to continuously evolving engagement patterns, learner behavior and academic progress. The existing Group-Theoretic Particle Swarm Optimization (GT-PSO) approach uses historical data to evaluate learner behavior but is rely on uses static decision rules and less capable of responding to dynamic events. To address these limitations, this research proposes a Dynamic Reinforcement Learning-driven Event-Aware Adaptive Management System (DREAM-EMS) for managing personalized and continuous educational delivery. Initially, learner interactions from the Open University Learning Analytics Dataset (OULAD) are modeled as an event-driven sequential decision-making process, while an online RL framework is employed to learn adaptive curriculum adjustment, intervention strategies and pacing modification in real-time. An Event-Triggered Adaptive Reward Reweighting (ET-ARR) mechanism dynamically adjusts optimization priorities based on detected learner events that enables timely engagement recovery, workload balancing and performance improvement. Finally, a closed-loop feedback mechanism continuously updates learner states and policies, which ensures stable and personalized adaptation over academic timelines to provide event-aware education management. The experimental results demonstrates that the proposed DREAM-EMS model achieved accuracy (97.8%), precision (95%) and recall (98%) compared to existing GT-PSO approach.

title: A High-temperature Three-phase Silicon Carbide MOSFET Power Module with Integrated Low Propagation and High Noise Immunity Gate Driver
authors: Qianheng Dong, Jing Zhu, Yifei Zheng, Bowen Xue, Xiang Fan, Siyang Liu, Long Yuan, Weifeng Sun
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: This paper presents a three-phase silicon carbide (SiC) power module for high temperature that reduces power electronic system volume and cost while enhancing reliability. The proposed module integrates silicon-on-insulator (SOI) gate drivers, featuring low propagation delay, robust transient dV/dt noise immunity and high drive strength, enabling operation at up to 225 °C. The propagation delay, sourcing and sinking current capability and transient dV/dt noise immunity of the gate driver are tested under various ambient temperatures. Furthermore, a three-phase 600V/50A SiC MOSFET power module has been developed using high-temperature packaging technology. The switching performance and protection function behavior of the fabricated power module are fully evaluated at different temperatures. The proposed module shows superior switching speed, transient dV/dt noise immunity and lower switching energy losses compared to the commercial INFINEON IM12S60EA2 SiC power module.

title:
Breaking the E
                    <sub>on</sub>
                    and Radiated Noise Trade-off via Deep Split-Gate CSTBT™ with dI/dt Shaping
authors: Kosuke Sakaguchi, Kazuya Konishi, Ryosuke Kobayashi, Gai Fujiki, Kazuki Nishida, Naoki Ikeda, Fumihito Masuoka
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: To effectively reduce both radiated noise and turn-on loss (E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</inf>) of CSTBT™, precise optimization of the current waveform during the turn-on period is crucial. In this study, we propose a novel deep split gate (DSG) CSTBT™, which achieved a significant 53% reduction in E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</inf> while simultaneously mitigating radiated noise. This substantial improvement is attributed to the DSG structure’s precise control over negative gate capacitance. This control enables the realization of a high dI/dt during the late stage of the current rise while maintaining a low dI/dt during the initial stage of the turn-on period. Consequently, the DSG structure also demonstrates a total SW loss reduction of 19% at the equivalent DC loss.

title:
Structural and Dielectric Features of Na, W dualdoped Bi
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Sr
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Co
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>x</sub>
                    Ceramics for Energy Storage Applications
authors: Uzma Hira, Tajammul Hussain Tahir, Muhammad Zeeshan Amjad, Zaki Uddin, Nabeela Noor
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robotics and Automation in Industry (ICRAI)
abstract: In this paper, a new series of cobaltite materials is designed for energy storage and supercapacitor applications. $\mathrm{Bi}_{2-2 y} \mathrm{Na}_{2 y} \mathrm{Sr}_{2} \mathrm{Co}_{2-y} \mathrm{~W}_{y} \mathrm{O}_{x}$ polycrystalline cobaltite materials have been fabricated using solid-state reaction. All of the prepared samples are phase-pure, according to X-ray powder diffraction (XRD) data, and have monoclinic misfit layered structures in which hexagonal CoO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> and rock-salt BiSrO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> layers are alternatively stacked along the unit cell’s c-axis. According to Koop’s theory, the loss tangent and dielectric loss both drop with increasing frequency, indicating Maxwell-Wagner type polarization in the material. Additionally, conductivity relaxation is shown by the hump in the electric modulus plot, and a non-Debye type conduction mechanism is revealed by the Nyquist and ColeCole studies.

title: EUNet: An Efficient Attention-Enhanced U-Net for Automatic Chronic Wound Segmentation
authors: Esrat Jahan, Md. Darun Nayeem, Rehanuma Tabassum, Md. Masudul Islam, Nishat Nayala Priyanka
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of chronic wounds from clinical photographs is essential for objective assessment and treatment planning, yet remains challenging due to irregular wound boundaries, heterogeneous backgrounds, and varying imaging conditions. This article presents EUNet, an attentionenhanced encoder-decoder framework for automatic chronic wound segmentation. EUNet builds on a ResNet50 backbone and integrates squeeze-excitation attention, residual depth-wise separable convolutions, a dilated bottleneck, and a shallow fullresolution skip connection to jointly capture global context and fine boundary details. The model is trained end-to-end using a combined binary cross-entropy and Dice loss, mixed-precision optimization, and a warmup cosine learning-rate schedule. Experiments conducted on a composite dataset of 2,760 wound images demonstrate that EUNet achieves a Dice score of 89.23% and an IoU of 8 0. 7 9%, outperforming multiple backbone-based U-Net variants. Ablation and multi-seed evaluations confirm the robustness and effectiveness of the proposed architectural components.

title: Research on the Restructuring Path of the Power Material Supply Chain Under the Background of Digital Transformation
authors: Chaoxia Gao, Shucheng Qi
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The emerging complexity of the power material supply chain, with the wave of digital transformation, requires data-driven solutions to reorganise and optimise the performance of operations. This paper focused on the analysis of the role of digital transformation on the efficiency, resilience, and decision-making in the power material supply network in terms of a quantitative framework that is implemented by a simulation. An artificial dataset was created that was two years of weekly operations and was analysed in Python (Google Colab) with high-level machine learning models such as Ridge, XGBoost, and LightGBM. Results of the regression showed that cost and lead-time prediction were highly accurate, with the Ridge model having MSE = 3.45, RMSE = 1.86 for cost and MSE = 0.00029, RMSE = 0.017 for lead time. Moreover, the classification models were 99% accurate and F1 = 0.99. The paper concludes that the digital transformation allows for quantifying the improvement of supply-chain efficiency as well as risk mitigation. Managers have been advised to use integrated digital systems and not to use isolated technologies. The results suggest that a form of strategic decision, such as the restructuring efforts, can be implemented using simulationbased analysis. The use of artificial data also restricts the applicability of findings to practice, though.

title: Lightweight and Interpretable Deep Learning for Automated Waste Classification
authors: Saad Ahmed, Razia Sultana, Qazi Masrafi Rahman, Aroshi Ali, Khalid Syfullah
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The increasing volume of solid waste poses serious environmental and public health challenges, especially in rapidly urbanizing countries like Bangladesh. Effective waste classification plays a key role in improving recycling rates and minimizing pollution. In this study, we present a deep learningbased framework for automatic waste classification using an extended version of the BDWaste dataset. We evaluated the performance of four pretrained convolutional neural networks which are MobileNetV2, VGG16, DenseNet121 and Xception alongside a custom lightweight CNN designed for deployment in resource-constrained settings achieving very high accuracay of 98.76%. MobileNetV2 achieved the highest classification accuracy, while the custom CNN showed competitive performance with significantly lower computational requirements. To enhance interpretability, we applied the Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations (LIME) technique, which offered visual insights into the model's decision-making process. This work supports the development of intelligent waste classification systems that contribute to urban sustainability and environmental protection efforts.

title: Edge Analytics for Real-Time Process Optimization in Smart Manufacturing: A Hybrid Machine Learning Approach
authors: Rahat Hasan, Md Shohan Bhuiyan, Ayan Barua, Md Nayem Rahman, Rakibul Islam, Khandakar Rabbi Ahmed
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The rapid pace of Industry 4.0 has heightened the need for real-time process optimization in smart factories. The proposed framework includes Random Forests for efficient feature selection and interpretation, as well as LSTM for handling complex temporal relationships in multivariable sensor data. The proposed accurate RF+LSTM framework is designed within an edge computing architecture to enable low-latency, realtime analytics and thereby optimize real-time decision-making. To validate the experimental results, the AI4I 2020 Predictive Maintenance challenge dataset, containing 10,000 instances and 14 features across industries, was used. Efficiency is validated to demonstrate that the proposed edge analytics-based framework is effective and accurate within smart factories and has higher values of accuracy at 98.90 % as compared to the existing RF, LSTM, XGBoost, and KNN models on the basis of different evaluation parameters like precision, recall, F1-score, MCC, and AUC values.

title: DiffusionDxNet: Improving Monkeypox Classification Using Diffusion-Based Data Augmentation and DenseNet121
authors: Md Mohaiminul Islam Sajib, Protik Chakroborty, Md. Adnan Sami, Md. Abdus Sami Shezan, Bishal Prosad
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Monkeypox is an emerging infectious disease whose skin manifestations closely resemble other dermatological conditions such as chickenpox and measles, making early visual diagnosis challenging. This diagnostic similarity may delay early detection, highlighting the need for automated and reliable computer-aided diagnostic systems. In our research, we develop a deep learning model using diffusion-based data augmentation and the DenseNet121 model to classify monkeypox into various categories. Because monkeypox image datasets are most often unbalanced, we create synthetic images of minor categories to develop a balanced dataset. This is followed by fine-tuned four-class classification tasks using a pretrained DenseNet121 model. To make our model more robust for better predictive performance, we investigate the combination of multiple learned models through both the uniform and greedy model soup methods. The experiments were conducted using the Monkeypox Skin Images Dataset (MSID), a publicly available four-class dermatological dataset containing 770 images collected from internet-based sources and curated by the Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Islamic University, Kushtia, Bangladesh. The proposed approach was evaluated on an independent test set using standard metrics including accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, Matthews correlation coefficient, and ROC-AUC. The model achieved an accuracy of 97.06 %, macro precision of 96.05 %, macro recall of 95.35 %, macro F1-score of 95.65 %, and ROC-AUC of 0.9969. These results indicate consistent performance across all classes and demonstrate the effectiveness of diffusion-based augmentation combined with robust feature learning. In conclusion, the proposed framework demonstrates strong potential as an efficient and scalable solution for automated monkeypox diagnosis and highlights the usefulness of diffusionbased augmentation in medical image analysis.

title:
A New 1200V SiC Trench MOSFET with Shallow Trench and High-Energy Deep P
                    <sup>+</sup>
                    Implantation for Enhanced Gate Oxide Electric Field Shielding
authors: Moufu Kong, Zhaoyu Ai, Hongfei Deng, Qizhi Feng, Mingliang Yang, Xuebing Yin, Bingke Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: This paper proposes a new 1200V silicon carbide (SiC) trench-gate MOSFET. The key structural feature is a 0.4-μm shallow trench formed before the high-energy (1.8 MeV) P<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">+</sup> implantation. Compared to the conventional device, this design enables a 0.4 μm deeper P<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">+</sup> doping profile under the same implant conditions without requiring an additional photomask. Benefiting from the shallow trench, a 43% reduction in the maximum gate oxide electric field (E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ox,max</inf>) is achieved compared to the conventional device, resulting from the decreased electric field at the gate trench bottom. This substantially improves the gate oxide reliability. Moreover, the shallow trench increases the ohmic contact area for the N+ source. Experimental results show that the proposed device achieves a specific on-resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on,sp</inf>) of 2.53 mΩ·cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, comparable to the conventional value of 2.58 mΩ·cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, while both maintain a breakdown voltage above 1500 V. This demonstrates a promising technical approach for high-voltage SiC trench MOSFETs.

title: Escaping Stability-Plasticity Dilemma in Online Continual Learning for Motion Forecasting via Synergetic Memory Rehearsal
authors: Yunlong Lin, Chao Lu, Tongshuai Wu, Xiaocong Zhao, Guodong Du, Yanwei Sun, Zirui Li, Jianwei Gong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
