abstract: Deep neural networks (DNNs) have achieved remarkable success in intelligent systems such as autonomous vehicles and robots. However, most DNN-based methods suffer from catastrophic forgetting, where the DNN may fail to maintain its performance in previously learned tasks after adapting to new data. This phenomenon gives rise to a critical tradeoff between memory stability of the DNN, that is, the ability to retain learned knowledge, and learning plasticity, that is, the capacity to acquire new information effectively. To address such stability–plasticity dilemma, this study proposes a novel CL method named synergetic memory rehearsal (SyReM). SyReM maintains a compact memory buffer to represent learned knowledge. To ensure memory stability, it employs an inequality constraint that limits increments in the average loss over the memory buffer. Synergistically, a selective memory rehearsal mechanism is designed to enhance learning plasticity by selecting samples from the memory buffer that are similar to recently observed data. This selection is based on an online-measured cosine similarity of loss gradients, ensuring targeted memory rehearsal. We validate SyReM under online CL tasks for motion forecasting. Comprehensive experiments on naturalistic driving datasets demonstrate that, compared to non-CL and CL baselines, SyReM significantly mitigates catastrophic forgetting in past scenarios while improving forecasting accuracy in new ones. The code is available at https://github.com/BIT-Jack/SyReM

title: Field Measurement-Based Modeling and Validation of Electric Arc Furnace Loads
authors: Firoz Ahmad, Mohammad Tayyab, S. M. Suhail Hussain, T. K. Abdel-Galil, Ibrahim El-Amin
abstract: Electric Arc Furnace (EAF) is an industrial load that generates large active and reactive power fluctuations, voltage flicker, and harmonic distortion at the point of common coupling. These characteristics present significant challenges to Power Quality (PQ) management and motivate the need for accurate, measurement-based EAF modeling. This paper presents a field measurement-based modeling and validation study of a large EAF installation connected to the Saudi Arabian power grid. This work uses field measurements collected at an operating industrial site, enabling comparison with existing studies. The measurements were conducted at multiple voltage levels in a large steel plant supplied at 230 kV in Saudi Arabia. One-week measurements at the 230 kV interface were used to evaluate voltage variation, flicker severity, and Voltage Total Harmonic Distortion (V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">THD</sub>), confirming compliance with IEEE Std. 519-2022. Additional synchronized recordings were collected at internal plant locations, including 34.5 kV buses before and after static volt-ampere reactive compensators and series reactors, and at the EAF transformer low-voltage terminals. The measured data were used to select, tune, and validate two EAF models. For the melting stage, the differential-equation model shows close agreement with measurements, with per phase voltage and current errors of 2.7% and 1.6%, respectively, and measured V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">THD</sub> of 5.5%, while the stochastic arc-length model gives per phase voltage and current errors of about 6.3%. These results support the use of the validated model for PQ and mitigation studies under the considered operating conditions.

title: A DC-DC Converter for Electric Vehicle Charging Station
authors: Fenglian Wang, Guibin Zou, Xiuyan Wei, Shengkun Pang
abstract: High voltage gain non-isolated dc-dc converters are finding an increasingly wide range of applications in clean energy generation systems. In applications, such as electric vehicle charging station, the low ripple input current of dc-dc converters can prolong its service life. Accordingly, this paper Improved and derived a dc-dc converters with low ripple input current and low voltage stress of switch. The converters are characterized by high efficiency, extra-high voltage gain, continuous input current, passive clamping, and common ground. It adopts a unique three-winding coupled-inductor and a switched capacitor to enhance its boost capability, and has three modulation indexes, i.e., the boost turn ratio (n1>1), the buck turn ratio (n2<1), and the duty cycle D. The introduction of buck turn ratio n2 not only avoids an increase in the capacity and size of the coupled-inductor, but also reduces the leakage inductance, total inductance, and core size of the coupled inductor, which in turn reduces the loss of the coupled inductor, and increases the efficiency of converter. This paper studies steady-state characteristics of the converter, deduces the current/voltage relationship for each component, analyses the efficiency of converter. Finally, the feasibility of the proposed converter is verified via experiments.

title:
Adaptive Cross Section Optimization Method for Oversized Circular TE
                    <sub>01</sub>
                    -Mode Waveguide Bends
authors: Zhipeng Tu, Xiaoyi Liao, Ran Zhang, Guo Liu, Wei Jiang, Jianxun Wang, Yong Luo, Zewei Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article proposes a synthesis method for highly efficient, broadband, and compact circular TE<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">01</sub>-mode waveguide bends based on adaptive optimization of the waveguide cross section. To enable convenient parametric adjustment of the waveguide cross section while meeting the structural continuity and smoothness requirements for practical high-power applications, a construction method for waveguide cross sections based on Gaussian radial basis function (GRBF) superposition is introduced. For the sake of achieving an optimized cross section for efficient TE<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">01</sub> mode transmission, an improved particle swarm optimization (PSO) algorithm incorporating shape-family clustering, cross-family weak competition, and geometry-weighted parameter scaling is proposed. Based on the proposed method, a Ka-band 90° high-power TE<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">01</sub> mode waveguide bend was designed, fabricated, and tested. Simulation results show that the bend achieves a transmission efficiency above 95% and a reflection coefficient below −35dB across the 18.5–40.3 GHz frequency range. Near-field measurements further confirm an output mode purity exceeding 98%. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach in realizing high-efficiency, high power-capacity, broadband, and compact circular TE<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">01</sub> mode bending, offering a new technical reference for the development of high-performance high-power microwave devices.

title: Smart 1.7 kV SiC Power ICs: Monolithic Integration of TCOX UMOSFETs and Tri-Gate CMOS
authors: Chia-Lung Hung, Jing-Neng Yao, Yun-Tin Huang, Ho-Lin Kao, Bing-Yue Tsui, Tian-Li Wu, Yi-Kai Hsiao, Hao-Chung Kuo
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: This paper presents a thick corner oxide (TCOX) located at the trench bottom of a 1.7 kV SiC trench UMOSFET and a monolithically integrated 20 V Tri-Gate SiC CMOS circuit to realize smart control and high-power IC applications for the first time. This integration provides a solution for simultaneously embedding Tri-Gate FET based complementary metal-oxide-semiconductor (CMOS) circuits into the UMOSFET power device, enabling the realization of smart power management integrated circuit (IC) functions in the future. Trench MOSFETs are key to achieving lower specific on-state resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on,sp</inf>) by enabling further scaling of power devices. Among CMOS technologies, FinFET CMOS has been used to further enhance performance due to its well-behaved short-channel effects (SCE) and its ability to overcome device scaling issues. In this work, we successfully demonstrated a process for integrating a 20 V SiC Tri-Gate FET CMOS into a 1.7 kV trench UMOSFET with a TCOX structure. The threshold voltage (Vth) of the Tri-Gate FETs is 1~2 V lower than that of the planar MOSFETs, which enhances circuit speed and reduces power consumption. The Ron,sp of the fabricated UMOSFET can be reduced to 3.5 mΩ-cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, accompanied by a similar density of interface states (Dit) at both the mesa and trench side wall regions. A variety of Tri-Gate FETs based CMOS circuits were designed and their functions verified on this platform, enabling the realization of smart power IC management.

title: Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning for Joint Scheduling of EV Charging, Solar PV, and BESS under Real-Time Tariff Feedback
authors: P. Muthukumar
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The rapid growth of behind-the-meter renewable energy resources and electric vehicle (EV) adoption has increased uncertainty in scheduling and operational planning in modern smart grids. Under conditions of stochastic user behavior, time-varying tariffs, and operational constraints on distributed energy resources (DERs), conventional centralized optimization techniques become difficult to apply. This study presents a Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning (MADRL) framework for coordinating electric vehicle (EV) charging, photovoltaic (PV) generation, and battery energy storage systems (BESS) under time-varying tariff signals. A federated actor-critic approach with shared global incentive feedback is employed, through which each agent independently learns control actions, including charge/discharge levels, flexible load shifting, and allocation of surplus renewable energy. The learning process accounts for real-time price signals, transformer loading limits, and end-user comfort constraints. The methodology is implemented in the MATLAB Reinforcement Learning Toolbox using the IEEE-33 bus distribution test system and stochastic demand and solar generation datasets. The results show a reduction in peak-hour demand, increased utilization of renewable energy, and lower overall energy costs when compared with rule-based and singleagent control strategies. The proposed architecture demonstrates scalability and coordinated tariff-responsive operation, which are required for practical transactive energy systems.

title: Efficient UAV Selection in K-Means Online Routing Protocol (K-Morp) using Heuristics and Weighted Values
authors: Rubiat Hasan Khan, Monir Hossen
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Within the dynamic and fast-changing architecture of aerial networks, the orchestration of several unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) is a multifarious challenge, especially when variables such as mobility, energy and signal reliability fluctuate at the same time. This paper focuses on this challenge and presents an advanced clustering algorithm that is designed to meet realistic operational conditions. The Enhanced K-MORP algorithm is based on the conventional K-means++ algorithm, but it includes telemetry information (energy reserves and signal strength) in the optimization routine. Performance of the proposed scheme was analyzed on the basis of synthetic UAV datasets under Gaussian Markov mobility assumptions under high-level simulation with increasing time. Comparative analyses were carried between Enhanced K-MORP and its baseline counterpart in terms of critical parameters like mean squared error, packet delivery ratio, end-to-end delay, throughput and normalized routing load.

title: Comparison between the Secrecy Capacity of Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface Aided and Relay-Assisted Wireless Communication Systems
authors: K. M. Nafis Tajwar, Md. Zahurul Islam Sarkar, Ankon Datta, Ahshan Habib, Asif Ur Rahman Akhand
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This paper studies the ergodic secrecy capacity (ESC) of two emerging wireless architectures: one assisted by a reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) and the other using a conventional relay, both in the presence of a passive eavesdropper. For instance, a legitimate source communicates private information to its intended legitimate destination, with a passive eavesdropper co-located in the background, attempting to overhear the signal. Under the considered system setup, the instantaneous signal-to-noise ratio distributions at the intended receiver and the eavesdropper are analyzed to evaluate the ergodic secrecy capacity. Considering independent and identically distributed Rayleigh fading, we derived the closed-form expression of the ergodic secrecy capacity and further supported the analytical results with numerical evaluations. The impact of key system parameters such as signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), number of RIS reflecting elements, and relay gain are examined to understand their influence on secrecy performance. From the simulation outcomes, it is shown that the RIS-assisted transmission achieves higher ergodic secrecy capacity compared to the relay-assisted scheme, particularly in high SNR regions and when a larger number of reflecting element is used.

title: Deep Learning for High-Fidelity Mpox Classification: From Data to Diagnosis
authors: Tonmoy Debnath, Apurba Adhikary, Sultana Jahan Soheli, Md. Shamim Ahsan, Avi Deb Raha, Yu Qiao, Mohammad Amzad Hossain, Abidur Rahaman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The recent surge in monkeypox (Mpox) outbreaks underscores the urgent need for rapid and accurate diagnostic tools. While deep learning (DL) has shown significant promise for analyzing skin lesions, prior research has been constrained by limited datasets and prominent accuracy. Addressing these critical gaps, this paper introduces an enhanced Mpox detection framework. Our approach leverages a novel dataset meticulously compiled from three public Kaggle sources (MSID, MSLD, MSLD v2.0). Through rigorous preprocessing, including a 14 fold data augmentation and a dHash deduplication process, we curated a unique dataset of 16,918 images, representing an approximate 20 times expansion over typical prior datasets. This significantly enhanced data foundation mitigates domain shift challenges and improves model generalization. We systematically evaluated two state-of-the-art Convolutional Neural Networks: ResNet50 and EfficientNetB2. ResNet50 exhibits outstanding classification accuracy, achieving a Receiver Operating Characteristic Area Under the Curve (ROC AUC) of 99.98 % and specificity of 99.70 %, alongside a test accuracy of 99.61 %. In contrast, EfficientNetB2 achieves a compelling balance of competitive 99.48 % balanced accuracy with significant computational advantages, including 69.8 % fewer parameters and a 71.59 MB smaller model size, making it highly suitable for resource-constrained environments. The high accuracy on an independent test set for both models further validates their robust generalization capabilities.

title: FedThreat-X: A Privacy-Preserving Federated Threat Intelligence Framework for Multi-Cloud Cybersecurity
authors: Md Anisur Rahman Chowdhury, Khandakar Rabbi Ahmed, Aizaada Akylbekova, Sabrina Mohona, Meena Momand, Gohar Masood Khan, Kefei Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: As more organizations distribute their workloads across multiple cloud platforms, detecting coordinated, privacy-sensitive cyber threats has emerged as an urgent challenge. Despite the increasing use of multi-cloud ecosystems, sensitive log data, inconsistent security policies, and heterogeneous cloud architectures make it unattainable to securely and cooperatively share actionable threat intelligence. We suggest FedThreat-X, a federated threat intelligence framework that protects privacy while fostering cooperative learning of attack behaviors without revealing raw surveillance data. Threat-aware adaptive aggregation, proximal stability management, and lightweight local MLP classification are the key components in our model, which is able to train in heterogeneous and non-IID multi-cloud environments. FedThreat-X (using CNN, LSTM, BiLSTM, and GNN-TI as a baseline) is able to achieve the state-of-the-art performance of 98.7 percent in the Microsoft GUIDE dataset containing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 3 M}$</tex> evidence records, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 M}$</tex> triaged events, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 4 1}$</tex> MITRE ATT&CK approaches. These strong results have a low false-positive rate and high generalization to demonstrate how resilient it is to distribution changes. This work hereby advances the state of the art in multi-cloud cybersecurity by enabling secure, scalable, high-fidelity, federated threat intelligence suitable for real-world SOC operations.

title: The BOOL.I Corpus: The Impact of Context on Child and Adolescent Bengali ASR
authors: Kawchar Husain, Samia Preity, Moksedur Rahman Sohan, Anowar Hossain, M. Shahidur Rahman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This paper presents a Bangladeshi Bangla speech corpus for children and adolescents and evaluates state-of-theart Bengali Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) models on this data. Although significant progress has been made in the construction of Bangladeshi Bangla corpora, dedicated resources for young speakers remain relatively underexplored. Our corpus contains 16,069 segmented utterances from speakers aged 5 to 18 years and is designed to support research on child and adolescent speech in Bangladeshi Bangla. Using a four-stage evaluation framework consisting of preprocessing, unified inference, error computation, and WER distribution analysis, we benchmark four leading pretrained models: bengali-whisper-medium and three wav2vec2-xls-r-300m variants. bengali-whisper-medium achieves the strongest performance with a Micro WER of 0.4092 and a Micro CER of 0.2131, outperforming all wav2vec2 based systems. A fine-grained analysis shows that 16.47% of segments achieve perfect recognition and 22.71% fall within WER < 0.3. Interestingly, performance varies by utterance length: short 1-5 word utterances yield the highest mean WER (0.4981), while longer utterances (16+ words) show substantially better accuracy (mean WER <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 0.37$</tex>). This finding reveals that contextual information helps ASR systems overcome acoustic variability in children's speech, demonstrating the critical role of context-aware modeling for young speakers.

title: AI-Powered Hybrid Management Framework for Autonomous Vendor Evaluation and Procurement Optimization
authors: Mohyedin Hamza, M Swapna, Navdeep Singh, K Bhasakara Rao, Krishna Bhargavi Y, Kala Priydarshini G
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The optimization of procurement is an acute issue that enterprise organizations face working in unstable supply chains and dynamic regulatory environments. The old vendor management systems tend to be based on restrictive and rule-driven appraisals that just cannot evolve to non-linear and complex procurement world. This paper suggests employing AI-based hybrid management and uses rare and advanced algorithms such as a Quantum-Inspired Decision Matrix (QIDM), Swarm-Driven Contractual Risk Assessment (SDCRA), Graph-Based Trust Propagation Network (GTPN) and Evolutionary Procurement Policy Optimizer (EPPO). The model itself analyses vendors, identifies risks, advances procurement policies, and makes transparency available with blockchain-backed audit trails. The comparison of 50 procurement scenarios conducted through experimental analysis demonstrated that QIDM obtained a vendor ranking accuracy of 96, SDCRA had a risk detection precision of 93.3, and EPPO could allow saving up to 13.4 in procurement costs. Moreover, GTPN achieved a mean error of prediction of trust of only 2.65 which is better than conventional evaluation models. Additional support of multimodal sentiment analysis and neuro-fuzzy reinforcement decision layers further enhanced the profile of vendors and minimized conflict in decision by 47%. This model shows a sound, decipherable, and changeable approach to contemporary procurement issues. The next phase of work will involve the adoption of real-time IoT-built supply chains, including moral AI limits, and joint procurement through federated learning architecture.

title: Predicting Genre Crossovers in Book Recommendations Using Hybrid Machine Learning Models
authors: Estiyak Rubaiat, Jahir Uddin Mohammad Babar, Tojammel Jakaria Jenin, Mirza Fahim Abdullah, MD. Saif Al Shahriar, Sharfuddin Mahmood
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Traditional book recommendation systems struggle to predict genre crossover tendencies in modern books that frequently blend multiple genres, as they rely heavily on static metadata like genre tags and titles. This paper proposes a hybrid model that integrates sentiment analysis of user reviews, sequential pattern mining (via Apriori), and machine learning techniques-including XGBoost for feature-based prediction, Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN), and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) for modeling reading sequences-connected through a novel sentiment-guided structured hybrid integration layer. The approach captures emotional engagement and longterm user behavior to predict dynamic genre transitions. Experiments on a dataset of 10,872 books and over 52,000 ratings/reviews from Goodreads and Amazon show the proposed model achieves a top- 3 accuracy of 98.3% and an F1score of 78.6% (micro-averaged), significantly outperforming prior single-genre or non-hybrid methods. Ablation studies confirm the contribution of sentiment analysis and pattern mining. This work advances personalized, adaptive book recommendations by better handling genre fluidity in contemporary literature.

title: Explainable Comprehensive Analysis of Cross-Dataset Phishing Detection Models
authors: Firoze Maliha, Gazi Tahsina Sharmin Jahin, Umme Habiba, Abu Nowshed Chy, M. Jamshed Alam Patwary
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Phishing remains one of the most common cybercrimes that uses various deceptive URLs to obtain users' personal and sensitive information illegally. While machine learning and deep learning methods have been widely used, most studies report high in-sample accuracies without addressing cross-dataset generalization or model interpretability. This paper considers a diverse collection of models, including traditional machine learning classifiers, deep neural and transformer models, and a hybrid CNN+BiLSTM, on two publicly accessible URL-based phishing datasets. The hybrid CNN+BiLSTM model outperformed with 97 % and 95 % accuracy on the respective datasets. However, cross-dataset testing revealed notable performance degradation, highlighting dataset dependency and the challenge of transferability across domains. Feature analysis confirmed that URL length and special characters are robust indicators across datasets, whereas other predictors are dataset-specific. We applied Local Interpretable Model-Agnostic Explanations (LIME), which illustrates that the models depend on rational URL indicators while also exposing dataset-driven variations. Overall, our findings emphasize that effective phishing detection requires both robust hybrid architectures and interpretable explanations, with future directions focused on domain adaptation to improve generalization.

title: Stray Current Mitigation With Reverse Running Rail Current Control Strategy for Virtual-Return-Based DC Traction Power Supply System
authors: Miao Wang, Jingyue Lu, Wenzhong Ma, Hailiang Xu, Pingjuan Ge, Xiaofeng Yang, Trillion Q. Zheng
abstract: The virtual-return-based DC traction power supply system (VRTPS) has been studied to solve the stray current and rail potential issues in urban rail transit. By utilizing the power electronic devices (PEDs) and return line, VRTPS constructs the zero-resistance virtual return path (VRP) by diverting traction current from the running rail into this newly established path. However, the mitigation performance of existing VRTPS control strategies is still suboptimal, leading to incomplete suppression of stray current and rail potential within the train-occupied section. Thus, the reverse running rail current control strategy of VRTPS is proposed to further reduce the stray current and rail potential. Firstly, the topology and modeling of VRTPS are investigated to analyze the inherent limitations of the ideal zero-resistance VRP. Secondly, the detailed analysis of the reverse running rail current control for VRTPS is presented, which optimizes the running rail current distribution with the variable-resistance VRP. Thirdly, the comparative researches on both the stray current and rail potential distribution are conducted between the conventional system and VRTPS with different control strategies. Finally, the correctness and effectiveness of VRTPS with proposed strategy on the stray current and rail potential mitigation are validated through simulation and experimental results.

title: AI-Powered Threat Intelligence Framework for Strengthening Cybersecurity in Multi-Cloud and DevOps-Driven Enterprise Environments
authors: Vijay Krishna, Siva Prasad Ponnuru, Jyothi Chandra Nath, Seetha Rama Rao Thirumala, Nagaalekhya Kypa, Rajesh Kamisetty, Shripad V Deshpande
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This paper proposes an AI-Driven Threat Intelligence Framework, which can be used to promote cybersecurity in multi-cloud and DevOps-based enterprise environments. The framework uses the Hybrid AI Threat Intelligence Pipeline (HATI) that incorporates deep learning, graph neural networks, and large language models to identify advance and emerging threats. Mostly used to support real-time data ingestion, scalable log processing, and automated anomaly detection, Apache OpenSearch with ML-dash and Security Analytics, is used on a variety of cloud environments and CI/CD pipelines. Experimental analysis proves the suggested solution to be highly accurate in detection, low rates of false positive and rapid incident response in comparison with conventional rule-based, single-model and SIEM-based solutions. The combination of hybrid AI and scalable analytics platform will provide advantages of complete correlation of threats, proactive defense, and increased operational resilience. On the whole, the framework can be seen as a dynamic and strong framework to offer a solution to enterprises that expect to have high-level cybersecurity features in more dynamic and distributed infrastructures.

title: Research on Power Domain Anti-Jamming Methods Based on Reinforcement Learning
authors: XingXiang Huang, Lin Qi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Statistics, Modeling and Advanced Algorithms (ASMA)
abstract: With the intensifying confrontation between intelligent jamming and anti-jamming technologies and the continuous enhancement of system adaptability, both sides are adjusting their strategies in response to the other’s actions. This paper proposes the anti-jamming method LOLA and its improved version LOLA-DQN based on reinforcement learning in the context of power domain confrontation. Also, we employ game theory to describe agent-agent interactions.This algorithm gains a greater advantage in power confrontation among agents by learning the opponent’s gradient and combining it with its own learning network.

title: Energy-Efficient Green Computing Techniques for Edge Devices Using Attention-Guided Federated Learning
authors: Deepa Dawadi, S R V. Prasad Reddy, R. Deepa, S. Punitha, Aarthy. G
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: In recent years, an energy efficient green computing methods for edge devices has gained attention because the rapid growth of intelligent Internet of Things (IoT) applications and the rising deployment of edge intelligence in resource needed environment. Whereas in such systems Federated Learning (FL) emerges as an effective pattern for distributed model training while storing data privacy. However, the existing Adaptive Decision Algorithm (ADA) method based on optimization driven resource allocation suffer additional computational and exhibit limited adaptability under dynamic workload and energy conditions. To address these issues, this research presents Federated Attention-Aware Network (FedAA-Net) a learning driven green computing framework was designed for edge devices operating under FL. Whereas, in this proposed system locally generated edge data is processed without centralized transmission and edge devices performs energy aware local training under the coordination of federated server. Then, an attention deep learning controller is embedded at every edge device to regulate computation and communication behavior by prioritizing low energy execution features. Next, this enables adaptive control of transmission scheduling, CPU utilization and federated participation without iterative optimization. Finally, the federated server aggregates lightweight model updates by using standard federated averaging, scalable coordination and providing privacy preservation. Experimental evaluation presents that the proposed framework reduced entire energy consumption and learning completion time while enhancing task level energy efficiency compared to the existing ADA model by confirming its effectiveness for sustainable edge intelligence.

title: An Imperical Study on Predicting Golden Beauty of Human Face Appearance
authors: Sharmin Akter Bithi, M. Akhtaruzzaman, Md. Golam Rabiul Alam, Aseef Iqbal, Md Meftahul Ferdaus
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This study explores the prediction of human facial beauty levels using machine learning models combined with Golden Ratio principles. A dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 0}$</tex> celebrities sourced from the internet, was analyzed using facial landmark detection techniques to compute Golden Ratio (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\phi}$</tex>) based features. The models were evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 score to assess classification performances. The Random Forest model outperformed the Decision Tree, achieving higher accuracy as <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 2. 0 8 \%}$</tex> over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 9. 6 8 \%}$</tex>. The confusion matrix confirmed that the Random Forest model performed well across all categories. These findings highlight that integrating facial landmark analysis with machine learning enhances automated beauty assessment and underscores the robustness of the Random Forest model in achieving higher accuracy and a balanced trade-off between precision and recall.

title: Towards Accurate Autism Detection: a Multimodal Machine Learning Approach
authors: Musfiq Alam Chowdhury, Md. Al-Hasan, Sabiha Alam Chowdhury, Mehedi Hasan, Rubina Aktar
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is known as a complex neurodevelopmental condition characterized by a wide range of behavioral and communicative deficits. The detection of ASD has been established as a critical area of research, wherein automated and reliable approaches have been enabled through deep learning methodologies. In this study, a multimodal fusion framework was proposed, in which data from image, audio, video and text modalities was integrated to capture complementary behavioral cues. Specialized deep learning architectures were employed for each modality so that high-level feature representations could be extracted, which were subsequently combined at the decision-making stage to enhance classification robustness. The curated dataset, comprising individuals with and without autism, was used to assess the proposed multimodal model. An exceptional average accuracy of 98.61% and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score of 98.61% were obtained across 5-fold cross-validation, thereby demonstrating the superior efficacy of integrating heterogeneous data sources compared to single-modality approaches. Interpretability methods, including SHAP, were utilized to elucidate the decisionmaking process, highlighting clinically relevant features such as gaze direction, vocal pitch and semantic content. Through its transparency, robustness and state-of-the-art performance, the proposed model is highlighted as a promising advancement toward practical, AI-driven, non-invasive autism screening tools.

title: An Ensemble Learning-Based Artificial Intelligence Framework for Postpartum Depression and Suicidal Risk Prediction
authors: Tamanna Akter, Monir Hossen, Najnin Khan Nipa
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The birth of a baby brings significant emotional changes; however, many women experience psychological distress such as postpartum depression (PPD) and, in severe cases, suicidal behavior. PPD is a major public health concern that adversely affects maternal well-being and infant development. Early and reliable detection is therefore essential. This study proposes an ensemble learning-based framework for predicting PPD and suicidal risk using demographic and symptom-based data collected from 1,877 postpartum mothers. To address class imbalance, the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) was applied. Several baseline machine learning classifiers, including Support Vector Machine, Naïve Bayes, Logistic Regression, and K-Nearest Neighbors, were evaluated and compared with ensemble models such as Random Forest, XGBoost, and an AdaBoost-Extra Trees hybrid. Model performance was assessed using 10-fold cross-validation and multiple train-test splits (60/40, 70/30, and 80/20), with the 70/30 split producing optimal results. Experimental findings indicate that ensemble models consistently outperform individual classifiers, achieving high predictive accuracy for suicidal risk (XGBoost: 94%) and postpartum depression (XGBoost: 95%). These results demonstrate the effectiveness of ensemble learning techniques for supporting early identification of high-risk postpartum women.

title: KNN-Based Real-Time Sign Language Recognition Using Flex Sensor Smart Glove with Real-Time Response
authors: Aadhithya T, Sharon Juliet P F, P. Nisha
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Communication challenges experienced by individuals with hearing and speech impairments significantly limit their participation in everyday social and professional activities. This paper presents a real-time sign language interpretation system developed using Internet of Things (IoT) and Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning (AI/ML) techniques through a wearable smart glove. The proposed system uses flex sensors to detect finger bending, hand orientation, and motion. The sensor outputs are processed using an Arduino Nano microcontroller and classified using machine learning algorithms to convert gestures into text output in real time. Experimental evaluation shows reliable gesture recognition with low latency and improved adaptability to different user signing patterns. The developed solution is lowcost, portable, and easy to use, making it suitable for applications in education, healthcare, workplaces, and public environments. The system can also be connected to a laptop or LCD display to provide immediate visual feedback, thereby enhancing usability and accessibility.

title: Compact Triple-Band Elliptical Monopole Antenna with Modified U-Slot Ground for Sub-6 Ghz 5G Applications
authors: Prabhu. T, Suganya. E, Boopathi Raja. K
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: This paper describes a compact triple-band monopole antenna that uses elliptical radiating elements and a modified U-shaped defected ground structure (DGS) for wireless applications below 6 GHz. The suggested antenna works at three resonant frequencies: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.49 \text{GHz}, 4.35 \text{GHz}$</tex>, and 5.12 GHz. These frequencies are for WiMAX, sub- 6 GHz 5 G NR, and WLAN bands, respectively. Geometrically scaled elliptical patches make it possible for the device to work on multiple bands. The modified U-slot etched on the ground plane is also very important for improving inter-band isolation and impedance bandwidth. The U-slot stops surface wave propagation, changes the distribution of ground current, and lowers the antenna quality factor. This makes impedance matching stable and bandwidth wider across all operating bands. The antenna's return loss is better than -23 dB, and its VSWR is less than 1.15. At the resonant frequencies, the highest gains are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2. 2 8 ~ d B i}, \mathbf{1. 3 5 ~ d B i}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-\mathbf{0. 9 3 ~ d B i}$</tex>. The upper band's relatively low gain is due to substrate losses and current confinement that come with compact slot-loaded structures. A prototype is built and tested with a Vector Network Analyzer and anechoic chamber setup. The results from the simulation and the measurement match up well. The proposed antenna is good for portable and short-range sub-6 GHz wireless applications because it is small, has a simple shape, and can work on multiple bands.

title: UDF-Net: Uncertainty-Aware Dynamic Fusion Network for Vision-LiDAR Autonomous Systems
authors: Lakshmi S R, Ghousia Sanober Sabreen, Tamilselvi B, K. Keerthana, Manosri T
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: In modern times, integrated vision and sensor fusion for autonomous systems gained attention because of increasing deployment of autonomous vehicles in complex and dynamic environments. The existing HydraFusion (HF) method employed context-aware sensor fusion for improving accuracy by choosing appropriate sensor combination. However, it depended on single-frame context identification and employe fixed set of predefined fusion branches which leads to imbalance fusion decisions and restricts adaptability in changing environmental conditions. To overcome these limitations, Uncertainty-Aware Dynamic Fusion Network (UDF-Net) for autonomous system is proposed. Initially, data is collected from RADIATE dataset which consist of camera images and LiDAR point clouds and features were extracted. Next, temporal context modeling is introduced employing transformer-based encoder for capturing scene information across many frames where uncertainty estimation enhances reliability of context prediction. Further, dynamic fusion graph is constructed utilizing Graph Neural Network (GNN) which allows flexible and adaptive sensor fusion. Furthermore, context-guided feature fusion is performed using attention-weighted message passing with dynamic graph. Lastly, resulting fused features are processed by same detection network for generating outputs. Therefore, proposed UDF-Net framework obtains enhanced accuracy (89.6 %) and mAP (71.8 %) which is more reliable context estimation and improved fusion stability which validates efficiency for autonomous system perception compared to existing HydraFusion approach.

title: Multi-Class Eye Disease Classification using Transfer Learning
authors: Sudan Madhu M P, Deekshith R, Deep Gagan P S, Lalithya Govardhan
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Doctors require accurate testing for eye disease diagnosis because early detection of eye diseases, which currently rank among the most common causes of worldwide blindness, must be done through accurate testing. Deep learning methods have shown they can effectively analyse medical images, helping identify retinal diseases. The present research investigates a multi-class eye disease classification system which uses transfer learning through convolutional neural networks that contain SqueezeNet MobileNet-V2, ResNet-18, ResNet-34 and ResNet-50. The research used a large retinal dataset, which contained ten disease categories. The accuracy assessment uses precision, recall, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-score and confusion matrix analysis to evaluate model performance. Experimental results show that ResNet-50 delivered the best classification accuracy, whereas ResNet-34 and ResNet-18 offered the most practical balance between performance and computational cost given the training time and hardware limitations. The study demonstrates that deep learning models, which use transfer learning, can function as dependable diagnostic tools for automatic eye disease detection in multiple classes.

title: Development of a Cloud-Integrated IoT Smart Attendance System Using ESP32 Edge Devices
authors: A S M Shahariar Robin, Sarang Chisim, Md. Hridoy Ahmed, Md. Taslim Arif, Sheak Rashed Haider Noori
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The manual attendance systems are tedious, prone to errors and can be abused by proxy and is prone to mismanagement of data. In order to address the shortcomings, this paper will suggest a cloud-based Smart Attendance System based on the NFC (Near Field Communication) protocol and ESP32 edge devices. Each user is issued with a special NFC card with a special secure identifier (UID), when entering a classroom or a workplace, the card is scanned and its identifier read by the reader. The received data is transmitted to a Node.js backend server, and it is securely stored in a MongoDB database that fills the real-time monitoring and administrative control in a web-based dashboard. Experimental testing under controlled environments revealed that the mean end to end response time is approximately 1 second, and it includes card scanning, cloud transmission, and dashboard update. Additional stress test with 100 simulated HTTP requests ensured that the back end is stable and checking can be obtained at greater load test. The suggested system is a cheap (approximately, 13 USD as the hardware cost), scalable, and efficient alternative to the old strategies of attendance that may be applied to educational institutions and organizational setting.

title: Data-Driven Psychological Stress Classification Among Higher Education Students: A Machine Learning Approach
authors: Ajit Sharma, Dhiman Sarma, Saikat Das Roky
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Psychological stress is a serious issue for students in higher education institutions, especially in Bangladesh. Many students deal with academic pressure, financial problems, and high social expectations. Although mental health issues are increasing, most institutions do not have effective ways to spot or address stress early. These challenges can harm students' academic results and overall well-being. There is a clear need for predictive models that consider personal stress factors like family expectations, financial struggles, and peer pressure. This study fills an important gap in research, since earlier studies have often missed the specific stressors students face in higher education or have not used a wide range of diverse data, like academic records, health information, behavior, and personal challenges. In this research paper, we propose a data-driven technique using machine learning (ML) algorithms to classify stress. To address this, we built a predictive model using 801 student records, with 80% used for training and 20% for testing. We tested five ML algorithms: Random Forest (RF), Naive Bayes (NB), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Logistic Regression (LR). The LR model performed best, reaching 94.56% accuracy and a macro Area Under the Curve (AUC) of 99.54. SVM came next with a slightly lower AUC. Random Forest had 74.15% accuracy, which may suggest overfitting. KNN and Naive Bayes achieved 67.35% and 76.19% accuracy, respectively. The work's potential to address the growing number of mental health issues among students by giving educational institutions a tool for predicting stress before it reaches critical levels makes it socially necessary. Early detection can improve student well-being and lower dropout rates. The model promotes inclusivity by considering socioeconomic factors, which makes it appropriate for a range of student demographics and enhances fairness in mental health support services.

title: Alongamento passivo de alta intensidade no músculo antagonista gera alterações no comportamento das unidades motoras do músculo agonista durante contração isométrica? Um estudo preliminar
authors: André Brand Bezerra Coutinho, Hélio de Veiga Cabral, Liliam Fernandes de Oliveira
publish: 2026 Global Medical Engineering Physics Exchanges/ Pan American Health Care Exchanges (GMEPE/PAHCE)
abstract: Static stretching is commonly used to increase range of motion (ROM) and prepare the neuromuscular system for exercise. While prolonged stretching of agonist muscles may impair performance, the effects of stretching antagonist muscles on agonist behavior remain unclear. This preliminary study investigated whether high-intensity passive stretching of the triceps surae (antagonist) alters the motor unit behavior of the tibialis anterior (agonist) during isometric dorsiflexion. Five healthy male participants performed maximal voluntary contractions and submaximal trapezoidal torque tasks before and after a stretching protocol consisting of five 45-s passive stretches separated by 15-s rest. High-density surface electromyography (HDsEMG) was recorded from the tibialis anterior and decomposed to identify motor unit discharge times, allowing matched motor units to be tracked across conditions. Peak torque and ROM were compared pre- and post-stretching, and motor unit parameters were analyzed using linear mixed-effects models. Stretching significantly increased ROM (p = 0.048) but did not significantly change peak torque (p = 0.107). No significant differences were observed in mean discharge rate (p = 0.28) or discharge rate variability (p = 0.08), although variability showed a tendency to increase. These findings suggest that high-intensity antagonist stretching may induce subtle neural adjustments in the agonist muscle.

title: System-Level Coordinated Control Model of Energy Storage Systems for Grid Security and Stability Analysis
authors: Yuhan Liu, Hanyang Dai, Xiong Xiao, Xiangtao Xiao, Xinli Song, Shaohang Hao, Yikang Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the large-scale integration of renewable energy, energy storage systems are playing an increasingly critical role in enhancing grid security and operational flexibility. This paper presents a system-level coordinated control model for energy storage systems participating in grid security and stability control, suitable for large-scale power system simulation. The study first analyzes the functions of energy storage systems in power flow control, frequency regulation, and voltage support. Based on this, a coordinated control framework is developed, integrating automatic generation control, automatic voltage control, security and stability control systems, and a priority-based weight allocation mechanism that coordinates command priorities with resource availability. The proposed model is validated through a four-machine two-area system simulation for secondary frequency regulation and a cascading fault case study based on an actual provincial power grid. Results demonstrate that the model accurately captures the multi-timescale dynamic characteristics of utility-scale energy storage systems and effectively supports grid security and stability analysis.

title: Reconstruction of Scan Scintigraphy and SPECT Images Using an Enhanced Iterative Blind Deconvolution Algorithm
authors: Letícia Alves Ferreira, Sérgio Querino Brunette, Bárbara Juarez Amorim, Eduardo Tavares Costa
publish: 2026 Global Medical Engineering Physics Exchanges/ Pan American Health Care Exchanges (GMEPE/PAHCE)
abstract: Image restoration is the process of estimating the original image content from a degraded image. It is assumed the main type of scintigraphy (scan) and SPECT images degradation is due to the Compton effect. In this paper we present a methodology for mitigating this effect by image deconvolution using the system PSF and dependent on the type of radiotracer and the patient phenotype. The methodology applies a blind deconvolution using Richardson-Lucy iterative algorithm along with other techniques, like automatic iterations interruption. Our approach consists of estimating the total PSF of the patient-detector system applying the blind deconvolution twice, using the PSF found on the first deconvolution, which has as parameters the lower energy window (Compton effect) phantom image and the image of the point source filled with the radiotracer of interest (<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">99m</sup>Tc MDP, <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">177</sup>Lut and <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">131</sup>I). The methodology was applied on scintigraphy and SPECT images of the phantom Quality Nema-NU2-2007 filled with water and one of the radiotracers. Reconstructed images of the phantom presented a reduced Compton effect and a better edge definition, after analyzing the ECBM metric (related to the borders definition) - any gain greater than zero represents a better border definition. This metric presented a gain of over 100% in most of the images.

title: An Interpretable and Efficient KNN-Based Approach for Object Segmentation
authors: Sina Saadati, Abdolah Sepahvand
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Computer vision and image segmentation play a critical role in diverse applications, including industry, urban infrastructure, and medical procedures. Deep convolutional neural networks (CNNs) are widely used for object segmentation; however, they demand high computational resources, leading to increased energy consumption. In this study, we propose a lightweight KNN-based algorithm for image segmentation that reduces computational cost. The method requires the user to select a few points around the target object, after which the algorithm automatically segments the object of interest with minimal computation. Since many objects can be distinguished by color, the proposed approach enables efficient image annotation and segmentation, producing labeled data that can be used to train deep CNNs. This method offers a practical solution for reducing computational overhead while supporting computer vision tasks.

title: Interactive Graphical User Interface for Spectral Doppler Simulation and Experimentation
authors: Gilson M. Kanashiro, Joaquim M. Maia, Amauri A. Assef, Eduardo T. Costa
publish: 2026 Global Medical Engineering Physics Exchanges/ Pan American Health Care Exchanges (GMEPE/PAHCE)
abstract: This paper presents an interactive computational environment developed for spectral Doppler simulation and experimentation, integrating B-mode image reconstruction and blood flow analysis. The system was implemented in MATLAB and provides customization features, including dynamic adjustment of insonation angle, spectral resolution, dynamic range, and baseline shift. Its modular architecture enables exploration of each stage of the Doppler pipeline - from raw data acquisition to spectral visualization - making it an effective tool for education and research in biomedical engineering. Preliminary results demonstrate that the environment enhances understanding of fundamental concepts of ultrasound signal processing and important principles such as aliasing, the relationship between pulse repetition frequency and resolution, and the impact of angle steering of plane waves on velocity estimation.

title: A Distribution Network Situation Awareness Method Based on Multi-Time-Scale Hybrid Measurements
authors: Yan Liu, Jiaxiao Wang, Yanyan Liang, Chengcheng Wu, Peng Xu, Yaya Xu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the rapid development of active distribution networks, measurement data sources have become increasingly diverse, and different types of data are updated at inconsistent rates. Therefore, characterizing uncertainty and achieving reliable situation awareness under multi-time-scale hybrid measurements has become an urgent challenge. This paper first proposes a distribution-network situation awareness method based on multi-time-scale hybrid measurements. To fuse real-time data, historical data, and short-term forecast data, a sliding-window mechanism is used to temporally align them to a unified evaluation instant. Then, affine arithmetic is adopted to model multi-source uncertainties in a unified manner: correlation among data from different sources is captured via shared error terms, while random errors are described by the inherent noise of each data source, ultimately transforming uncertainty into interval-valued state estimates. Finally, a risk situation index is constructed based on the upper and lower bounds of the voltage intervals, and comparative experiments are conducted on the improved IEEE 13-bus and improved IEEE 33-bus test systems. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method improves state prediction accuracy under incomplete measurements and correlated errors, providing interpretable situation information and technical support for distribution-network operational risk assessment and active dispatch.

title: Research on Control Strategy for Seamless Transition Between Grid-Connected and Islanded Modes
authors: ZhiWei Wang, Xu Jun Ma, Xiao Wei Li, Kai Zhang, XuHuan Zhao, Cheng Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Seamless transitions between grid-connected and islanded modes in hybrid microgrids are critical for uninterruptible power and system stability. However, dynamic mode transitions frequently cause voltage flicker, transient current surges, and phase jumps. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a comprehensive seamless transition control strategy for T-type neutral-point-clamped (NPC) energy storage converters. For the grid-to-island transition, an improved dual-loop control method with load current feedforward is introduced. Pre-setting the integrator states ensures continuous controller output across the transition boundary, effectively suppressing DC-bus voltage fluctuations and transient current surges. Conversely, during the island-to-grid recovery, a real-time phase pre-synchronization loop adjusts the converter’s output angular frequency to track the grid phase. This guarantees smooth and continuous phase alignment before the dynamic reconnection. Underpinning these strategies, the control loops are modeled and parameterized using standard Type-I and Type-II system theories. Simulation results verify that the proposed approach successfully eliminates transient surges and achieves high-quality bidirectional seamless transitions.

title: An Intra-Area VSC-HVDC Back-to-Back Retrofit Scheme Based on Transfer Impedance
authors: Zuohong Li, Feng Li, Hua Pan, Haochun Chen
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the continuous expansion of power grid scale and the large-scale integration of renewable energy, short-circuit current levels in the China Southern Power Grid have increased markedly, posing severe challenges to the safe operation of densely meshed AC networks. Existing mitigation measures, including inter-area VSC-HVDC back-to-back links, are primarily designed for large-scale grid decoupling and exhibit limited effectiveness in suppressing excessive short-circuit currents within high-density intra-area partitions. To address this limitation, an intra-area VSC-HVDC back-to-back retrofit scheme is proposed in this paper. Transfer impedance is introduced as a key indicator to identify candidate AC transmission lines whose replacement can effectively reduce fault current contributions to critical nodes. On this basis, a comprehensive evaluation framework is established by considering short-circuit current variation, line loading characteristics, power flow magnitude, and voltage deviation, enabling systematic comparison among multiple retrofit schemes. A typical zonal power supply model is established using the BPA platform to represent the current grid topology of the China Southern Power Grid. Three-phase short-circuit simulations are carried out to evaluate system performance before and after the proposed retrofits. Simulation results show that the intra-area VSC-HVDC back-to-back integration significantly increases transfer impedance between key nodes and surrounding network areas, resulting in a pronounced reduction of short-circuit current levels. All critical nodes are maintained within acceptable safety margins while normal power exchange capability and operational flexibility are preserved. The results demonstrate that the proposed planning approach and evaluation framework provide an effective and practical solution for mitigating short-circuit currents in large-scale AC grids with dense internal coupling.

title:
CO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    emission prediction based on the Type 2 Fuzzy Recurrent Neural Network (T2FRNN)
authors: Moammad Fiuzy, Saber Ghasemiyan Mazaher, Ali Mousavi
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: The deterioration of atmospheric quality has become a pressing global environmental concern. The focus has always been on reducing or avoiding environmentally harmful gas emissions. This study introduces an original methodology for estimating levels of carbon dioxide emissions. A new modeling framework based on a Type-2 Fuzzy Recurrent Neural Network (T2FRNN) is proposed, using real-world data obtained from Delhi, one of the world’s most heavily polluted urban areas. We have also presented the new learning algorithm for this purpose and also proposed a method that has shown satisfactory prediction accuracy, with a noteworthy value of 10units for its prediction RMSE error, signifying its effective functioning.

title: System Hang reduction on TEL Track Act8 Tools: A Case Study on Legacy Slave HDD Failure
authors: Shine Arkar Aung, Liu Houdong, Fazuli Sani
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: Intermittent system hangs on TEL Track Act8 tools were causing unpredictable production interruptions despite repeated software reimaging, firmware alignment, and controller replacement. A structured root-cause investigation revealed that aging secondary ("slave") hard disk drives—originally intended only for local logging—were injecting intermittent timing disturbances and power-rail ripple during retry events. These effects degraded controller timing margins, triggering watchdog resets and system hangs. We combined field data, Self-Monitoring, Analysis, and Reporting Technology (SMART) attribute trend analysis, oscilloscope-based power integrity measurements, and reliability modeling using Mean Time Between Failure (MTBF) and Arrhenius acceleration to characterize the degradation mechanism. Removing the slave HDDs and migrating logging to network storage eliminated >80% of unexplained hangs across the fleet, restoring deterministic controller behavior and improving overall equipment availability. The findings highlight the reliability risks of legacy electromechanical storage within mature semiconductor tools and provide practical obsolescence-management strategies for High-Volume Manufacturing (HVM) environments.

title: AI-Enhanced Learning Environments to Foster Communicative Competence in French. A Mixed-Methods Study with STEAM Students
authors: Miriam Iliana Navarrete Bear, Adriana Morales Rodriguez, Cristian Tatiana Segura Dominguez
abstract: This paper presents an educational innovation project designed to enhance communicative competence in French as a Foreign Language (FLE) through the integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools. Conducted between November 2024 and May 2025 at PrepaTec Campus Tampico, Tecnológico de Monterrey (Mexico), the study involved 68 students aged 1618, enrolled in the Multicultural High School Program. These learners, coming from Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, and Mathematics (STEAM) disciplines, participated in FLE courses at levels 2, 4, and 5, corresponding to CEFR A1-B1. The project integrated applications such as ChatGPT, TecGPT, Hello History, Fliki, HeyGen, Suno, Piclumen, Hailou, and SkillStudio to strengthen oral and written production in digitally enriched learning environments. A mixed-methods approach was applied: quantitative data were obtained through pre- and post-tests (TCF and DELF-style simulations), while qualitative insights emerged from student feedback, audio/video productions, and teacher observations. Results indicate significant gains in fluency, pronunciation, argumentation, accuracy, and learner autonomy, with increased motivation and confidence. This study demonstrates that AI-enhanced environments can foster communicative competence in non-immersive contexts, offering replicable models for integrating emerging technologies into language education.

title: A Hierarchical Carbon Emission Factor Correction Method Considering Non-Grid-Connected Electricity
authors: Dawei Ma, Haotian Zhang, Ming Kong, Chao Ji, Wei Tang, Yang Su, Bin Xu, Chao Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Accurate carbon emission factors (CEFs) are essential for tracking and reducing emissions in power systems. Traditional average CEF methods suffer from coarse granularity, while carbon flow tracing approaches are computationally intensive and unsuitable for large-scale grids. Moreover, both ignore the environmental benefits of non-grid-connected electricity (NGCE), such as local rooftop PV or self-consumed distributed generation. This paper proposes a hierarchical correction method for regional CEFs that accounts for NGCE. Based on electrical partitioning theory, the grid is divided into three layers: provincial, prefectural, and end-user. A recursive calculation strategy is adopted to compute CEFs for grid-connected electricity, while NGCE is attributed to local nodes for final correction. Case studies validate the method’s feasibility, showing improved spatiotemporal resolution and accurate reflection of distributed generation benefits. The approach offers a practical pathway for carbon accounting in regional power systems.

title: Study on Si-contamination in Developable BARC (DBARC) in High-K Metal Gate Process
authors: Wu Wenbin, Zhang Lei, Li Xiaojing, Fu Yong Feng
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: This paper presents a study on the effect of a Si-containing DBARC chemical during lithography step in HKMG process. Compared to normal good batch and unopen bottle of same type of DBARC chemical, an additional thin film was observed if the Si-containing DBARC material was coated together with photoresist. The formation of such Si–O film on top of wafer surface later caused related device failure. Cross-section TEM and EDX mapping techniques were used to characterize the film. Further experiment of characterization of this thin film with SIMS analysis indicates a potential application of in-line SIMS tool to monitor contamination from DBARC.

title: Intelligent Modeling for Deriving Human Lens Stiffness Based on Patients’ Demographic Features with Outcome in Phacoemulsification Surgery
authors: Sara Rahmati, Hadi Tabesh
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Background: Accurate preoperative assessment of human lens stiffness is a critical determinant in modern cataract surgery, particularly for optimizing ultrasound energy in phacoemulsification. Current clinical grading methods often rely on subjective judgment, which limits personalized surgical planning. Methods: We developed a predictive framework utilizing routine demographic and clinical features to estimate the gold-standard ex vivo elastic modulus (Young's modulus) of 50 cataractous human lenses, obtained via extracapsular cataract extraction (ECCE). A suite of supervised regression algorithms, including Support Vector Regression (SVR), was implemented and rigorously compared based on Mean Squared Error (MSE) and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> metrics. Results: SVR achieved the highest predictive performance on the test set (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>=0.9409). Feature importance analysis consistently identified cataract grade (LOCS III), lens diameter, and age as the most influential factors driving stiffness prediction. Conclusion: This study establishes a successful intelligent proof-of-concept demonstrating the feasibility of correlating readily available clinical data with fundamental biomechanical properties using advanced machine learning. The models lay the necessary groundwork for developing non-invasive, AI-driven diagnostic tools to enhance surgical safety and efficiency.

title: Quantum Enhanced Data-driven Learning for Defect Reduction in Etching Process
authors: Zusing Yang, Li-Wei Wang, Yi-Che Chen, Yi-Han Chao, Cheng-En Tsai, Ming-Tsung Wu, Hong-Ji Lee, Nan-Tzu Lian, Tahone Yang, Kuang-Chao Chen, Chih-Yuan Lu
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: Defect reduction in plasma etching is a critical challenge in semiconductor manufacturing, traditionally addressed through empirical tuning of multiple process parameters with significant resource and time consumption. This study presents a quantum-enhanced data-driven learning framework that integrates quantum neural networks (QNN), quantum annealing (QA), and quantum evolution (QE) to optimize 25 etching parameters simultaneously for improved defect mitigation and profile control. The quantum circuits and annealing procedures were simulated using PennyLane’s variational quantum simulator backend. The proposed approach enables extrapolative prediction beyond historical domain knowledge, overcoming limitations of conventional machine learning methods such as artificial neural networks (ANNs). Experimental results demonstrate a substantial reduction in total defect density from 16.39 to 0.42 defects/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> while maintaining stable etching critical dimension (ECD) control. Notably, QE uncovers hidden sensitivities in parameters previously held constant, highlighting the potential of quantum-enhanced learning to both optimize processes and reveal latent manufacturing insights. This work underscores the promise of hybrid quantum-classical machine learning frameworks as the means for systematic process optimization in advanced semiconductor fabrication.

title: First-Year Engineering Design: Active Learning with Rockets, Simulation, and AI
authors: Jorge Alvarez, Jorge Hidalgo, Juan-Gabino Diaz-Martinez, Irandi Gutiérrez-Carmona, Zahira Gabriela Cruz-Netro, L.F. Guevara-Chavez
abstract: This paper presents the design, implementation, and evaluation of the HydroRocket project, a project-based learning (PBL) experience for first-year engineering students. The activity combined the construction of a water-propelled rocket with digital simulation in MATLAB, data acquisition through Arduino-based sensors, and personalized tutoring supported by artificial intelligence. The primary objective was to enhance engineering design self-efficacy, defined as students' confidence in their ability to address complex design tasks. The validated Engineering Design Self-Efficacy Scale was administered to 62 students, yielding mean scores ranging from 3.56 to 4.05 with high internal consistency <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\alpha=0.91)$</tex>. Quantitative results were reinforced by qualitative evidence. Additionally, prototype performance improved by 44% over successive iterations, providing objective evidence of design improvement. Findings indicate that the integration of emerging technologies into hands-on projects can foster motivation, autonomous learning, and the development of professional identity in engineering from the earliest stages of education.

title: The Development of Foundations of Open Practices Online Courses
authors: Diana Andone, Vlad Mihaescu, Radu Vasiu, Silviu Vert, Andrei Ternauciuc, Marius Tataru
abstract: This paper introduces the analyses of the design, create, and pilot four modular courses - Licensing and Open Licensing, Open Education, Open Access and Open Science, and Open Innovation, as integrated in a micro-specialisation of Foundation of Open Practices - to equip students and staff across the E<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>UDRES<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> European Universities Alliance with the skills, knowledge, and credentials needed to engage with open practices. The course development methodology involved a collaborative, cross-institutional design process based on the EU DigComp 2.2 Digital Competences framework, Design-Based Research (DBR), intersected with the ABCtoVLE method and refined based on instructional design principles and the Open Educational Practices (OEP) framework. The integration of the Foundations in Open Practices training programme into the E<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>UDRES<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> Arena Virtual Learning Environment (VLE), built on Moodle, represents a strategic step toward creating a coherent, scalable, and pedagogically aligned digital learning ecosystem for these courses. The courses contribute to embedding openness within higher education curricula and align with European digital competence frameworks.

title: Fast Partitioning Strategy for Three-Phase Unbalanced Distribution Networks Based on Electrical Coupling Strength
authors: Jiaxiao Wang, Yan Liu, Yanyan Liang, Can Liu, Yaya Xu, Yan Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the increasingly widespread application of distributed computing in distribution network analysis, efficient network partitioning methods have become a crucial foundation for supporting parallel computing and distributed state estimation. This paper proposes a partitioning strategy suitable for three-phase unbalanced distribution networks. Based on the inherent electrical characteristics of the network, the strategy constructs a partitioning model for distribution network scenarios without distributed generation. First, the electrical distance is defined using the node impedance matrix to quantify the coupling strength between nodes. On this basis, a community detection algorithm with modularity optimization is adopted for initial partitioning, resulting in multiple highly cohesive initial subareas. Subsequently, a merging strategy is proposed, which iteratively merges the adjacent subareas with the strongest electrical coupling and the smallest scale until a predefined number of partitions is achieved. This ensures tight electrical connections within each subarea while maintaining relatively balanced scales across subareas.

title: Joint Frequency-Phase Estimation of Power Grid Signals Based on Frequency-Domain Attention Mechanism and Kalman Filter Synergy
authors: Junhao Feng, Tongyin Dai, Chen Liu, Shuguang Liu, Xiandong Zhang, Shuai Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Grid frequency is a core indicator of power quality. Its precise measurement serves as the benchmark for safe and stable grid operation while directly impacting the control accuracy of renewable energy grid integration. The increasing non-stationary characteristics of renewable power systems lead to frequent frequency fluctuations. Frequency measurement faces challenges such as slow dynamic response, cumulative phase errors, and low accuracy under scenarios involving sudden frequency changes, phase jumps, and harmonic pollution. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a joint frequency-phase estimation method for grid signals based on the synergy of frequency-domain attention mechanisms and Kalman filtering. This approach constructs a lightweight neural network architecture that extracts joint time-frequency domain features through cross-timescale convolutional layers. A frequency-domain attention module is designed to adaptively enhance response weights for fundamental and key harmonic components, effectively suppressing high-frequency noise and irrelevant frequency interference. Physical constraint phase encoding and Kalman dynamic correction mechanisms are introduced to eliminate phase jump errors and mitigate window truncation effects. Experimental validation demonstrates superior tracking accuracy and dynamic response performance under complex disturbance conditions.

title: A Learning Framework for Control Systems in Manufacturing Processes Using the FrED Platform
authors: David Ángeles-Rojas, Pedro Ponce, Bradley Russel, Brian Anthony
abstract: This paper presents a training framework using the Fiber Extrusion Device (FrED) from MIT to enhance manufacturing process education and strengthen the connection between control theory and practical implementation. A common challenge in engineering education is that students often struggle to relate theoretical concepts to real-world systems. This work addresses that gap through an integrated experimental approach. The framework links academic learning with industrial practice by guiding participants through data acquisition, system identification, and PID controller design using FrED's actuator. Experimental data are modeled with the least squares method to obtain a second order transfer function, allowing learners to observe how parameter estimation and gain tuning influence system behavior. Using MATLAB and Python for analysis and implementation, the methodology improves both conceptual understanding and practical competence, contributing to workforce development in smart manufacturing.

title: Multi-Time-Scale Flexible Regulation Capability Evaluation and Bi-Level Optimal Configuration of Integrated Energy Systems Considering an Improved Sparrow Search Algorithm
authors: Yishu Kong, Guangqian Ding, Jinhan Zhao, Zhuoqun Wu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of insufficient flexibility and high penalty costs caused by severe source-load fluctuations in high-penetration renewable energy microgrids . This paper proposes a bi-level optimization configuration model that takes into account flexibility across multi-time-scales. The upper level employs improved sparrow search algorithm(ISSA) for global optimization of flexibility resource capacity, while the lower level relies on MILP to realize refined day-ahead and intra-day multi-time-scale scheduling. The study analyzed the trans-medium flexibility mechanism under multi-physics field coupling. Through simulation, it revealed the envelope accommodation capability of the low-inertia electrolyzer at noon for the overflow of photovoltaic power, as well as the economic arbitrage phenomenon where redundant electrical energy is transferred to P2G hydrogen production under the rigid constraints of the system at night. The results show that, compared with the traditional single- level planning, the ISSA-MILP bi-level framework proposed in this paper reduces the penalty for short-term flexibility deficiency and achieves high proportion of local consumption of new energy, and lowers the comprehensive annualized cost of the system over its full life cycle. This research provides quantitative theoretical support for the flexible autonomy and economic operation of future integrated energy systems(IES).

title: Research Progress on Flexible Interconnection Devices for Active Distribution Networks
authors: Ran Chen, Peng Zhang, Wei Bao, Chengzhi Li
