publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: The continuous growth of distributed generation and loads is driving the evolution of distribution networks towards greater flexibility and intelligence. As a key enabler of this transformation, flexible interconnection devices can effectively enhance the controllability, reliability, and renewable energy hosting capacity of distribution networks. This paper firstly outlines the basic structures and operational principles of typical flexible interconnection devices. Subsequently, it systematically summarizes the current research status and challenges in key technologies within the planning and design dimension. Finally, the paper delves into the future application bottlenecks and development trends in this field, aiming to provide valuable references for in-depth research and the scaled deployment of flexible interconnected distribution networks.

title: A Cross-Validation Framework for Robust ANN Modeling: Application to Electrical Discharge Machining of Inconel 718
authors: Mohammad Hossein Aminian, Arash Khosravi, Ahmad Azarnik
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Artificial Neural Networks are widely used for modeling nonlinear processes in experimental fields such as machining, where datasets are often small and noisy. However, many studies rely on weak baseline models, manual single train–test splits, and unclear hyperparameter tuning, which limit reproducibility and model reliability. This work introduces a systematic and reproducible pipeline for ANN training and evaluation under data-scarce conditions. The framework integrates k-fold cross-validation, structured hyperparameter tuning, and a balanced-score selection criterion that accounts for both predictive accuracy and generalization stability. The approach is applied to Electrical Discharge Machining of Inconel 718, comparing traditional backpropagation with two metaheuristic optimizers—Genetic Algorithm and Gray Wolf Optimization. Results show that while backpropagation achieves the lowest training error, metaheuristic training offers improved robustness and stability across folds. The proposed framework minimizes bias from arbitrary data splits and provides a transparent, fair basis for evaluating ANN-based models in small experimental datasets.

title: Triboelectric Charges induced from Detaping and the Influencing Factors
authors: Ke Kian Seng Joseph
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: This paper presents a study on the charges induced by de-taping process because of the electrostatic discharge (ESD) events caused by the separation of insulating tape from the wafer surface. The removal process induced the triboelectrification on the tape and wafer. High drain current with outliers was observed on a NMOS depletion transistor at the Process Control Monitoring (PCM) structure as a result of these charges. Several design of experiments were carried out to investigate the factors that influence these charges and the result are discussed and illustrated in this paper.

title: Optimizing Municipal Teams: Personalized Learning for T-Shaped Profile Development
authors: Klára Kubíčkova, Janka Marschalková, Zuzana Hruška, Leonard Walletzký
abstract: As Smart Cities mature from technological pilots to complex Cooperative Cities, the primary barrier to success is often not technical, but organizational. Traditional municipal structures rely on vertical silos that inhibit the crossdepartmental collaboration required for climate neutrality and system-level resilience. This paper argues that the Cooperative City requires a fundamental shift in human capital development toward T-shaped professionals-experts who combine deep vertical specialization with broad horizontal skills in boundary spanning and strategic collaboration. We present findings from a qualitative study of Czech municipalities identifying a critical gap in these horizontal competencies and a reactive approach to upskilling. In response, we introduce a Proof of Concept for an AI-driven system and explore the potential of Large Language Models (LLMs) to transform municipal education. We specifically identify the challenges of moving beyond static recommendations toward automated, individualized content generation. By synthesizing practical best practice examples into personalized learning paths, we demonstrate a vision for a “human infrastructure” that fosters true inter-institutional cooperation.

title: Aligning Engineering and Technology Management Education with AI-Driven Industry Expectations
authors: Ehud Menipaz, Natalia Bukhshtaber, Ben Bulmash
abstract: The paper examines how the integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) is reshaping work practices, elevating performance expectations, and redefining competencies in engineering and technology management. Drawing on interviews with industry professionals, it identifies a cognitive shift toward human-AI co-thinking as the mechanism driving these transformations. The study links empirical findings to a conceptual framing that explains why and how AI alters practices, performance benchmarks, and skill demands within hybrid socio-technical systems, and translates these insights into recommendations for curricula, pedagogy, and assessment. By exposing the widening gap between industry requirements and educational provision, it underscores the urgency of reform. The paper calls for systemic change to prepare graduates not only to function effectively in AI-augmented workplaces but also to lead innovation and socio-technical transformation in the AI era.

title: Photoacoustic signal from hemoglobin target
authors: Isabel Nunes Ramalho da Rocha, Juliana Oliveira da Silva Fernandes, Raymundo Cordero Garcia, Krishynan Santhy Fernandes Meirelles Araujo, Beatriz Ferreira de Carvalho Patricio, Ana Beatriz Furlanetto Pacheco, João Carlos Machado
publish: 2026 Global Medical Engineering Physics Exchanges/ Pan American Health Care Exchanges (GMEPE/PAHCE)
abstract: Photoacoustic (PA) systems have been extensively studied for their potential to obtain functional information from biological organs and tissues. Studies report the potential of endogenous molecules, such as hemoglobin (Hb), to generate photoacoustic signals. This study presents an experimental evaluation of a PA system developed at the Ultrasound Laboratory of the Biomedical Engineering Program at COPPE/UFRJ. For this purpose, a rectangular phantom was constructed from a 2% agarose solution using a mold produced by 3D printing, which had internal channels molded by catheters, which were removed after the agarose solidified. Initially, graphite was analyzed, used as a positive control for optical absorption, followed by a solution of human Hb at a concentration of 1 mg/ml. Photoacoustic excitation was performed using a DPSS laser source operating at 1064 nm, and the acoustic signals generated were detected by an ultrasonic transducer. The results demonstrated the acquisition of clearly detectable photoacoustic signals for both absorbents, highlighting the sensitivity of the system to Hb detection, as found in the literature. This study validates the proposed experimental methodology and establishes a basis for future studies.

title: Optimization Strategy for Micro Energy Grid Transactions in Multi-Level Energy Markets Based on Asymmetric Nash Bargaining
authors: Shan Liu, Kouyu Sun, Yiheng Liang, Kangyang Lv, Chenhui Xu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the advancement of the multi-level energy market, micro energy grid (MEG) operators are confronted with core issues such as insufficient transaction synergy and imbalance in the distribution of benefits among entities. This paper proposes a coordinated multi-level electricity market trading strategy and constructs an integrated multi-level energy trading framework covering the energy wholesale market, the peer-to-peer electricity market, and the shared energy storage leasing market. To balance efficiency and fairness in multi-level energy market coordination, an optimization method based on the asymmetric Nash bargaining theory is further developed. First, the strategy quantifies the actual contribution of each participating entity in multi-level transactions, and scientifically sets differentiated bargaining weights for all entities. On this basis, it realizes the global optimization of the overall operating cost of the MEG system while taking into account the benefit demands of each trading entity, thus achieving a reasonable and fair distribution of benefits. Finally, the alternating direction method of multipliers is adopted to solve the proposed optimization model, and case studies based on actual scenarios are conducted to verify the strategy. The results show that the proposed strategy can effectively improve the market coordination efficiency of the multi-level energy market, significantly reduce the comprehensive operating cost of the entire MEG system, and achieve a fair and reasonable distribution of trading benefits for each participating entity, which fully verifies the feasibility and effectiveness of the strategy.

title: Maintaining Lot Schedule Adherence through Overlapping Q-times via Admission Control
authors: Thomas Hewlett, Srikanth Ramakrishnan, Himanshu Mogra, Shiladitya Chakravorty, Detlef Pabst
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: Dispatching into a time-constrained sequence (Q-time or Time-link zone) poses a unique challenge for high volume manufacturing (HVM) factories, as schedule priority is, instead, often trumped by the protection of wafer quality. As fab processes mature, these constrained sequences may also continue to increase in complexity. This paper outlines a method by which both objectives of quality and schedule adherence can be achieved in an automated manner.

title: Unload Clean Removal for Showerhead Pattern Defects Improvement
authors: Hong Kai Tan, Kenneth Chih Yuang Mah, Ker Peng Lau, Edward See, Chek Chou Chiam
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: In CVD systems, clean recipe settings are closely related to the three core tenets of manufacturing, which are cost, productivity, and quality. In the interest of throughput, a cleaning configuration termed as "Clean on Unload Loadlock" may be utilized to process multiple wafers consecutively before the cleaning process is triggered to maximize output, which is synchronized to the wafer loading and unloading cycle. In this paper, we show that this cleaning configuration introduces a critical process imbalance. Specifically, in twin-reactor CVD systems, one chamber becomes repeatedly exposed to cyclic cleaning erosion, due to final wafers of a lot processing only in one chamber, leading to the partner chamber being uncoated during the subsequent cleaning and subjected to chemical attack by fluorine radicals. Hence, the removal of such cleaning configuration is proposed to mitigate showerhead pattern defects, with an improvement from an average of 4.6 cases per year to zero cases since implementation to date, and gas cost savings.

title: A Data-driven Approach to Synergistic Resilient Assessment and Investment Optimization for Sustainable Operation of Island Power Grids
authors: Yuetong Li, Haoyang Yu, Hongfan Li, Xueyong Ding, Shifeng Wang, Liansheng Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Island power grids face the core challenge of improving power supply reliability while controlling investment costs. To scientifically quantify the trade-off between these two factors and seek the optimal solution for social welfare, this paper constructs a data-driven resilience-economy synergistic assessment and investment decision optimization framework. Firstly, comprehensive system resilience metrics, total social costs, and marginal resilience costs are defined. Subsequently, a three-part methodology comprising power outage loss prediction, core metric calculation, and visualized decision-making is proposed. Finally, taking the Hainan power grid as a case study, five investment scenarios and three disaster severity levels are designed. The research demonstrates that there exists a clear optimal economic equilibrium point for grid resilience investment, with distinct patterns in marginal cost variations. Optimizing investment strategies can effectively enhance social welfare. This study provides a scientific quantitative analysis tool for refined grid planning and investment decision-making.

title: Electronic Fratricide in Air Defence Operations While Countering Uas in Tactical Battle Areas
authors: M Y Khan, Pranjal Agarwal, Rajagopal Chellamani Srikanth
publish: 2026 International Conference on Air Defense and Security (ICADS)
abstract: Electronic fratricide in air defence operations refers to the unintended interference, degradation or neutralisation of friendly sensors, communication networks, navigation aids and weapon control systems due to uncoordinated electronic emissions within a contested electromagnetic environment. The rapid proliferation of Unmanned Aerial Systems (UAS), operating across diverse frequency bands with low radar crosssections, has compelled air defence forces to increasingly rely on electronic countermeasures, jammers, spoofers and directed energy weapons[1] [2]. While these measures are essential for countering hostile UAS, inadequate spectrum coordination and decentralised control can inadvertently disrupt friendly systems, resulting in loss of situational awareness, delayed engagements and increased risk of blue-on-blue incidents[2] [4]. This paper analyses the evolution of the UAS threat, its employment in contemporary conflicts, existing counter-UAS measures and the primary causes of electronic fratricide. Case studies from recent conflicts are examined to highlight operational risks, followed by recommendations to mitigate electronic fratricide through centralised command and control, integrated spectrum management, training and procedural reforms.

title: Analysis and Prediction of Influencing Factors of Power Carbon Emissions in Guizhou Province Based on LMDI Decomposition and Multi-NAR Integrated Nonlinear Regression
authors: Chen Luo, Julong Chen, Xuepeng Mou, Bin Wang, Shiping Yang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Understanding the drivers of power carbon emissions is essential for improving emission forecasting and supporting low-carbon energy planning. Taking Guizhou Province as a case study, this study analyzes and predicts power sector carbon emissions during 2007–2022. The logarithmic mean Divisia index method is applied to decompose emission changes into multiple driving effects. The results indicate that urban per capita GDP, electricity intensity, and population scale are the dominant drivers. The GDP effect accounts for about 59% of the emission growth, while improvements in electricity intensity offset nearly 47% of the increase. Based on these factors, NAR, NARX, and FNN models are employed for forecasting analysis. Rolling test results show prediction errors ranging from 12.89% to 15.31% MAPE, providing a reliable evaluation of carbon emission trends in Guizhou.

title: Innovative, effective and cost saving strategies for post implant ashing wet clean and post poly etch wet clean
authors: Janice Koh, Zhenjie Zhu, Sicong Chen, Tek Sing Lim, Christopher Lim
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: This paper presents the organic residue removal performance using low-concentration O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> water in place of Sulfuric Peroxide Mixture (SPM) in post implant and poly etch wet clean. The replacement demonstrated better performance in Yield and Defect Density (YDD), Electrical Test (ET) and sort. With this replacement, we can save approximately 55 % of our chemical cost.

title: An Ensemble Deep Learning Framework for Precise Diagnosis of Lung Diseases Using Medical Imaging
authors: Nasibeh Termeh Mousavi, Azamossadat Nourbakhsh, Asadollah Shahbahrami
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Due to the rising burden of respiratory pathologies and the critical necessity for swift, precise diagnostics, especially amidst crises like the COVID-19 pandemic, this research introduces a hybrid deep learning architecture rooted in ensemble techniques for detecting lung disorders via CT and chest X-ray imagery. The methodology incorporates a bilateral filter to suppress noise without compromising edge integrity. Subsequently, Mask R-CNN is deployed to segment potential regions of interest (ROI), followed by feature extraction utilizing the VGG16 backbone. Classification is handled by an optimized ensemble of CNN classifiers with fine-tuned hyperparameters. Empirical outcomes reveal a marked enhancement, attaining 98% accuracy and sensitivity, thereby surpassing conventional baseline models in diagnosing COVID-19, pneumonia, and lung cancer. By merging sophisticated image processing with deep ensemble learning, this framework exhibits significant promise for clinical deployment.

title: VPETD: A Generative AI Driven Novel Privacy Themed Cybersecurity Educational Game
authors: Abhishek Shah, Ankur Chattopadhyay, Levi Pfantz
abstract: This paper introduces a first of its kind generative artificial intelligence (AI) driven visual privacy themed educational game (VPETD), which is an enhanced version of the Visual Privacy Enhancing Technology (VPET) themed game. The VPETD game was designed to teach data privacy plus security concepts in an interactive and hands-on learning mode. Building upon the original VPET game, VPETD makes innovative use of generative AI in the form of Stable Diffusion, which is a text-to-image generator model, to create diverse character sets. VPETD introduces a novel Doppelganger feature, that enables players to select lookalike disguises using a slider-based interface. This Doppelganger functionality helps improve the game engagement and experience with a redesigned user interface and dynamically variant image sets. VPETD employs computer vision-based image processing techniques (via OpenCV) and multimedia synthesis to support de-identification driven image obfuscation tasks and to illustrate privacy through visual anonymity as an essence of how VPET works. To assess the potential value and efficacy of VPETD, we performed a user assessment study with a diverse set of participants, including K-12 learners plus college-level students, as well as educators, who played the game in our cybersecurity training camps and workshops. This paper shares and analyzes the preliminary data collected from these participants in the form of survey responses, as part of this conducted research study. Our study demonstrates the prospects of VPETD, increased participant interest plus awareness levels in privacy and security, plus a high recommendation rate for our game. In summary, our findings show that VPETD raises overall awareness plus understanding levels and drives meaningful engagement, thus highlighting its utility as a generative AI-powered educational game in an experiential learning setup.

title: Stability Analysis of Control Systems with First-order Reset Elements, a Frequency-Domain Approach
authors: Ali Hosseini, Hassan HosseinNia
abstract: Reset control systems (RCSs) can achieve performance beyond that of conventional linear time-invariant (LTI) controllers, while also allowing analysis directly in the frequency domain using measured frequency response functions (FRFs). Despite this potential, existing frequency-domain stability approaches are typically restricted to specific RCS architectures and commonly depend on parametric plant models, which limits their applicability in practice. In this paper, a generalized Hβ framework is developed for the most comprehensive class of RCS structures, incorporating pre-, post-, and parallel LTI filters, as well as nonzero after-reset values. Based on this formulation, an FRF-based representation corresponding to the Hβ transfer function is derived, and frequency-domain sufficient conditions are established to certify the Hβ-based quadratic stability criterion. As a result, the proposed framework enables direct FRF-based assessment of quadratic stability and convergence for the considered class of reset control systems, using the measured plant FRF together with the known controller and filter transfer functions, without requiring an explicit parametric plant model. The effectiveness and practical relevance of the method are demonstrated through an illustrative industrial case study.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning Framework for Real-Time Traffic Risk Prediction and Safety Enforcement
authors: Md. Owafeeuzzaman Patwary, Apurbo Biswas, Tonny Shekha Kar, Firoz Ahmed
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The increasing frequency of road accidents and traffic violations highlights the need for proactive, real-time risk detection systems. This paper presents a hybrid deep learning framework for real-time traffic risk prediction and safety enforcement using traffic surveillance footage. The proposed system integrates YOLOv8 for vehicle detection, DeepSORT for multi-object tracking, Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks for temporal behavior modeling, and XGBoost for final risk classification. Driving behavior features such as speed variation, braking patterns, and lane changes are extracted as time-series representations and fused with contextual factors including simulated weather conditions and driver fatigue. The lightweight YOLOv8-nano backbone enables efficient real-time deployment, while multimodal feature fusion enhances robustness. Experiments conducted on the UA-DETRAC dataset demonstrate strong detection performance with an mAP@0.5 of 85.3%. The risk prediction module achieves 92.4% classification accuracy and an AUC-ROC of 0.961, indicating reliable discrimination between low- and high-risk driving behaviors. Ablation studies further confirm the importance of temporal modeling and contextual feature integration. The proposed framework provides an effective and scalable solution for intelligent transportation systems, contributing to proactive traffic monitoring and enhanced urban road safety.

title: Enable Scalable and Secure ISAC-WPT in Wireless Scenarios
authors: Xiufeng Liu, Xiulong Liu, Xin Xie, Ke Wang, Jiuwu Zhang, Xinyu Tong, Zheng Gong, Keqiu Li
publish: IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Communications
abstract: Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) and Wireless Power Transfer (WPT) technologies enable the transmission of wireless waves to power battery-free user devices while simultaneously supporting their communication and environmental sensing functions. However, as the number of users increases in such scenarios, existing ISAC-WPT schemes face challenges of overload interference and security vulnerabilities. To this end, this paper proposes a Scalable and Secure ISAC-WPT (S²SCPT) scheme. Specifically, the uplink base station integrates OFDMA and NOMA technologies, utilizing a three-dimensional resource optimization strategy to suppress multi-user interference, which balances communication and sensing performance while supplying power to energy harvesting receivers (EHR). For security, a dynamic subcarrier hopping and directional power modulation scheme based on OFDMA is developed to enhance Physical-layer Key Generation (PKG); downlink users utilize cyclic codes and an ACK verification mechanism to locate subcarriers and protect WPT security. Experimental results indicate that, in multi-user scenarios, the proposed method increases the communication rate by up to 26% while maintaining sensing performance; during WPT, it reduces power consumption by 11% without compromising communication rates, and enhanced PKG demonstrates significant advantages in the system’s sum secret key rate.

title: Quadrature-Spoof Surface Plasmon Polariton Unit Cell With Circuit Characterization for Circular-Polarization Generation
authors: Fang Hu, Qiao Cheng, Lehu Wen, Wei Hu, Qi Luo, Yuhui Ren, Steven Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: A quadrature-spoof surface plasmon polariton (SSPP) unit cell (QS-UC) is proposed and theoretically characterized for circularly polarized (CP) wave generation. The proposed QS-UC is composed of a fishbone SSPP waveguide (FSW) and a spine quadrature mode converter (SQMC), which converts the SSPP mode into a quadrature radiating mode. An equivalent circuit is established to derive the cascaded ABCD matrix and the conditions for quadrature CP generation. Scattering parameters and dispersion characteristics are extracted from the established circuit model. The generated CP far-field wave is calculated through the quadrature-mode current distribution on the SQMC. The theoretically calculated results are also compared to the EM-simulated results, with good agreement. Based on the proposed QS-UC, a quadrature SSPP linear array (QS-LA) is developed, fabricated, and measured for the verification of CP generation. Detailed measured results show that the developed QS-LA has a wide impedance bandwidth of 5.28–11 GHz (70.3%). More importantly, the developed QS-LA shows a wide CP bandwidth covering 5.8–10 GHz (53.2%), with CP beams covering (−65°, 16°) for right-hand CP(RHCP) generation at port 1 and symmetrical range of (−16°, 65°) in left-hand CP(LHCP) generation at port two. These excellent performances verify the effectiveness of the proposed QS-UC in CP generation.

title: Demonstration of E-Mode GaN Monolithic Bidirectional Switch Based on Gate Oxygen Plasma Treatment with Blocking Voltage over 2.9 kV
authors: Zifeng Qu, Jiachen Duan, Ang Li, Xuanming Zhang, Yunsong Xu, Hao Tian, Yizhou Jiang, Guohao Yu, Chao Long, Wen Liu, Huiqing Wen
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: This study presents an enhancement-mode (E-mode) GaN monolithic bidirectional switch (MBDS) that achieves a blocking voltage exceeding 2.9 kV at both terminals through the application of post-annealing-free in-situ gate oxygen plasma treatment (GOPT). The approach significantly enhances the gate reliability, reduces gate leakage current, and decreases overall device leakage in the off-state, enabling a high blocking voltage without field-plates design. The GOPT GaN MBDS demonstrates symmetric output characteristics in both directions, with a threshold voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">TH</inf>) of 1.1 V and a specific on-resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON,SP</inf>) of 9.6 mΩcm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, which surpasses the performance limit of a bidirectional switch (BDS) consisting of two discrete unidirectional devices. The device exhibits a competitive trade-off between R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON,SP</inf> and BV, providing a promising solution for high-voltage power electronics applications.

title: AI-Driven Therapeutic Robot Incorporating Amber-Based Contact Interfaces for Personalized Health Intervention
authors: Yufei Zhang, Hema Zulaika Hashim, Weijing Hu
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The use of artificial intelligence in therapeutic robotics has created new opportunities in the provision of personalized health interventions. In this paper, the author proposes an AI-powered therapeutic robot which uses Deep QNetworks (DQN), a method of reinforcement learning (RL), to optimize patient-centered therapy dynamically by using amberbased contact interfaces. DQN framework allows the robot to continually learn best intervention plans by monitoring the responses of the patient in real time and modifying interventions like the level, length, and nature of the therapy according to the patient. Surface contact made of amber is bio-interactive and increases the sense of touch, facilitating natural interaction between humans and robots, which is essential to promote patient comfort, trust, and compliance. A cognitive model is also used to determine the behavior of the patient and this can be used to advise the DQN agent to take actions that will deliver maximum therapeutic benefits to the patient during the session. Both simulated experimental and real-world assessments indicate that the proposed system can be much more effective in-patient engagement, intervention accuracy, and the effectiveness of the therapeutic process in general than the nonadaptive robotic system used orally.

title: Real-Time Vehicle License Plate Recognition Using YOLOv9 and OCR Framework
authors: Md. Sadi Iftia Khairul, A F M Zainul Abadin, Mohammad Kamrul Hasan, Sohag Sarker, Liton Chandra Paul, A K M Ahasan Habib
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Vehicle License Plate Recognition (VLPR) is a fundamental component of intelligent transportation systems, enabling automated vehicle identification for traffic monitoring, law enforcement, and smart city applications. In densely populated urban environments, license plate recognition remains challenging due to occlusion, motion blur, varying illumination, diverse plate layouts, and multilingual character usage. In the context of Bangladesh, vehicle license plates commonly contain both Bangla and English characters, further increasing recognition complexity. This paper presents a realtime vehicle license plate detection and recognition framework based on a deep learning driven object detection model integrated with an optical character recognition (OCR) pipeline. A YOLOv9-based detector is employed to accurately localize license plates from video streams, followed by region-of-interest extraction and multilingual text recognition with postprocessing validation. The proposed system is evaluated on a custom dataset captured under diverse urban traffic conditions, including dense traffic flow, variable lighting, and partial occlusion. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves a detection Precision-Recall (F1-score) 0.99 while the OCR module attains an overall character recognition accuracy above 99.97%, maintaining real-time performance at more than 35 frames per second. These results indicate that the proposed framework is robust, efficient, and suitable for practical deployment in real-world traffic surveillance and intelligent transportation systems.

title: Bridge-Selective Saturation-Guided Topology Optimization within a Hierarchical Framework for IPMSM Rotors
authors: Ziran Cheng, Jian Gao, Yufeng Zhao, Zhixiang He, Kun Liu, Shoudao Huang
abstract: This paper proposes a novel bridge-selective saturation-guided topology optimization (TO) method for interior permanent magnet synchronous motor (IPMSM) rotors within a hierarchical optimization framework. First, the outer-loop parameter optimization provides a baseline rotor configuration, and the nonlinear magnetic behavior of the rotor core is incorporated into the TO framework through a saturation-consistent material interpolation model. Then, the effects of element variation on the saturation states of both the main flux path and the flux-barrier bridge region are simultaneously considered. Next, a region-dependent saturation indicator is embedded into the sensitivity analysis and design updating scheme, such that excessive saturation in the main flux path is suppressed while a higher saturation level in the flux-barrier bridge region is promoted to increase local magnetic reluctance and reduce leakage flux. Finally, a 3-kW IPMSM prototype is built to validate the proposed method, demonstrating its effectiveness and accuracy.

title: GNN-Enhanced Transfer Learning for Joint Power and Spectrum Allocation in Underlay D2D Communications
authors: Linfeng Chen, Yijun Wang
abstract: Device-to-Device (D2D) underlay communications can significantly improve spectrum efficiency, but joint power and spectrum allocation under imperfect Channel State Information (CSI) remains challenging due to complex non-Euclidean interference relationships. This paper proposes a Graph Neural Network (GNN)-enhanced transfer learning framework for joint resource allocation in underlay D2D systems. The framework first employs a transfer learning-based DNN to migrate knowledge from perfect CSI to imperfect CSI scenarios, greatly reducing training data requirements. A novel GNN-based interference graph module is then introduced, which dynamically constructs interference graphs from CSI and user locations. Multi-layer graph convolutions and a lightweight edge-attention mechanism extract high-level spatial embeddings, which are fused with raw features and fed into the transfer learning DNN for end-to-end optimization. Simulations show that the proposed scheme achieves up to 18.4% higher sum-rate, 17.5% improved energy efficiency, and 33.5% lower outage probability compared to the transfer learning baseline, with only 15% additional training overhead, while approaching 93% of CVX optimal performance. Results validate the effectiveness of graph-based modeling for practical D2D resource allocation under imperfect CSI.

title: Enhancing Code Generation Using Instruction-Tuned Transformer Models for Multi-Language Software Development
authors: A. Sathiyavathi, C. Gethara Gowri, R. Sundar, J. Arockia Theo, L. Poornimadevi, Suberiya Begum S
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The rapid growth of multi-language software development has increased the demand for intelligent systems capable of generating accurate and reliable source code from natural language instructions. Transformer-based models have shown significant potential in automated code generation; however, their effectiveness is often limited by inadequate alignment with user intent and challenges in cross-language generalization. This research investigates the enhancement of code generation using instruction-tuned transformer models to improve adaptability across multiple programming languages. A structured experimental methodology is adopted, incorporating a pretrained transformer baseline, instruction tuning, and systematic evaluation supported by a built-in benchmark dataset. Exploratory data analysis and visualization techniques are employed to understand data characteristics and support reliable interpretation of results. The findings demonstrate that instruction tuning improves instruction adherence, code consistency, and generalization capability in multi-language scenarios. The proposed framework provides a reproducible and scalable foundation for advancing automated code generation systems in modern software development environments.

title: A Transformer-Based Sequential Modeling Network for Multimodal Stroke Diagnosis with Enhanced Explainable AI
authors: Md. Shahariar Hasan Sakib, Minhajul Alam Tuta, Noushin Jahan Ahmad, Ismatul Ferdush Liva, Ashikul Islam, Amimul A Hasan Rofik, Faisal Ahmmed, S M Mahim, Md Emamul Hossen
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Brain stroke is a medical disorder where an interruption in blood flow to the brain causes brain cells to die. Progression can lead to significant neurological impairments, including loss of cognitive abilities. Accurate early diagnosis of stroke is key to ensuring appropriate clinical intervention. Artificial intelligence (AI) has shown immense potential in surpassing these challenges, particularly through deep learning models that automate the diagnosis process. The proposed architecture in this study fuses a Vision Transformer(ViT) and Gated Recurrent Unit(GRU) to detect Stroke using both Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) and Computed Tomography (CT) imaging modalities. ViT encodes spatial features from images, and GRU captures dependencies in the sequence of embeddings. Our model tackles the problem of class imbalance faced by both datasets of MRI and CT images and provides better accuracies compared to state-of-the-art methods. achieves superior accuracy and performance compared to existing methods. The model was trained on a Brain stroke MRI and CT image dataset acquired from kaggle, attaining impressive accuracies of 99.25% for MRI image dataset and 98.08% for CT scan image dataset, each comprising three classes. Furthermore, Grad-CAM, an XAI method, was employed to improve interpretability and provide clinicians with clearer insights into its decision-making.

title: MangoNet-SA: a Lightweight MobileNetV2-Based Spatial Attention CNN for Robust Mango Leaf Disease Classification
authors: Md. Samiul Alom, Abrar Rakin, Ferdaus Anam Jibon, Nazmul Hasan Shouvo, A. H. M. Kamal, Md Istiak Ahamed
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Mango leaves often show small marks and soft texture changes that are hard to detect in real orchard light. Most lightweight CNN models miss these details, and this limits their use in field conditions. Early and reliable detection can help farmers react on time and reduce the loss caused by common leaf diseases. This study presents MangoNet-SA, a lightweight CNN built on MobileNetV2 with a short spatial attention step. The model highlights the parts of the leaf that carry disease signals and keeps the rest of the network simple. MangoNet-SA reached 92.04 % accuracy in the test set and maintained a strong balance between precision, recall, and F1-score. The spatial attention step improved fine-grain features, and the model stayed ahead of MobileNetV2, MobileNetV3-Large, EfficientNet-B0 and B1. The ROC and PR curves also showed a clear separation in all five classes of diseases. The model held well even when the images varied in light, angle, and camera quality. The results suggest that lightweight models can still perform well when guided to the right regions. MangoNet-SA is easy to deploy and runs smoothly on common hardware, making it suitable for orchard-level use. The approach can support farmers and help build practical disease monitoring tools without large computing needs.

title: Performance Evaluation of Advanced Ensemble Methods for Flood Risk Prediction Using Multi-Feature Analysis
authors: Sathiyamoorthy M, Subramanian P
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Flood disasters pose significant threats to communities worldwide, causing extensive damage to infrastructure, agriculture, and human lives. Early and accurate flood risk prediction is crucial for effective disaster management and mitigation strategies. This research presents Flood Guard, a novel ensemble machine learning framework that leverages environmental and geographical features to classify flood risk with unprecedented accuracy. We evaluated thirteen state-of-the-art machine learning algorithms including Bagging Classifier, AdaBoost, Decision Tree, Support Vector Machine, Naive Bayes, Gradient Boosting, Stacking Classifier, and Voting Classifier on a comprehensive dataset containing critical environmental parameters such as humidity, river discharge, temperature, water level, population density, and geographical coordinates. Our proposed Bagging Classifier achieved the highest performance with 82.5 % accuracy, 52.47 % precision, 52.5 % recall, and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex>-score of 52.34 %, demonstrating superior classification capability compared to conventional approaches. The comprehensive evaluation includes precision-recall trade-offs, cross-validation performance, training time analysis, ROC curve comparisons, and feature importance rankings, revealing that humidity (14 %), river discharge (13 %), and temperature (12 %) are the most influential predictors. This research contributes a robust, scalable, and interpretable machine learning solution for flood risk assessment, enabling proactive disaster preparedness and resource allocation.

title: Wristnet: A Novel Deep Learning Approach for Detecting Fractures in Wrist X-Ray Images
authors: MD. Mostofa Hasib, Ahmed Farhan Amin, Nokibul Arfin Siam, Alif Hossain Talha, Noboranjan Dey, Mahfujur Rahman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Wrist fractures are some of the most frequent injuries in emergency medicine, and correct interpretation is demanded for clinical management. Recent advances in deep learning offer promising solutions. However, their performance is typically limited by the lack of medical data and is architecture type-sensitive. In this work, we propose WristNet, a novel deep learning approach that fuses DenseNet169 and InceptionV3 through feature-level concatenation for classifying wrist fractures. The proposed approach leverages DenseNet's ability to preserve fine structural details and InceptionV3's multiscale feature extraction to construct a rich and discriminative representation. It was evaluated on an extended version of a publicly available wrist X-ray dataset with data augmentation. It is compared with five state-of-the-art convolutional neural networks, showing a higher accuracy of 99.63%. In addition, the study shows that data augmentation does not help all architectures equally, emphasizing the need for architectureaware training strategies. Our approach exhibits strong potential to be incorporated into a computer-aided diagnostic system for ensuring the detection of wrist fractures.

title: Multi-Static Integrated Sensing, Identification and Backscatter Communication for IoT Networks
authors: Songmin Li, Ying-Chang Liang
abstract: In this paper, a multi-static integrated sensing, identification and backscatter communication (ISIBC) system is proposed for Internet-of-Things (IoT) networks, where the backscatter devices (BDs) attached to the targets utilize ambient radio frequency (RF) signals for data transmission and multiple receivers are used for target localization. Each receiver estimates the directions of arrival (DoAs) of the incident signals from the RF source, the targets with BDs and the environmental scatterers, detects the activities of each BD, and demodulates the symbols transmitted by each active BD. With the DoA estimation and activity detection results, the receivers are required to identify the targets, i.e., associating the estimated DoAs with the corresponding targets attached with active BDs. These tasks, however, are challenging as the ambient RF signal is unknown. To tackle this issue, we propose a semi-blind generalized likelihood ratio test (GLRT) detector to estimate the activities of the BDs, which tests the homogeneity of the received signal samples over different BD symbol periods without requiring any information about the ambient RF signal and backscattering channels. A constant false alarm rate (CFAR) threshold is derived for the proposed GLRT detector. Further, we propose a novel semi-blind eigenvector-based identification method to identify the targets without using knowledge of the ambient RF signal. The eigenvector-based demodulator is proposed to demodulate the symbols transmitted by active BDs. Finally, the estimation and identification results from each receiver are utilized to jointly localize the active BDs. Extensive simulation results are presented to show the effectiveness of the proposed methods for the studied ISIBC system.

title: An Improved Graphical Method for Layout Optimization of Field-Limiting Ring Edge Termination
authors: Kiyoshi Takeuchi, Munetoshi Fukui, Takuya Saraya, Kazuo Itou, Toshihiko Takakura, Shinichi Suzuki, Hiroyuki Takase, Toshiro Hiramoto
abstract: An improved graphical method for optimizing the layout of field-limiting rings (FLRs) for edge termination is proposed. The required number of rings and the optimal combination of spacings between adjacent rings can be efficiently determined through a simple procedure based on TCAD simulations of three-terminal model structures. In a 1.2kV-class Si diode design example, the proposed method achieved 97% of the target breakdown voltage and 92% of the ideal parallel-plate breakdown voltage using 24 rings. Acomparison with a previously proposed method and numerical optimization results is also provided to further validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: Behavioral Generative Augmentation for Network Intrusion Detection Under Extreme Data Scarcity
authors: Sazidul Islam Hira, Samiur Rahman Wasi, Md. Tasin Islam, Md. Saiful Chowdhury, Abdullahil Kafi
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The challenge of learning trustworthy classifiers in the extreme cases of label scarcity has been a core issue of contemporary machine learning systems. New attacks in network intrusion detection can be represented by a small number of labeled examples, leading standard classifiers to have high variance, unsteady decision boundaries, and poor detection performance. This paper explores how such failures can be systematically countered in the ultra-low-sample regime by generative modeling. Our data-centric architecture is based on the use of a multiclass Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) to supplement sparse behavioral traffic data, without identity-based leakage of features. The model is a time- and flow-based model, where attack dynamics can be learned in a port-agnostic manner. In order to measure robustness, we run controlled experiments with different levels of scarcity (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n \in\{10, \ldots, 200\}$</tex>) and classifier families (linear, kernel-based, and tree-based) and 10 random seeds. Empirical evidence indicates that GAN-augmented training is effectively a stability anchor, which improves macro-averaged F1-scores by up to 23 % at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n=10$</tex> and significantly lowers the performance variance compared to classical oversampling techniques and real-data baselines. The statistical significance is verified using confidence intervals and non-parametric tests. Subsequent discussion also shows that the suggested framework can help achieve consistent detection of a synthetically generated and never-seen-before type of attack, which is a sign of minimal zero-shot generalization in the learned behavioral space. On the whole, this paper suggests that generative augmentation could be a principled stabilizing mechanism to be used to learn in highly-data-sparse settings, and it may serve as a foundation to be used in data-centric and robustness-oriented machine learning studies.

title: Contextual Dependency Learning Transformer Based Natural Language Understanding for Conversational Agents
authors: Tejeswar Reddy Velpucharla, Maulik Bhatt, Meet Patel, Chitra A, Gadegouni Pranusha
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: In recent years, Natural Language Understanding (NLU) for conversational agents has been essential for applications within the fields of virtual assistants, task-oriented dialogue systems and interaction. Existing NLU model was used for intent classification and entity extraction as independent tasks in error propagation and inconsistency in the semantic interpretation of certain utterances across multiple conversation types. To address these limitations, this research proposes a Contextual Dependency Learning Transformer for Conversational NLU (CDLT-NLU) framework, which jointly models intent prediction and entity extraction in an end-to-end manner. Initially, the framework is evaluated using NLUbenchmark dataset, which contains 25,716 annotated utterances form 64 intent and 54 entity categories across diverse serviceoriented domains. CDLT-NLU preserves the context of the original text by performing context-preserving textnormalization and subword tokenization with entity span alignment are applied to ensure semantic integrity and transformer compatibility. A context-aware transformer encoder is then employed to capture both inter-utterance conversational dependencies and intra-utterance semantics. By using shared contextual representations, joint intent-entity prediction is performed while a dependency-consistency regularization mechanism implements semantic compatibility between predicted intents and extracted entities during training. Experimental results demonstrates that the proposed CDLTNLU framework achieved Exact Match Accuracy (EMA) of 0.943 when compared with existing NLU model.

title: CAFC: A Convolutional Neural Approach for Sentiment Recognition in Speech Signals
authors: Mahmudul Haque Shakir, Sunipun Seemanta, Md. Saef Ullah Miah, Omar Faruk, Rifat Tasnim Anannya
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Sentiment analysis using speech is an emerging field that leverages vocal cues such as pitch, tone, and rhythm to capture human emotions beyond textual content. This paper shows audio-based sentiment classification using Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs) and compares the performance of traditional machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) models introduced in this study. A novel Convolutional Acoustic-Feature Classifier (CAFC) is proposed to address the limitations of existing approaches which is based on a 1D Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architecture optimized for sequential acoustic features. The proposed CAFC model can automatically capture temporal and spectral dependence relationships in speech, and it is more efficient and accurate compared with current supervised methods in audio sentiment analysis. The CAFC model was validated using 5-fold cross-validation which demonstrated that CAFC not only consistently outperforms all baseline models but also achieving an accuracy of 96%, with superior precision, recall, and F1-score. For sentiment recognition in speech the findings indicated the effectiveness of CNN-based architectures and underscore the importance of leveraging acoustic features for robust human–computer interaction systems.

title: Attention-Enhanced Hybrid CNN-ViT Framework for Traffic Sign and Light Detection in Autonomous Driving
authors: Sree Suvro Kumar Biswas, Sunayana Ghosh
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Accurate traffic light and sign recognition in a variety of environmental conditions is necessary for autonomous driving systems. In order to capture both local and global contextual features, we present in this work an Attention-Enhanced Hybrid CNN-ViT framework that combines Vision Transformers (ViT) with ResNet-50 and EfficientNet-B0 backbones. We use Convolutional Block Attention Modules (CBAM) and comprehensive data augmentation techniques to enhance resilience in difficult scenarios like Highway, Night, Rainy, and Urban. The recommended approach outperforms the traditional CNN and ViT baselines with an average classification accuracy of 98.78 %, according to experiments conducted on the aiMotive 3D Traffic Light and Sign Dataset. According to these findings, hybrid CNN-ViT architectures with attention mechanisms can safely identify and categorize signs and traffic lights in intricate settings, promoting safer autonomous driving.

title: Performance Trade-Offs in HEMTs Due to Differences in Gate Length and Gate-to-Drain Spacing
authors: Hrithik Mohanto Himel, Md. Rafiqul Islam, Arefin Ahamed Shuvo, Partha Jyoti Roy, Durjoy Sarkar Dhrubo
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: A theoretical analysis is carried out on an AlGaN/GaN high electron mobility transistor (HEMT) in order to enhance its breakdown voltage (BV) and current characteristics. These features need to be improved to enable devices to run faster. Silvaco TCAD software was used to analyze the trade-offs between gate length and gate-drain spacing for improved performance. Increasing gate length <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mathbf{L}_{\mathbf{g}}\right)$</tex> decreases maximum drain current (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{I}_{\mathbf{d} \boldsymbol{(} \mathbf{m a x} \boldsymbol{)}}$</tex>) and increases gate-todrain spacing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mathrm{L}_{\text{gd}}\right)$</tex> up to the ideal limit, resulting in a breakdown voltage of 250 V to 350 V. The electric field under the gate grows as the gate-source spacing (Lgs) decreases, increasing drain current and transconductance. Balancing high current, threshold stability, and breakdown voltage requires adequate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{L}_{g}$</tex> (about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.4-3 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>), minimizing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{L}_{g s}$</tex>, and using a sufficiently large <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{L}_{g d}$</tex> (several microns). Designing reliable and high-performing devices requires striking the right balance between performance and short-channel effects.

title: Joint Secure Localization and Attack Detection for Vehicular Networks With Quantized Measurements: A Variational Bayesian Approach
authors: Yunfei Li, Zhengyao Zhang, Yiting Luo, Weiqiang Tan, Zheng Shi, Shaodan Ma
abstract: This letter addresses the critical challenge of secure and reliable localization in vehicular networks, where stringent bandwidth constraints and malicious attacks coexist. We propose the Quantized Variational Bayesian Learning-based Attack Detection and Secure Localization (QVBL-ADSL) algorithm, a novel method that jointly estimates the position of a target vehicle and identifies potential attacks using quantized measurements. By formulating the problem within a principled variational Bayesian framework, our approach efficiently approximates the complex posterior distribution, simultaneously handling the nonlinearities introduced by quantization and the distortions caused by adversarial interventions, while also accounting for node position uncertainty. The algorithm is computationally tractable and guaranteed to converge, making it suitable for real-time vehicular applications. Numerical simulations demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior performance in both localization accuracy and attack detection rate compared to state-of-the-art techniques, demonstrating particular efficacy in resource-limited and adversarial settings.

title: Optimizing XGBoost for Predicting Student Academic Success: A Unified Approach to Feature Engineering and Model Evaluation
authors: Fatima Amir, Nawar Anzara, Jahanara Suchi, Shadia Sajneen, T. M. Shahriar Sazzad
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Accurate prediction of student academic performance is essential for early intervention, personalized learning, and enhancing educational strategies. This study explores the use of the XGBoost algorithm for predicting student performance based on behavioral and academic data. While prior studies have demonstrated XGBoost's efficiency, they often focus on isolated optimization techniques-such as specific feature selection methods or hyperparameter tuningtested on disparate datasets. Consequently, there is a lack of a unified comparative framework to evaluate these diverse methodological strategies on a common baseline. This paper addresses this gap by conceptually replicating and adapting six distinct state-of-the-art XGBoost approaches found in literature for a regression-focused analysis on a single student performance dataset. Furthermore, we propose an enhanced modeling pipeline that integrates target distribution balancing via discretization for regression, Optuna-based hyperparameter optimization, and threshold-based feature selection. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed unified approach outperforms the adapted baseline methods, achieving an Adjusted R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score of 0.9685 and the lowest RMSE (3.6764).

title: Deep Hybrid Neuro-Ensemble Network for Intelligent Supply Chain Resilience and Disruption Forecasting
authors: Omar Fareed Shaqqour, S Girija Lakshmi, R. Vettriselvan, D Vidyanadha Babu, Sorabh Lakhanpal, Srilakshmi Aluri
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Contemporary supply chains are operating in extremely dynamic, fragile, and globally connected systems, making them more susceptible to geopolitical uncertainties, transportation delays, manpower shortages, cyber-attacks, and climate-related occurrences. The non-linear, spatio-temporal and interdependent nature of such disruptions cannot be captured by conventional forecasting methods which involve simple statistical models or shallow learning models. To address these weaknesses, this paper presents Deep Hybrid NeuroEnsemble Network (DHNEN) as the means to smartly predict the disruption of the supply chain and calculate its resilience. In the proposed architecture, CNNLSTM encoder-based module is proposed, which is used to extract the spatio-temporal patterns, GRU-based attention mechanisms, which are used to prioritize the disruption-relevant signals, and Transformer encoder, which is used to capture global dependencies over time. Also, an embedding graph layer is used to capture topological relations between supply chain nodes, which can be used to model systemic risk propagation. The model was tested at ten disruption categories and at different geographic locations. Examination shows that the proposed framework attains classification accuracy of 97.6, F1-score of 96.9 and an AUROC of 98.4, which is superior to current Bi-LSTM, GAT-LSTM, and Transformer-based baselines. The model supports also an accuracy of over 94.6% of 7-day-ahead predictions with the resilience scores indicating robust agreement with the real world findings (MAE <0.03). The findings reveal that DHNEN is effective in proactive and real-time supply chain risk management.

title: Privacy-Preserving, Scalable EV Charging Optimization
authors: P. Muthukumar, M. V. Ramesh, Valantina Stephen, T Baldwin Immanuel, K. Sasikala, M. Rajavelan
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The growing penetration of electric vehicles (EVs) leads to non-uniform and time-dependent charging demand at the distribution level, which complicates feeder loading and voltage regulation. To manage this variability, a federated learning-based coordination architecture is developed in which EV charging stations deployed at different network locations train LSTM-based load forecasting models and reinforcement learning (RL) charging controllers using locally measured power and usage data. Model training is performed without transferring individual charging records, relying instead on aggregated parameter updates. Secure aggregation and differential privacy mechanisms are incorporated to bound information leakage, while decentralized policy optimization is adopted to avoid centralized control dependence. Communication and update synchronization are designed with explicit bandwidth and latency constraints to ensure feasibility under operational charging-network conditions. The proposed architecture is evaluated using MATLAB simulations that represent stochastic EV arrival behavior, bounded charging time flexibility, and time-varying utility tariff structures. Simulation outcomes show lower forecasting error, reduced training effort for RL policy convergence, observable feeder-level peak load reduction, and improved privacy protection compared with centralized and non-federated learning approaches.

title: A Multi-Scale Approach to ECG Arrhythmia Classification Using Transformer Encoders
authors: Saidul Alam Sakib, Asifur Rahaman, Naimul Abedin Rafsan, Md. Abdul Aziz
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Electrocardiogram (ECG) arrhythmia classification is a critical task for early detection of cardiac abnormalities, yet remains challenging due to signal variability, class imbalance, and the rarity of certain arrhythmia types. This paper proposes a novel Transformer-based architecture with multi-scale feature extraction for accurate ECG beat classification. Our approach leverages multi-scale convolutional kernels to capture temporal features at different resolutions before applying a Transformer encoder with attention pooling. We evaluate the model on the MIT-BIH Arrhythmia Database containing 33,435 beats across five AAMI-defined classes. To address severe class imbalance (normal beats comprise 65 % of data), we implement a weighted loss function and data augmentation pipeline including Gaussian noise, time warping, and amplitude scaling. The proposed model achieves 93.2 % accuracy, 91.4 % weighted F1-score, and 0.95 ROC-AUC, outperforming standalone CNN and traditional Transformer baselines. Ablation studies demonstrate that multiscale feature extraction contributes 2.3% accuracy improvement, while attention pooling provides 1.8 % gain over global average pooling. Model interpretability through attention weight analysis reveals alignment with clinically relevant ECG segments. This work establishes a robust framework for arrhythmia detection that balances state-of-the-art performance with clinical applicability.

title: Research on Customer Service Abnormal Behaviour Identification and Early Warning Mechanism Based on Power Big Data
authors: Li Zhang, Longzhu Zhu, Meicui Qin, Jingjing Ran, Jun Fu
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The growing digitalisation of the electricity sector has increased the relevance of the current research in understanding customer service interactions to identify the abnormal behavioural patterns that can be a signal of emerging service problems. The paper seeks to explore how customer complaint texts, with the inclusion of power-sector big data, can be utilised in the detection of abnormal behaviour and the creation of an efficient early warning system. To relate to 5,500 customer complaints, a quantitative research approach has been chosen, which involves processing of text in the form of structured text, feature engineering and multi-stage classification. The outcomes affirm a high predictive agent, and Support Vector Machine has an accuracy of 91% and BERT-enhanced classification has an accuracy of 93%, assisted by visual results. To summarise, the paper concludes that the patterns in the lingo of customer complaints may be regarded as the credible indicative variables in behavioural monitoring; the paper suggests that the system of behaviour monitoring should be incorporated into the standard utility service management to facilitate the effective initiation of the operations. The findings imply that an electrical provider can employ this framework to enhance service responsiveness and avoid sudden growth of service failure, but the study is constrained by the fact that textual complaints were used, and there was no integration of real-time operational records, like outage or smart-meter records.

title: Impact of Physical Layer Configuration on the Performance of IEEE 802.11a Infrastructure-Based Wireless Sensor Network in NS-3
authors: Md. Galib Hasan, Anik Debnath, Md. Jobayer Hossain, Md. Jarez Miah
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: IEEE 802.11a standard has the extreme potential to enhance the performance of infrastructure-based Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs). High-bandwidth and reliable data transmission can be achieved in these networks through the use of 5 GHz frequency band and Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing (OFDM). However, the performance of infrastructure-based WSNs is significantly influenced by different physical layer parameters. To manage this issue, it is necessary to analyze the effects of these parameters on the network's overall efficiency and reliability. In this paper, we design an NS-3 script for an infrastructure-based WLAN systematically organized according to the hierarchical structure of the TCP/IP model. We configure all layers using NS-3 model objects and set and modify the values used by objects to look into the effects of physical parameters (packet size, data rate, and OFDM data and control mode) on packet delivery ratio, packet loss ratio, aggregated throughput and average delay. The simulation results show that smaller packet sizes generally provide a higher packet delivery ratio and lower the packet loss ratio by minimizing susceptibility to interference. Larger packets and higher data rates improve aggregated throughput at the cost of increased average delay. Our design procedure serves as a valuable guideline for new NS-3 users in scripting and modifying, providing significant insights for network design and management.

title: Connect. Support. Inspire.
authors: N/A

title: MmWave NLOS Sensing under Rough Relay Surface: Challenges and Solutions
authors: Guanghua Liu, Xiaotong Lu, You Xu, Haoran Yuan
publish: IEEE Aerospace and Electronic Systems Magazine
abstract: Non-Line-of-Sight (NLOS) perception is essential for preventing potential collisions and finds applications in areas such as autonomous driving, target localization, and human recognition in urban environments. Millimeter-wave (mmWave) radar distinguishes itself by robust NLOS sensing abilities. However, in urban environments, the reflective properties of relay surfaces have become complex. Diverse construction materials, intricate architectural designs, and dynamic environmental variations introduce complexity, violating the conventional assumption of smooth, deterministic geometries and degrading NLOS perception performance. To improve mmWave NLOS sensing under these realistic conditions, we investigate the characteristics of rough relay surfaces and develop a sensing framework to manage multipath interference. Our approach specifically focuses on three specific challenges often overlooked in current research: precise detection under small-scale rough relay surfaces, high-resolution imaging under large-scale relay surfaces, and multipath scattering classification under uncertain surfaces. We establish scattering signal models under different roughness conditions and develop effective NLOS sensing systems based on the distribution characteristics of targets behind different rough surfaces, achieving accurate and reliable sensing.

title: A Zero-Trust Security Architecture for 6G-Enabled Mobile Banking and Peer-to-Peer Payment Networks
authors: Abdullah Rakib Akand, Saiful Islam, Md. Rayanuzzaman Bishwas, Sarminda Islam, Khandakar Rabbi Ahmed
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The advent of sixth-generation (6 G) networks is redefining mobile banking and peer-to-peer (P2P) payment systems, characterized by low latency and high connectivity, while also increasing the security threat. The classic perimeterbased security model does not fit a dynamic and decentralized environment. In this regard, a Zero-Trust Security Architecture for the mobile financial network enabled by the 6 G network will be presented, enabling behavior-aware fraud detection via the Zero-Trust Session Graph Transformer Network (ZT-SGTNet). Continuous verification is performed by enforcing the framework through user activity modeling sessions, based on the principles of zero trust. Each transaction session will be represented as a directed heterogeneous graph to capture structural, temporal, and behavioral dependencies in the data. An experimental study using a large-scale benchmark data set from the prominent PaySim platform shows that the traditional ML baselines are outperformed by the proposed method across all metrics, including accuracy (99 %), precision, recall, and F1-score. We verify the potential to guarantee next-generation 6 G mobile payment systems in an environment with zero trust principles by considering graphs at the session level alone.

title: Performance Comparison of Best Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface and Asymmetric Relaying Strategy in Enhancing Security of Wireless Networks
authors: Subir Kumar Ghosh, M. Z. I. Sarkar
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This paper mainly focuses on a performance comparison study between asymmetric relaying and bestreconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) in terms of ergodic secrecy capacity (ESC) for secure wireless communication. Closedform analytical expressions are derived by assuming different probability density functions (PDFs) for asymmetric relaying and best-RIS, respectively, for a single user in the presence of an eavesdropper under Nakagami-m fading channels. Numerical results reveal that the best RIS provides significant performance gains in the high signal-to-noise (SNR) regime with reflecting elements, while conventional asymmetric relaying networks offer competitive performance with low complexity for enhancing security. Overall this study shows that for high data-rate secure transmission, RIS deployment has more advantages, highlighting its potential as an alternative relaying technique. The analytical results are verified through Monte Carlo simulations. This study will be further extended for multiple users and eavesdroppers.

title: Secure and Privacy-Preserving Federated Deep Learning Approach for Android Malware Detection
authors: Md. Mostafizur Rahman, Tasnin Khan, Mostak Ahmed, Md. Alamgir Hossain, Md. Samiul Islam, Muhammad Masud Tarek
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Android malware has presented serious obstacles to mobile security, which have conventionally been met by centralized machine learning models that invade privacy of users by necessitating the collective compilation of sensitive information. The paper will discuss a Federated Learning (FL) architecture that preserves privacy and uses Deep Neural Networks (DNN) to detect Android malware. With the use of the CIC MalDroid dataset we simulate several clients, each having a mixture of benign applications and different types of malware. Local models are developed on client devices and then combined with the Federated Averaging (FedAvg) algorithm, which realize a promising detection accuracy of 95.86 %. In order to increase the detection capability, local client weights are applied to the global model resulting in local performance improvements and false positives are minimized. The experimental findings prove that the proposed framework is not only highly accurate but also ensures strong generalization among the clients, therefore, the issue of privacy is resolved by storing sensitive data on the local devices.

title: Federated Artificial Neural Network for Privacy-Preserving Financial Fraud Detection
authors: Nadia Zaman Jeni, Farzana Akter, Rafiqul Islam Munna
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Effective fraud detection is most important in digital banking and electronic payment platforms. But the rapid growth of digital banking has significantly increased the scale and sophistication of financial fraud. Fraud detection is getting more complex because of extreme class imbalance, changing fraud behaviors, and data privacy regulations that restrict centralized data sharing. To solve these issues, in this paper we propose a privacy-preserving fraud detection framework based on Federated Learning (FL) using an Artificial Neural Network (ANN). In the proposed framework, local banks or clients ensure their data privacy by a federated ANN, which is trained across multiple decentralized clients without sharing raw transaction data. The ANN learns rich hidden behavioral representations that capture complex transactional patterns indicative of fraudulent activity. A tree-based Vertical Stacking Tree (VST) classifier is trained on the learned ANN embeddings to enhance discrimination and ranking performance. Then, the meta-learning strategy combines the prediction outputs of the federated ANN and the VST model. The final fraud risk scores are claimed through an ensemble meta-learning strategy. Experiments carried out on large-scale financial transaction data show that the proposed ANN-VST ensemble usually performs better than individual models when they are used alone.In addition, we explicitly compare centralized and federated configurations to isolate the contribution of distributed representation learning. All experiments are conducted under a fixed random seed configuration to ensure reproducible evaluation. The results indicate that this approach works well for ranking tasks, even in fraud detection settings where the data is highly imbalanced. In particular, the proposed method helps improve the identification of high-risk transactions, which is important for real banking operations and financial investigation processes. Overall, the framework provides a solution that is effective, scalable, and aware of privacy needs, making it suitable for fraud detection in distributed financial environments.

title: A Lossy Decoupling Network for Anti-Resonance Suppression and Capacitor Reduction in CoWoP
authors: Yuying Li, Mu-Shui Zhang, Zi-Xin Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology
abstract: Chip-on-Wafer-on-Platform (CoWoP) technology has recently been reported as a potential shift in the packaging technology for NVIDIA’s next-generation AI GPUs for higher performance. CoWoP excludes package inductance and reduces the parasitic inductance of the voltage regulator module (VRM), which results in a fast and smaller printed-circuit-board (PCB) decoupling network. Small and fast decoupling networks are also accompanied by severe anti-resonance, causing unwanted sharp peaks in the frequency-domain impedance profile and voltage oscillations in the time-domain. The traditional target impedance design method leads to a larger number of decoupling capacitors for anti-resonance suppression. In this paper, the anti-resonance-induced power noise of a multiple-capacitor decoupling network is studied first. It is recursively simplified to ࢔ second-order systems, and then the resonant parameters of each loop are derived. The transient response exhibits two types of voltage droops: inductive/resistive voltage drops and anti-resonance-induced oscillations. According to the analysis, a new decoupling network design method is proposed, in which lossy components are introduced for anti-resonance suppression and capacitor reduction. Anti-resonance-induced power noise is analyzed under transient conditions first. Through simulation and experimental validation, the lossy decoupling strategy can not only improve the robustness and efficiency of decoupling networks but also reduce nearly two-thirds of component usage compared to conventional methods. Some practical design guidelines are developed to balance the voltage drop and the loop loss, ensuring reliable decoupling network performance in CoWoPs.

title: RLAPR: Reinforcement Learning-based Adaptive Fusion for Visual-LiDAR Place Recognition in Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Shuhuan Wen, Kaikang Bai, Peng Li, Qichao Zhao, Huaping Liu, F. Richard Yu
abstract: Place recognition is essential for robust long-term autonomy in autonomous vehicles. Single sensor is limited for complex environments, while multi-modal fusion of camera and LiDAR is effective. Static or attention-based fusion methods struggle to adapt to dynamic environments. To address the limitation, we propose a novel reinforcement learning (RL) framework for dynamic weight adaptation in visual-LiDAR place recognition tailored for autonomous vehicle perception systems. In our approach, the RL agent acts as a meta-controller. It observes the current environmental state, which is derived from the characteristics of the input data, and learns a policy to dynamically adjust the contribution weights of the visual and LiDAR feature streams. This allows the network to dynamically favor the more reliable modality, such as LiDAR in low-light conditions or camera data in geometrically sparse scenes. We train and evaluate our model extensively on the challenging nuScenes dataset, which encompasses a diverse range of driving conditions across various times of day and weather scenarios. Experimental results demonstrate that our RL-based adaptive fusion strategy consistently outperforms existing static and attentional fusion methods, achieving superior recall rates and enhanced robustness to environmental variations for reliable vehicle navigation.

title: Adaptive Approaches to Mitigating Extreme Verification Latency in Dynamic Environments - A Research Meta-Analysis
authors: Mobin M. Idrees, Frederic Stahl, Atta Badii
abstract: Existing Data Stream Mining algorithms assume the availability of labelled and balanced data streams. However, in many real-world applications such as Robotics, Weather Monitoring, Internet of Things Fraud-Detection systems, Cybersecurity, Human Activity Recognition, etc., a vast amount of high-speed data is generated by sensors and real-time transaction data on the Internet, and this data is often unlabelled.
