In the domain of machine learning and data-driven decision-making, the challenge of Extreme Verification Latency (EVL) in non-stationary environments has gained significant attention. EVL refers to the considerable delay between the time a prediction is made and the time the true outcome becomes available. This latency is particularly problematic in non-stationary environments, where the underlying data distribution changes over time, rendering the delayed feedback less useful for model adaptation and accuracy improvement. This paper presents a research meta-analysis of adaptive approaches proposed to mitigate EVL in dynamic environments. The meta-analysis highlights common trends, trade-offs, and performance benchmarks as reported by across prior studies on EVL in non-stationary environments, providing both qualitative and quantitative insights into the state-of-the-art. By consolidating findings from the literature, this study identifies key research gaps and outlines promising directions for future work in handling EVL in evolving data streams. The key contribution of this meta-analysis is the systematic evaluation of EVL mitigation techniques across dynamic data stream contexts, providing practical insights and guidelines for selecting appropriate strategies in different application domains.

title: Development and Performance Study of an Ultra-Wideband Microstrip Patch Antenna
authors: Sifat Hossain, Md. Shahriar Kabir, Md. Hasib Mahbub, S.M Yousuf, Md. Abtabuzzaman Raza, Md. Nahid Hasan Shoaib
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: In a wireless communication system, the antenna is crucial. Antenna design is increasingly important for current and next wireless systems. This results in the development of the 6.3 GHz ultra-wideband (UWB) wireless communication technology, which can transmit data at high rates (from 2.7 GHz to 10.3 GHz). A UWB antenna presents more difficulties to build than a narrow-band antenna. The Roger RT5880LZ substrate and microstrip feeding line are used to develop antenna efficiency. This study presents a novel method for the enhancement of the bandwidth of the proposed patch antennas, increasing their effectiveness, and lowering their VSWR by utilizing edge-cutting rectangular patches next to the radiating patch. The proposed design is modeled and analyzed using CST simulation software. The bandwidth is 7.6 GHz, the VSWR is 1.0007, and the reflection coefficient is -68.66 dB. The designed antenna gain is 3.8 dBi and the directivity is 3.9 dBi. The results show that the suggested design offers advantageous qualities for S, C, and X-band applications.

title: Automated Synthetic Dataset Generation for Object Detection and Segmentation Using 3D Rendering and Contour Extraction
authors: Rahim Ahmed Druba, Nafis Ibn Mahabub, Turken Gulzat
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Deep learning models for object detection and segmentation need large labeled datasets. Manual labeling is slow, costly, and often has errors. This paper introduces an automated pipeline to generate synthetic segmentation datasets using 3D rendering and contour-based mask extraction. It removes the need for manual annotation. The method uses Blender with the Cycles engine to render 3D object models from multiple viewpoints on plain backgrounds. And, OpenCV is then used to remove the background and extract contours to create binary segmentation masks compatible with YOLO. The pipeline of this study was run on Google Colab with a T4 GPU. It generated 360 images per object with automated mask creation. A YOLOv5s-seg model trained on a dataset of two objects (720 images) for 50 epochs achieved 0.942 mAP@0.5 for detection and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.887 \text{mAP} {@} 0.5$</tex> for segmentation. Precision was 0.94, and recall was 0.91. White backgrounds gave about 98% mask accuracy (IoU), while black backgrounds gave 89%. The system kept a real-time inference speed of 20ms per frame and generalized well to unseen viewpoints. This approach shows that synthetic data generation can create high-quality datasets without manual labeling. It reduces preparation time to zero manual steps and provides a scalable solution for object-centered computer vision tasks where collecting real data is hard or expensive.

title: Three-Way Conflict Resolution in Fuzzy Situation Tables With Nonlinear Conflict Functions_supp1-3685391.pdf
authors: Guangming Lang
abstract: Three-way conflict analysis resolves real-world conflicts in two main steps: identifying the causes of conflict and designing feasible strategies to mitigate disputes. Existing approaches predominantly rely on linear conflict functions, which are insufficient for capturing nuanced variations in inter-agent relationships. Moreover, systematic development of feasible strategies has been limited. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a three-way conflict resolution model incorporating nonlinear conflict functions. Firstly, we introduce nonlinear conflict functions that accurately model the subtle fluctuations in conflict degrees. These functions are used to trisect both the set of agent pairs and the set of issues. We further propose a novel method to determine optimal trisections along with the corresponding optimal threshold pairs. Once the trisection phase is completed, we precisely identify the critical conflict issues that require resolution, where the parameter of conflict tolerance level is induced from the optimal issue trisection. Subsequently, conflict resolution of critical issues is formulated as an optimization problem, and two adjustment mechanisms are developed to derive distinct adjustment strategies. Finally, the applicability and superior performance of the proposed model, in terms of accurately identifying conflict issues and generating effective resolution strategies, are demonstrated through a commonly-used case study, a sensitivity evaluation, and a comparative analysis. The results confirm that our approach offers a flexible, efficient, and practical framework for three-way conflict analysis in complex decision-making contexts.

title: MRI-Based Stroke Detection Using Fine-Tuned ResNet50, Custom CNN, and Stacking Ensemble Learning
authors: Sajia Sultana, Sajia Afrin Adita, Shefayatuj Johara Chowdhury
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Stroke remains a critical public health concern, with significant impacts on both survival and long-term neurological function. Reliance on manual MRI interpretation can be inefficient and susceptible to subjective variation. This paper proposes an automated stroke-detection framework that integrates a finetuned ResNet50 model with a custom CNN using a stacking ensemble strategy. A dataset of 2,846 MRI images (1,295 stroke, 1,551 normal) is preprocessed using normalization, resizing, and augmentation to enhance robustness. The custom CNN achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 7. 0 3 \%}$</tex> accuracy, while the fine-tuned ResNet50 reaches 97.48%. The stacking ensemble provides the best performance with 97.63% accuracy. Explainable AI tools (LIME and Grad-CAM) are used to highlight stroke-affected regions, improving clinical interpretability. The proposed model demonstrates strong potential for computer-aided stroke diagnosis.

title: Highly Sensitive Diamond Shaped Dual Core PCF SPR Biosensor for Multi-Cancer Cells Detection
authors: Tridib Chakrabortty Prapya, Swajan Sitho Paul, Mohammad Shaifur Rahman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: A diamond-shaped, dual-core photonic crystal fiber (PCF) surface plasmon resonance (SPR) biosensor has been developed for label-free detection of six types of cancer cells: Basal (skin), HeLa (cervical), Jurkat (blood), PC-12 (adrenal), MDA-MB-231 and MCF-7 (breast). The PCF structure features 27 air holes: 24 larger ones with a diameter of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{d}_{1}=0.4 \times$</tex> pitch and 3 smaller ones with a diameter of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{d}_{2}= 0.3 \times$</tex> pitch, arranged in a hexagonal lattice with a pitch of 1.8 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>. A 30 nm gold coating on the surface of the fiber enables strong plasmonic coupling. COMSOL Multiphysics-based fullvectorial finite element method indicates that the optimal wavelength detection sensitivity reaches <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$30,714.29 ~\text{nm} /$</tex> RIU for breast cancer cell types, along with an amplitude detection sensitivity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{- 1, 6 7 3. 9 ~}$</tex> RIU <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$^{-1}$</tex> for cervical cancer cell types. The sensor achieves a figure of merit of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$415.05 \text{RIU}^{-1}$</tex> and a theoretical resolution of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.26 \times 10^{-6}$</tex> RIU. Phase-matching analysis confirms a confinement loss peak of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$119.47 ~\text{dB} / \text{cm}$</tex> at 830 nm, demonstrating efficient energy transfer between guided modes and surface plasmons. Compared to existing PCF-SPR designs, this diamond-shaped dual-core configuration exhibits higher sensitivity and sharper resonance peaks, significantly enhancing its ability to detect multi-cancer cells for early-stage screening and real-time biomedical monitoring.

title: Goal-Oriented DRL for Digital Spectrum Twin Network Enhanced UAV Spectrum Sharing
authors: Rui Ding, Yihe Diao, Chunyu Liu, Fuhui Zhou, Qihui Wu
abstract: The rapid proliferation of low-altitude and IoT devices is intensifying spectrum congestion, making efficient spectrum sharing a critical challenge. Conventional resource allocation methods suffer from high computational complexity and limited adaptability to dynamic environments. Although deep reinforcement learning (DRL) offers a promising agentic decision-making paradigm for autonomous spectrum management, existing approaches are hindered by the simulation-to-reality gap and reliance on hand-crafted reward functions, limiting their practical effectiveness and generalization. To address these issues, this paper proposes a digital spectrum twin network (DSTN) enhanced low-altitude spectrum sharing UAV network based on an integrated sensing-communication-control loop. Within the DSTN, the digital twin operates as an active agent, dynamically interacting with the environment to adapt resource allocation and data flows. To further enhance the agent autonomy, a generative goal-oriented adaptive DRL scheme is developed, allowing the agent to derive learning objectives from interactions, thereby eliminating dependence on manually designed rewards. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme achieves superior spectrum efficiency, faster convergence, and more reliable constraint satisfaction compared with baseline methods.

title: Hybrid Compression of LiDAR Point Clouds Using Density-Based Partitioning
authors: Nuno A. B. Martins, Abel Mendes, Fernando J. P. Lopes, Luís A. Da Silva Cruz
abstract: LiDAR point clouds exhibit large density variations, and these variations have a direct effect on compression efficiency. Geometry-based codecs such as the MPEG Geometry-based Point Cloud Coding standard (G-PCC) perform well on sparse point clouds, while learning-based methods such as the JPEG Pleno Verification Model (JP-PCC) are more effective on dense point clouds. Hybrid coding exploits this complementarity by selecting the most suitable codec for each subset of points according to local density. This work evaluates five point cloud partitioning methods based on distance, fixed density thresholds, adaptive density quantiles, neighbourhood-based refinement, and morphological smoothing. Experiments are conducted on three LiDAR point cloud datasets with distinct global density levels: KITTI, Ford, and Flowbotic, a new industrial indoor dataset introduced in this study. Density-based clustering is used to reduce biases caused by intra-dataset density variations, and all evaluations focus on practical operating points below 4 bits per point. Results show that density-based hybrid coding consistently improves compression efficiency over single-codec operation. Among the tested methods, adaptive density thresholding with a 0.25 quantile achieves the best average performance for the KITTI and Ford datasets, while neighbourhood-based sparse-to-dense refinement gives the best results for the Flowbotic dataset. These findings show that density-aware partitioning supports effective hybrid coding across diverse LiDAR acquisition conditions.

title: Recent Advances in Bangla NLP: A Survey of Few-Shot, Zero-Shot, and Transfer Learning Techniques
authors: Sharmin Sultana
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Bangla gets spoken by a ton of people, but in natural language processing, it's seen as a low resource language. That is mostly because there is not much annotated data out there, plus the grammar can be really tricky, and benchmarks for specific tasks are kind of lacking. This paper looks at how modern ways of learning and pretrained models work for Bangla NLP, doing a bunch of empirical stuff to check them out. They go through transfer learning, few shot, and zero shot methods, using models like mBERT, XLM R, mT5, BLOOM, those GPT style ones, and even BanglaBERT. All this on tasks such as sentiment analysis, named entity recognition, machine translation, question answering, and summarization. The experiments show that transfer learning, especially with models adapted for Bangla or multilingual ones, tends to do better than few shot or zero shot in terms of being robust and accurate. But I think prompt-based approaches still hold up pretty well in situations where resources are super low. It seems like how you preprocess the language matters a lot, and picking the right metrics for evaluation can change things too. Our findings, well, give some practical ideas for making Bangla NLP systems that scale and include more people. Not everything is fully sorted out here, like how to balance all these factors perfectly.

title: Bridging the Short-Term and Long-Term Gap: A Cross-Task Continuous Learning Person Re-Identification Problem
authors: Wei Liu, Xin Xu, Kui Jiang, Zheng Wang, Chia-Wen Lin
abstract: Person Re-IDentification (ReID) plays an important role in the application of intelligent security systems. Significant advancements have been made in Short-Term (ST) ReID tasks, where person appearances remain relatively constant. Similarly, progress has been achieved in Long-Term (LT) ReID tasks, which are characterized by drastic changes in person appearances. However, a vital yet overlooked challenge in real-world ReID is maintaining the continuity of the retrieval process. When transitioning from ST to LT ReID, simply discarding the ST model in favor of the LT model can lead to a severe performance drop during the early retrieval stage, due to the loss of valuable appearance-related knowledge in the ST model. Conversely, discarding the LT model and relying solely on the ST model may significantly reduce accuracy during the extended retrieval stage, as it overly depends on appearance-related knowledge. Therefore, we delve into a novel ReID problem with practical ramifications, namely short-term to long-term ReID (S2L-ReID), which continuously learns from ST to LT task. Existing continuous learning methods, only designed to mitigate domain differences across ST domains, face substantial challenges when dealing with the significant task gap in the S2L problem. These challenges manifest as severer catastrophic forgetting due to knowledge conflicts between ST and LT tasks and mutual constraints during optimization between over-coupling new knowledge adaptation and old knowledge non-forgetting tasks. To address these challenges, we propose a unified Meta-Knowledge Accumulation framework (MKA), which enables continuous learning of universal meta-knowledge for all tasks. It comprises a plug-and-play Meta-knowledge Purify Module (MPM) to alleviate knowledge conflicts by filtering out task-specific knowledge. Additionally, a transferable Peer Learning Strategy (PLS) is included to weaken mutual constraints by alternating between old and new models and enabling mutual learning. Finally, considering that real continuous application processes need to process the retrieval from ST to LT data, we propose a new Joint-Tests evaluation for evaluating the performance of the model on the more realistic hybrid data. Notably, MKA requires only a small computational resource usage with good portability. Empirical evaluations on all test settings exhibit that our MKA attains the best performance and is highly portable.

title: Data-Driven Optimized Output Regulation for Markov Jump Linear Systems and Its Application
authors: Hao Shen, Zheng Huang, Jiacheng Wu, Jing Wang, Jinde Cao
abstract: The linear optimized output regulation problem (LOORP) for Markov jump linear systems (MJLSs) is investigated in this article. First, by improving previous methods for solving the regulator equations, a model-based scheme is proposed to address the LOORP of MJLSs. Subsequently, a reinforcement learning (RL)-based iteration scheme is developed, which enables the solution of the LOORP even when the system dynamics are partially unknown and the initial control gains are unstable. Finally, an LCL-coupled inverter-based distributed generation system is provided to demonstrate the performance of the proposed RL-based scheme.

title: Parameter-Efficient Adaptation of Vision Transformers for Retinal OCT Classification
authors: Md. Sazib Ahmed, Firoz Ahmed
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Vision Transformers (ViTs) deliver strong retinal optical coherence tomography (OCT) classification performance, but full fine-tuning is computationally heavy and can overfit limited medical datasets. We benchmark parameter-efficient finetuning (PEFT) for a DeiT-Small ViT across five public OCT datasets (OCT2017, NEH-UT, Srinivasan2014, THOCT1800, and OCT-C8). We compare linear probing (head-only), VPT-Deep, AdaptFormer, and LoRA against standard full fine-tuning. PEFT methods consistently reduce trainable parameters to well under 3% while remaining competitive with full tuning. On the 8-class OCT-C8 benchmark, with frozen-backbone, AdaptFormer achieves the best Macro-F1 (97.6%), and LoRA reaches 96.9% while training only 0.7% of parameters, both outperforming full fine-tuning (95.6%). Across NEH-UT, Srinivasan, and THOCT, VPT-Deep/AdaptFormer/LoRA generally match or exceed full fine-tuning; OCT2017 is the main exception where full tuning remains superior. Calibration also holds up: PEFT variants show comparable or lower expected calibration error (ECE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 7-9\%$</tex>) than the fully tuned ViT. These results show that ViTs can be adapted to diverse OCT classification tasks with minimal weight updates, enabling cheaper training and storage and supporting deployment of multiple task-specific adapters on a shared backbone in compute and data-constrained clinical settings.

title: Mathematical Models and System-Level Analysis of Modern Geolocation Technologies
authors: Lilia Rotaru, Irina Cojuhari, Maria Gutu, Artur Kazak
publish: 2026 International Conference on Development and Application Systems (DAS)
abstract: Accurate geolocation is essential for modern cyber-physical systems supporting applications such as autonomous navigation, intelligent transportation, mobile communications, and Internet of Things infrastructures. Contemporary solutions rely on complementary technologies, including Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS), terrestrial wireless networks, and inertial sensors. This paper presents a theoretical system-level analysis of major geolocation technologies and their mathematical models, examining GNSS trilateration with clock bias, multi-constellation architectures, RSSI-based cellular and Wi-Fi localization, and inertial navigation. Estimation frameworks, including weighted least-squares and Kalman filtering for multi-sensor fusion, are also analyzed. A comparative evaluation of satellite, terrestrial, and inertial approaches highlights their operational characteristics, error sources, and accuracy regimes, emphasizing the importance of hybrid architectures for reliable positioning across diverse environments.

title: Hierarchical Global Average and Max Pooling 3D CNN-MLP Network for MRI Brain Tumor Segmentation and Classification
authors: Prashanth. N R, Sreerangappa. M
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Brain tumors are the abnormal growth of tissues in a brain, which is in different type, location, and size. However, the accurate segmentation and classification of brain tumor from Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) scans are remains challenging, because of various difficulties such as limited ability to capture spatial relationships, and inter-slice spatial context. To address these issues, this research developed an enhanced technique called HGAP-3DCNN-MLPNet to effectively capture inter-slice spatial context. Initially, the 3D Convolutional Neural Networks (3DCNNs) is included with attention gates to effectively extract local and global features. These attention gates are included in the filter skip connection to effectively focus on tumor regions. Subsequently, the Hierarchical Global Average and Max Pooling (HGAP) layers is employed in a classifier to effectively capture subtle regions. The HGAP supports to combine local and global features for classification, which preserves discriminative features for an effective brain tumor classification. Subsequently, the Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) is employed as the classifier, which obtain input features from the bottleneck encoder. Finally, the proposed technique achieved an enhanced performance of accuracy of 98.8%, precision of 97.6%, recall of 98.1, HD of 4.3, DSC, IoU of 0.95, MioU of 0.96, DSC of 0.94, and specificity of 0.97%, thereby outperforming the traditional segmentation and classification techniques.

title: CerebralChat: A Hybrid 3D-CNN and BiLSTM Model for Imagined Speech Recognition from EEG Signals
authors: Smitha B A, Mahantesh K, Dharini E, Impana P Nittur, Sumukh P Shetty, Shri Vidya V K
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Brain computer interfaces (BCIs) are emerging as transformative technologies, enabling direct communication between the human brain and external systems without muscular or verbal effort. Among various BCI paradigms, imagined speech recognition, which decodes silently thought words from neural activity, offers a natural and intuitive interaction pathway, particularly for individuals with speech or motor impairments. However, the non-stationary nature of EEG signals, low signal to noise ratio, and inter subject variability make accurate recognition a challenging task. A hybrid deep learning framework is proposed for decoding imagined speech from EEG signals. The proposed model integrates 3D Convolutional Neural Networks (3D-CNN) for spatial feature learning with Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi-LSTM) networks to capture temporal dependencies across EEG sequences. EEG recordings are transformed into spatio-temporal topographic brain maps, preserving both spatial distribution and temporal dynamics. The presented framework demonstrates improved accuracy relative to recent state-of-theart methods. The proposed approach achieving an average accuracy of 84.67%. These findings highlight the potential of hybrid architectures for robust and real time BCI applications, paving the way for advanced assistive communication technologies.

title: Factoring Multi-Channel LFPs into Interpretable Components Using Locally-Competitive Sparse Coding
authors: Nicholas G. Bruns, Garrett T. Kenyon
publish: 2026 IEEE Southwest Symposium on Image Analysis and Interpretation (SSIAI)
abstract: Technology now exists to record local field potentials (LFPs) from multiple sites simultaneously across large areas of the brain. To interpret such recordings, it is necessary to first reduce their dimensionality. Typically, dimensional reduction is accomplished via extensive spatial and/or temporal averaging in which critical stimulus information encoded in the fine grained LFP structure may be lost. Here, we present a technique based on sparse coding using the Locally Competitive Algorithm (LCA) for resolving multichannel LFP data into a tractable number of generative components that retain non-trivial structure across spatiotemporal scales. To train our model, we use a subset of the Allen Brain Observatory Neuropixels Visual Coding LFP dataset[1], segmented into spatiotemporal blocks spanning 22 channels of the mouse primary visual cortex (VISp) with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$20 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$</tex> spatial separation and 1250Hz sampling frequency. An LCA model with nonconvolutional feature patches, each spanning the entire spatiotemporal LFP block, is then optimized for sparse reconstruction. Large dictionaries (2048) of learned features were able factor the LFP data into potentially functional ensembles although small dictionaries (256) were not. The 2048 learned features exhibit non-trivial spatiotemporal structure that may in turn encode non-trivial information about visual stimuli, a hypothesis we are currently investigating.

title: Scientific Paper's Domain Prediction using Classical Machine Learning Models
authors: Md. Rawha Siddiqi Riad, Sharier Mahmud Akash, Tahmid Hasan, Abdus Salam
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The growth of research publications in different scientific domains is increasing rapidly. So, there is a need to an automated organization or classification system to help in correctly identifying the domain of a research paper. This paper performs a comparative method of classical machine learning algorithms in predicting the research area of scientific papers based on textual data information derived from the title and abstract of the papers. Exactly 40,000 research papers were obtained from arXiv in four domains: Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Computer Vision, and Robotics. Various popular algorithms like Support Vector Machines (SVM), Decision Tree (DT), Random Forest Classification (RF), Naive Bayes Classifier, k-Nearest Neighbors Classifier (KNN), Logistic Regression Classifier, and XGBoost Classifier were used with TF–IDF and Bag of Words with a stratified five-fold cross validation process. Results demonstrate that the overall best algorithms that can be used for classification of research papers belong to the category of XGBoost with a maximum F1 Score of 97.2%, and in terms of result interpretation and discussion, the method focuses on common points of failure of research domains in classifying domains of research papers.

title: Simulation-Based Performance Optimization of Tapered Slot Antennas for Early Cancer Detection
authors: Shahela Akter, Md. Mahfuzul Meheraz, Sourav Chandra Das, Md. Takvir Anam, Md Ashraful Islam, M M Naushad Ali
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The imperative for non-invasive, cost-effective, and accurate diagnostic tools for the early detection of cancer has led to significant research in microwave imaging. This study details the design, simulation, and performance optimization of a broadband Tapered Slot Antenna (TSA), specifically a Vivaldi configuration, engineered for operation within the ultra-wideband frequency spectrum. Utilizing COMSOL Multiphysics 6.2, the antenna was modeled on a dielectric substrate with a relative permittivity of 3.38 and a thickness of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$60 \text{mil}(1.524 ~\text{mm})$</tex>. The design incorporates an exponentially tapered slot defined by the function <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{y}=\mathbf{e}^{-\mathbf{4 4 x}}$</tex>, facilitating a smooth impedance transition and high directivity. Simulation results indicate a robust reflection coefficient <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{S}_{-11}$</tex> reaching −10.88 dB and -11.19 dB in the presence of a tumor model at 6.5 GHz, with a consistent Voltage Standing Wave Ratio (VSWR) maintained below the threshold of 2.0 across primary diagnostic frequencies. To evaluate clinical efficacy, a spherical tumor phantom with a 3 mm radius and high dielectric contrast was integrated into the simulation environment. The analysis confirms that the antenna exhibits high sensitivity to localized tissue anomalies, as evidenced by significant perturbations in the electric field distribution and S-parameter shifts. This research demonstrates that the proposed optimized TSA provides a viable framework for low-cost, portable, and non-ionizing cancer screening systems, particularly beneficial for resource-constrained medical environments.

title: Robust Radar Target Detection under Deceptive Jamming via Spatiotemporal Multi-Frame Fusion
authors: Xiaoying Chen, Ying Liu
abstract: In complex electromagnetic adversarial environments, radar target detection is significantly degraded by the coexistence of low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) and coherent deceptive jamming. Existing methods predominantly rely on single-frame spatial analysis, failing to exploit crucial temporal evolution characteristics across observation periods. This paper proposes a spatiotemporal multi-frame fusion network, termed MFR-YOLO, to enhance detection robustness. By performing feature-level integration across multiple successive coherent processing intervals (CPIs), the architecture effectively accumulates target energy to improve the detection capability of true targets under low signal-to-jamming-plus-noise ratio (SJNR) conditions, while accurately characterizing the spatiotemporal feature differences between true targets and deceptive jamming. Experimental results on a simulation dataset with dynamic parameter configurations demonstrate that MFR-YOLO significantly outperforms traditional algorithms, achieving a high detection probability while maintaining an extremely low false alarm rate under low SNR conditions. This work provides a robust framework for radar anti-jamming detection in challenging environments.

title: A Modified Non-Isolated Buck Converter Using Zsource Topology
authors: Md. Abdullah Al Hossain, Md. Asif Imties Alvy, Md. Manzurul Hasan, Md. Mofazzal Hossain
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The Z-Source Buck topology is used in this study to investigate the methods used to configure a DC-DC converter. In the modern power industry, efficient energy conversion is critical due to the challenges of ineffective power transformation. Compared to conventional Z-Source and Buck converters, the proposed design achieves higher efficiency and output current while utilizing fewer components. With the rapid rise of solar energy as a renewable source, this converter is particularly suited for applications like fast battery charging in electric vehicles. The proposed system delivers a 268 W capacity with minimal ripple and stable output. By arranging components in series, switching losses are distributed across multiple devices, reducing thermal stress and improving thermal management. The converter achieves an efficiency of 89.17 % with an output current of 5.37 A. Furthermore, adjusting component values allows the output voltage to be adjusted to meet diverse application requirements.

title: Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) for Crop Simulation and Enhancement: Exploring Synthetic Data for Agricultural Research
authors: Khandakar Rabbi Ahmed, Shaharior Islam Chowdhury, Saif Tasnim Chowdhury
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Real-world data collection in agriculture is not only highly expensive but also faces challenges due to seasonal variations and limited generalization capabilities for capturing unusual disease patterns and environmental changes, as is required for deep learning models. We propose a new GAN-based model aimed at crop simulation and disease data augmentation. A class-conditional Deep Convolutional GAN will be designed to generate high-resolution, biologically plausible crop disease images by jointly learning visual structures and class-specific disease characteristics. The real samples in our dataset will be combined with these generated synthetic samples to increase training for crop disease classification. The multi-class plant dataset available in PlantVillage is used in the experimental scenario, with classes for healthy and diseased crops. Extensive ablation experiments confirm that the classifier's performance can be enhanced by GAN-based training compared to state-of-the-art deep learning architectures such as ResNet50, DenseNet121, EfficientNetB3, and VGG16. The accuracy of the proposed model, ResNet50 + GAN, is about 98.65%. This shows not only higher precision levels but also a higher recall rate with an F1-score computed accordingly. This validates the realism of the synthetic images developed. This is an implication of the usefulness of synthetic data in addressing data scarcity and class imbalance in the domain of intelligent agriculture.

title: Multi-Class Road Traffic Accident Severity Prediction Using Machine Learning and Neural Networks: A Comprehensive Study with Imbalance-Aware Techniques
authors: Mahmuda Islam Rifah, Sadia Isnat, Mosammet Rabaka Sultana, Jannatul Mauwa Mitu, Saiful Islam, Zinnia Sultana
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Road traffic accidents represent a critical public safety challenge, particularly in developing countries where rapid urbanization, increasing vehicle density, and limited enforcement mechanisms contribute to high injury and fatality rates. This study conducts a comprehensive investigation of multi-class road traffic accident severity prediction using machine learning and neural network models based exclusively on categorical accident attributes derived from official accident reports. A cleaned real-world dataset comprising 12,316 accident records from Addis Ababa, Ethiopia (2017-2020) with 14 categorical predictor variables is utilized, and accident severity is formulated as a multi-class classification problem. In addition to a baseline Random Forest classifier and a fully connected neural network, the study incorporates gradient boosting algorithms (XGBoost, LightGBM, Gradient Boosting), imbalance-aware techniques including SMOTE and focal loss, and statistical significance testing. Feature importance analysis using SHAP and permutation importance provides explainability insights. Experimental results show that the Random Forest achieves 84% accuracy, while the neural network attains 85% in the baseline study. Random Forest with SMOTE achieves 69.0% accuracy on sample data, outperforming other models. Despite improvements, minority severity classes remain challenging to predict due to severe class imbalance, indicating the need for further methodological enhancements.

title: A Multi-Criterion Taxonomy of Cybersecurity Beneficiaries
authors: Svetlana Cojocaru, Alexandru-Andrei Ghita, Ludmila Peca, Marius Dumitrascu, Rostislav Călin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Development and Application Systems (DAS)
abstract: Cyber-attacks generate increasingly large global losses while financial resources for securing information systems remain limited. Existing reference frameworks (CIS Controls, ISO/IEC 27001, NIST CSF) lack fine-grained differentiation between beneficiary types, leading to inefficient resource allocation. This paper proposes a multi-criterion taxonomy consisting of ten organizational categories (O1–O10) and five population categories (P1–P5). The taxonomy extends the preliminary categorization matrix from [1], which was based solely on organizational size and occupational status, by introducing three additional classification criteria: sectoral risk profiles derived from attack frequency data, alignment with national legislative requirements (Republic of Moldova), and digital literacy assessment for population categories. Organizational classification employs EU SME definitions, while a four-level cybersecurity scale (Ordinary, Medium, Enhanced, High) correlates with CIS Controls implementation groups for practical application. Comparative analysis demonstrates advantages in sectoral granularity, population coverage, and applicability under resource constraints. The paper acknowledges the theoretical nature of the framework and presents a detailed three-stage validation protocol for future empirical testing, with limitations explicitly identified and primary contributions being conceptual and methodological.

title: Tyndall-Effect-Based Optical Monitoring of Micro-Plastic Pollution in Aquatic Environments
authors: Victor Ababii, Viorica Sudacevschi, Galina Marusic, Viorel Carbune
publish: 2026 International Conference on Development and Application Systems (DAS)
abstract: The paper presents the development and validation of an experimental system for monitoring microplastic pollution in aquatic environments, based on the Tyndall effect and artificial intelligence techniques. The proposed system uses a laser light source and a video acquisition module to detect light scattered by microplastic particles, with data processed in real time on the NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano Developer Kit (SBC) platform. Mathematical models and discrete algorithms are developed for correlating the optical signal with the concentration of microplastics, and the performance of the method is improved by integrating artificial neural networks such as CNN and LSTM. The experimental results, presented in the form of images and quantitative graphs, demonstrate the system's ability to identify the presence of microplastics and dynamically monitor concentration variations. The proposed solution offers a fast, non-invasive, and scalable approach with high potential for continuous monitoring of the aquatic environment.

title: Output Characteristics of Photovoltaic Arrays Driven by Big Data and Multi-Factor Coupled Prediction Algorithm
authors: Gaomeng Jia, Xianjun Wang, Yanan Li, Yong Li, Yafei Liu, Haoqiong Yang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: In the context of the global energy transition, photovoltaics due to its clean and renewable benefits have become the core of the development of new energies. However, the release of photovoltaic devices is affected by a wide range of factors such as solar radiation and ambient temperature and shows obvious interruptions and volatility that significantly limit the safe and stable operation of the power grid. Existing prediction methods have problems such as low use of data from multiple sources, neglect of factor coupling effects, and insufficient prediction accuracy. This article proposes a big data-based framework for the analysis of the output characteristics of photovoltaic devices and multifactor coupling prediction technologies. Firstly, this article completes the preprocessing of data from multiple sources by cleaning data, removing abnormal values, normalizing and other methods, to ensure data quality. Secondly, based on big data mining technologies such as FCM clustering and correlation analysis, this article analyzes the output characteristics of photovoltaic devices in different scenarios and identifies the key factors; finally, in this article, an algorithm for Memetic Coral Reef Optimization (MCRO) to a multifactor coupling prediction model for extreme value learning machines (ELM) was developed. It can optimize to enable a highly accurate prediction of photovoltaic cost. Experimental results based on actual data from PV power plants showed data loss after pretreatment from 8.7 % to 1.2 %, the anomaly rate decreased from 4.2 % to 0.8 %, and the ADF test P -value from 0.21 to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.03(<0.05)$</tex>. The significantly improved data stability confirms the value of their technical applications.

title: Controller Tuning for First- to Fourth-Order Inertia Object Models with Astatic Behavior Using the Polynomial Method
authors: Bartolomeu Izvoreanu, Adrian Secrieru, Nadejda Palamarciuc, Ion Fiodorov, Dumitru Moraru
publish: 2026 International Conference on Development and Application Systems (DAS)
abstract: This paper describes the procedure for tuning a controller for models of control objects with first- to fourth-order inertia and astatic behavior using the polynomial method, with imposed values of the damping ratio and settling time of the synthesized system. The numerator and denominator polynomials of the transfer function of the object model are factorized into factors with zeros located in the left-hand and right-hand parts of the complex plane.Based on the order of the object model and on the conditions for solving the system of algebraic equations, the physical realizability of the control algorithm, and the robustness of the system, the desired polynomial of the synthesized system of the corresponding degree is constructed. This polynomial contains two polynomials with unknown coefficients, and the degrees of the unknown polynomials as well as the degree of the desired polynomial are determined.From the specified damping ratio and settling time, the dominant poles of the synthesized system are determined, and on their basis the characteristic polynomial of the closed-loop system is constructed. If necessary, additional real poles located on the negative real semi-axis are added as far as possible from the dominant poles in order to ensure the physical realizability of the controller and to satisfy the required system performance.By equating the desired characteristic polynomial with the characteristic polynomial of the constructed system, and by matching the coefficients of the same powers of the variable s on both sides of the equality, a system of algebraic equations is obtained, from which the unknown coefficients and polynomials are determined.Based on the stable components of the object and the determined unknown polynomials, the transfer function of the controller is constructed. Examples of controller synthesis for first- to fourth-order object with astatic behavior using the proposed method are analyzed. The synthesized systems demonstrate high performance and good robustness.

title: Simulation Study of a "Human-Machine" Dual Picking System Based on AnyLogic
authors: Xu Zhang, Heng Zhao, Jiongcong Zhong, Weixiang Guo, Junze Wang, Shiqi Chen, Linghua Zhao, Xinyu Yin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Statistics, Modeling and Advanced Algorithms (ASMA)
abstract: In warehouse logistics systems, picking operations are a key factor constraining both operational efficiency and costs in distribution centers. Traditional manual picking offers high flexibility but limited efficiency, whereas AGV-based automated picking provides high efficiency at the expense of flexibility. To balance efficiency and flexibility, "human–machine" dual-picking systems are attracting increasing attention. This study uses AnyLogic software to build a warehouse operation simulation model that integrates discrete-event and agent-based modeling approaches. Simulation experiments on dual-picking operations were conducted and analyzed comparatively. The results show that manual picking offers greater flexibility and efficiency in handling diverse order demands, while AGV systems demonstrate long-term cost advantages and sustainability. This study provides simulation-based support for the implementation of a human– machine collaborative picking model in the warehousing of small electrical materials for power grid enterprises.

title: A Lightweight Mutual Authentication and Secure Communication Protocol for Medical Body Area Networks
authors: Jinat Farjana, Md. Abu Talab Anik, Rokaiya Sultana Bonhy, Mahiya Akter Mim, Habiba Sultana Othy, Rakibul Hasan, Md. Shafiqul Islam
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Medical Body Area Networks (MBANs) leverage IoT-enabled wearable and implantable devices to continuously monitor patients' vital signs. This enables real-time healthcare services and supports informed treatment decisions. However, MBANs face significant security challenges due to the constrained resources of IoT devices and the use of wireless communication. These vulnerabilities can lead to data interception, tampering, or unauthorized access. To address these challenges, this article proposes a lightweight cryptographic protocol for MBANs that ensures confidentiality, integrity, and mutual authentication. The protocol is specifically designed to operate efficiently on resource-constrained devices while providing strong security guarantees. Security analysis demonstrates the protocol's robustness against common attacks, while experimental evaluations confirm that it can be efficiently implemented on resource-constrained hardware. The results show that the protocol successfully protects patient sensitive data, maintains data integrity, and detects unauthorized access attempts, providing a secure and reliable framework for MBAN communications.

title: Research on Optimization of Manufacturing Process Inspection Scheme Based on Bayesian Optimization Algorithm
authors: Haoke Wang, Jiacheng Gao, Zihan Shu, Ting Dou
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Statistics, Modeling and Advanced Algorithms (ASMA)
abstract: This study explores an efficient sampling strategy that minimizes the number of samples while maintaining representativeness of the entire population. In quality management, sample representativeness critically affects data validity and decision-making. Optimizing sampling under resource constraints remains challenging. This study uses binomial distribution and Bayesian optimization to simulate success probabilities and determine optimal sampling iterations. Through robustness analysis, an inspection scheme minimizing inspections while maximizing acceptance probability is designed. Monte Carlo simulation generates samples, and the proposed inspection scheme estimates defect rates. The estimated defect rates are incorporated into DC-ADMM and MCADM models for solution. Model outputs provide defect rate predictions and optimal inspection recommendations, effectively reducing inspection costs.

title: AI-Enabled Document Verification Automation System: Transforming Academic Claim Processing Using Azure Document Intelligence
authors: Jaya Mary Jennifer D, Shristy Das, Margaret Savitha P, Lakshmi Sankaran
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Traditional academic document verification relies on manual, paper-based workflows that are time-consuming, error-prone, and administratively inefficient. This research proposes an AI-powered document verification system that leverages Azure AI Document Intelligence for optical character recognition and structured extraction of text and key-value pairs from academic claim documents. The system integrates rule-based cross-document validation, AI-driven categorisation, and a Human-in-the-Loop mechanism to ensure reliability and oversight for ambiguous cases. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that the system achieves approximately 90 % extraction accuracy, reduces claim processing time by nearly <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 0 \%}$</tex> (from 35-50 minutes to 3-5 minutes per claim), supports a threefold increase in claim volume, and lowers human error rates from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 - 1 2 \%}$</tex> to below 2%. The proposed solution transforms document-intensive administrative workflows into efficient, automated digital ecosystems, laying a strong foundation for scalable digital transformation in academic institutions.

title: Intelligent Road Surface Crack Detection Using a Lightweight CNN on an Autonomous Inspection Robot
authors: Basma M. Yousef, Manar Sabry, Marwa Sabry
abstract: Traffic loading, roadway aging, and inherent pavement defects significantly contribute to the development of cracks, which in turn adversely affect transportation infrastructure safety and performance. This forces civil engineers to spend significant amounts of time, effort, and cost on inspection, classification, and maintenance. Conventional crack detection relies on manual surveys or offline image analysis, which are labor intensive, time-consuming, and unsuitable for real time decision making. This research presents a low-cost autonomous robotic system that detects and classifies road surface cracks in real time according to standard engineering categories. Using embedded artificial intelligence and computer vision, the robot monitors pavement conditions and makes independent navigation decisions. It wirelessly transmits structured reports to a central platform that allows users to interact with all aspects of the project comprehensively and effectively. Field experiments demonstrate classification and autonomous navigation for monitoring. The autonomous road inspection system (ROVIL), which combines cloud-based monitoring, real-time wireless communication, and embedded deep learning into a single smart infrastructure platform, is presented in this study. A lightweight CNN model is used for crack detection and classification on device, with up to 98% confidence in real-world inference and 94.6% validation accuracy. A centralized server receives inspection data in JSON format over Wi-Fi, stores it in a SQL database, and displays the crack type, timestamp, confidence score, and GPS location on a web-based dashboard. This system is useful for smart transportation applications, the suggested system exhibits dependable, scalable, and real-time road condition monitoring.

title: Adaptive Intelligent Deep Learning Based Online English Teaching System for College
authors: Xiang Zhou, Yang Xiao, Ke Peng
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: In recent years, rapid growth of online english language teaching systems has been significantly increased. However, traditional online english teaching systems lacks intelligent personalization and limited adaptability for learner learning needs. To overcome these challenges, an Adaptive Intelligent Deep Learning based English Teaching Framework (AIDLET) is proposed for developing an online english teaching system for colleges. Initially, data was collected from a publicly available dataset namely english proficiency dataset which contains of both quantitative and qualitative data like study time, age, gender and digital learning resources, academic scores and evaluation measures. After, entire data is preprocessed using min max normalization, all the missing and outlier values are fixed. Later entire preprocessed data was fed as input to embedding layer where input features are converted into dense latent representations and these are further processed into self attention mechanism where it learns factors which are influencing english proficiency. Furthermore, a Bi directional sequential layer is incorporated for model learning progression for processing into fully connected layers and to provide proficiency predictions. Finally, this system categorizes students into three proficiency levels. In experimental results, AIDLET Framework obtained with an accuracy (99.03 %), recall (92.48 %), precision (97.69 %) and F1 score (98.19 %).

title: Cognitive Computing-Based Large-Scale Traffic Data Stream Mining for Intelligent Traffic Control
authors: Deepa Dawadi, Riaz Ahmed Mohammed Sait, Nikita Chawla, S. Leopauline, Anumanthu Ramya Sri
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: In recent years, growth and the widespread deployment of intelligent transportation systems have led to the generation of large-scale, continuous traffic data streams from distributed sensors. Cognitive computing has emerged as a promising paradigm for extracting actionable intelligence from such data by enabling learning, reasoning, and adaptive decision-making in real time. However, existing traffic management approaches primarily rely on reactive control or static optimization techniques, which lack incremental learning capability, concept drift awareness, and long-term knowledge evolution when processing high-velocity data streams. To overcome these challenges, this paper proposes a cognitive computing-based large-scale data stream mining framework for intelligent traffic signal control. Initially, begins with Distributed Traffic Data Stream Acquisition, where real-time traffic data is collected from heterogeneous sensors among multiple intersections. This is followed by stream preprocessing and feature abstraction to provide the data quality and scalability. The incremental data stream mining was employed to continuously learn developing traffic patterns without storing historical data. A cognitive reasoning and concept drift detection module interprets mined patterns and adapts to dynamic traffic changes. Based on these cognitive states, cognitive decision-making and adaptive traffic control optimizes signal timings in real time. Lastly, feedback-driven learning and knowledge evolution applied for effective control strategies by continuous performance evaluation. Experimental results demonstrate improved adaptability, reduced congestion, and sustained optimization under dynamic traffic conditions.

title: Robust Dorsal Hand Vein Recognition with Reduced Computational Overhead via Multibranch Learning
authors: Rana Nour, Hossam El-Din Moustafa, Mohamed Maher Ata
abstract: Dorsal hand vein recognition is a reliable biometric approach for confirming identity; however, it is frequently hampered by issues such as lighting fluctuations, rotation, and occlusion. To address these limitations, this research presents an improved Multibranch Lightweight Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for vein recognition. The suggested design uses many parallel branches to capture different and distinguishable vein properties while drastically decreasing computational complexity and memory consumption. When tested on two publicly available datasets, the model obtained an impressive 99.9% accuracy, with 98% precision and recall, and a total processing time of 1086.33 seconds. Experimental results reveal that the suggested model beats numerous existing techniques approaches in terms of accuracy, speed, and memory efficiency, indicating its effectiveness and suitability for real-world biometric applications.

title: Explainable Federated Learning Approach for Early Detection and Diagnosis of Brain Stroke in Clinical Healthcare Systems
authors: Siliveri Rajender, Pramod Kumar. P
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Stroke continues to be among the major causes of death and disability globally. On the other hand, stroke predictive models developed using centralized machine learning approaches remain hampered by poor interpretability, issues related to data privacy, and inefficiency in processing decentralized patient data. In this study, to mitigate these challenges associated with stroke predictive tasks, there is a proposal for the development of an Explainable Federated Learning platform incorporated with a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network to develop a transparent stroke predictive model. Based on the Stroke Prediction Dataset available on Kaggle, the new model was tested among decentralized nodes to maintain data privacy. The new platform exhibited improved communication complexity and quicker model convergence compared to previous centralized approaches. The developed model indicated 98.9% accuracy, 98.6 % precision, 98.2 % recall rate, 99.1 % specificity rate, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$98.4 \% \text{F1}$</tex> score. Fundamentally, the Explainable Federated LSTM model offers a good enhancement in predictive accuracy, privacy maintenance, and interpretable results to help clinicians arrive at reliable and transparent decisions for early detection of stroke. This study focuses on the evaluation of an explainable federated learning system for tabular stroke prediction and is not oriented toward the development of new algorithms. The results are indicative that privacy-preserving training coupled with explainability will allow clinicians to make transparent and reliable decisions.

title: Indistinguishability in Generative AI: Detection Challenges, Techniques, and Ethical Considerations
authors: Hamza Abbasi, Qurat Ul Ain Aini, Nadeem Ahmad, Zaki Uddin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robotics and Automation in Industry (ICRAI)
abstract: Generative AI enables the creation of highly realistic synthetic text that is increasingly difficult to distinguish from human-written content. This paper examines the technological, ethical, and social ramifications of this phenomenon in the media, customer service, and creative writing industries, as it relates to the technologies, ethics, and social situations of the industries. For this type of analysis, the author describe, in a mixed-methods style, empirical modeling, a human-survey, and the modeling of policy. The authors find that even advanced detection models that claim high accuracy, such as RoBERTa, are significantly impaired in their performance in the face of humanized AI text. The author describes the paradox of the growing use of Gen AI tools as being characterized by pervasive skepticism toward AI content. Without resolving the tension surrounding misinformation, deep-fakes, and the disconnected authorship, there is insufficient time to address the ethical shortcomings surrounding their most pressing dilemmas. In the case of Grad, the author endorse a mixed approach of detection technologies, labeling, and the collection of worldwide policies as the most effective solution across the greatest number of countries. The author encourages the study of the psychological fallout from long-term exposure to AI-generated content, as well as the effective detection technologies developed in multiple languages and more culturally appropriate settings in the future.

title: PM2.5 Timeseries Forecasting with Weather-Aware Attention Mechanism: A Multivariate Deep Learning Approach Based on Long-Term Data from Dhaka
authors: Zul Ikram Musaddik Rayat, Md. Naiem Mobasshir Monim, Mohammad Mamun
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Dhaka is among the world's most polluted megacities, with its Air Quality Index (AQI) frequently reaching Unhealthy to Hazardous levels, necessitating accurate air quality forecasting to mitigate public health risks. This study presents a multivariate time series analysis of PM2.5 in Dhaka using advanced deep learning architectures. The proposed framework integrates eight years (2017-2024) of meteorological variables—temperature, rainfall, humidity, and wind speed—alongside PM2.5 concentrations. We introduce a novel “Weather-Aware” Variable Attention mechanism coupled with LSTM and GRU recurrent layers to dynamically prioritize meteorological features. Experimental results demonstrate that the Variable Attention-based GRU model significantly outperforms standard benchmarks, achieving an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> of 0.9956 and a Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of 3.65% for hourly forecasts. For daily predictions, the model remains robust with an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> of 0.9892 and a MAPE of 5.56%. Robustness analysis across five random seeds further validates the model's stability with a narrow 95% confidence interval. These findings highlight the critical importance of multivariate, weather-informed modeling for urban air quality prediction and provide actionable insights for policymakers to address pollution risks in South Asian megacities.

title: Adaptive Cross-Layer Routing and QoS-Aware MAC for Reliable Team Communication in Complex Terrain
authors: Haoran Dou, Siyu Dai, Jinfeng He, Jianing Wu, Jiajia Chen
abstract: Team communication in complex terrain faces link obstruction, rapid topology variation, and heterogeneous service priorities. Existing routing or MAC-only (Medium Access Control) schemes are difficult to balance reliability, overhead, and QoS (Quality of Service) guarantee. This paper proposes ACLRM (Adaptive Cross-Layer Routing and MAC), an adaptive cross-layer routing and QoS-aware MAC framework. A relay utility model combining link quality and residual energy is constructed, controlled flooding with spatiotemporal deduplication and dynamic TTL (Time-To-Live) is designed, and CAD-based (channel activity detection) congestion sensing is coupled with differentiated MAC scheduling. A cross-layer interface is further specified to clarify how RSSI (Received Signal Strength Indication), residual energy, packet priority, queue state, and channel activity are exchanged inside each node. Simulation results, including comparisons with traditional and recent-work-derived baselines, show that ACLRM achieves near-100% delivery for high-priority packets, suppresses broadcast storms, and preserves emergency services under overload.

title: 3D Metric Tracking of Laryngeal Movement for Automatic Evaluation of Swallowing Function
authors: Makoto Hyakutake, Kentaro Mori, Takumi Ise, Yutaka Hata, Naomi Yagi
publish: 2026 IEEE 56th International Symposium on Multiple-Valued Logic (ISMVL)
abstract: Non-invasive and quantitative assessment of swallowing function is important for preventing complications such as aspiration pneumonia. We propose a 3D metric estimation method for laryngeal movement using 2D tracking with DeepLabCut and metric depth estimation with Depth Pro. Experimental results showed that the distance from the camera to the larynx can be estimated with an error of approximately 3.7 cm, and the standard deviation of laryngeal movement across different camera distances was 0.29 cm. Using laryngeal movement values from previous studies, the coefficient of variation was estimated at 9–14%, indicating reproducibility across varying distances.

title: Some Properties of 2-valued Majority Functions and Minimal Clones
authors: Hajime Machida
publish: 2026 IEEE 56th International Symposium on Multiple-Valued Logic (ISMVL)
abstract: Let E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">k</inf> = {0,1,…,k−1}, k > 2, and W<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">k</inf> be the set of triples in $E_k^3$ with mutually distinct components. A majority function f defined on E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">k</inf> is called 2valued if it is 2-valued or a constant on W<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">k</inf>. Let the range of f on W<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">k</inf> be a subset of {0,1}.We study some basic properties of such functions f and, concerning {0,1,a} for a ∈ E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">k</inf>\{0,1}, present the invariants shared by functions contained in the clone generated by f.In addition, a group of minimal functions on E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">k</inf> is given which are 2-valued majority functions.

title: Meta-Learning-Enhanced Task Assignment and Resource Scheduling for UAV-Assisted WSNs in 6G-Enabled ITS
authors: Mesfin Leranso Betalo, Amr Mohamed, Amin Sharafian, Zongze Wu, Jianqiang Li, Xiaoshan Bai
abstract: The integration of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), wireless sensor networks (WSNs), and 6 G technologies is transforming the design of next-generation Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS), enabling real-time, energy-efficient, and adaptive urban mobility services. This paper proposes a novel meta-learning-enhanced UAV-assisted WSN architecture, tailored for large-scale, dynamic ITS environments characterized by high vehicular mobility, dense sensor deployments, and stringent latency requirements. We introduce a new task assignment framework to address critical challenges such as coverage limitations at urban intersections, energy constraints of sensor nodes, and the need for rapid decision-making under complex traffic conditions. We aim to maximize energy-efficient data throughput (EEDT) and ensuring Quality of Service (QoS) in UAV-assisted WSNs. The framework jointly optimizes traffic sensor node selection, UAV trajectory planning, and communication resource allocation by formulating the problem as a Constrained Markov Decision Process (CMDP). To solve this, we develop the Meta-Learning Weighted Multi-Agent Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (MW-MAD3PG) algorithm, which embeds model-agnostic meta-learning (MAML) within a cooperative multi-agent reinforcement learning (MADRL) setting. MW-MAD3PG enables UAV agents to rapidly adapt to dynamic traffic patterns, fluctuating road conditions, and evolving network states with minimal retraining, ensuring high energy efficiency and system scalability. Extensive simulations show that the proposed framework achieves up to a 25% improvement in UAV coordination efficiency, a 30% increase in data offloading capacity, and substantial enhancements in energy-aware operations compared to baseline methods such as MADDPG, Meta-SGD, and Meta-Q-Learning. These results validate our architecture and framework as robust, intelligent solutions for future 6G-enabled ITS, supporting resilient, real-time, and energy-optimized traffic monitoring and control.

title: Quantum Computing for Insurance Fraud Detection
authors: Yang Bai, Wenyu Jiao, Chao Ju, Xinjie Feng, Jiawei Lu, Nuo Qian, Lingge Kong, Qi Ao, Jiajun Zhang, Feifei Zhu, Lian Zhou
abstract: Collusive insurance fraud causes large financial losses for insurance companies. While community detection algorithms based on modularity can be used to detect collusive insurance fraud, the related optimization problem is NP-hard, motivating the application of quantum computers. In this work, we employ a specialized quantum computer, the Coherent Ising Machine (CIM), for the insurance fraud detection problem. By modeling insurance fraud detection as a Quadratic Unconstrained Binary Optimization (QUBO) problem, we efficiently solve it on a physical CIM with over 1500 qubits. Our results demonstrate the applicability of CIM to insurance fraud detection and its significant acceleration capability compared to classical methods. Furthermore, CIM can obtain degenerate ground-state and near-optimal solutions due to its sampling capability and the community partitions of CIM manifest diversity while each solution maintaining a competitive fraud detection performance. We explore how this solution diversity can be used to strengthen CIM fraud detection performance and demonstrate that, when top 2% or less nodes are reviewed, the precision and recall rate of the solution ensemble are better than that of the best single solution up to 2.5 times and 2 times, respectively.

title: Deep Feature Correlation Analysis for KPI-related Fault Detection with Missing Data
authors: Yanyu Chen, Hao Ma, Yan Wang, Xiang Liu, Xiao Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Missing data commonly exists in industry processes, primarily due to sensor failures, signal transmission issues. Effective key performance indicator (KPI)-related fault detection for processes with missing data is essential for improving product quality and yield. This paper proposes a novel framework to monitor KPI-related faults, which consists of data imputation and fault detection. During the data imputation process, a novel location embedding generative adversarial imputation network (LEGAIN) is proposed. The LEGAIN transforms the distance between each pair of observation points into a position-aware weight matrix, which captures the location information to facilitate accurate data imputation. LEGAIN introduces a symmetric window mask strategy to enhance its ability in capturing local dynamic characteristics. During the fault detection process, the deep feature correlation analysis (DFCA) is proposed. The DFCA introduces a dual-branch variational autoencoder (VAE) to separately extract nonlinear features from process variables and KPIs. The spatial-temporal correlation between these two sets of features is analyzed and considered as a part of the loss function. Generalized singular value decomposition is applied to effectively distinguish KPI-related subspace and KPI-unrelated subspace. Corresponding theory analysis is provided. Experiment results demonstrate the superiority of the proposed method.

title: Memristor-Based Sensitization Nonassociative Learning Circuit and Its Application in Overheat Protection of Industrial Robots
authors: Junwei Sun, Mengfan Wu, Zicheng Wang, Yanfeng Wang
abstract: Most memristor-based nonassociative learning circuits only focus on habituation, while neglecting the research of sensitization and secondary sensitization. Therefore, this article designs a memristor-based sensitization learning circuit, which mainly consists of a habituation module, sensitization module, voltage control module, and secondary sensitization module. After receiving the signal from the voltage module, the habituation module and the sensitization module implement their functions, respectively. Second, this study investigates the influence of voltage stimuli on the formation rates of habituation and sensitization. Moreover, the functions of secondary sensitization and long/short-term sensitization processes have been successfully realized through the synergistic effect of the sensitization module and the secondary sensitization module. Finally, PSPICE software is used to verify the correctness of the design. The outcomes of simulations demonstrate that the circuit is capable of simulating the complex biological mechanisms mentioned earlier. Meanwhile, the circuit also realizes the application simulation of overheating protection for industrial equipment.

title: RVV-Morpher: A Semantic-Aware Static Binary Rewriter for Cross-Version Portability in RVV
authors: Yuexi Zou, Jianing Yang, Jianmei Guo, Bo Huang
publish: ACM Transactions on Embedded Computing Systems
abstract: None

title: Reconsidering “Reconsidering Custom Memory Allocation”
authors: Nicolas van Kempen, Emery D. Berger
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 ACM SIGPLAN International Symposium on Memory Management
abstract: None

title: Military Digital Twins - a Data-Hardened Architecture for Digital Twins to Enable Operational Resilience in Adversarial Environments
authors: Anand Rath, Manas Ranjan Patra
publish: 2026 International Conference on Air Defense and Security (ICADS)
abstract: We propose a data-hardened architecture for military digital twins that can enable resilient, integrated warfare under contested environments. We first elicit the foundational requirements of a military digital twin and use them to derive its defining architectural choices. Designed explicitly for adversarial environments, it adopts a zero-trust posture in every design decision and employs Bayesian consensus mechanisms to build trust dynamically over time, enabling battlefield assets to verify one another for signs of compromise. Furthermore, the architecture mitigates the challenges of intermittent, low-bandwidth connectivity at the edge by employing a hybrid construct: a lightweight, low-fidelity edge twin paired with a high-fidelity command-center twin. This division of labour enhances resilience, maintains operational continuity, and strengthens the overall robustness of the military digital twin ecosystem.

title: Work in Progress: Enhancing Online Certification Through Quality Improvement to Support Workforce Skills in Engineering
authors: Jorge Ortega-Moody, Ghazal Barari, Kouroush Jenab, William Muldoon
abstract: University-industry certificates are increasingly designed to help employees move into new roles while continuing their work responsibilities. This work in progress describes the evaluation of two early courses in a fully online certificate codeveloped with an aerospace manufacturer. Electric Circuits uses mailed component kits and vendor-aligned digital multimeter training, while Industrial Robotics relies exclusively on offline simulation that mirrors teach-pendant programming when plant access is not possible. New course-specific pre and post surveys capture both confidence ratings and open-ended reflections. Early results suggest that industry-relevant topics and tools closely aligned with day-to-day responsibilities can drive meaningful improvement even in an online format. The analysis applies smallsample appropriate methods and highlights quality-improvement actions that will shape future offerings. Planned additions to the certificate include Thermodynamics, Computer-Aided Manufacturing, Sensors and Actuators, Safety, and Mechatronics, which will allow for certificate-level evaluation after Summer 2026.

title: Analyzing the Impact of LOS and NLOS Propagation on Underwater Localization Accuracy Using Data Collected by Autonomous Underwater Vehicles (AUVs)
authors: Ashish Kumar Dass, Sudhir Ranjan Pattanaik, Manjushree Nayak
publish: 2026 International Conference on Air Defense and Security (ICADS)
abstract: The underwater localization technique is indispensable for marine operations like exploration, habitat monitoring, and search-and-rescue. The acoustic propagation path mainly determines the accuracy of localization methods that may either be line-of-sight (LOS) or non-line-of-sight (NLOS) based. Acoustic propagation through LOS gives a direct path; NLOS, on the other hand, involves a combination of reflection, refraction, and scattering which can either be caused by the seabed, surface, or obstacles. The integration of different methods for underwater localization via AUVs has been evaluated in this review. Considering the existing literature, theoretical models, and experimental observations, the paper sheds lights on NLOS's considerable range bias and position estimation decay. Several techniques such as NLOS detection, bias compensation, sensor fusion, and geometry-aware methods are reviewed under the topic of mitigation approaches. Accompanying simulation and experimental results demonstrate that neglecting NLOS effects can lead to an increase in localization error of more than 40%, while adaptive modeling and data fusion can effectively lessen the impact considerably. The review points out the present research gaps and suggests future directions for AUV-based underwater localization in scenarios with mixed LOS/NLOS conditions.

title: Parameter Optimization Design for Suppressing Second Harmonics in Star-Configured High-Voltage Transformer-less Battery Energy Storage System
authors: Yilin Liu, Xiqi Wu, Rui Li, Han Wang, Wenfei Liu, Xu Cai
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: This paper presents a novel methodology for suppressing second harmonic current in the DC bus of power units within star-configured high-voltage transformer-less battery energy storage systems (BESS). The research extends beyond suppression techniques to explore optimized system design parameters that enhance harmonic current mitigation capabilities. The proposed approach introduces a third harmonic zero-sequence voltage injection method to effectively counteract the second harmonic current components flowing into the power unit's battery DC side. From a system optimization perspective, this methodology significantly improves battery current ripple performance while enhancing overall economic efficiency. Through comprehensive theoretical analysis and multi-objective optimization techniques, this study thoroughly investigates the suppression mechanisms affecting second harmonic current in power module DC sides. The optimization framework incorporates practical engineering constraints to determine optimal parameter configurations for high-voltage transformer-less BESS implementations. Unlike existing approaches that utilize third harmonic zero-sequence voltage for harmonic suppression, this research adopts a holistic system perspective that accounts for critical engineering factors including filter volume requirements and component oversizing costs. The resulting solution effectively minimizes module current ripple while maintaining cost-effectiveness, representing a significant advancement in transformer-less BESS design methodology.

title: Uncertainty Quantification of XLPE Cable Insulation Lifetime Based on Accelerated Life Testing and Bootstrap Methods
authors: Mingzhen Li, Shuangcheng Song, Enci Zhou, Kejun Qian, Haojie Xie, Shun Sang, Xinsong Zhang, Zaixin Song
