publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Accurate lifetime prediction of cross-linked polyethylene (XLPE) cable insulation is essential for reliability assessment and asset management in modern power systems. However, lifetime estimates derived from accelerated life testing (ALT) are inherently affected by significant uncertainty due to limited sample sizes, experimental variability, right-censored observations, and model parameter estimation. In this paper, an uncertainty quantification framework for XLPE insulation lifetime prediction is proposed based on ALT and bootstrap methods. Thermal accelerated ageing tests were carried out at 125 °C, 135 °C, 150 °C, and 160 °C, with elongation at break (EAB) adopted as the degradation criterion to define end of life. An Arrhenius-based lifetime model was employed to extrapolate ageing data from accelerated conditions to service-relevant temperatures, while maximum likelihood estimation was used to properly account for right-censored data. To quantify parameter uncertainty, a nonparametric bootstrap resampling approach was applied to the ALT dataset, yielding empirical distributions of key model parameters, including activation energy and reference lifetime. The resulting parameter uncertainty was further propagated to obtain probabilistic lifetime predictions under service conditions. The results demonstrate that the predicted insulation lifetime exhibits considerable variability, and that conventional point-estimate-based approaches may obscure important risk information. By providing lifetime distributions and confidence intervals, the proposed framework offers a statistically rigorous and practical basis for reliability-oriented decision-making and condition-based management of XLPE cable insulation.

title: Fine-Grained Correction Method for Distributed Photovoltaic Hosting Capacity in Rural Distribution Networks
authors: Fengzhang Luo, Ranfeng Mu, Shengyuan Wang, Kuangyu Kong, Lukun Ge, Zhishuang Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the rapid growth of distributed photovoltaic (PV) installations in rural areas, traditional PV hosting capacity assessment methods based solely on electrical constraints fail to reflect actual interconnection conditions, resulting in deviations between the evaluation results and the practical level of engineering implementation. To address this issue, a refined correction method for the distributed PV hosting capacity of rural distribution networks is proposed. Based on the conventional hosting capacity optimization model constrained by voltage deviation, thermal limits, short-circuit current, and the "N-1" criterion, practical factors such as implementation conditions, user installation willingness, government subsidy policies, and the existing level of PV deployment are incorporated to construct a multidimensional constraint–based hosting capacity correction model, together with a corresponding computational procedure. A 10 kV feeder is used as a case study to analyze and compare the distribution of PV hosting capacity before and after correction. The results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively bridges the gap between idealized electrical assessments and practical engineering conditions, providing a more reliable basis for PV integration planning and management decisions in rural distribution networks.

title: Adaptive Frequency-Constrained Robust Unit Commitment Considering Adjustable Virtual Inertia and Multi-Stage Frequency Security Indices
authors: Zhengxi Yin, Xueyan He, Jiayan Lu, Yingjun Wu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: High penetration of wind power significantly reduces synchronous inertia and increases frequency stability risks in modern power systems. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an adaptive frequency-constrained robust unit commitment (AFCRUC) model integrating adjustable virtual inertia scheduling and multi-stage analytical frequency security constraints. An extended average system frequency (ASF) model is developed to derive closed-form RoCoF and frequency nadir expressions. Wind virtual inertia uncertainty is modeled using a bounded set, and a two-stage robust optimization framework is constructed. The nonlinear nadir constraint is reformulated into mixed-integer second-order cone programming (MISOCP). Case studies on a modified IEEE 118-bus system demonstrate that the proposed method reduces total operating cost while maintaining frequency security under severe contingencies.

title: Complementing Traditional Mathematics Instruction with AI-Assisted Learning Activities
authors: Claudia Hernandez-Mena, Adriana E Martinez-Canton, Mariana Olivares Avalos
abstract: This paper presents an evidence-based instructional methodology for mathematics education that combines traditional instruction with AI-assisted problem solving using ChatGPT integrated with Wolfram Alpha. The approach was implemented in two first-year engineering topics, integral calculus and graphical analysis, to evaluate its effectiveness across different mathematical contexts. The instructional design included a traditional session and an AI-assisted session. Students' perceptions were measured through a Likert-scale survey applied to 49 participants, assessing perceived difficulty, attention, confidence, and ease of error correction. Statistical analysis showed higher perception scores for the AI-assisted activity, with significant improvement in the first implementation (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p=0.024,95 {\%}$</tex> confidence) and a positive trend in the second (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p=0.053,90 {\%}$</tex> confidence). Additionally, 90% of students rated the AI feedback as helpful, and 84% reported increased confidence. Future work will focus on analyzing whether this perception translates into measurable learning gains. These results suggest that AI-assisted activities can effectively complement traditional instruction.

title: Plenary: The Role of Engineering and Computer Technology as Artificial Intelligence Turns Into Reality: A Challenge for Professional Education
authors: Melany M. Ciampi, Claudio R Brito, Maria Feldgen, Osvaldo Clua, Agatha da Silva Ovando
abstract: This paper has the goal to present a detailed account of an initiative that has been carried out continuously over the past ten years, the EDUNINE2026 Conference. Educating leaders for continuous technological change requires rethinking engineering and computing in the age of real AI. Today's professionals must go beyond mastering technical tools; they need to understand how artificial intelligence systems are designed, deployed, evaluated, and governed in dynamic, realworld environments. This means integrating strong foundations in computer science and engineering with data literacy, ethical reasoning, systems thinking, and interdisciplinary collaboration. Future leaders must be prepared to adapt to rapid innovation, guide responsible AI adoption, and make strategic decisions that balance technical possibilities with societal impact. By fostering curiosity, resilience, and a commitment to lifelong learning, educational programs can empower engineers and computing professionals to lead confidently in an era defined by continuous technological transformation.

title: PathSteiner: Improving PathFinder with Quasi-Optimal Steiner-Tree Initialization
authors: Shashwat Shrivastava, Luka Kurešević, Alexandros Poupakis, Chirag Ravishankar, Dinesh Gaitonde, Stefan Nikolić, Mirjana Stojilović
publish: 2026 IEEE 34th Annual International Symposium on Field-Programmable Custom Computing Machines (FCCM)
abstract: FPGA routing has been studied for a few decades due to its critical role in determining both the quality of results and compilation time. Recent work has shown that constructing more resource-efficient routing trees can improve both runtime and wirelength. Yet, the proposed approach of constructing multiple routing trees per net via random sink orderings—within the PathFinder routing paradigm—and selecting the best candidate becomes increasingly impractical as net fanout grows. This limitation arises because the space of possible sink orderings grows super-exponentially, allowing only a small fraction of it to be explored in practice. Moreover, even exhaustive enumeration of all sink permutations for low-fanout nets does not necessarily yield an optimal routing tree. To address these limitations, we initialize routing with quasi-optimal rectilinear Steiner trees to better cover the solution space across net fanouts. Our results show that, because these trees are only weakly congestion-aware, the final routed wirelength gains are real but fall short of the wirelength advantage implied by the seeded trees. How much of the gain remains depends on how effectively PathFinder resolves congestion while staying close to the seed. We report an average 6% improvement in wirelength, along with an 8× reduction in runtime on the most congested benchmark, which otherwise takes more than half a day to route.

title: Reconfigurable Computing Challenge: FPGA-Based WebAssembly Stack Co-Processor
authors: Qiuping Wu, Mugeng Liu, Yixuan Zhang, Hongxiao Zhao, Yihan Fu, Gang Huang, Yun Ma, Bonan Yan
publish: 2026 IEEE 34th Annual International Symposium on Field-Programmable Custom Computing Machines (FCCM)
abstract: Large language models suffer from hallucinations when performing scientific computing, motivating the use of AI agents such as IronClaw that offload computation to specialized tools. IronClaw invokes tools implemented as WebAssembly (Wasm) plugins for security and extensibility, but the stack-based Wasm bytecode is mismatched with register-based processors (x86, ARM), causing runtime overhead. We propose PAWS, a native Wasm coprocessor that directly executes Wasm bytecode in hardware. PAWS features: (1) full support for all five Wasm instruction types; (2) dual digital stack circuits (operand stack and control stack) replacing register files to minimize memory access latency; (3) dedicated control logic for block-based branching; and (4) a sliding-window instruction fetch unit that decodes variable-length Wasm instructions. Evaluated on the PolyBench suite, PAWS achieves average execution latencies 28.6× lower than an Intel Xeon processor and 40.6× lower than an Nvidia Jetson TX2, making it highly suitable for IronClaw’s compute-intensive scientific applications. The design is available at https://github.com/Iris-WQP/PAWS_FPGA_softcore.

title: A Framework for Evaluating Metro Vehicle Layouts to Maximize Satisfied Passenger Demand: A Case Study of Genoa Metro
authors: Summair Anis, Davide Giglio, Nicola Sacco
abstract: Metro systems of a region play an important role in the provision of high-speed and seamless connectivity between two stops. They also play an important role in addressing the issues related to congestion and environmental concerns while operating at underground levels. However, they often face problems related to the overcrowding of carriages and platforms which affects their efficiency and the comfort they offer to commuters. To this effect, the vehicle carriage layout plays an important role in handling the passenger demand. In this framework, this paper focuses on identifying the best layout of vehicle carriages that should be adopted during different operational periods of the metro to better serve passenger demands with the best achievable resource utilization. In doing so, a mathematical model is formulated with the aim of maximizing the average capacity utilization of metros while analyzing the resulting impacts over satisfied passenger demand. The efficiency of the proposed model is evaluated using the case study for the metro network of Genoa, Italy. In this context, a number of different scenarios of metro vehicle carriage layouts are tested to identify the best layout during two selected time periods, namely peak hour and off-peak hour periods. Overall, the results provide insights to stakeholders for making informed decisions by analyzing the average occupancy of metros in relation to the satisfied passenger demand, under different tested scenarios.

title: Transformer Fault Diagnosis Method Based on Multi-Source Domain Generalization
authors: Bingsheng Li, Yuan Li, Jiaqi Peng, Shunqing Ma, Hui Pan, Shouping Yang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: As a core device in the power system, the fault diagnosis of transformers under unknown operating conditions is crucial for ensuring the stable operation of the power grid. Traditional diagnosis methods are susceptible to changes in operating conditions, leading to decreased accuracy. Graphene vibration sensors can accurately collect vibration characteristic data from transformer cores and windings, providing a high-quality data source for fault diagnosis. Therefore, a multi-source domain generalization intelligent diagnosis algorithm that integrates data-level and feature-level strategies is proposed for fault identification of transformer vibration signals. At the data level, an improved multi-source domain data augmentation strategy is designed to mix and generalize vibration data and labels under different operating conditions and construct augmented domains, enabling information exchange between domains and enhancing sample diversity. At the feature level, a supervised contrastive learning mechanism is introduced into a multi-source domain adversarial network. While extracting domain-invariant vibration features, the contrastive loss constrains the feature space to minimize the inter-class sample distance and maximize the inter-class sample distance, thereby mining more discriminative and generalizable vibration feature representations. Experimental results show that this intelligent algorithm can effectively address the distribution shift of vibration features in transformer cross-operating condition scenarios. Compared to traditional intelligent diagnosis methods such as CNN and DDC, the accuracy of fault diagnosis under unknown operating conditions of transformers is significantly improved.

title: Coordinated Source-Network-Storage Expansion Planning Method for Active Distribution Networks Based on LSGAN Scenario Generation
authors: Shengyuan Wang, Fengzhang Luo, Jiacheng Fo, Ranfeng Mu, Lukun Ge, Zhishuang Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: To address the insufficient characterization of uncertainty and the coordinated optimization of multiple resources in active distribution network planning under high penetration of distributed renewable energy, this paper proposes a coordinated source-network-storage expansion planning method for active distribution networks based on the Least Squares Generative Adversarial Network (LSGAN). First, LSGAN is employed to learn historical wind and photovoltaic generation data and generate renewable energy operating scenarios with strong statistical representativeness. Based on these scenarios, an expansion planning model for active distribution networks is established, incorporating distributed generation, electric power soft open points (E-SOPs), and distribution network topology, with the objective of minimizing the annual comprehensive system cost. Case study results demonstrate that the scenarios generated by LSGAN exhibit a high degree of consistency with historical data in terms of probability distribution. When the uncertainty of renewable generation is considered, the overall planning investment increases slightly; however, the electricity purchased from the upstream grid is effectively reduced and the renewable energy hosting capacity is improved. The proposed method provides effective technical support for active distribution network planning under high renewable energy penetration.

title: Sparse Representation-based Plant Disease Detection using Leaf Image Processing
authors: Nooshin Derakhshan, Keyvan Asefpour Vakilian
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Existing image-based crop disease recognition approaches commonly determine multiple feature types from images of diseased plant leaves. Yet, a shared limitation remains: those discriminative features are commonly assumed to contribute uniformly to the classification decision. In this study, we present an apple leaf disease recognition pipeline composed of three sequential modules: segmenting diseased leaf images through K-means clustering, deriving shape and color features from the diseased region, and then performing classification of diseased leaf images via the sparse representation (SR) method. One notable strength of this pipeline is that conducting classification within the SR domain can notably decrease computational load while simultaneously boosting recognition accuracy. We benchmark this method against three baseline classifiers - support vector machines (SVM), artificial neural networks (ANN), and decision tree (DT) - using a leaf image dataset containing three apple leaf disease categories: black spot, frogeye leaf spot, and cedar apple rust. Experimental results indicate that the presented pipeline attains 93.9% overall accuracy, whereas the SVM, ANN, and DT achieve 76.8%, 83.7%, and 82.9%, respectively.

title: SYN Cookie Mechanism for Transmission Control Protocol SYN Flood Mitigation in LEO Satellite Networks Using Hypatia Simulator
authors: Jeevitha S, Nachiket Joshi, M. Giridhar Kumar, Pushparaghavan, G. Kaushik
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Past decade, Transmission Control Protocol (TCP) SYN flood attacks pose a significant threat to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite networks. Directly targeting the TCP connection establishment process, which is critical in high latency environment with limited satellite resources. However, existing simulation framework mainly focus on analyzing the impact of Denial of Service (DoS) and Distributed-DoS (DDoS) attacks and does not address any mitigation for protecting server resources during attacks. To overcome this limitation, this paper proposes TCP SYN Flood Mitigation using SYN Cookies (SYN-SC) model for countering TCN SYN flood attacks in LEO satellite network. The SYN-SC model integrates a SYN cookie mechanism into a TCP three-way handshake process, eliminating the need to maintain half-open connection sate and allocating resource only after successful acknowledgement. SYN-SC model is implemented and evaluated using a Hypatia simulator under TCP SYN flood attack. Simulation results demonstrate that the SYN-SC model achieves a TCP connection success rate of 95% and maintains throughput of 850 Mbps compared to baseline TCN models. These results confirm that the SYN-SC model effectively mitigates TCN SYN flood attack while maintaining stable TCP performance in LEO satellite networks.

title: Intelligent Document Querying with LLMs and Speech Input: A Scalable Semantic Framework
authors: Ranganatha. K, Veeresh Biradar, Shilpa. B, Manjula. S, Gurusiddayya Hiremath, Narayan Naik
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: The information era has created challenges in terms of retrieving domain-specific information from voluminous unstructured documents (like Education and Businesses), whether via informer attacks or database attacks. Researchers have developed an information framework that includes LlamaIndex - which does intelligent document analysis; Gradient AI - which provides superior inferences; and Astra DB - which provides the appropriate storage solutions for these systems. More recently, we have expanded our research to include varied modalities of multi-modal data such as text and voice, using different configurations, e.g., BGE for embeddings, Wav2Vec2 for speech. We verified that the use of LlamaIndex, Gradient AI and Astra DB on varying document sets produced results averaging 2.7 seconds per query with an average of the “top 3” results indicating a semantic search performance rate of 91.2%. Overall, the proposed framework, when compared to existing technologies such as LangChain (88.4%) and Haystack <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(86.9 \%)$</tex>, has produced the highest level of performance. The response to user queries has been done in an informative and concise manner inline with the context and has been achieved via the use of RESTful APIs. The proposed model is ready to be tested in real-world applications and will be made available for additional trials beginning early next year.

title: Design & Development of an AI-ML Driven Image Processing Framework for Accurate Brain Tumor Detection Using UFBI, IoT & SFA
authors: T. C. Manjunath
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Malignant brain cancer is ultimate outcome of uncontrolled proliferation of brain cells, which in turn causes a host of other neurological disorders. A background survey is given w.r.t. significant obstacles to early diagnosis that can be overcome with use of Computer-Aided-Diagnosis system. One of these obstacles is the proper identification of various diseases in MRI images. To improve accuracy of (MRI) scans we use the method of malignancy concepts. Finally, image containing brain tumor is located in the quadrant with highest MSF. Present research compares the suggested architecture to existing models that are discussed in order to demonstrate its merit. MRI brain tumor detection accuracy results of an impressive 97 % with a precision of 95 %, sensitivity of 98 %, a specificity of 96 %, a F1score with 97 %. Their experiment & simulation results conclude that developed architecture is robust, leading to an improvement in detection accuracy of brain tumors in various locations.

title: Smart Industrial Liquid Level Solution for Textile and Process Industry
authors: Humayun Khan, Fahama Sheikh, Hashir Waqar, Riaz Uddin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robotics and Automation in Industry (ICRAI)
abstract: In the field of industrial automation, real-time liquid level monitoring in large-scale tank systems is extremely important to increase the production efficiency. This is especially true for textile and process industries where hundreds of tanks require continuous monitoring of parameters such as liquid-level, flow, volume, concentration, wastage, and plant capacity. This paper introduces NED-Smart Industrial Liquid Level (NED-SILL), an innovative, low-cost IoT-enabled device designed for non-contact ultrasonic measurement of liquid levels up to 6 meters (19.6 feet). The device is integrated with an ESP8266 microcontroller for its Wi-Fi connectivity, an AJSR04T waterproof ultrasonic sensor mounted on the tank roof to compute surface distance via time-of-flight principles, a 3Dprinted enclosure to increase its durability, and an OLED display for real time monitoring. Raw sensor data is refined using a Kalman filter which suppresses environmental noise and improves accuracy, while processed readings are transmitted in real-time to a cloud-based Google Sheets platform via Sheets API for timestamped data logging, multi-unit conversions, volume/flow calculations, and analytical insights displayed on the custom-built mobile application. Device configuration includes Wi-Fi credentials, calibration parameters, and data transmission intervals which are smoothly handled through an access point mode, to eliminate the need for proprietary applications or complex setups. The device is capable of 24/7 operation across a temperature range of $-20^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ to $70^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, while also being adept in accommodating diverse industrial liquids (e.g., caustics, acids, bases, oils, fuels). Through rigorous industrial validation of two years, the device has demonstrated cost-effectiveness, ease of deployment, reliability and real-time accessibility compared to other conventional systems, which has encouraged advanced cyber-physical integration in industrial environments.

title: Green-NAS: A Global-Scale Multi-Objective Neural Architecture Search for Robust and Efficient Edge-Native Weather Forecasting
authors: Md Muhtasim Munif Fahim, Soyda Humyra Yesmin, Saiful Islam, Md. Palash Bin Faruque, Md. A. Salam, Md. Mahfuz Uddin, Samiul Islam, Tofayel Ahmed, Md. Binyamin, Md. Rezaul Karim
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: We introduce Green-NAS, a multi-objective neural architecture search (NAS) framework designed for low-resource environments using weather forecasting as a case study. By adhering to ‘Green AI’ principles, the framework explicitly minimizes computational energy costs and carbon footprints, prioritizing sustainable deployment over raw computational scale. The Green-NAS architecture search method is optimized for both model accuracy and efficiency to find lightweight models with high accuracy and very few model parameters; this is accomplished through an optimization process that simultaneously optimizes multiple objectives. Our best-performing model, Green-NAS-A, achieved an RMSE of 0.0988 (i.e., within 1.4% of our manually tuned baseline) using only 153K model parameters, which is 239 times fewer than other globally applied weather forecasting models, such as GraphCast. In addition, we also describe how the use of transfer learning improves the weather forecasting accuracy by approximately 5.2%, in comparison to a naive approach of training a new model for each city, when there is limited historical weather data available for that city.

title: Adaptive Hybrid Swarm-Reinforcement Learning Model for Public Basketball Courses Teaching Based on TPACK Framework
authors: Xiaoyan Zhao, Chenweiwen Liu
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: From the past few years, the effective teaching of public basketball courses in colleges and universities has become an important component in enhancing students' physical literacy, engagement, and technical proficiency. In particular, with the proposal of the TPACK framework, the teaching efficiency of basketball public courses has been further enhanced. However, traditional models significantly depend on static assessment parameters and small, homogeneous datasets collected from single institutions, which restrict their flexibility and generalization in various academic atmospheres. To overcome these limitations, this research proposes an Adaptive Hybrid Swarm-Reinforcement Learning (HSRL) framework incorporated with a Multi-Campus Basketball Teaching Dataset (MCBT-Set) to attain intelligent and dynamic reliability evaluation. Initially, data is collected from unified multi-source dataset which consist of objective performance, contextual, and behavioral data from various universities through edge-enabled devices. Then, an adaptive HSRL model fuses swarm intelligence with Reinforcement Learning (RL) to enhance dependability parameters in real time, allowing self-tuning of the evaluation process. Furthermore, the system endlessly progresses its decision boundaries by learning from new student data and feedback loops by confirming the persistent development in accuracy and responsiveness. This hybrid datadriven and adaptive optimization approach enhances evaluation fairness, improves instructional feedback, promotes sustained student interest and performance, and improve the teaching efficiency of public basketball courses under the TPACK framework. Experimental results demonstrated that the proposed HSRL framework achieves higher results in terms of Improvement Rate (IR) as 66.37 %, compared to existing static evaluation methods.

title: Developing Custom Arc Flash Protection Logic for Non-Standard BESS Substation Configurations
authors: Ivan Burdukov
publish: 2026 International Conference on Development and Application Systems (DAS)
abstract: The integration of large-scale battery energy storage systems (BESS) into medium-voltage distribution networks introduces complex protection challenges, particularly concerning arc flash protection (AFP) and fault current supervision. This paper investigates the vulnerabilities of implementing highly selective AFP in a non-standard 35 kV BESS topology equipped with a grounding transformer and a neutral grounding resistor. In such configurations, the specific routing of zero-sequence currents during single-line-to-ground faults fundamentally alters the network's fault signature. Consequently, conventional overcurrent supervision algorithms become unreliable, risking complete failure to trip during severe enclosed arc faults. To resolve this critical safety gap, the dynamic fault current distribution across multiple operational modes is analyzed using DIgSILENT PowerFactory. Based on this analysis, a custom, topology-adaptive protection logic is developed to ensure secure phase and ground fault supervision without introducing unnecessary complexity. The proposed logical scheme guarantees absolute selectivity and was successfully verified and commissioned in microprocessor-based intelligent electronic devices, demonstrating robust stability and operational reliability in a live industrial environment.

title: Research on Automatic Fault Diagnosis System for Marine Electrical Equipment Based on Computer Vision
authors: Shengjie Zhang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Background: Marine electrical systems are subjected to severe environmental factors (kicking, high moisture levels, salt spray), and as such, marine faults are prevalent such as overheating, loose connections, etc. which pose a threat to navigation. Manual inspection is a process that is not efficient and cannot be sustained in continuous monitoring requirement in vessel automation. Approaches: A smart fault diagnostic system, which consists of deep learning and multimodal vision, is suggested and it is built over an extended ResNet-50. It applies use of multi-scale feature fusion and channel attention mechanisms to gain more fault recognition with a customized image enhancement strategy (defogging, illumination correction, denoising) to marine environmental interference. Results: The trained system with 8, 500 annotated samples of 7 common fault classes recognizes results with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 4. 3 \%}$</tex> accuracy at an average of 0.18 seconds/image and outperforms the traditional CNN methods by 8.7 points. Tests aboard certify its close-to-perfect performance as a malfunction detector with 0 false omissions and a false alarm of 2.1 percent. In conclusion In this paper, it has been shown that the system is a dependable, real-time autonomous monitoring system of marine electrical equipment that will solve the major issues of environmental flexibility and diagnostic efficiency in maritime conditions.

title: Proceedings of the 27th ACM SIGPLAN/SIGBED International Conference on Languages, Compilers, and Tools for Embedded Systems

title: Decoupling Control of a Multi-segment Hybrid-Actuated Soft Origami Continuum Robot Through Variable Stiffness_supp4-3700107.mp4
authors: Erbao Dong
abstract: Soft origami continuum robots have attracted considerable attention because of their flexibility and adaptability, but they face challenges in control accuracy. This study presents a novel design and control scheme for large-deformation hybrid-actuated soft origami continuum robots to improve their motion performance in real tasks. An origami-based pneumatic chamber is used as the robot backbone to achieve a high extension ratio, and the hybrid variable stiffness principle combining antagonistic actuation and layer jamming further enhances the robot's overall structural stiffness. The robot performs precise movements by controlling tendons distributed in an external origami. An iterative training strategy based on long short-term memory is employed to model the inverse kinematics of the robot in consideration of the inherent hysteresis of origami robots. Step size features are introduced to improve model accuracy with limited data. The capability for variable stiffness enables the migration of the training model on the basis of a single segment, the accuracy of which is close to the submillimeter level. Decoupling control of each segment for a rear-driven multi-segment origami continuum robot is also achieved. Experiment results reveal that the trajectory tracking errors for single-segment and multi-segment of the robot are 1.58, and 2.81 mm, respectively, with relative errors of 0.71% and 0.63% over the robot length, demonstrating the good performance of the proposed design and control method. Orientation data can also be added to the dataset to achieve orientation control of the robot, with an angle error of 0.75°. The robot shows its stability and safety in a series of real tasks, including writing, LED tracking, board cleaning, and pick and place. This study demonstrates a potential solution for continuum robots performing precise tasks without any sensory feedback in confined spaces and specialized environments where electronic sensors fail.

title: Waveguide-Compatible Terahertz Direct Amplitude Modulator Based on a Schottky Barrier Diode Reconfigurable Square-Ring Unit
authors: Chunyang Bi, Zhichao Bi, Zhixiang Xu, Yu Ao, Sen Gong, Shixiong Liang, Ziqiang Yang, Yaxin Zhang
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: Direct amplitude modulation above 300GHz is challenged by strong device parasitics, high structural sensitivity, and limited separation between high- and low-transmission states. This letter presents a waveguide-compatible terahertz (THz) direct amplitude modulator using a Schottky barrier diode (SBD) reconfigurable square-ring unit. Two Schottky diodes are mounted across the gaps between metal patches and form a reconfigurable square-ring unit with the metal structure. With bias control, the unit changes between an open-ring, weak-loading state and a closed-ring, resonant-loading state. When the SBDs are cutoff, the unit weakly loads the incident THz field and maintains a high transmission. When the SBDs conduct, the metal patches and diodes form a closed square ring, which enhances the local resonance and suppresses the transmitted signal. Measurements show clear static transmission modulation above 300GHz and OOK-NRZ direct amplitude modulation up to 25Gb/s. The proposed structure provides a practical route for waveguide-compatible, THz direct amplitude modulation above 300GHz.

title: AirDC: Adaptive Iterative Depth Refinement Framework for Full-Range Metric Depth Completion
authors: Hongyu Shi, Yunqi Du, Hongjuan Zhang, Wenzhuo Li, Zhen Dong, Bijun Li, Luliang Tang
abstract: Accurate metric depth completion across wide depth ranges is critical for autonomous systems. However, existing methods often struggle to efficiently capture depth features at both close and long ranges, primarily due to the inadequate modeling of fine-grained depth cues specific to different depth ranges. To address these limitations, we propose AirDC, an adaptive iterative depth refinement framework for full-range metric depth completion. The core contributions of our model lie in the design of two key modules. Specifically, we first construct an adaptive fine-grained stereo-LiDAR feature fusion module to fundamentally strengthen the model’s capacity to preserve original full-range depth information. Built upon metric-aligned depth volumes (i.e., a 3D representation composed of cubic voxels uniformly partitioned in real-world metric space), this module employs an adaptive sub-voxel depth attention mechanism to enhance sensitivity to subtle depth variations across the full range, thereby both avoiding long-range accuracy degradation introduced by conventional disparity conversion and alleviating the coarse near-range granularity inherent in metric depth representations. Second, we introduce an iterative hypothesis-guided depth refinement module to improve prediction accuracy while maintaining memory efficiency. By integrating multi-scale multi-modal guidance information from depth hypotheses, this module enables explicit and progressive refinement of the initial depth estimation with a small parameter overhead. Experiments on multiple mainstream real-world and synthetic benchmarks demonstrate that AirDC achieves state-of-the-art performance, providing an effective solution for full-range metric depth completion. The code and data are available at https://github.com/yunqidu/AirDC.

title: Automated Multi-Class Nail Disease Detection using Deep Learning Architectures
authors: Md. Shahriar Imtiaz, Morshed Al Jaber Bishal, Sanviraj Aynul Siam, Samiul Alam Rafi, Md. Mazid-Ul-Haque, Sultanul Arifeen Hamim, Muhammad Firoz Mridha
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Nail abnormalities are important indicators of systemic diseases but are often underdiagnosed owing to subjective evaluation and limited dermatological expertise. Early detection is clinically crucial, as these conditions may indicate infectious, inflammatory, neoplastic or cardiopulmonary disorders. Traditional diagnostic approaches face challenges of subjectivity, cost, and specialist scarcity, highlighting the need for automated solutions to address these issues. This study introduces MSIA, a MobileNetV2-based model a domain-specific data augmentation for multi-class nail disease detection. The architecture is lightweight, enabling deployment in resourcelimited settings, and incorporates saliency map visualization to enhance interpretability and clinical trust. Evaluation on a realworld nail disease dataset demonstrated 98% accuracy and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 6. 9 9 \%}$</tex> F1-score, outperforming standard CNN, VGG19, and GM (GCNN + MobileNet) models in accuracy, generalization, and computational efficiency. MSIA is a cost-effective, interpretable, and accurate diagnostic tool that facilitates the early recognition of nail disorders, particularly in underprivileged healthcare environments. The future study will consider the use of GCNNbased models to achieve higher diagnostic performance. This study complied with ethical standards and used only publicly available datasets.

title: On the Sum of Inverse Gamma RVs and its Application to Wireless Communications Networks
authors: Petros S. Bithas, Liang Yang, Qiuming Zhu, Hector E. Nistazakis, Daniel Benevides Da Costa
abstract: Investigating the statistics of the sum of random variables (RVs) is fundamental in wireless communication systems, since it can be used to estimate the outage probability and error rates. In this paper, exact closed-form expressions are derived for the probability density function (PDF) and cumulative distribution function (CDF) of the sum of inverse-gamma (IG) RVs. The IG distribution is especially suitable for performance analysis purposes because it provides a tractable yet accurate model of shadowing/local mean power variations. Unlike prior studies on sums of shadowing RVs, the resulting formulas are simple to evaluate and numerically stable. Their utility is demonstrated in two practical settings: unmanned aerial vehicle (UAVs)-assisted networks and high-speed railway communications. Across all scenarios, the analytical results agree with Monte Carlo simulations to within machine precision, while the asymptotic approximations closely track the exact results.

title: Bringing Distributed Ordering to Heterogeneous Memory Consistency
authors: Yanpeng Yu, Nicolai Oswald, Anurag Khandelwal
abstract: CORD is a novel cache-coherence protocol that brings distributed-systems techniques to tightly coupled heterogeneous processors, unlocking efficient and programmer-friendly shared memory for emerging heterogeneous workloads such as AI/ML. Cache-coherence protocols have historically enforced consistency at the source processor, inflating application latency and interconnect traffic. Central to CORD’s efficiency is its reframing of heterogeneous coherence as a distributed-systems problem: drawing on logical-timestamp algorithms for event ordering, CORD assigns consistency-tailored timestamps to write-through stores and orders them at the cache directory — near each store’s completion point at the LLC. In our evaluation, CORD approaches message-passing efficiency while incurring a fraction of the programming complexity, with negligible storage, area, and power overheads. The result is a concrete demonstration that long-standing architectural challenges in shared-memory designs can be tackled through the lens of distributed systems.

title: Synergizing Digital Twin and UAV for Intelligent Network Recovery and Management in Smart Cities
authors: Dong Jia, Qiang Ye
abstract: Resilient network connectivity is critical to the operation of smart cities, yet post-disaster network recovery faces significant challenges, including unknown network traffic dynamics, partial observability, and distinctive user mobility patterns. While uncrewed aerial vehicles (UAVs) offer a rapid solution for recovering connectivity, their effectiveness depends on accurate and continuously updated network state estimation. In this article, we propose a digital-twin-assisted network recovery and management (DT-NRM) framework that tightly synergizes DT evolution with UAV operations through two-stage closed-loop DT lifecycles, comprising DT lifecycle 1 (DT-C1) and DT lifecycle 2 (DT-C2). In DT-C1, the DT model is established under partial network observability using incomplete post-disaster traffic data by integrating a graph neural network (GNN)-based traffic prediction model with a first-stage residual-based calibration. In DT-C2, DT-assisted UAV deployment is conducted, which restores network connectivity in disaster-affected areas, while UAV-assisted data collection and aerial-terrestrial data fusion progressively refine DT fidelity through continuous physical-digital information synchronization. The DT-UAV synergy is then realized through continuous DT-UAV interplay that jointly improves network recovery performance and DT accuracy. A case study is presented to demonstrate that the proposed DT-NRM framework effectively captures post-disaster traffic dynamics and outperforms state-of-the-art approaches. We further discuss some open research directions on the interplay between DT and generative artificial intelligence (GAI) for supporting future smart city applications with higher intelligence.

title: Real-Time Propagation Model Parameter Inversion-Aided Indoor Positioning: Integrating PDR with LTE Signal-Based Proximity/Fingerprinting Methods
authors: Jingnan Tian, Li Cong, Honglei Qin
abstract: Integrating Long-Term Evolution (LTE) signal which are readily available through smartphones and stable in indoor environments with Pedestrian Dead Reckoning (PDR) provides a low-cost and accurate solution for indoor positioning. Existing LTE-based indoor positioning methods typically rely on fingerprinting, where databases are built using offline-collected data or regression models. These approaches, however, often ignore the influence of building structure on signal propagation, which limits database accuracy and degrades positioning performance. In fact, some studies have applied propagation models to improve database efficiency and accuracy. However, they primarily focus on signals from indoor stations such as Wi-Fi, and generally use fixed model parameters, making them sensitive to environmental changes and device heterogeneity. To overcome these limitations, this paper introduces an indoor positioning method that integrates LTE signal with PDR, aided by real-time inversion of propagation model parameters. Firstly, the spatial distribution characteristics of LTE signal are analyzed, then a suitable propagation model is selected. Secondly, the model parameters are inversely estimated in real time to enhance environmental and device adaptability. The propagation model is then applied for database construction, and position observations are extracted through fingerprint matching. Meanwhile, the proximity method is introduced to maintain positioning performance as well as support parameter inversion with reliable position references. Finally, the observations extracted from LTE signal are combined with PDR to achieve positioning. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method enhances the adaptability of the fingerprint databases to device and environmental changes without requiring prior data, thereby improving positioning performance.

title: Lecturify AI: Smart Lectures to Multiple Choice Questions Generator
authors: R Ranjana, S R Indurekaa, V Eswaramurthy, S Saran, V Tamilarasu, G Shanmuga Vishnu
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: There is a lot of time taken to grade papers for students and the time for teachers to lose the ability to improve the accuracy of their grades through correction materials. Manual procedures have created inconsistencies in terms of quality of the assessment process, which contributes to delays in the ability to create assessments. This paper proposes a system called Lecturify AI, a technology that can convert audio, video and text lecture material into a structured way to create multiple choice questions (MCQs). This technology combines several components including Speech to Text (STT), Optical Character Recognition (OCR) and NLP to get the important concepts from the materials. The constructed MCQs will be assigned to different levels of cognitive learning as outlined by Bloom's Taxonomy or how deep a student can learn about any given concept with respect to the various cognitive processes used to evaluate readiness for the assessment process. The teacher is able to assess the constructed assessments from one web-based portal, and this will give the student an opportunity to use the assessments as study aids. Therefore, the purpose of this system will provide digital tools and resources will promote efficient and effective use of technology to allow for increased accessibility and scalability of learning be delivered using technology.

title: Efficient Text-to-Image Retrieval Using CLIP Embeddings: A Comparative Study of Faiss Indexes
authors: Mohammad Rezwanul Huq, Tasnova Haque Mazumder, Moniruzzaman Sarkar
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This study provides an overview of the performance of three FAISS indexing strategies-Flat, IVF, and HNSW-for a text-to-image retrieval system based on CLIP embeddings. Metrics such as search latency, mean similarity, Recall{@}K, and Precision{@}K are analyzed to measure retrieval efficiency and accuracy. The results indicate that although the Flat index achieves perfect recall, it incurs higher computational latency. In contrast, IVF and HNSW significantly improve search efficiency, with HNSW yielding higher Recall{@}K and better approximate retrieval quality. These findings highlight the speed-accuracy trade-off in large-scale vector search and provide practical guidance for selecting indexing approaches for real-time retrieval applications.

title: Robust PI Control with Improved Performance for Nonlinear Systems under Saturated Actuation
authors: Zeqiang Li, Jason J. R. Liu, George A. Rovithakis, Yongduan Song
abstract: It is interesting and challenging to investigate the problem of prescribed performance constraints of uncertain nonlinear systems subject to inelastic control input saturation due to their potential conflicts. By designing a novel reference compensation mechanism that depends simultaneously on the input saturation error and the relative distance between performance boundaries and tracking errors, the reference signals are adaptively modified only when absolutely necessary, making the tracking control with pre-specified performance attributes affordable even in the presence of strict input constraints. In addition, a robust proportional-integral (PI) control scheme featuring with structure simplification and disturbance rejection is developed for a class of nonlinear systems with mismatched uncertainties. It is proved that all the closed-loop signals are bounded, and the proposed method can also ensure the output tracking performance. Numerical simulation results verify and clarify the effectiveness and superiority of the theoretical results.

title: Explainable and Trustworthy AI for Data-Driven Infrastructure Decision-Making
authors: Hemraj Parate, Kishore Bandela, Kishore Subramanya Hebbar, Amey Waikar, Suraj Balaso Desai, Anil Kumar Kotte, B. Nithya
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This paper will discuss how to develop Explainable and Trustworthy AI to decide on infrastructure in a data-driven way with a particular focus on Transportation Infrastructure and Highway Engineering. This paper suggests a Hybrid Explainable AI model combined with Federated Learning to overcome major challenges of Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) and Smart Infrastructure. The proposed approach, based on the use of TensorFlow Federated (TFF), can guarantee privacy of data by training models in a decentralized manner, as well as increase the level of decision transparency with the help of such tools as SHAP and LIME. This blend of high-tech approaches will lead to the development of Decision-Centric AI Systems that are not only maximizing traffic flow and infrastructure planning but also in line with the concepts of Responsible Infrastructure AI. This research has shown that the hybrid solution can enhance scalability, interpretability, and real-time decision-making performance, being a strong solution to the implementation of AI-driven smart infrastructure systems in the city and highway context.

title: Surrogate Validation of a Graph Attention Network (GATv2) for Detecting Psychotic Biomarkers: Implications for Early Diagnosis in Maternal Mental Health
authors: Md Merajul Islam, Ajay Krishno Sarkar, Mohammod Abdul Motin, Abid Ahasan, Mithela Mehenaz, Hassan Jubair
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Postpartum Psychosis (PP) is a severe psychiatric emergency carrying high risks of suicide and infanticide, yet the development of automated diagnostic tools is impeded by the scarcity of labeled maternal EEG datasets. To address this data gap, this study proposes a surrogate validation framework, hypothesizing that the “Gamma-band topological disintegration” observed in established chronic psychosis serves as a reliable neurological proxy for the acute functional disconnection characteristic of PP. We developed a Graph Attention Network (GATv2) to analyze functional connectivity in the Gamma frequency band <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(30-50 \text{Hz})$</tex>, validating the model on a benchmark Chronic Schizophrenia EEG dataset. The proposed framework achieved a classification accuracy of 75.0% and an Area Under the Curve (AUC) of 0.76. Crucially for a clinical screening tool, the model demonstrated a high Sensitivity of 81.7%, successfully identifying the specific Frontal-Temporal dysconnectivity signature associated with psychotic states. These results establish a computational proof-of-concept, demonstrating that topology-aware deep learning can reliably detect psychotic biomarkers, paving the way for transfer learning applications in low-resource maternal healthcare.

title: Trust in AI Recommendations on Social Media and Green Purchase Intention: Evidence from Social Commerce Users in Vietnam
authors: Hong Minh Ngoc Tran, Anh Phan Ngoc Que, Nhu Tran Huynh Tam
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This research focuses on examining the role of trust in artificial intelligence (AI) recommendations on social media and its effect on green purchase intention of social commerce users in Vietnam. Drawing on previous studies on algorithm based recommendations, personalization and green consumption, the research has put forward a parsimonious model where green purchase intention could be explained by trust in AI recommendations, the quality of AI personalization, social influence, green concern and privacy concern. Data was collected using an online questionnaire from 316 respondents who self identified themselves to have a green living orientation using a convenience sampling approach. Multiple linear regression results show that level of trust in AI recommendations is a positive predictor of green purchase intention with the highest absolute value. AI personalization quality, social influence and green concern also have positive associations with green purchase intention, which suggested a role in intention formation in social commerce for both technology allowed relevance and social and environmental motivations. In contrast, the privacy concern is negatively related to the green purchase intention, implying that data risks perceived can prohibit green purchase intention despite the usefulness of recommendations. The results imply the dual nature of AI in green marketing, in which algorithmic trust and personalization can help people develop intention and privacy violations can limit it. The study has some practical implications for social commerce platforms and marketers in Vietnam including the importance of trustworthy recommendation environments, the use of careful personalization practices and the need for privacy assurance mechanisms to accommodate green consumption.

title: Machine Learning-Assisted Sampling Frequency Optimization for Traveling-Wave Fault Analysis in Two-Ended Power Transmission Networks
authors: Md. Abu Salman, Mehedi Hassan, Md. Mehedi Hasan, Md. Sohel Rana
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The continuous growth in electrical power demand necessitates highly reliable and fast fault detection and localization techniques for modern transmission networks. This study presents an advanced Traveling Wave (TW) based Fault Detection and Localization Algorithm (FDLA) for a two-ended transmission network comprising two synchronous generators with parallel-connected loads. The suggested system setup is initially verified for realistic operating conditions using steadystate Load Flow Analysis (LFA). After that, the TW voltage and current signals from both ends of the transmission line are used to find faults and locate their exact location in a number of different fault scenarios. A Machine Learning (ML) method based on Polynomial Regression (PR) is used to find the Optimum Sampling Frequency (OSF) for the faulty signals, which is employed by a Python program. This method focuses on the sampling rate point that gives the lowest percentage of localization error (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<=0.001 \%$</tex>). This optimization with the help of ML makes the TW-based fault analysis much more reliable and accurate. A MATLAB code is used to implement the full FDLA framework, which includes signal processing, fault distance, and error computation. The results in MATLAB/Simulink show that the proposed method finds faults with high accuracy and low computational error, which is superior to traditional methods. The suggested method is excellent for protecting high-speed connections in modern twoended transmission networks.

title: Gesture Controlled Robotic Hand Designed for Enhancing Industrial Automation and Innovation
authors: Fairooz Alam, Shamvi Md Abdullah, Saidur Rahman, Afnan Ur Rayan, Md Kauser Islam Robayet, Emran Ahmed Tanzim, Sajid Hossain
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The robotic hand was controlled by hand gestures using a camera to track hand movement and replicate those motions. No wearable sensors or physical controllers were required for this research. The system was built using cost effective materials, which made it cheaper to produce. Simple components were used, which made the system easier to repair. It was developed to reduce direct human involvement in hazardous environments. At the centre of this work, computer vision was applied. Gestures were captured through MediaPipe, a pretrained machine learning model. It was used to detect 21 hand landmarks and calculate finger angles. In addition, a wrist mode was implemented to control the motion of the hand itself. This allowed a greater range of mobility. The core principle of the gesture controlled robotic hand was to examine whether an artificial intelligence integrated system could support industrial tasks in a safe and affordable manner while promoting resilient infrastructure, sustainable industrialization, and responsible technological innovation aligned with SDG 9 goals.

title: CardioTML: ESP32-Based TinyML Wearable System for Early Identification of Cardiac Risk to Enhance Well-Being and Support Healthcare
authors: Nahian Akber Chowdhury, Md Iftakhar Ahsan Jarif, Tanzil Ahmed Rahin, Saruar Hossain Shovo, Md Sajid Hossain
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Cardio vascular disease (CVDs) are a leading cause of mortality worldwide, emphasizing the need for early detection and continuous monitoring to reduce emergency outcomes. This paper proposes an ESP32 hybrid edge-cloud wearable platform for real-time cardiac risk analysis using ECG and vital signs. The proposed CardioTML system combines an AD8232 ECG sensor and a MAX30102 PPG sensor for ECG, heart rate (BPM), and oxygen saturation (SpO2) measurements. A digital low-pass filter and 187-point morphological segmentation are also designed for real-time processing. The proposed system was tested on the MIT-BIH Arrhythmia Database, while maintaining the official training and testing splits, three classifiers: Logistic Regression, KNN, and Random Forest, Random Forest reported the highest Macro F1-score of 0.9589 for multi-class risk classification. Inference is performed using a cloud-based Flask API in a hybrid edge-cloud setup, and medical safety limits from WHO and AHA guidelines are incorporated to send emergency notifications through Telegram bot. The proposed framework offers a cost-effective and scalable approach for preventive cardiac surveillance and can be extended to a TinyML-based fully embedded system in the future. The system provides a low-cost, scalable solution supporting preventive healthcare, contributing to SDG 3 (Good Health and Well-Being) and promoting accessible remote monitoring.

title: Screen-Printed Electrodes for Advanced Sensing: Multiphysics Approach and Experimental Validation
authors: Md. Nazmul Islam, MD Raiyan Firoz, Md. Emon Hasan Sourav, Md Tawabur Rahman, Mohammad Abu Yousuf
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Screen-printed electrodes (SPEs) have become popular in electrochemical sensing, mainly because they are inexpensive and suitable for portable use. In this study, a three-dimensional multiphysics simulation model was built in COMSOL Multiphysics and then checked against experimental cyclic voltammetry (CV) data. To describe the CV response, Butler-Volmer kinetics together with Nernst-Planck diffusion was included in the model. The CV experiments were carried out using a ferri/ferrocyanide redox system, with scan rates ranging from 10 to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$100 \text{mV} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>. The simulation results followed the experimental trends reasonably well, and both anodic and cathodic peak currents showed a strong correlation with the measured data <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}} \boldsymbol{\approx} \mathbf{0. 9 9})$</tex>. It was also found that the peak current increased with the square root of the scan rate, which is characteristic of diffusion-controlled behavior. The anodic electroactive surface area was estimated to be between 4.3 and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.8 \text{mm}^{2}$</tex>, while the cathodic surface area was slightly higher, lying between 6.0 and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7.4 \text{mm}^{2}$</tex> over the examined scan rates. As a whole this framework can be used as a reliable tool for analyzing SPE-based electrochemical sensors.

title: A Multitask Crisscross Network for One-Shot Bearing Multiattribute Fault Diagnosis
authors: Fei Wang, Guangyu Jia, Jianbin Qiu
abstract: The scarcity of samples presents a significant challenge in data-driven fault diagnosis, particularly for bearing malfunctions where swift shutdowns are imperative to avert accidents. This operational constraint makes it difficult to obtain bearing fault data, resulting in catastrophic performance on the test set, particularly when only a single sample is available for each fault. This article proposes a multitask crisscross network (MTCCN) for one-shot fault diagnosis based on transfer learning theory. The semantic information of faults is treated as attributes, serving as knowledge to be transferred. A multitask learning approach is used to predict multiple attributes, which provides more valuable fault information. The horizontal structure of MTCCN is a task-sharing network, where global features are extracted for task-specific networks to predict attributes. The vertical structure consists of an attribute classifier chain (ACC), and the correlation between attributes is modeled by a directed acyclic graph (DAG) for the attribute prediction tasks. An information map integrates multiple heterogeneous sources of information to improve prediction accuracy. Finally, extensive experiments were conducted on the different datasets, and the transfer effect was quantified, which demonstrated the efficiency and robustness of MTCCN.

title: Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer Recurrence Prediction from CT Scans Using a Hybrid CNN-Vision Transformer Model
authors: Abu Kausar, Kishon Kumar Pashi, Samiya Islam Asha, Gawshil Rahman Rifat, Md. Salah Uddin, Fahim Fardin
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) in analyzing computed tomography (CT) images has significantly advanced computerassisted diagnosis (CAD) systems for lung cancer (LC). Nevertheless, there are complications like the complicated anatomy, superimposition of radiographic features and mobile positions of abnormalities which complicate obtaining the features to be used in successful CAD implementation. The cause of lung cancer is a major cause of disability in the entire world. The initial diagnosis is vital in preventing the progression and the development of effective management strategies. In this paper, the author suggests Hybrid-RViT, a hybrid neural network that combines both the local texture capture of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and limited global context modeling capability of Vision Transformers (ViTs) to predict non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) recurrence with high accuracy on CT scans. The framework also presents a number of innovations: Smoothbased data augmentation (Gaussian blur, median filtering, bilateral smoothing) to increase the size of the dataset to 6,000 to augment the representation in transformers; CLAHE-based contrast enhancement to enhance the visibility of noisy images; and Intensity-Based Patch Tokenization (IBPT), which divides the CT images into low-, mid-, and high-intensity regions to increase the representation in transformers to 12 channels. CNN and ViT branch features are fused by Multi-Head Cross-Attention (MHCA) mechanism and they are classified by a ResMLP head. The Hybrid-RViT using ResNet50 obtained an accuracy of 97.12 percent and a macro-F1 of 0.97 and ROC-AUC of 0.9982 which is a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 9. 2 8}$</tex> percent absolute and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 4. 7 7}$</tex> relative increase over the baseline Balanced ViT. To make it clinical, Explainable AI (XAI) techniques were used, namely, Grad-CAM, SHAP, LIME, used to make it interpretable. The trained model was quantized using TensorFlow Lite to run on real time as a light weight, transparent and efficient diagnostic system that could be used on early lung cancer screening on a low resource clinical setting.former (ViT), Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), CrossAttention Fusion, Explainable AI (XAI), CT Scan

title: Machine Learning-Based Comparative Study for Predicting Earthquake Probability
authors: Md Ahasan Habib, Hridoy Pal, Md. Amirul Islam, Md Sabbir Ahamed, Md. Musfiqur Rahman Foysal, Rehnuma Tasnim
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Seismic processes exhibit complicated and nonlinear behavior, making earthquake forecasting difficult. Earthquake prediction is framed in this study as a regression problem. It uses laboratory acoustic emission signals from the LANL Earthquake Prediction dataset to predict continuous time-tofailure (TTF). Support Vector Regression (SVR), Kernel Ridge Regression (KRR), and Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM) are the three regression models used to analyze statistical information taken from segmented acoustic windows. To provide fair comparison, all models are assessed using the same preprocessing procedures and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0}$</tex>-fold cross-validation. SVR has the lowest mean absolute error, according to the results, even if there aren't many variations between the models. Overall, a systematic comparison of regression techniques for controlled seismic forecasting scenarios is presented in this article.

title: STAIR-Tr: Transformer-based Gap Filling for Multi-source Satellite Data Fusion
authors: Kejie Zhao, Kaiyu Guan, Qu Zhou, Sibo Wang, Sheng Wang, Yizhi Huang, Alexander Schwing
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Satellite imagery offers consistent and scalable data for large-scale ecosystem monitoring and provides inputs for earth system modeling. However, two primary challenges hinder the applications of satellite images: (1) the presence of data gaps due to clouds or sensor malfunctions, and (2) the trade-offs between spatial and temporal resolutions of an individual satellite sensor. Multi-source satellite data fusion is widely adopted to address these challenges, such as ESTARFM and STAIR. These methods heavily rely on cloud-free images for reference and are either manually intensive or computationally expensive, potentially limiting their scalability and application to large regions. In this study, we proposed a novel transformer-based framework that addresses these challenges by efficiently filling gaps and fusing multi-source optical satellite data, STAIR-Tr (STAIR-Transformer). Our approach utilized a transformer-based model for gap filling, followed by a multi-sensor fusion model that generates high-resolution, daily, and cloud-free satellite data. Specifically, we tested STAIR-Tr for fusing Landsat-8 (16-day and 30-m) and MODIS (daily and 500-m) data at three regions in North and South America. Compared with STAIR, STAIR-Tr significantly improved the accuracy and efficiency of gap filling, with improvements in normalized Mean Absolute Error (nMAE) from 4.83% to 3.48%, the coefficient of determination (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">${\mathrm{R}}^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula>) from 0.763 to 0.883, an 18-fold speedup with GPU acceleration, and a 2.3-fold cost reduction. Compared with ESTARFM, STAIR-Tr overcame the limitation of requiring cloud-free images and expanded the pool of available reference images, which further enhanced the fusion process, with an increase in <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">${\mathrm{R}}^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> from 0.51 to 0.64 and a decrease in nMAE from 23.2% to 20.4%. STAIR-Tr improves the accuracy and efficiency of multi-source satellite data fusion using a transformer-based gap filling architecture, making it more suitable for applications requiring frequent and high-resolution satellite observations.

title: An Intelligent Machine Learning Approach to Guide Patients Toward the Appropriate Level of Healthcare in Bangladesh
authors: Md. Niamul Ferdous Rahi, Sudwip Roy Antor, Md. Arifuzzaman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: In Bangladesh, people often go to secondary level health care centers and tertiary level specialized hospitals when he have no severe health problem. As a result, these Secondary and Tertiary level healthcare centers face many challenges to provide treatment to their genuine respective patients. Here one key reason is, there is no referral-based health care system. To solve this problem, this paper proposes a machine learning based decision support system that recommends the appropriate healthcare level, where they should go - Primary, Secondary or Tertiary based on symptom-based inputted data. Our dataset is collected from a Kaggle repository which contains 131 symptoms related to disease prediction. 15 most impactful symptoms are selected through Random Forest feature importance to improve model efficiency and interpretability. Several machine learning models - Logistic Regression, Support Vector Machine (SVM), Random Forest, and XGBoost are implemented. XGBoost achieved superior performance of 95.73 accuracy when Logistic Regression achieves 88.82 accuracy, Random Forest 94.51 accuracy, SVM 95.33 accuracy. This model may helps to solve the problem of absence of strong referral based health care system.

title: Application-Oriented Design of Broadband High-Efficiency Power Amplifiers Considering FET Parasitic Harmonic Load Constraints
authors: Kotaro Hata, Tatsuya Itoh, Shinichi Tanaka
abstract: Broadband high-efficiency power amplifiers (PAs) are often designed based on continuous-mode concepts, under which various harmonic-tuned operation modes have been proposed to extend bandwidth. In practical implementations, however, FET parasitics significantly constrain the controllability of harmonic load impedances, limiting the realizability of theoretically ideal operation modes. This paper presents an application-oriented design perspective for broadband high-efficiency PAs by clarifying how FET parasitics shape intrinsic harmonic-load conditions. The analysis reveals that, in realistic circuits, attainable harmonic loads are fundamentally confined to specific regions of the Smith chart, regardless of the nominal continuous-mode class. Based on this understanding, practical design guidelines are derived in which wideband performance is primarily achieved by optimizing the fundamental load, while allowing the harmonic loads to naturally settle within the feasible regions dictated by FET parasitics, rather than strictly enforcing a specific continuous-mode framework. An experimental broadband PA is implemented to validate this perspective, demonstrating that state-of-the-art performance can be achieved through a straightforward and efficient design approach, without extensive trial-and-error optimization.

title: Optimized Small Object Detection Algorithm for UAV Perspective in Complex Traffic Scenarios
authors: Shijun Liu, Honghao Zhu, Pengcheng Li
abstract: Small object detection in UAV vision systems faces difficulties when the traffic environment is complex because the detection accuracy is limited and the feature representation is not sufficient. Balancing real-time performance with robustness can be hard. The paper proposes OSOD-UAV, an optimized small object detection algorithm for complex traffic scenarios. First, we introduce Spatial Adaptive Dynamic Convolution module into the backbone network. It can adjust the convolution weights. Second, the object distribution is discrete from UAV perspective and modeling long-range dependencies is difficult. The paper designs a Bi-Directional Axial Attention module to handle this. This module decouples the spatial dimensions. It enhances global contextual information interaction and can improve the model's perception ability in occluded and dense scenes. Finally, the paper uses WIoU (Wise-IoU) loss function instead of the traditional CIoU. A dynamic focusing mechanism is used to optimize the bounding box regression process and the detection accuracy is expected to improve. The experiments show that the paper's model achieves 32.8% in mAP@0.5. It is worth noting that the accuracy and robustness of small object detection in complex UAV scenes can be enhanced. Also, it provides a feasible solution for UAV visual detection in complex traffic flow environments.

title: ETTJO: An Event-Triggered Two-Timescale Framework for Joint Caching, Trajectory, and Offloading in Maritime MEC
authors: Kai Yin, Yuxiang Zhao, Ruiting Wang, Xinxin Ye, Ting Gan, Zongpeng Li
abstract: In maritime Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) networks, Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) serve as aerial relays to overcome offshore coverage limitations. However, sustainable UAV operation is challenged by the tight coupling among trajectory planning, content caching, and task offloading under strict energy constraints and stochastic maritime channels. Conventional periodic optimization further incurs excessive backhaul overhead and computational costs. To address these issues, we propose ETTJO, an Event-Triggered Two-Timescale Joint Optimization framework. Leveraging Lyapunov optimization, ETTJO decouples long-term energy constraints from instantaneous decisions without future network information. It further decomposes the problem into two timescales: event-triggered caching updates content only when service popularity changes significantly, while a hybrid short-timescale control algorithm integrates multi-agent Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) for continuous trajectory control with matching theory for discrete task offloading. We provide theoretical analysis to support the proposed framework. Extensive simulations demonstrate that ETTJO achieves lower latency and improved energy efficiency compared with representative baselines.

title: Accelerating Top-K Subgraph Matching With Topology-Aware Pruning and Parallel Processing
authors: Linglin Yang, Yuqi Zhou, Yue Pang, Lei Zou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Given a query graph, top-k subgraph matching finds up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$k$</tex-math></inline-formula> matches in a data graph with the highest scores according to a user-defined scoring function. It has wide applications across many fields, including knowledge graphs and social networks. Due to the enormous search space, existing methods are not efficient enough on large graphs. In this paper, we propose <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">PTAB</monospace>, an efficient serial algorithm for top-k subgraph matching. It traverses an efficiently pruned search space by topology-aware subspace score upper bounds computed from a novel hop index, which stores the range of node properties in a constrained multi-hop neighborhood of each node. Additionally, <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">PTAB</monospace> integrates a cost-aware root selection strategy, which chooses query nodes leading to a search process that utilizes the pruning power of the hop index as much as possible. Furthermore, we use a novel edge-cut strategy to handle general query graphs with cycles. We also extend <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">PTAB</monospace> to a parallel version, <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">PTABP</monospace>, which further improves performance via multi-threading and enhanced pruning techniques. Experimental results on real and synthetic datasets demonstrate that our methods outperform existing methods.

title: Trajectory Tracking and Reinforcement Learning Based Obstacle Avoidance for a Cable-Driven Parallel Robot With Aerial-Ground Mobile Bases
authors: Ziyu Wan, Wenrui Xie, Hao Xiong, Long Li, Hantao Jiang, Lin Zhang, Yunjiang Lou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: A Cable-Driven Parallel Robot (CDPR) is a robot driven by a set of cables and has advantages such as large workspace and low inertia. Scholars have proposed to apply Aerial Mobile Bases (AMBs) and Ground Mobile Bases (GMBs) to a CDPR to achieve a large workspace and fully constrain the moving platform. However, trajectory tracking and obstacle avoidance for CDPR-AGMBs remain open and challenging problems due to their heterogeneous actuation and complex dynamics. This paper proposes a trajectory tracking planner based on optimization and a real-time obstacle avoidance planner based on reinforcement learning (RL) for CDPR-AGMBs. Both planners are developed for a CDPR-AGMB system consisting of four length-variable cables connected to four GMBs and one fixed-length cable connected to an AMB. A physical prototype of the CDPR-AGMB has been built to evaluate the proposed methods. The trajectory tracking planner is implemented according to the prototype’s physical parameters, while the obstacle avoidance planner is trained in simulation using MuJoCo and then deployed on the prototype for real-world validation.
