title: Compound fault diagnosis of motorized spindles based on multi-scale dynamic convolution and AMSoftmax
authors: Liang Wang, Jialong He, Guofa Li, Baodong Zhang, Chunlei Hua
abstract: Traditional data-driven models struggle to extract comprehensive fault features for the compound fault diagnosis of motorized spindles and exhibit poor inter-class discrimination, resulting in suboptimal diagnostic performance. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a compound fault diagnosis method for motorized spindles based on multi-scale dynamic convolution and AMSoftmax. First, a multi-scale dynamic convolution module is designed, in which parallel convolution kernels of varying scales are employed to capture local and global features inherent in monitoring signals. An expert mechanism is introduced to adaptively select convolution kernel parameters based on sample-specific features, thereby enhancing the model's adaptability to evolving compound fault patterns. Second, the AMSoftmax loss function is incorporated. By imposing angular margin constraints, the decision boundaries between fault categories are expanded, effectively mitigating the classification confusion inherent in the traditional cross-entropy loss function within similar inter-class scenarios. Finally, a dataset comprising bearing and rotor compound faults was collected on a motorized spindle experimental platform for verification. Experimental results demonstrate that the average accuracy of the proposed method in motorized spindle compound fault diagnosis reaches 94.17%, offering a new solution for the intelligent operation and maintenance of motorized spindles under complex operating conditions.

title: For Miniaturized Gas Chromatographs: An Acquisition Module with a High-Stability Constant-Current Source for Micro-Thermal Conductivity Detectors
authors: Shunlong Xu, Shaojie Ma, Wenbo Li, Zhirui Li, Fei Feng
abstract: Miniaturized gas chromatographs (GCs) are ideal for in situ detection due to their compact size and low power consumption. The MEMS-based micro-thermal conductivity detector (μTCD) is widely used in these systems. Its acquisition module typically includes an excitation source, protection circuitry, and differential acquisition circuitry. A constant current excitation reduces signal non-linearity more effectively than constant voltage excitation, while its high stability significantly suppresses output noise. This paper presents a voltage-controlled constant current source (VCCS) and analyzes the factors influencing its stability. Experimental results demonstrated that the VCCS outperformed commercial alternatives. At 50 mA, the current fluctuation remained within 3 μA over 12 hours—approximately one-tenth that of commercial products. The VCCS achieved a maximum accuracy of 0.0035% full scale (FS) and a relative standard deviation (RSD) of 0.0011%. This module provides a reliable solution for miniaturized GC design. Tests using light hydrocarbons (20–2000 ppm) showed a peak area RSD below 3.8% and excellent linearity with concentration. Comparative tests with a commercial GC module showed that, at an excitation current of 50 mA and a 10 ppm gas concentration, the proposed module provided better detection capabilities. It enabled clear observation of chromatographic peaks and achieved a limit of detection (LOD) of 6 ppm for propane.

title: MELT-FSIL: Adaptive Hybrid-Loss Few-Shot Learning for Inclusive Sign Language Control in Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Rahman Shafique, Erol Kina, Gyu Sang Choi
abstract: Despite significant advances in autonomous vehicle (AV) technologies, current interaction modalities remain insufficiently accessible to people with physical and communication impairments. Conventional AV interfaces, particularly voice-activated and touchscreen-based systems, may create usability barriers for users who are deaf, non-verbal, or have limited motor control, thereby limiting effective human–vehicle interaction. To address this gap, this study proposes a vision-based ASL recognition framework as an initial step toward accessible AV interaction. The proposed framework uses Few-Shot Incremental Learning (FSIL) to recognize American Sign Language (ASL) hand gestures from static RGB hand-sign images, enabling the model to learn new gesture classes from limited examples while maintaining performance on previously learned classes.The core contribution of this work is a hybrid loss function, termed Metric–Entropy Learning Tradeoff (MELT), which combines prototypical metric learning with cross-entropy-based discriminative learning within an FSIL training regime. By balancing prototype-based class separation with entropy-guided classification, MELT improves the model’s ability to adapt to newly introduced ASL gestures while reducing catastrophic forgetting. In addition, prototype normalization and episodic memory replay are incorporated to stabilize incremental learning performance. Empirical results demonstrate that the proposed FSIL framework with MELT loss improves gesture recognition accuracy from 96.0% using the baseline CNN to 97.5% in the FSL setting and 97.9% in the FSIL setting after the integration of novel gesture classes. These findings demonstrate the potential of hybrid loss-based FSIL for ASL gesture recognition in assistive AV interaction scenarios. While the broader long-term goal is to develop a fully multimodal AV interface, the present study is limited to static RGB image-based ASL recognition; future work will extend the system by incorporating additional modalities such as depth, audio, wearable signals, facial expressions, and vehicle-state information. Thus, this work represents an early step toward more inclusive and accessible AV interaction systems.

title: An Interpretable Ensemble Learning Framework for Predicting Academic Performance: Quantifying Social Media's Influence Through Explainable AI
authors: Shahariar Hossain, MD Ajijul Hakim Bhuiyan, Saifuddin Sagor, Tashrif Ahmed Nishat, Zakir Hussain Monir, Md Aubdullah
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Social media's pervasive adoption among students has increasingly been linked to academic procrastination and reduced productivity, yet existing studies remain largely correlational and lack predictive robustness. This paper proposes an interpretable ensemble learning framework to forecast the impact of social media usage on students' academic productivity. Using a primary survey dataset of 1,501 students from Dhaka, Bangladesh, a rigorous machine learning pipeline is developed incorporating stratified train-test splitting, training-only preprocessing, and SMOTE-based class balancing to prevent data leakage and address class imbalance. The proposed AdaBoost-DT ensemble synergistically combines three heterogeneous base learners, a Multi-Layer Perceptron Classifier, Gradient Boosting, and SVM with RBF kernel, through adaptive weighting. Evaluated against eleven baseline classifiers and four ensemble strategies, the model achieves a state-of-the-art accuracy of 98.5%, further validated through 5-fold stratified cross-validation, yielding a mean accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$97.8 {\%} \pm 0.4 \%$</tex>, confirming stable generalization. An ablation study isolates the contribution of each component, demonstrating that SMOTE and adaptive weighting each contribute meaningfully to the final performance. SHAP-based Explainable AI provides global and instance-level interpretability, identifying study delay, platform preference, and time spent on social media as the most influential predictors. The framework offers a reliable decision-support tool for educators and policymakers designing evidence-based digital wellness interventions.

title: EffiNet-Insect: A System for Agricultural Insect Identification Using EfficientNet
authors: Arnab Baidya Joy, Pritam Nath Dipta, Arpana Biswas Arpa
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The effective detection of insects in agricultural fields is a crucial aspect of precision farming, enabling timely pest management to minimize crop damage and enhance yields. This research introduces EffiNet-Insect, an advanced detection system leveraging the EfficientNetV2-L deep learning architecture for accurate and efficient insect identification in agricultural environments. The system was trained on a dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5, 4 8 1}$</tex> insect images spanning 12 distinct classes, curated to reflect the diversity of pests commonly found in Bangladeshi agricultural fields. Comprehensive experiments were conducted to evaluate the proposed method. Among the models tested, EfficientNetV2-L demonstrated the best performance, achieving an accuracy of 90.65 %, compared to 85.32 %, 79.75 %, and 79.12 % for InceptionResNetV2, MobileNetV3, and ResNet50V2, respectively. To enhance usability, a mobile application was developed to deploy the trained model, providing farmers with an accessible tool for real time pest detection. This system has the potential to significantly reduce pesticide misuse and improve crop protection practices, contributing to sustainable agriculture in Bangladesh and globally.

title: Impact of Cell Orientation on 1.2 kV 4H-SiC MOSFETs with Channeling Implanted Deep P-well
authors: Dinuth C. Y. B. Yapa Mudiyanselage, Skylar DeBoer, Stephen A. Mancini, Justin Lynch, Seung Yup Jang, Adam J. Morgan, Woongje Sung
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: This study investigates the impact of layout orientation on the electrical performance and ruggedness of 1.2 kV planar 4H-SiC MOSFETs employing channeling implanted deep P-well structures. For this, MOSFETs designed with a stripe-type unit cell, either parallel or orthogonal to the wafer flat edge, were fabricated using both channeling (deep P-well) and conventional ion implantation processes for reference. The static characteristics, including threshold voltage, on-resistance, and breakdown voltage, exhibited no significant differences between the two device designs under the conventional random implantation condition. However, for devices fabricated on the channeling wafer, notable variations were observed. In particular, the fingers-orthogonal design shows a significant reduction in threshold voltage (~0.7 V), while achieving lower specific on-resistance (~0.3 mΩ∙cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) compared with the fingers-parallel device. Additionally, for the same orthogonal device, a considerable increase in drain-to-source leakage current during blocking mode operation was detected for the channeling wafer, compared to the parallel device. Furthermore, the short circuit withstand times were observed to be considerably higher in devices fabricated on the channeling wafer. Among these, the device with fingers aligned parallel to the flat edge demonstrated the highest withstand time and a lower peak current compared to the orthogonal finger design. Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) imaging and TCAD simulations revealed asymmetry in the channels and P-well regions of the orthogonal design, which is attributed to being the root cause of observed differences between the two designs in the channeling wafer.

title: Planning 6-D Grasps for Two-Finger Grippers Considering Edge and Corner Contacts
authors: Zhengtao Hu, Ruishuang Liu, Mocong Qiu, Hao Chen, Liang Du, Takuya Kiyokawa, Weiwei Wan, Jianjun Yuan, Kensuke Harada
abstract: This article presents a novel model-based algorithm for planning antipodal grasps, especially considering the edge and corner contacts. Conventional model-based planners search for face contacts to build grasp synergies, which ensure superior grasp stability, but inherently restrict the geometric versatility of feasible grasp configurations. With soft contact, edges and corners can reshape fingerpads to form equivalent face contacts, enhancing the ability to resist gravity. This allows candidates with greater configuration variability, rather than being constrained solely by face normals. The developed planner detects regions of faces, edges, and corners from a computer-aided design (CAD) model, samples contacts from each region, detects paired contacts using contact detectors corresponding to contact types, and screens the pairs by stability and collision checking. It generates grasp candidates with qualified stability and flexible configurations, breaking the limits of planning for polyhedra, asymmetric objects, and objects with various sharp angles, etc. Experimental results quantitatively demonstrate that the proposed method has superior performance on grasp distributions and success rates using the selected objects from open datasets and off-the-shelf items.

title: Spatial-Frequency Structure-Aware Deep Hashing with Adaptive Gated Fusion (SF-AGDH) for Remote Sensing Image Retrieval
authors: Linglong Xiong, Yueshun He, Jie Chen, Zhenyang Hui
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Retrieving remote sensing images on a massive scale remains challenging because geospatial targets often display intricate scale variations and heterogeneous textures. While deep hashing offers a highly efficient retrieval metric, current methods predominantly rely on spatial convolutions. This makes it difficult for them to perceive the global frequency properties needed to separate visually similar classes. Moreover, standard multi-scale aggregation techniques are largely static and cannot dynamically adapt to different image contents. To solve these problems, this paper introduces the Spatial-Frequency Structure-Aware Deep Hashing (SF-AGDH) network. Utilizing an inference-efficient dual-stream design, our framework integrates a Dual-Domain Synergistic Attention (DDSA) module. By combining 2D Fast Fourier Transforms with coordinate attention, DDSA effectively captures both macroscopic periodic textures and localized geometric details. We also propose an Adaptive Gated Fusion (AGF) module that uses a learnable gating mechanism to balance shallow textures and deep semantics automatically. Finally, to combat the extreme positive-negative sample imbalance inherent in hash optimization, we apply a Weighted HashNet loss to dynamically recalibrate gradients. Extensive experiments on UCMD, AID, and NWPU-RESISC45 demonstrate that SF-AGDH achieves superior, state-of-the-art retrieval accuracy, especially at low bit rates.

title: Intelligence Resource Management in 5G/6G D2D-SIoET Network Through Centralized Training with Decentralized Execution Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
authors: Prakash Murugesan
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Wireless communication is developing rapidly towards 5 G and 6 G networks, while necessitating advanced resource management techniques to handle massive device connectivity, high data rates and low latency requirements. Existing methods faced difficulty with spectrum efficiency, latency and high energy consumption and dynamic environments particularly Device-to-Device (D2D) communication and Social Internet of Education Things (SIoET). Static resource management frameworks struggle to adapt to the non-stationary nature of user behavior and device mobility leading to degraded Quality of Service (QoS). To overcome this issue, Centralized Training Decentralized Execution for Social Internet of Education Things (CTDE-SIoET) is applied to 5 G/6 G D2D and edge resource allocation. This method integrates Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) framework that employs centralized critic network during training, while also allowing the agents from global system information to address non-stationary and credit assignment problems. During execution phase, centralized critic is discarded and each agents uses its own decentralized actor network based on local observation to select actions independently, thereby ensuring scalability and efficient operation, where continuous global communication is not feasible. The results demonstrate that the proposed method archives superior performance metrics compared to existing RL techniques showing lower latency (20-35%), reduced energy consumption (15-30%) and higher network throughput (1025%).

title: Measuring Generalization in Cow Behavior Recognition with LOCO and Day-Blocked Splits Using Cow Nose Ring Dataset
authors: Retina Islam, Shah Rafatur Rahman, Masud Ibn Afjal, Md. Abdulla Al Mamun
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Recognizing cow behavior using wearable sensors is important for farm monitoring. However, many studies evaluate using random window splits, which can yield overly optimistic results. In this paper, we work with the Cow Nose Ring accelerometer dataset and propose a reproducible, cow and dayindependent evaluation protocol. We used LOCO (Leave-One-Cow-Out) and day-blocked LODO (Leave-One-Day-Out) splits and compared them with random splits using Macro-F1 and other balanced metrics. Our experiments show that random split results can be much higher than realistic evaluations. Deep Learning models achieve around 90% Macro-F1 in a random split, but drop to around 75% to 79% Macro-F1 in LOCO/LODO splits, giving a clear generalization gap of about 11% to 15%. To improve robustness, we also propose a lightweight pipeline using orientation-invariant engineered features like magnitude, jerk, per-axis statistics, spectral band energies, and axis correlations, along with simple augmentation (random <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 D$</tex> rotation and Gaussian noise). In our ablation study, the engineered features clearly improve Macro-F1 compared to the raw signals. Finally, we analyze window length sensitivity on the splits to understand the accuracy vs latency trade-off for the optimal window length.

title: An Explainable Hybrid CNN–Vision Transformer Framework for Lung Cancer Classification on LungHist700
authors: Tonmoy Debnath, Prosenjit Das, Mijanur Rahaman, Md. Bipul Hossain, Anupam Kumar Bairagi, Apurba Adhikary
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Globally, lung cancer is the leading cause of cancer-related mortality, with current statistics indicating approximately 2.5 million new cases and 1.8 million deaths annually. In this context, accurate early diagnosis is critical, and histopathological analysis plays a key role in determining the underlying cause and severity of the disease. Nonetheless, tissue heterogeneity and subtle inter-class variations are some of the challenges that make it difficult to have reliable classification. This paper presents a hybrid deep learning model with explainability that combines ResNet50, which builds local hierarchical feature by using convolutional blocks, and Vision Transformer (ViTB/16), which estimates the global contextual dependency by using selfattention on patches in the image. The model was trained using a weighted soft voting strategy that was optimized using Optuna on the LungHist700 dataset, which consists of high-resolution images of adenocarcinoma (ACA), squamous cell carcinoma (SCC), and normal (NOR) lung tissue, and then reprocessed by resizing, normalizing, and augmenting. Testing on a stratified test set, the framework had an overall accuracy of 97.14%. It showed the false negative of 0.036 on ACA, 0.038 on SCC, 0.062 on NOR, and ROC-AUC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim \text{0. 9 8}$</tex>, which was great discriminative strength and low risk of false negative outcomes. It was demonstrated that probability calibration had a Brier score of 0.041-0.046, and the decision curve analysis demonstrated additional net benefits compared with treat-all/none strategies. Grad-CAM visualizations showed that it focused on regions of pathological relevance and improved interpretability and explainability. This hybrid ensemble will feature a powerful, clinically applicable instrument for diagnosing lung cancer, potentially enabling timely interventions. The shortcomings include the use of a relatively small dataset, which will be validated by large groups in the future.

title: Automatic Reconstruction Algorithms for in vivo Skeletal Muscle 3D-SWEI Data
authors: Courtney Trutna Paley, Felix Q. Jin, Anna E. Knight, Ned C. Rouze, Mark L. Palmeri, Kathryn R. Nightingale
abstract: Rotational 3D Shear Wave Elasticity Imaging (3D-SWEI) offers many opportunities for the development of biomechanical biomarkers of muscle health. However, methods for material parameter reconstruction to date have involved manual processing steps, which are impractical for clinical implementations. In this work we describe and optimize algorithms for the automatic processing of 3D-SWEI skeletal muscle data and explore the most important factors affecting the success of automatic estimation of the shear wave speed (SWS) along and across the fibers. We compare the results of several automatic algorithms to the results obtained using manual processing across 130 in vivo 3D-SWEI datasets, evaluating the percent of valid acquisitions (non-gross-error), percent bias, and absolute percent error for each algorithm. We evaluate three major factors for algorithm optimization: wave detection method (comparing Radon Sum, time to peak, and cross correlation), lateral range selection (comparing a single range for all acquisition angles, changing the range with fiber orientation, and dynamic range selection, in addition to analyzing the start and end positions of the lateral range), and methods to improve robustness (exploring detecting the wave through 3D fits, and methods to isolate individual waves when multiple waves are detected). We conclude the optimal algorithm for passive muscle is a fixed lateral range (2-18 mm) Radon Sum method with multiwave detection and SWS fitting to the ellipse predicted in transversely isotropic materials, and we provide results that can guide algorithm choice for other applications. Additionally, we have made the implementation of all our algorithms publicly accessible at: DOI 10.5281/zenodo.18883176.

title: An Intelligent and Optimized Reconciliation Technique for Enhanced Secret Key Rates in Quantum Key Distribution
authors: Upama Rajan M. N., Reema Mathew A, Shamya A., Pasupula Madan Mohan Reddy, Sumanlal M. S, Mahima Mary Mathews
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Quantum key distribution (QKD) is a secure communication technique that uses quantum mechanical principles. This method enables two trusted users to exchange a secret key or message securely by exploiting the fundamental properties of quantum mechanics. Strong long-distance secure communication is provided by QKD but its effectiveness is mainly limited by quantum bit error rate (QBER), finite-key effects, and the effectiveness of classical error-correction during information reconciliation. In this work, we proposes a neural-aided adaptive reconciliation (NAAR) framework based on artificial neural networks for QKD that integrates a lightweight neural belief propagation decoder with QBER-driven adaptive LDPC coderate selection. By lowering frame-error rate and information leakage, neural-assisted log-likelihood ratio calibration enhances belief-propagation convergence for binary symmetric channels. The result shows an improvement in secret key rates (SKR) and lower error rates over traditional LDPC-based reconciliation.

title: Agentic AI Frameworks: Orchestration Architectures and Autonomous LLM-Based Reasoning Systems
authors: Yashwanth L, SU Rishwanth, Shiny R. M
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Agentic AI systems shift from static generative models to autonomous agents capable of reasoning and action. However, research remains fragmented across definitions, architectures, and evaluation practices, limiting informed adoption. Methods: 20 publications were reviewed (2021-2025) analyzing reasoning paradigms (CoT, ReAct, ToT), orchestration models (single-agent to multi-agent), memory designs, and evaluation methodologies using a five-dimensional analytical framework. Results: ReAct-based loops dominate (68 % adoption). Only 32% report safety mechanisms; evaluation is largely qualitative. Multi-agent architectures improve robustness but introduce 2-5x latency; hierarchical hallucination mitigation reduces errors by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 0 - 5 0 \%}$</tex>. Conclusion: The proposed research provide practitioners a structured framework for selecting reasoning, orchestration, and guardrail strategies. Key gaps remain in standardized benchmarks, safety governance, and interoperability, outlining opportunities for production-grade agentic systems.tonomous Reasoning, Multi-Agent Systems, Tool-Augmented Intelligence

title: Motorcycle-Specific Real-Time Multi-Task ADAS Using Single-Camera Vision and Risk Fusion
authors: Md. Tauseef, Bimal Ramesh, Kumar Swamy Naik.O, Thanush A.B, Bhuvan C
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: This paper presents a motorcycle-specific, vision-based multi-task Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS) that performs pothole detection, lane detection, and object detection using a single forward-facing monocular camera. Unlike existing car-centric or single-task ADAS solutions, the proposed framework is designed to address motorcycle-specific constraints, enabling simultaneous perception and fusion-based risk assessment under real-time embedded operation. YOLOv8-based deep learning models are employed for pothole segmentation and object detection, while a lightweight edge-based pipeline is used for lane detection, allowing parallel inference with low computational overhead. A fusion-based decision module integrates the outputs of all perception tasks to generate prioritized rider alerts. Experimental evaluation on publicly available pothole, lane, and traffic object datasets achieves detection accuracies of 70% for potholes, 83% for lanes, and 60% for objects, with corresponding AUC values exceeding random-baseline performance, indicating reliable class separability. The complete system achieves an average end-to-end latency of approximately 48 ms per frame (around 20 FPS) on an embedded ECU-class platform, demonstrating its feasibility for real-world motorcycle ADAS deployment.

title: Hybrid Convolutional Model for Vegetation Height Estimation Using InSAR Data
authors: Yuejuan Chen, Pengyu Xu, Pingping Huang, Xiujuan Li, Zhiguo Wang, Linkai Fan
abstract: Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar (InSAR) technology utilizes two Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) images to measure interferometric coherence, which incorporates volumetric decorrelation, a factor that is associated with the height of the vegetation canopy. The height, density, and structure of the vegetation affect the scattering of the radar signal, leading to changes in coherence. Therefore, InSAR technology can invert the vegetation height by analyzing the variations in interferometric coherence, providing an effective estimate of forest canopy height. Convolutional neural networks (CNN) and their variants have been widely used for vegetation height estimation. This paper presents a DCN-FiLM-CNN (DFN) architecture, which augments a standard CNN backbone with Deformable Convolution Network (DCN) and Feature-wise Linear Modulation (FiLM). In particular, DCN introduces learnable kernel deformations, allowing the network to adapt to geometric variations of objects in the input images, thereby enlarging the effective receptive field and improving the model's ability to handle objects of different shapes and scales. Meanwhile, FiLM performs conditional adjustments to the input, dynamically weighting the outputs of the convolutional layers, which enhances the network's spatial modeling capabilities when dealing with heterogeneous vegetation. The proposed model retrieves vegetation height using interferometric coherence together with the SAR backscatter coefficient as inputs. The experimental results show that using the backscatter coefficient and coherence coefficient together in a CNN model for vegetation height estimation improves the R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> by 0.1, compared with using only coherence. Furthermore, the DFN model that jointly uses both the backscatter coefficient and coherence improves the R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> by 0.04 over the CNN model.

title: Blockchain-Based E-Voting System Using Hyperledger Fabric
authors: Sajid Hossain Khan, Md. Shohel Rana, Shah Md. Rafiul Kadir, A. H. M. Saiful Islam, Afsana Sharmin Shanta, Fernaz Narin Nur
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Voting systems have long been associated with high expenses and complexities, such as high costs, fraud, and particularly challenging auditing systems. This paper proposes a framework using a permissioned blockchain based on Hyperledger Fabric, which is secure and decentralized. The automated and digitized voting system encompasses the entire election lifecycle from the digital registration of citizens to the voting and auto counting of votes. The automated systems, or smart contracts, perform the business logic on a blockchain and are responsible for checking and controlling eligibility, as well as managing the verification of results. In order to ensure privacy, participation identifiers and the votes are combined using secure multi-party cryptographic techniques. Thus, the votes are remniscent of immutable anchoring and are aethostatic. The framework guarantees that only real and authorized citizens can cast votes and that the records are also protected from unauthorized alterations. The results guarantee that Hyperledger Fabric can be employed in complex and regulated situations where sensitive civic and legal identity management, governance, and data integrity need to be balanced. The system will be secure, streamlined, and reduce election fraud. Full accessibility for auditing also serves to protect the system.

title: An Enhanced U-Net Architecture for Semantic Segmentation of Aerial Images from Egypt
authors: B. M. El-Den, Ahmed Ibrahim Mohamed Elnabway Asl
abstract: A significant task in remote sensing is context-aware segmentation for high-resolution aerial images, which supports applications such as land cover classification, urban analysis, and environmental monitoring. In this paper, we propose a new framework directed to enhance the segmentation accuracy of aerial images by combining a split-depthwise convolution layer to handle high-resolution aerial images with lower computational complexity and a focus-based feature modulation module to emphasize spatial regions of interest. We trained and evaluated our model on aerial images collected from geographically distinct areas within Alexandria. The results show that the model with our proposed framework exceeded the original U-net model in terms of accuracy and IoU value. Our framework provides an efficient solution for processing large-scale aerial image analysis and can be adapted for remote sensing applications, such as infrastructure development in Egypt, urban planning, and environmental monitoring.

title: CompenKNet: An Intelligent State Estimation Method with Model Errors Compensation
authors: Bing Huang, Han Zou, Chao Ma, Yuan Huang, Miao Li, Weidong Sheng
abstract: Due to the absence of precise prior knowledge, model errors induced by model approximation are ubiquitous in state estimation. Such errors impose substantial degradation on the performance of conventional state estimation approaches. To address this issue, we propose CompenKNet, which incorporates KalmanNet with a dedicated compensation network to enable adaptive state estimation with explicit model error compensation. Featuring a modular dual-branch structure, the algorithm decouples yet jointly optimizes the learning of the Kalman gain and model error compensation, thereby alleviating the adverse impacts of model errors. Furthermore, a three-stage training pipeline is devised to optimize network parameters progressively via pre-training and joint fine-tuning. Extensive experiments on both synthetic and real-world datasets validate that CompenKNet achieves superior performance over state-of-the-art algorithms.

title: Analysis of Body Diode in Trench Field Plate MOSFETs and Reverse Recovery Charge Reduction by Shrinking the Cell Pitch
authors: Tatsuya Nishiwaki, Seiji Inumiya
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: Carrier distribution and reverse recovery charge (Qrr) of the body diode in Trench Field Plate MOSFETs (FPMOSs) are analyzed using TCAD simulations and discussed. For a conventional power MOSFET, the electron density (n) and hole density (p) during diode forward conduction are commonly assumed to be equal (n = p) because of charge neutrality. However, in FPMOSs, the drift layer doping concentration (N<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">D</inf>) can be increased due to the charge compensation effect, and ionized donor concentration $\left( {{N_D}^ + } \right)$ cannot be neglected. Therefore, carrier distributions satisfy the condition $n = p + {N_D}^ + $. This suggests that low-level injection state, where p is lower than ${N_D}^ + $ can occur in the body diode of the FPMOSs. In this paper, we predict that fine cell pitch FPMOSs which have higher N<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">D</inf> realize low-level injection state and could reduce Qrr. By shrinking the cell pitch from 2.6 um to 2.0 um and introducing a stepped oxide structure or a graded doping profile, Qrr is reduced by 7 to 22% and RonQrr are reduced by over 40% in the 100 V-class FPMOS.

title: 1.2 kV 3D Quasi-Planar Trench SiC MOSFET with P-Body Layer as Trench Protection
authors: Mansha Kansal, Iulian Nistor, Kyrylo Melnyk, Giuseppe Capasso, Alessandro Borghese, Michele Riccio, Arne Benjamin Renz, Giovanni Breglio, Neophytos Lophitis, Andrea Irace, Marina Antoniou, Munaf Rahimo, Luca Maresca
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: In this paper, a new trench shielding concept against high electric fields is presented by means of an experimental demonstration backed up with 3D TCAD simulations. The novel concept has been applied on an advanced 3D Quasi-Planar Trench (QPT) SiC MOSFET rated at 1200 V and 50 A. The protection approach consists of two key design pillars by employing: 1) shallow trench depths embedded in deeper P-Well Channel regions which are accordingly 2) shallower than deeper highly doped P-Body regions under the source contact. This design provides a double protection scheme for enhancing the device blocking performance and shielding the gate oxide at the critical regions along the trench MOS gate structure. Three different QPT design structures were examined with varying protection levels, with each design having a consistent trench depth but varying trench dimensions across the JFET region (QPT1 with no embedded Trench in P-Well), P-body (QPT2 with partially embedded trench in P-Well), and into the N+ source region (QPT3 with fully embedded trench in P-Well). The blocking characteristics, including the off-state leakage behaviour, were analyzed for the proposed device structures. The optimized QPT structure performs showcases BV performance close to the best-in-class reference planar structure with a deviation of only ~ 0.3 %. The experimental results were further validated through advanced 3D TCAD simulations, which examined electrostatic behavior, and studied electric field distributions along different cross-sections of the trench.

title:
450 V/18 mΩ Radiation Hardened P-GaN Gate HEMT With Single-Event Hardness at 82 MeV•cm
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    /mg
authors: Xuan Xie, Minze Wang, Ziang Wang, Zhi Wang, Yawen Zhang, Qiuyi Tang, Chenyue Chu, Guangwei Xu, Shibing Long, Bo Shen, Shu Yang
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: In this work, a 450 V/18 mΩ radiation hardened p-GaN gate HEMT with gate-junction termination extension (G-JTE) structure has been demonstrated. The multi-finger G-JTE HEMT exhibits an enhanced single-event burnout (SEB) voltage of 475 V with a relatively short L<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">GD</inf> of 6.5 μm under Ta ion irradiation with a high LET of 82 MeV•cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/mg, while maintaining a low on-resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf>) of 18.3 mΩ (or R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON,sp</inf> of 1.64 mΩ•cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) and low R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf>•Q<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">G</inf> of 252 mΩ•nC. By virtue of the G-JTE to suppress the electric field concentration and consequently reduce the non-equilibrium carriers, the G-JTE HEMT can deliver a ${\text{BFO}}{{\text{M}}_{{\text{SEB}}}}\,\left( {{V_{{\text{SEB}}}}^2/{R_{{\text{ON}},{\text{sp}}}}} \right)$ of 138 MW/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, which is the highest among the state-of-the-art GaN HEMTs. This work demonstrates great potential of p-GaN gate HEMTs for high-frequency and high-efficiency power converters in aerospace applications.

title: RepAir: A Framework for Airway Segmentation and Discontinuity Correction in CT
authors: John M. Oyer, Ali Namvar, Benjamin A. Hoff, Wassim W. Labaki, Ella A. Kazerooni, Charles R. Hatt, Fernando J. Martinez, MeiLan K. Han, Craig J. Galbán, Sundaresh Ram
publish: 2026 IEEE Southwest Symposium on Image Analysis and Interpretation (SSIAI)
abstract: Accurate airway segmentation from chest computed tomography (CT) scans is essential for quantitative lung analysis, yet manual annotation is impractical and many automated U-Net-based methods yield disconnected components that hinder reliable biomarker extraction. We present RepAir, a three-stage framework for robust 3D airway segmentation that combines an nnU-Net-based network with anatomically informed topology correction. The segmentation network produces an initial airway mask, after which a skeleton-based algorithm identifies potential discontinuities and proposes reconnections. A 1D convolutional classifier then determines which candidate links correspond to true anatomical branches versus false or obstructed paths. We evaluate RepAir on two distinct datasets: ATM'22, comprising annotated CT scans from predominantly healthy subjects and AeroPath, encompassing annotated scans with severe airway pathology. Across both datasets, RepAir outperforms existing 3D U-Net-based approaches such as Bronchinet and NaviAirway on both voxel-level and topological metrics, and produces more complete and anatomically consistent airway trees while maintaining high segmentation accuracy.

title: An Integrated Deep Learning Architecture for Illumination-Aware Object Detection
authors: Mostafa Habibi, Mehrdad Nourani
publish: 2026 IEEE Southwest Symposium on Image Analysis and Interpretation (SSIAI)
abstract: Images captured in low-light environments suffer from reduced visibility, low contrast, and noise, which degrade visual quality and hinder reliability in automated object detection. This paper presents a deep learning illuminationaware object detection framework that integrates a lightweight encoder-decoder enhancer with an object detection architecture to improve performance under poor lighting conditions. A threestep training strategy further improves the model's robustness and transferability. The proposed framework provides a scalable, real-time, and resource-efficient approach to illuminationaware object detection in low-light environments. The proposed architecture achieves 69.1% mAP on ExDark dataset with approximately 50% fewer parameters, demonstrating that an integrated architecture with joint training leads to an efficient solution for low-light visual monitoring.

title: ISPSD 2026 Committees
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)

title: Comparative Load Flow Analysis of a 6-Bus Power System Using MATLAB and ETAP
authors: Fahmid Amin Zawad
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Load flow analysis is a fundamental and essential study in power system operation, planning, and optimization. It provide detailed insights into bus voltages, phase angles, real and reactive power flows, and system losses under varying load and generation conditions. In this study, a 6-bus power system is modeled and analyzed to evaluate its overall performance. The network is simulated in Electrical Transient Analyzer Program (ETAP) to obtain bus voltage magnitudes, phase angles, and line power flows, while a MATLAB implementation of the Gauss-Seidel iterative method is used for independent verification. A close agreement between the two approaches confirms the accuracy and reliability of the results. The analysis demonstrates the importance of combining software-based simulations with classical numerical methods to ensure both precision and practical understanding of power system behavior. Accurate load flow results help identify critical busses, evaluate line loadings, minimize system losses, and support informed decision-making in planning and operation. This study highlights the significance of load flow studies in achieving reliable, efficient, and safe operation of modern power networks, providing engineers with a foundation for further analysis, optimization, and contingency planning.

title: Conjunctiva Image Analysis for Anemia Detection: Evaluation on Eyes-Defy & CP-AnemiC
authors: Tonmoy Paul, Dip Muhuri, Aditta Chowdhury
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: We have aimed to study anemia detection from conjunctival eye images using two public datasets and a lightweight, interpretable feature pipeline. First, we designed a full workflow on the Eyes-Defy dataset from India and Italy. Then we applied illumination normalization using the Hue-Saturation-Value color space (HSV) with Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE) on the value channel, extract a 24 dimensional color and texture feature set, and train standard machine-learning classifiers for Italy, India, and their joint set. We also run cross-country experiments to examine robustness across populations. Then, we applied the same preprocessing and feature extraction to the CP-AnemiC dataset from Ghana and compared several classifiers, where a tuned Radial Basis Function Support Vector Machine (RBF-SVM) has achieved the best within-dataset accuracy. Finally, we evaluated cross-dataset transfer between Eyes-Defy and CP-AnemiC and observed clear dataset differences; direct transfer is unstable, while a small calibration set improves decision thresholds. To further improve transfer without target labels, we used Correlation Alignment (CORAL) on the feature space, which raised CP to Eyes-Defy generalization to a balanced accuracy of about 0.56. Overall, the study shows that combining simple preprocessing, compact features, and lightweight domain alignment can provide practical performance across diverse data sources, while also highlighting where further adaptation is required.

title: Meta-Ensemble Transformer Framework for Multi-Class Mental Health Disorder Classification on Social Media
authors: Sayma Medha, Iffat Ara Babli, Md Abdul Masud
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The increasing use of social media platforms such as Reddit has created new opportunities for analyzing user-generated text to support early identification of mental health disorders. Despite this potential, multi-class mental health classification remains difficult due to noisy language, overlapping symptom expressions, and contextual ambiguity commonly found in social media posts. This study introduces a meta-ensemble transformerbased framework that combines BERT and RoBERTa using a logistic regression meta-learner to enhance multi-class mental health disorder classification. The framework is evaluated on two publicly available Reddit datasets, Amurark and Mavinsao, which include up to eight mental health categories. The experimental results show that the proposed ensemble consistently outperforms individual transformer models. An accuracy of 0.8649 with a macro F1-score of 0.8646 is achieved on the Amurark dataset, while the Mavinsao dataset yields an accuracy of 0.8501 and a macro F1-score of 0.8502. Although the framework demonstrates improved performance, it is limited by its dependence on Englishlanguage text and the computational cost associated with transformer-based models. The proposed approach is suitable for automated mental health monitoring, early risk detection systems, and large-scale psychological research using social media data.

title: Neural Implicit Surface Reconstruction for Satellite Imagery with Adaptive Normal Priors
authors: Yingjie Qu, Song Ye, Ying Wang, Qin Li, Zhengli Yao, Bocheng Liao
abstract: Multi-temporal satellite image-based 3D reconstruction leverages satellite imagery acquired over the same region at different time periods to recover the three-dimensional structure of the Earth’s surface. However, the inherent complexity and uncertainty of multi-temporal satellite imagery often lead to reconstructed surfaces that are irregular and lack smoothness. To address this issue, we propose a neural radiance field-based surface reconstruction method incorporating normal vector priors. Specifically, surface normals predicted by a deep learning model from satellite images are introduced as additional geometric priors, and their regularization properties are integrated into the reconstruction of neural implicit surfaces. To mitigate evident generalization errors in the prior normals, a normal correction method based on the Poisson equation is further developed, which adjusts the gradient information of the prior normals to align with the initial scene normals, thereby producing accurate and smooth corrected normals. In addition, an adaptive normal validation strategy is proposed to eliminate erroneous normals that are inconsistent across multiple views, further enhancing the robustness of geometric constraints. Experimental results demonstrate that the hierarchical filtering mechanism effectively ensures the reliability of normal constraints, significantly improving the accuracy, regularity, and smoothness of the reconstructed surfaces.

title: Hybrid-TPR-ABE: a Blockchain-Enabled Verifiable Framework with Differential Privacy for Secure Healthcare IoT Analytics
authors: Harish K, Madhumala R B
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Healthcare Internet-of-Things (IoT) and electronic health record (EHR) analytics demand strong privacy and auditable integrity, yet resource constraints make heavy cryptographic tools (e.g., zk-SNARKs) impractical for routine use. We present Hybrid-TPR-ABE+B+C, a practical architecture that combines (i) decentralized attribute-based encryption (ABE) and threshold proxy re-encryption (TPR) for fine-grained, revocable access control, (ii) a lightweight verifiable computation layer based on Merkle commitments and compact BLS aggregated attestations, and (iii) a differential privacy (DP) layer at the edge/fog with on-chain privacy-budget accounting. Large EHR objects are stored off-chain (IPFS) and referenced on-chain to preserve throughput. We describe the protocols, workflow, and a prototype on a private Ethereum Proof-of-Authority network with IPFS, and evaluate overhead, latency, and privacy-utility trade-offs.

title: WaveCaps-RF: A Wavelet Capsule-Enhanced Random Forest Framework for Multi-Class Android Malware Detection
authors: Ashraful Goni, Asif Ahammad Miazee, Md Amir Hussen, Orakatul Zannath Mim
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The proliferation of Android applications has led to heightened exposure to diverse malware threats that continuously evolve beyond traditional signature-based defences. Conventional machine learning and ensemble approaches have achieved notable accuracy yet remain constrained by single-criterion feature selection and inadequate modelling of temporal-spectral dependencies in malware behaviours. To overcome these challenges, a hybrid WaveCapsEnhanced Random Forest (WaveCaps-RF) model has been developed, integrating multi-criteria feature selection with wavelet-inspired multi-scale decomposition. The proposed framework extracts hierarchical temporal and frequencydomain features through discrete wavelet decomposition, statistical moment analysis, and spectral energy quantification, producing 544 enhanced attributes from 223 selected features. A heterogeneous ensemble of five base learners is fused via soft voting to ensure balanced performance and generalisation. Experimental evaluation on the CICMalDroid 2020 dataset has achieved 92.16% classification accuracy and 99.03% AUC, demonstrating strong discriminative capability with computational efficiency suitable for real-world Android security deployment.

title: Tasmanian Devil Integrated Greylag Goose Optimization for Secure and Energy Efficient Clustering and Routing in Wireless Sensor Network
authors: Ashish Anand, Sumithra Devi. K A, Suresh. A, Sangeetha. S, Mukesh Soni
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSN) are a collection of sensor nodes that monitor and transmit data from diverse environments. Clustering and routing are critical in WSN to minimize energy consumption and extend network lifetime. However, existing algorithms often fail to address this effectively because they suffer from premature convergence and weak local search mechanism, it leads to high latency and insecure data transmission. To overcome this problem, this paper proposes a Tasmanian Devil Greylag Goose Optimization Algorithm (TDGGOA) for secure and energy efficient clustering and routing in WSN. The Tasmanian Devil Optimization (TDO) for global exploration to identify regions and Greylag Goose Optimization (GGO) for exploitation to refine the local search, ensuring a robust for balance security with energy conservation. Experimental results demonstrate that TDGGOA achieves maximum PDR of 0.98, throughput of 0.90, network lifetime to 4700 rounds and delay as 0.2 ms, outperforming compared to ML-AEF, SECR, O-ABS and TWHSBO algorithms.

title: Attention-Enhanced Hybrid Collaborative Filtering for Personalized Recommendation of Chinese Traditional Culture
authors: Jiate Sun, Fengchen Liu
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: With the growing digitization of cultural heritage resources there has been an increase in the need for an intelligent personalized recommendation system that promotes Chinese Traditional Culture. However, current collaborative filtering (CF) methods suffer from issues of data sparsity and cold-start when attempting to recommend items on cultural platforms where users have limited interaction history. The present research proposes an Attention Enhanced Hybrid Collaborative Filtering with Semantic Embedding (AE-HCFSE) approach to provide personalized recommendations of Chinese Traditional Cultural Content. The proposed AE-HCFSE utilizes normalized user-item interaction data and semantic embeddings generated through an Autoencoder in order to construct common dense representation of Culture. The proposed recommendation framework incorporates a hybrid recommendation methodology that combines collaborative filtering score with semantic similarity, as well as using an attention layer to dynamically learn feature importance weights and increase the accuracy of personalized recommendations. The final personalized recommendation list will be produced by using the rankings of the attention enhanced score. The results of the experimental evaluation indicate that the proposed AEHCFSE provides increased precision, recall, and NDCG relative to traditional CF methods, confirming the effectiveness of the proposed AE-HCFSE in alleviating the sparsity problems and improving the personalization of cultural content.

title: Distributed Collaborative Search for Heterogeneous UAVs in Dynamic Disaster Environments
authors: Yi Jiang, Ling Xu, Zitong Shen, Yuxia Gao
abstract: To address the target search problem in dynamic disaster scenarios, a distributed collaborative search method for heterogeneous Uncrewed Aerial Vehicle (UAV) swarms is proposed. First, the search task area is discretized into a grid map, and the grid properties are initialized based on a priori information. Subsequently, flight trajectories are planned within a distributed Model Predictive Control (MPC) framework, incorporating platform kinematic constraints. A multilevel speed reduction mechanism is introduced to resolve the obstacle avoidance deadlock problem caused by the approach of dynamic moving obstacles. Finally, various heuristic optimization algorithms are compared through simulation experiments to verify the effectiveness of the proposed method in dynamic disaster task planning. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method can effectively generate safe escape trajectories when faced with encirclement by dynamic moving obstacles, significantly improving the target discovery rate of the UAV swarm while minimizing collision risks.

title: Resource-Efficient Network Intrusion Detection for IoT Edge Devices: Balancing Precision and Latency
authors: Jannatul Mawa
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: As Internet of Things (IoT) devices and high-speed networks proliferate, the complexity of cyberattacks has escalated. While Deep Learning (DL) models have recently dominated Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) research due to high accuracy, their computational demands often render them unsuitable for resource-constrained edge devices. This study addresses this gap by proposing a resource-efficient NIDS based on an optimized Random Forest classifier tailored for low-latency environments. Utilizing the NSL-KDD benchmark, we implemented a streamlined preprocessing pipeline involving Label Encoding and robust feature scaling. Unlike recent approaches that rely on computationally expensive synthetic oversampling (SMOTE) or heavy Neural Networks, our proposed model achieves a superior accuracy of 99.37% through rigorous hyperparameter tuning focused on Gini Impurity minimization. Comparative analysis against State-of-the-Art DL models demonstrates that our solution matches their precision while reducing training latency by approximately 75%, providing a lightweight solution suitable for deployment on battery-powered IoT sensors.

title: LTRA: A Lightweight Reputation-Enhanced Transmission Random Access Protocol for Low-Altitude Aviation Networks
authors: Yang Gao, Yifan Yan
abstract: In low-altitude aviation networks, heterogeneous nodes, including unmanned aerial vehicles and manned aircraft, perform distributed communications over shared spectrum in the absence of centralized infrastructure. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a Lightweight Reputation-Enhanced Transmission Random Access (LTRA) protocol for distributed slot self-negotiation. The proposed protocol integrates adaptive learning with a local reputation mechanism, where reputation serves as an internal state variable that drives the convergence of transmission probability rather than as an identifier of malicious nodes. Specifically, each node selects access slots from the idle-slot set via weighted random selection and updates its transmission probability through imitation learning and reputation feedback. Simulation results show that LTRA outperforms S-ALOHA (Slotted ALOHA), SP-ALOHA (Slot-Planning ALOHA), and SP-ALOHA-HP (Slot-Planning ALOHA with High Probability) in terms of throughput, fairness, and robustness. Moreover, it can adaptively converge without prior knowledge and maintains a Jain fairness index above 0.99 even when 10%–30% of nodes are malicious.

title: Hierarchical Risk-Aware Transformer Actor Critic Framework for Intelligent Financial Management and Decision Making
authors: Xinyue Quan, Chunfang Shi
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: In recent years, intelligent financial management decision making become important because of the market volatility, economic uncertainty and growing complexity of corporate financial structures. Moreover, the accurate capital allocation, risk control and liquidity management played an important role in ensuring organizational sustainability and long-term profitability. However, financial decision processes remained challenging because of high-dimensional financial indicators, temporal dependencies, multicollinearity and dynamic macroeconomic fluctuations which reduce model stability and predictive consistency. To overcome these challenges, a Hierarchical Risk-Aware Transformer-ActorCritic (HRAT-AC) framework is proposed for adaptive financial management optimization. Initially, Principal Component Analysis (PCA) is applied for removing the redundancy and reduce dimensionality by preserving dominant financial variance patterns. Subsequently, temporal attentionbased feature extraction mechanism captured inter-period financial dependencies and volatility-sensitive representations. Finally, HRAT-AC model integrates hierarchical policy learning, transformer-based contextual encoding and risksensitive reinforcement learning with conditional value at risk to perform sequential financial optimization. The proposed HRAT-AC ensures robust profitability maximization, controlled leverage exposure, improved liquidity stability and enhanced risk-adjusted performance under dynamic economic conditions.

title: Real-Time Output Linearization in Adaptive Predictive Control for Variable Rate Control of Agricultural Sprayers
authors: Deniver Reinke Schultz, Lucas Jonys Ribeiro Silva, Vilma Alves De Oliveira, Paulo E. Cruvinel
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Precise flow rate control in agricultural sprayers is critical for variable-rate application, but is challenged by nonlinear dynamics, dead zones, and model variations due to operational changes and nozzle switching. This work aims to develop an efficient control strategy capable of handling these challenges while ensuring compliance with operational constraints. To this end, a generalized predictive controller (GPC) integrated with a dynamic output-linearization scheme is proposed. The dynamic linearization plays a key role to enable adaptive control under practical operating conditions, without requiring a detailed characterization of the hydraulic system. To support this framework, a simplified Kalman filter is employed, to estimate, in real time, the total equivalent fluidic resistance of the system. Experimental validation was conducted using an agricultural sprayer development system, demonstrating superior performance compared to traditional methods, with improved accuracy and adaptability in the presence of disturbances. The proposed output linearizing fuzzy GPC controller (L-FGPC) effectively handles nonlinearities and operational constraints, offering a practical solution for advanced agricultural spraying systems.
