The mean Young's modulus obtained using graphite was 6.12 kPa, while for Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> was 12.14 kPa for healthy tissue. For benign tissue phantoms, a 15% PVA concentration was utilized, in combination with two FTCs. The mean Young's modulus resulting from the use of graphite was found to be 38.50 kPa, while for Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>, it was 39.21 kPa for benign tissue.

title: Analysis of Resistance Performance of High Dose Boron Doping
authors: Chea Hui Lee, Bohesharvind N. Murthi, Hank Chen, Wan Chun Zhang, Leonard M. Rubin
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: Thanks to their flexibility, single wafer implanters, such as the Axcelis Purion H200 are capable of delivering device electrical performance comparable to conventional multiple wafer machines, despite different machines having different beamlines and scanning systems. In this work, we study the impact of dose rate of a very high dose p-type implantation, boron 15 keV 8.50×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">15</sup> ions/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> on a polysilicon layer and compare the sheet resistance between a single wafer high current implanter, Axcelis Purion H200, and a 13-wafer batch high current implanter, Axcelis GSD200E<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. Different energies, dose, beam current, chiller temperature, implant mode and vertical scan speed are implanted on the Purion H200 on this low energy/high dose polysilicon layer to match the sheet resistance performance of the batch tool. On the Purion H200, the energy trim factor (deceleration) has been selected for a better polysilicon sheet resistance performance matching with minimal recipe modification to reduce logistical efforts.

title: Research on EMT Simulation of Power Systems Based on Coordination of ADPSS and MATLAB/Simulink
authors: Minli Gao, Haiyan Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the in-depth advancement of new-type power system construction, power systems are exhibiting the "dual-high" characteristics of high proportion of renewable energy and high proportion of power electronic equipment integration. This places higher demands on the accuracy, flexibility, and modeling efficiency of electromagnetic transient (EMT) simulations. A key limitation of traditional EMT simulation tools is the inherent difficulty in developing custom component models, a process characterized by significant complexity and time consumption. Consequently, these platforms create a bottleneck for the timely evaluation and validation of control algorithms for next-generation power electronic systems. To address this issue, this paper proposes an ADPSS-MATLAB/Simulink co-simulation method for power system EMT analysis, integrating the high-precision EMT simulation capability of ADPSS with the powerful numerical computation and modeling advantages of MATLAB/Simulink. Firstly, the core characteristics of ADPSS and MATLAB/Simulink and their interface interconnection principle are elaborated. Based on the ADPSS User-Defined Model (UDM) interface specification and combined with the MATLAB Embedded Coder code generation functionality, a complete technical workflow is established: "MATLAB/Simulink modeling - automatic code generation - ADPSS interface encapsulation - co-simulation validation". Key steps including model conversion, interface function customization, dynamic link library generation, and configuration file writing are designed in detail. Taking a Dual Active Bridge (DAB) DC-DC converter as the simulation object, employing a single phase-shift control strategy for validation, the control model is built in Simulink while the main circuit is constructed in ADPSS. EMT simulations are carried out in both a pure ADPSS environment and the ADPSS-MATLAB co-simulation environment. By comparing key simulation results, such as the transformer primary and secondary voltages, the engineering feasibility of the proposed ADPSS calling MATLAB co-simulation method is verified, providing a highly practical technical solution for the development and application of custom models in power system EMT simulations.

title: Innovative Generative AI-Resistant Assessment Methods for Engineering Education in the AI Era
authors: Khaled Shaaban
abstract: The use of advanced generative AI presents a significant challenge to the integrity and effectiveness of traditional engineering assessments. This work addresses this challenge by proposing various assessment methods designed to be resistant to AI use. These methods change the focus from productbased and text-heavy outputs to process-oriented and competencybased evaluations. These methods are built on concepts emphasizing hands-on creation, real-time performance, reflection, and complex interpersonal skills. The strategies range from inclass practical exams and project-based learning to oral examinations, peer reviews, and design reviews. The different methods outlined in this work provide a clear pathway forward, demonstrating that the most effective defense against generative AI is not more sophisticated detection, but more human-centered pedagogy. By focusing assessments on uniquely human skills such as hands-on creation, real-time adaptation, and ethical reasoning, engineering instructors can not only maintain academic integrity but also more effectively foster the professional competencies required of future engineers.

title: Beyond Geography: A Multidimensional Approach for New Geography Economic and Practical Application in Engineering Education
authors: Yuxuan Xiong
abstract: This paper builds on Krugman's New Economic Geography (NEG) by introducing a framework that extends traditional geography-only cluster analysis to include individual-level characteristics using clustering algorithms and Kernel Density Estimation (KDE). Rather than depending exclusively on geographic proximity, this multidimensional approach conceptually integrates diverse individual-level attributes to enable a richer and more detailed identification of agglomeration patterns. This paper offers guidance and a conceptual foundation for future empirical research aiming to capture the complex interaction between geographic and non-geographic factors that influence economic clusters. To better illustrate how the method generalizes to social sectors, we apply it conceptually to engineering education setting: institutions are embedded in a joint space of latitude-longitude and characteristics such as research intensity, faculty size, endowment, etc. We then relate multidimensional density to outcomes including graduate placement rates, research output (e.g., publications and citations), and external funding. Furthermore, to bridge macro-level patterns with micro-level behaviors, this paper proposes a visionary LLM-driven simulation, utilizing the latest agent-toagent (A2A) technology. In this framework, each individual in a cluster is represented by an LLM-based “agent” that is aware of its own profile and characteristics. These agents make decisions based on non-linear behaviors, leveraging the current state-of-the-art agent-based modeling (ABM) techniques. Although the agent-to-agent simulation is conceptual at this stage, it sets the foundation for future work that combines spatial econometric models with agent-based methods to gain a deeper understanding of how clusters form and evolve.

title: Impact of Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization on Banknote Recognition
authors: Nima Sharifinia, Mohammad Javad Parseh
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: The detection of old and worn banknotes is crucial for banking automation and counterfeit prevention. Traditional methods rely on machine learning models, which require large datasets and high computational resources. This study proposes an efficient image-processing-based approach for detecting worn banknotes without machine learning. The method employs grayscale histograms, HSV histograms, and Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE) to enhance contrast and extract wear-related features such as discoloration, faded ink, and creases. We further introduce a novel grid-based evaluation mechanism with Best-Match Voting and Thresholding to localize key regions and reduce misclassification. Experimental results show that combining CLAHE with the grid mechanism ensures reliable detection while maintaining computational efficiency, making the approach suitable for real-time automated banking systems.

title: Challenges in Generalizing Automated Breast Density Classification Across Mammography Datasets
authors: Rodrigo Leite Prates, Wilfrido Gómez-Flores, Wagner C. A. Pereira
publish: 2026 Global Medical Engineering Physics Exchanges/ Pan American Health Care Exchanges (GMEPE/PAHCE)
abstract: Breast tissue density is associated with increased breast cancer risk and reduced lesion visibility in mammography, motivating the development of automated density classification methods. However, generalization across heterogeneous datasets remains a major challenge. This study investigates factors that limit the transferability of automated breast density classification models across public digitized mammography datasets. We systematically evaluate preprocessing strategies and four convolutional neural network architectures (VGG16, ResNet50, InceptionV3, and EfficientNetV2-L) under both within-dataset and cross-dataset settings, including contrast normalization, artifact handling, pectoral muscle removal, and view-specific training strategies. In particular, we analyze single-view training, in which models are trained on a single mammographic view and tested across all available views to assess cross-view generalization. Experiments conducted on two public digitized mammography datasets show that VGG16 and ResNet50 consistently achieve the most stable and competitive performance across datasets. In within-dataset evaluations, the best models achieve per-class F1-scores above 0.80, while cross-dataset performance degrades substantially, with F1-scores typically ranging from 0.50 to 0.70. Overall, the results highlight the impact of dataset shift on breast density classification and provide practical recommendations for building robust, reproducible systems suitable for deployment across heterogeneous clinical environments.

title: A Comparative Study on Data Structures for Power Electronical Circuit Topology Derivation
authors: Botao Cao, Ke Jin, Xujun Jiang, Xingshuo Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: The conventional topology derivation methods suffer from low efficiency and difficulties in exhaustively enumerating all possible topological structures. New approaches utilizing different graph-based storage structures still lack a comparative study on the applicability of various storage formats under different circuit conditions. Using the Buck converter as an example, this paper conducts a systematic comparative study of three graph-based storage structures, which include the adjacency matrix, adjacency list, and orthogonal list. Their performance is evaluated across three key dimensions: memory usage, runtime efficiency under different circuit scales, and capability to handle bidirectional components. It is indicated that each structure exhibits distinct advantages: the adjacency matrix is suitable for small-scale topology verification; the adjacency list offers balanced performance in medium-scale circuits; while the orthogonal list achieves optimal efficiency in large-scale circuits with multiple bidirectional components. This study provides a quantitative basis for selecting data structures in automated topology derivation. All findings are thoroughly validated through simulations.

title: Liquid Democracy: An Annotated Reading List
authors: Georgios Papasotiropoulos, Ulrike Schmidt-Kraepelin
abstract: None

title: Editors' Introduction
authors: Irene Lo, Sam Taggart
abstract: None

title: SONIC: Event-Driven Gate-Level Simulator of Ternary VLSI Circuits using Delta Cycles
authors: Steven Bos, Sondre Bitubekk, Ole Christian Moholth, Henning Gundersen
publish: 2026 IEEE 56th International Symposium on Multiple-Valued Logic (ISMVL)
abstract: The simulation of ternary and mixed-radix very large scale integration (VLSI) circuits has largely been unaddressed in multiple-valued logic (MVL) literature. In this work we present SONIC; a zero-delay, event-driven gate-level simulator that iterates delta cycles to quiescence per stimulus. This novel and open source MVL electronic design automation (EDA) tool enables for the first time simulation of ternary integrated circuits (SONIC) with VLSI designs (eg. 10.000+ switching devices). It can automate post-synthesis verification of our REBEL-2 ternary CPU and comes with a REBEL-2 assembler to program it. We report that SONIC drastically improves the simulation speed of our Mixed Radix Circuit Synthesizer (MRCS) by 5-6 orders of magnitude (120.000x - 1.6Mx) on various benchmark circuits. The C#/.Net8 simulator features design rule checks (DRC), a benchmark suite, binary coded ternary (BCT) Verilog export to test on FPGA’ss or tape-out as ASIC, a database and a command line interface (CLI) for integration in other EDA tooling.

title: Event-Triggered YOLO Segmentation for Measuring Drop-Induced Cavities Over Submerged Sediments
authors: Rishikesh, Arian Iqbal, Hossein Haghgou, Brian Nutter, Hadis Matinpour, Hamed Sari-Sarraf
publish: 2026 IEEE Southwest Symposium on Image Analysis and Interpretation (SSIAI)
abstract: Quantitative analysis of droplet-impact experiments for sediment erosion studies is traditionally hindered by laborintensive, subjective manual video annotation. This paper presents an automated, hybrid computer vision system developed to address this bottleneck. The system combines a lightweight, classical background-subtraction trigger (MOG2) with a deep learning instance segmentation model (YOLOv11) to efficiently detect and segment three classes: Cavity, Sediment (coarse beads), and Cluster (fine beads). This analysis pipeline is embedded in an interactive, “human-in-the-loop” dashboard that allows for realtime validation and operator-guided fine-tuning. We demonstrate the system's efficacy on a 2×2 experimental matrix, comparing shallow and deep water regimes with both coarse and fine-grained sediments. Our tool successfully extracts key metrics (e.g., cavity area, sediment area) over time, clearly quantifying the distinct dynamics of direct cavity-bed impact versus indirect, time-delayed vortex-driven erosion. This work provides an efficient, objective, and reproducible tool that enables high-throughput data extraction for complex fluid-dynamics research.

title: A Multimodal Large Language Model Approach for Cross-modal Alignment of Remote Sensing Imagery and Open Source Intelligence
authors: JianXu, Xiang Cui, Ping Wang, Xiaoyu Li, Wenbo Zuo, Xiwei Zhang
abstract: The fusion of remote sensing(RS) imagery with open-source intelligence(OSINT) is hindered by a fundamental cross-modal semantic gap. This letter presents a lightweight multimodal large language model(MLLM) tailored for RS-OSINT alignment and geospatial reasoning. Our approach integrates a contrastive alignment module with a novel geospatial entity constraint to unify optical, SAR, and text features in a shared semantic space. A geography-aware reasoning backbone is obtained by fine-tuning LLaMA-3-8B via Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA). We also contribute a benchmark dataset of aligned image-text pairs and QA samples from three distinct regions. Experiments demonstrate that the proposed method consistently outperforms existing RS-specific vision-language models across retrieval, fact verification, and answer generation tasks, while substantially reducing hallucinations. Ablation studies confirm the contribution of each component. The framework shows that explicit geospatial constraints enable reliable RS-OSINT fusion with limited annotations, offering a practical solution for real-time intelligence applications.

title: Identify Imaging-Genomics Association in Adolescents via Decentralized Parallel Independent Component Analysis
authors: Chan Aek Panichvatana, Bishal Thapaliya, Vivek Benegal, Bharath Holla, Gunter Schumann, Nilakshi Vaidya, Vince D. Calhoun, Jingyu Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE Southwest Symposium on Image Analysis and Interpretation (SSIAI)
abstract: This study integrates brain structural MRI and genomic data to identify brain-genetic associations during adolescence to help understand the pathology of major depressive disorder (MDD) and attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD). Using data from three international adolescent cohorts (a total of 8306 participants), we applied Decentralized Parallel Independent Component Analysis (dpICA), a federated multimodal fusion algorithm that enables joint analyses of MRI images and single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs) without data sharing. For MDD risk SNPs, dpICA revealed a significant imaging-genetic association (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r=0.066$</tex>), where the brain component highlighted the bilateral precuneus and cuneus within the default network. The associated SNP component comprised 21 loci across 6 genes, and intergenic and noncoding regions. For ADHD risk SNPs, dpICA identified a stronger association (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r=0.08$</tex>) linking widespread gray matter alterations in the paracentral, the middle and inferior frontal, the superior and middle temporal, and inferior parietal gyri, with 30 SNP loci across 13 genes and non-coding regions. The genes are significantly enriched for targets of microRNA and transcription factor regulation mechanisms. Although the effect sizes of such brain-genomic associations are small, likely due to the very small percentage of participants with MDD or ADHD in the general population, these results demonstrate dpICA's potential to capture interpretable multimodal associations using distributed datasets. Future investigation leveraging advanced techniques is warranted to reveal nonlinear relations between the brain and genomics.

title: Design and Development of High-Current E-mode GaN p-FET and Complementary Logic Circuits on UID-GaN/AlGaN Platform
authors: Xuanming Zhang, Yunsong Xu, Jiachen Duan, Haotian Ji, Jun Wen, Zhiwei Sun, Zifeng Qu, Jiangmin Gu, Ye Liang, Ivona Mitrovic, Harm Van Zalinge, Jie Zhang, Xi Chen, Sen Huang, Wen Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: The low current density of enhancement mode (E-mode) GaN p-channel heterojunction field-effect transistors (p-FETs) is fundamental limitation in complementary logic (CL) circuits. In this work, we propose a high-current E-mode GaN p-FET and demonstrate its implementation in CL circuits on the UID-GaN/AlGaN platform. Hall measurement results demonstrate a high hole sheet density (p<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">SH</inf>) of 3.22 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">13</sup> cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−2</sup> at the UID-GaN/AlGaN interface in the proposed heterostructure at 300 K, compared to 1.91 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">13</sup> cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−2</sup> in the conventional p-GaN/AlGaN structure. The fabricated p-FETs exhibit a high ON-state current (I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf>) of 12.3 mA/mm, a high I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf>/I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OFF</inf> ratio of 1.8 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">8</sup>, an E-mode threshold voltage (V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">TH</inf>) of −1.5 V. Furthermore, we also demonstrate n-FET on the same epitaxial platform, an I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf> of 200 mA/mm, a high I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf>/I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OFF</inf> ratio exceeding 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">9</sup>, and a V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">TH</inf> of 1.1 V was obtained. Finally, a GaN CL inverters, not-and (NAND) and not-or (NOR) were successfully fabricated, achieving both rail-to-rail voltage swing and ultralow static power consumption. The high-current GaN p-FET on UID-GaN/AlGaN on Si platform provides significant opportunities toward complementary circuit integration.

title: SCM-Label: A Spectral Correction-based Pseudo-Label Generation Method for Dual-Polarization SAR Image Colorization
authors: Yongkang Chen, Peng Wang
abstract: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) colorization aims to enhance the interpretability of microwave imagery by providing intuitive spectral information. However, the scarcity of high-quality ground truth (GT) labels remains a primary bottleneck. Traditional methods often directly adopt optical RGB images as labels, which leads to significant radiometric misalignment and the loss of intrinsic SAR scattering characteristics due to the disparate imaging mechanisms of SAR and optical sensors. To address this, we propose a Spectral Correction Module (SCM)-based label generation method designed for dual-polarization SAR images consisting of vertical-vertical (VV) and vertical-horizontal (VH) polarizations. The SCM effectively integrates the spectral information of optical imagery with the radiometric texture of SAR data through histogram matching and residual fusion, ensuring the generated labels possess both natural color semantics and physical SAR backscattering properties. Based on this method, we construct the Dual-Polarization SAR Colorization Dataset (DPSCD), comprising 4,223 image pairs from Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2. Experimental results demonstrate that labels generated by SCM exhibit superior radiometric consistency compared to raw optical data. Furthermore, downstream task validation demonstrates that training with SCM-generated labels significantly improves the Overall Accuracy (OA) of land-cover classification. This work provides a robust data foundation and a novel labeling paradigm for SAR image colorization and multi-modal Earth observation.

title: Research on the Development of Satellite Communication Network Technology for Low-Altitude Economy
authors: Huifang Su, Xuemei Li, Zheng Zhao
abstract: As a representative of new quality productive forces, low-altitude economy is entering a stage of substantive promotion and has become a key direction for new growth drivers. In response to the explosive growth of future low-altitude economy demands, this paper systematically analyzes the satellite communication requirements of different low-altitude aircraft during specific missions. Based on user demands, we investigate the design of satellite networks and the evolution of service technologies, and explore application scenarios of the low-altitude economy and required capabilities of satellite networks. The system ability and design philosophy for massive user management and control, as well as the low-altitude economy service models for applying satellite networks are also proposed.

title: Spatially Adaptive Tensor Regularization for Sanjiangyuan-1 Multispectral Image Destriping to Enhance AI Interpretation
authors: Yuxuan Guo, Guoxin Chen, Xu Ma, Qiong Li, Kanzhuo Suonan, Xiaobin Dai
abstract: Currently, multispectral satellite observations serve as the foundational data source for intelligent earth observation systems, particularly in automated land-cover segmentation and target recognition. Nevertheless, the inherent hardware limitations of push-broom scanners often contaminate the captured scenes with structured vertical stripes, severely corrupting both spatial geometry and spectral signatures. Such degradation is catastrophic for data-driven algorithms. To mitigate this challenge, we introduce a spatially adaptive tensor regularization paradigm. Diverging from conventional methods, our approach seamlessly integrates a gradient-aware weighting mechanism into a third-order tensor frame. This mathematically ensures the preservation of high-frequency edges while enforcing profound spectral correlation across multiple bands. The formulated non-smooth objection is robustly decoupled and optimized via an customized Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM). Evaluations utilizing authentic Sanjiangyuan-1 satellite observations confirm that our solver effortlessly eradicates directional artifacts without compromising fine-grained textures. Ultimately, this framework delivers pristine datasets, acting as a highly reliable preprocessing pipeline to minimize misclassification errors in downstream deep learning applications.

title: Big Data Analysis for Forest Cover Prediction with PySpark
authors: Meherunnesa Hossain Ibnath, Sadia Sabrin Neha, Farhana Najnin Mahi, Nusrat Jahan
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Forest cover type prediction is a highly critical task in environmental monitoring and management. In this study, PySpark, a distributed computing engine, is used to analyze the large forest cover data and predict the type of forest cover based on various machine learning models. The dataset has four variables - soil type, elevation, slope and hydrologic distance. The data has been preprocessed which included: feature scaling; handling missing data and synthetic minority oversampling technique (SMOTE). Vectorization and scaling are also applied to the data to prepare it for machine learning algorithms. A variety of classification algorithms such as Logistic Regression, Multilayer Perceptron (MLP), Random Forest, Decision Tree, Support Vector Machine (SVM), and CatBoost are used on the preprocessed data for the prediction of forest cover type. It is noted that CatBoost is better than the rest of the models with 96.18 % accuracy. This work demonstrates the potential of big data analytics and distributed computing frameworks like PySpark in environmental prediction applications, with a high-performance solution to large-scale classification problems in forestry and ecology.

title: Explainable Gradient-Boosted Malware Detection Using SHAP and Static PE Features
authors: Abdullah Al Siam, Abdul Kadar Muhammad Masum, Dewan Md. Farid, Abubokor Hanip, Md Maruf Hassan
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Machine learning has become an important part of modern malware detection. However, many high-performing models act like black boxes. This makes it difficult to use them effectively in security operations. This research presents an interpretable framework for classifying malware. It uses gradient-boosted decision trees that are trained on static data from Windows PE files. The proposed model is trained on a dataset from the EMBER benchmark. It delivers strong detection results, with an accuracy of 90.72%, a recall of 93.51%, and an ROC-AUC of 0.9691. To improve transparency, SHAP (Shapley Additive Explanations) is added to the pipeline. This helps identify which structural and statistical file attributes most influence model predictions. Experimental evaluations show that SHAP successfully identifies important features like entropy patterns and header anomalies. This provides analysts with useful insights that boost confidence and help with incident response. While creating detailed explanations can be computationally intensive, especially at scale, the findings suggest that combining machine learning with explainability leads to a more reliable and useful malware detection system. Future improvements could involve real-time explanation techniques, integration with threat intelligence frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK, and the addition of dynamic behavioral attributes.

title: TomatoNet-R50: A ResNet50-Based Deep Learning Architecture for the Detection of Tomato Leaf Disease
authors: Md. Samiul Alom, Md Istiak Ahamed, Ferdaus Anam Jibon, Nazmul Hasan Shouvo, A. H. M. Kamal, Abrar Rakin
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Tomato leaf diseases spread rapidly and reduce crop quality, and early detection is still difficult when the data set is small. Deep models work well, but many of them overfit and do not stay stable in limited images. This study aims to build a model that learns useful patterns without relying on a large data set. This study proposes TomatoNet-R50, a modified version of ResNet50 with a light and regularized classification head. The backbone stays fixed in the first stage of training, and only the new head learns the class boundaries. The model uses batch normalization, dropout, and a small dense layer to keep the features clean and avoid noise. A simple training pipeline with controlled augmentation prepares the images for the network. Three deep models and three machine-learning classifiers are included for comparison. TomatoNet-R50 reaches the highest score among all tested models, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{9 8. 3 3 \%}$</tex> accuracy, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{9 8. 5 4 \%} \text{F 1}$</tex>-score and 99.88% ROC-AUC. It performs better than EfficientNet-B0, MobileNetV2, VGG16, and the default ResNet50 head, and it also beats SVM, Random Forest, and XGBoost. The model remains stable during training and shows low signs of overfitting even with only 303 images. The results show that a simple change in the classifier head can make a strong difference when the data set is small. TomatoNet-R50 is lightweight, easy to train and works well in images of tomato leaf disease. With more samples and stronger explainability tools, such as LIME and SHAP, the model can be extended for real-world use.

title: EffiViT-Hybrid: A CNN–Transformer Framework for Pancreatic Cancer Detection from CT Images
authors: Mst.Masuma Akter Lameya, Md.Sani Islam Shanto, Sultana Rokeya Naher, Md Istiak Ahamed, Abrar Rakin, Ferdaus Anam Jibon
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Pancreatic cancer is one of the most fatal diseases, with a survival rate under 11% as it is very difficult to detect in the early stages. The tumors are often small and ill-defined in CT scans, making manual diagnosis time-consuming and prone to errors. To solve this problem, we propose a new deep learning model called EffiViT-Hybrid, which combines the local feature extraction strengths of EfficientNet with the global context modeling of Vision Transformers (ViT). This hybrid design allows the model to capture both fine details and long-range dependencies in the medical images. Our experimental results show that this approach works very well. The proposed model achieved a high testing accuracy of 97.74% and an ROC AUC of 99.90% on our internal dataset, performing better than standalone CNN and Transformer models. To ensure our model is truly robust, we also tested it on a completely separate external validation dataset. The model confirmed its reliability by achieving an accuracy of 95.54% on this unseen data. This study shows that the EffiViT-Hybrid model is a powerful and generalizable tool that can assist radiologists in making faster and more accurate diagnoses, potentially improving clinical outcomes.

title: A 27-year Longitudinal Study on the Structure of the Internet Topology: Scale-freeness and Preferential Attachment
authors: Wei Koong Chai, Assemgul Kozhabek, Benjamin Dodwell
abstract: In this paper, we present a 27-year longitudinal study of the Internet topology at Autonomous System (AS)-level, from 1998 to 2025. The Internet has been claimed to be scale-free exhibiting power-law degree distribution. This claim has been challenged from several fronts (e.g., rigor of the statistical method, assumptions made on finiteness of the network, completeness of available data). Using the latest statistical methodology, we revisit and verify whether this claim still holds. Using power-law as the reference distribution, we use maximum-likelihood fitting method with goodness-of-fit tests based on the Kolmogorov-Smirnov statistic and likelihood ratios to identify the best fitting distribution among several common statistical distributions. In fact, being scale-free directly implies the property would remain over the passage of time and hence, we investigate how this has evolved over the past nearly three decades.We found that power-law is not the best fit for the degree distribution of the Internet. For most years, truncated power-law offers better fit while log-normal is often better than power-law. Furthermore, the simple preferential attachment model has been attributed as the underlying generative mechanism that consequently results in the topology we have today. Based on the two methods we used, we found that while there is indication of preferential attachment as previously discovered, this is only true for smaller values of degree rather than the entire distribution and that the attachment behavior hovers between both sub-linear and super-linear attachment regimes at different time points. Finally, we also found that new links do not only tend to attach to high-degree nodes. There is also a large number of new links that join low-degree nodes, contradicting the claim on new nodes prefer to join high-degree nodes. Nevertheless, when weighted by the number of nodes with specific degree ranges, the net effect of “rich-gets-richer” still stands due to imbalance node population across different degrees.

title: Trend and Anomaly Detection of Weather Variables and Their Agro-Implications for Mango Cultivation in Rajshahi
authors: Abdullah Al Bassam, Abdullah Tamim, Md. Mahfujur Rahman, Md. Mahmuduzzaman Ikbal, D.M. Asadujjaman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This research analyzes the impact of long-term climate variability on mango cultivation in Rajshahi, Bangladesh, based on daily weather data from 2012 to 2023. Being the most important mango-producing region in the country, Rajshahi is undergoing temperature increase, humidity decrease and more and more unpredicted rainfall which mainly affects mangoes flowering, fruit set and yield stability. A time-series analytical framework was implemented to analyze these changes, which included exploratory data analysis, K-Means pattern clustering, anomaly detection, and multi-model forecasting with ETS, Prophet, XGBoost, LightGBM, and LSTM. The data points towards a consistent warming trend through the mango season (February–June) with corresponding higher solar radiation and lower humidity. By clustering, researchers were able to determine four major seasonal weather regimes normal, hot-dry, humid, and cool-dry suggesting changes in the pre-monsoon climatic behavior. Heatwaves analysis revealed the occurrence of more than 150 days of extreme heat during some years, which means that the thermal stress and the moisture imbalance have increased. Predictions made up to 2030 indicate that there will be a warming trend continued with a very slight recovery in humidity, thus the interaction between heat and humidity will be stronger which may lead to increased disease pressure and shortened fruit maturation cycles. In general, this research work serves as a quantitative standard for the understanding of Rajshahi's changing agro-climate and provides important information for the climate-resilient mango production, water management, and regional agricultural policy planning.

title: Differential Performance Analysis of Schmitt-Trigger-Based SRAM Arrays for Low-Power Applications
authors: Sabiha Amin Tonny, Shihab Ahmed, Hasan Maruf, Rashedul Islam Rashed, ASM Shihavuddin, Chayan Mondal
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Low-power and energy-efficient memory systems are critical for reliable operation and extended lifetime of remote healthcare devices. Static random-access memory (SRAM), owing to its fast access time, low latency, and nonrefresh operation, is well suited for such energy-constrained applications. This paper presents a differential performance analysis of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 2$</tex> SRAM arrays implemented using conventional 6T, modified Schmitt-trigger 11T (MST-11T), Schmitt-trigger 12T (ST-12T), and Schmitt-trigger 13T (ST-13T) SRAM cells. Key performance metrics, including power consumption, delay, bandwidth, energy consumption and Energy Delay product (EDP), are systematically evaluated to assess the suitability of each architecture. Simulation results demonstrate that the ST12 T array achieves the lowest power consumption, exhibiting reductions of 2.06 times, 1.88 times, and 1.19 times compared to the 6T, MST-11T, and ST-13T arrays, respectively. In addition, the ST-12T array delivers superior bandwidth, achieving approximately 0.47 times, 0.54 times, and 0.55 times improvement over the corresponding designs. Overall, the ST-12T-based SRAM array offers an effective trade-off between low power, high energy efficiency, and high-speed performance, making it a strong candidate for energy-constrained and highreliability healthcare applications. All designs and performance evaluations are carried out using Cadence Virtuoso in 45 nm CMOS technology.

title: Automated Vegetation Encroachment Monitoring in Power Line Corridors Using Deep Semantic Segmentation of Uav Imagery
authors: MD. Tanvirul Haque, Shaptapurna Chowdhury, Md. Shahid Iqbal, Masuk Billah, Naim Ahmed
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The encroachment of vegetation along power lines is a major cause of grid outages and even safety risks in power line corridors. Traditional surveillance systems are expensive and can be extremely infrequent that require automated and highquality analysis. In this paper, we present a single deep learning backbone to perform semantic segmentation of vegetation, power lines and background of Unmanned Aerial Vehicle images. To train an effective end-to-end model to overcome the severe class imbalance intrinsic to this task, especially the problem of thin power lines segmentation, we use UNet architecture with a pretrained ResNet34 backbone. With a composite loss form based on weighted Cross-Entropy and multiclass Dice loss, our singlemodel system provides a much simpler workflow than the later multi-model ensemble models. Our structure shows the state of the art performance with respect to publicly available benchmark dataset as evaluated using Intersection over Union, Precision and Recall measures. The model achieves the overall mean IoU of 0.9877 on real-world test data, which is further supported with Precision and Recall values across all classes, with the most notable values associated with the power line class. It is an accurate, efficient and practical approach to automated monitoring of the power line infrastructure which is a valuable tool in improving the resilience of the grid and avoiding failures that happen due to vegetation.

title: MC-BCNet: MobileNet Family-Based Feature-Fusion CNN with Grad-CAM and Monte Carlo Dropout for Efficient Breast Cancer Screening
authors: Israth Jahan Worthy, Nusrath Jahan Worney, Samiha Muntaha Mahin, Tahmina Hasan, Humaira Adiba, Sara Karim
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The accurate and timely diagnosis of breast cancer is critical for improving patient survival; however, manual interpretation of medical images is time-consuming and prone to inter-observer variability. Although deep learning–based approaches have demonstrated strong diagnostic performance, many state-of-the-art models are computationally intensive and lack transparency, limiting their adoption in real-world clinical environments. This study proposes MC-BCNet, a resourceefficient and trustworthy diagnostic framework that evaluates lightweight Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) from the MobileNet family for automated breast cancer screening. A comparative analysis of MobileNet, MobileNetV2, MobileNetV3-Large, and MobileNetV3-Small is conducted using transfer learning backbones integrated into a highly feature-extractive CNN architecture. Experiments are performed on two medical imaging modalities: the BreakHis histopathology dataset (1,600 images with offline data augmentation and oversampling) and a breast ultrasound dataset (3,000 images). Stratified train–validation-test splitting is employed to ensure unbiased evaluation, and performance is assessed using accuracy, sensitivity, and ROC–AUC metrics. To enhance clinical trustworthiness, the framework incorporates Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (GradCAM) for visual explainability and Monte Carlo Dropout for predictive uncertainty quantification. Experimental results show that the baseline MobileNet achieves superior sensitivity on complex histopathology images with 91% accuracy, while all MobileNet variants attain 99% accuracy on ultrasound scans. High-confidence predictions are confirmed through uncertainty histograms, and Grad-CAM visualizations successfully highlight clinically relevant tumor regions. Overall, the proposed framework effectively balances computational efficiency, diagnostic accuracy, and interpretability, offering a reliable solution for automated breast cancer screening in resource-constrained healthcare settings.

title: An Analytical Theory of Avalanche Breakdown in p-n Junctions Derived From Semiconductor Band Structure_supp1-3698391.pdf
authors: Yaping Dan
abstract: This article presents a comprehensive analytical theory for the avalanche breakdown process in p-n junction diodes to address the critical reliance on empirical models in the design of avalanche photodiodes (APDs) and single-photon avalanche diodes (SPADs). The proposed model is derived from fundamental carrier continuity equations and impact excitation probability. Eventually, it yields closed-form expressions for the avalanche current density and the breakdown voltage. It demonstrates excellent agreement with experimental current–voltage characteristics across various semiconductors (Si, GaN, 4H-SiC, and InP) and a wide temperature range. TCAD simulations are also adopted to validate this model. The theory establishes a unified, physics-based criterion for defining the avalanche breakdown voltage, revealing its functional dependence on material parameters (bandgap, dielectric constant, and mean-free path) and doping concentrations. Significantly, it explicitly predicts the theoretical minimum and maximum limits for the breakdown voltage in a given material, which were well known but only implicitly reflected in previous empirical models. This work provides a rigorous theoretical framework for the steady-state dark avalanche current for the targeted design of diverse avalanche-based devices, ranging from low-breakdown-voltage SPADs for integrated quantum photonics to high-breakdown-voltage power devices, moving beyond trial-and-error approaches.

title: Transfer Learning-Based Explainable Deep Learning Model for Lung Cancer Detection Using CT Scan Images
authors: Sadek Al Prince, Gazi Mehraj Sakib, Aziz Misher Mishu
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Lung cancer is among the most common causes of cancer death in the entire world. Therefore, timely and precise diagnosis of lung cancer is essential in facilitating the survival chances of patients. Computer-aided diagnosis (CAD) systems based on deep learning have demonstrated outstanding potential in recent years to enable automatic medical image analysis. The research paper would suggest an effective and explicable deep transfer learning system of multi-class lung cancer based on computed tomography (CT) images. Several state-of-the-art convolutional neural network (CNN) models have been implemented and tested to make a comparative analysis; these are DenseNet121, ResNet50, ResNet101, ResNet152, VGG19, EfficientNetB4, InceptionV3, and a custom CNN. The highest-performing one was the DenseNet121 model, which has an accuracy of 99.55 %, a precision of 0.9944, a recall of 0.9960, and an F1-score of 0.9952. This is due to the high level of performance that is caused by dense connectivity of DenseNets, which promotes feature reuse and reinforcement of gradient flow that facilitates better learning of complex patterns in lung tissue. Explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) methods, such as Grad-CAM++ and Sharp-CAM, were also incorporated to generate salient points in the CT images to ensure that the model emphasizes clinically significant points. Experimental findings indicate that the suggested DenseNet121-based system is robust, trustworthy, and very efficient with regard to automated classification of lung cancers; thus, it is a potentially viable decisionsupport methodology to aid radiologists in early diagnosis and detection of lung cancer.

title: PestVRrice: A Unity3D-Based Virtual Reality System for Paddy Field Pest Identification and Farmer Training
authors: Argha Aditi Roy, Mst. Kamrun Nahar Bithi, Nusrika Aktar Hia, Bushra Akter, Zannat Hossain Tamim, Most. Sharmin Sultana, Md. Sazzadur Rahman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Paddy farming should also have good pest management practices and these processes involve the identification of the pest, knowledge of the pest life cycles and decision-making based on informed communication in the multifaceted farm systems. Training techniques that were used in the past, where textbooks are used and to a degree, static pictures or written descriptions do not depict the dynamic and spatial interactions that occur in the environment, thus leading to delayed or suboptimal decisions. In order to nullify these shortcomings, PestVRrice, a Virtual Reality (VR)-based training model, is suggested in this study, which will bring the users into the microscopic and macroscopic agricultural environments. This system will enable the user to view the pest development stages, including the eggs, nymphs, pupa and the adult insects in an entirely interactive three-dimensional crop setting. Besides the canopy of crops, other rural aspects like irrigation channels, homesteads, access roads, local fauna and field equipment are also added to bring out the realworld environment. These environments will allow the trainees to move freely, explore them, and touch them and therefore increase their knowledge of spatial relationships, pest progression and ecology whilst at the same time making decisions which are not risky in a safe and controlled environment. PestVRice will enable learners to anticipate pest outbreaks, detect early warning signs and formulate proactive management strategies with the assistance of high-quality visual imagery, simulation of realistic environmental indicators, and an interactive VR navigation. This engaging approach bridges the gap between the theoretical and practical knowledge, and therefore it is a sustainable, universal and successful solution to the problem of imparting agricultural education and in particular to those trainees who have little prior experience in the field.

title: Forensic Invariant Learning for Synthetic Image Detection: Bridging Benford's Law and Topological Analysis with Machine Learning Ensembles
authors: Md Abu Sayeb Rayhan, Ajoy Shutradhar, Hustagna Hiya
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: In recent days, The widespread usage of synthetic images from architecturally diverse generators has been significantly influencing society, culture and political environment. This is particularly concerning for developing nations like Bangladesh. A massive number of AI-generated images are circulating on social media, fueling misinformation and sometimes triggering social conflict or personal harassment. Advanced models such as GANs, Diffusion and Transformer-based systems are generating increasingly realistic visuals, often indistinguishable from genuine photos. Moreover, rapid evolution of gen-AI is weakening the reliability of existing detection systems by eliminating generator-specific artifacts. Existing detectors achieve high indistribution accuracy, but suffer catastrophic degradation (often <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>30 \%$</tex>) on unseen generators. We propose Forensic Invariant Learning (FIL), a detection framework grounded in generatoragnostic forensic signals. A six-model stacking ensemble with a logistic regression meta-learner is combined with a conservative physics-informed gating mechanism calibrated at the 95th percentile of the natural-image distribution. Evaluated on eight generators with over 38,000 images, FIL achieves 85.4% accuracy (AUC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=0.933$</tex>) on the combined test set and 74.1% mean LOGO accuracy across all eight generator families, demonstrating robustness to unseen architectures. Statistical significance is confirmed via McNemar's test <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(p<0.05$</tex> against 4 of 5 baselines), Wilcoxon signed-rank test and 2,000 iteration bootstrap confidence intervals <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(95 \%)$</tex> CI: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$[0.846,0.862])$</tex>. A controlled comparison against six baseline methods on identical data splits demonstrates consistent advantages (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{+ 0. 8 - 1 0. 5 \%}$</tex> over baselines). A five-configuration ablation study confirms that novel features contribute <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{+ 1. 2 \%}$</tex> accuracy, physics features <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{+ 1. 6 \%}$</tex> and stacking architecture <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{+ 0. 9 \%}$</tex> over voting. The CPU-only pipeline (12 MB model, 40 features) enables deployment on commodity hardware.

title: RBE-Sent: A Synthetic Roman Bengali-English Code-Mixed Dataset For Sentiment Analysis
authors: Dalia Barua, Tarandeep Singh Walia
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Datasets play a crucial role in natural language processing (NLP), enabling the development and evaluation of various tasks. However, in the Bengali-English code-mixed context, sentiment analysis research faces a major challenge due to the severe scarcity of dataset resources. This work, in this paper, presents RBE-Sent, a new synthetic Roman BengaliEnglish (RBE) code-mixed product review dataset designed for sentiment analysis. The corpus was constructed from English Amazon web shopping reviews with a hybrid method that combined translation, transliteration, and a novel lexicon-based ensemble approach for sentiment tagging. RBE-Sent comprises Bengali-matrix code-mixed reviews and showed benchmark performance for training and testing on Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), multilingual Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (mBERT), and Support Vector Machine (SVM) models. The new dataset generation process can similarly be applied to generate other Englishengaged code-mixed resources.

title: A Comparative Study of Classical and Hybrid Quantum Machine Learning Models for Skin Lesion Classification Using Latent Feature Embeddings
authors: Rifat Hossain, Habiba Rafique, Tasnin Tanvir, Abidar Rahaman, Ali Shiri Sichani
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This study investigates the potential of Hybrid Quantum Machine Learning (HQML) to improve the classification of skin lesions, a critical task for early detection of skin cancer. We utilized latent feature representations extracted by a Mobile CLIP model from a skin lesion dataset. These features were classified using three leading classical machine learning (ML) models-Logistic Regression (LR), Gradient Boosting (GB), and Support Vector Machine (SVM)-and compared against two quantum models: the Quantum Kernel Support Vector Machine (QSVM) and a Variational Quantum Classifier (VQC). All models were trained on features reduced to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4}$</tex> dimensions via PCA. The results indicate that while classical models achieved high overall accuracy (GB: 0.9452, LR: 0.9424, SVM: 0.9292), they struggled significantly with the minority class (malignant lesions). The SVM achieved the highest recall for the minority class (0.36), outperforming LR (0.32) and GB (0.17). The QSVM achieved 100 % accuracy on the initial synthetic test data, while the VQC achieved an accuracy of 0.8933. These results highlight the potential of quantum feature spaces to enhance separability, establishing a baseline for further exploration of HQML in robust clinical decision support.

title: SwinEffNet: A Hybrid Transformer-CNN Architecture for Lemon Leaf Disease
authors: Saifuddin Sagor, Shahariar Hossain, Md. Faysal Hossan, Abdullah Bin Faruk, Hasibul Hasan, MD Ajijul Hakim Bhuiyan
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Lemon leaf diseases pose a significant threat to agricultural productivity, highlighting the need for early and accurate diagnostic solutions. This study proposes SwinEffNet, a hybrid deep learning architecture that integrates the local feature extraction strength of EfficientNetV2-S with the global contextual modeling capability of the Swin-Tiny Transformer. To address data scarcity and class imbalance, a custom dataset of 3,266 high-resolution lemon leaf images collected from Bangladesh was curated, covering six classes: Algal Leaf Spot, Black Spot, Citrus Canker, Citrus Pest, Greening, and Healthy. The Synthetic Minority Over-Sampling Technique (SMOTE) was employed to balance the dataset, while a hierarchical multistage feature fusion strategy was utilized to enhance feature representation. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that SwinEffNet achieves a high classification accuracy of 99.19%, with consistently strong precision and recall across all disease categories, outperforming standalone architectures such as DenseNet201 and ConvNeXt-Tiny. The proposed framework exhibits strong robustness and generalization capability, making it suitable for large-scale citrus disease monitoring. Overall, this research presents an effective and scalable automated solution that can support sustainable citrus farming practices and contribute to economic stability within the citrus industry.

title: Adaptive Neural Network with Dynamic Feature Weighting for Enhanced Power Grid Stability Prediction
authors: S. Gomathi
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: One of the most significant projects in the modern electrical systems is the assessment of the stability of the power grid as it is impossible to determine the events which occur in the grid in real-time without accurate information to prevent the appearance of cascades. A novel adaptive multi-layer ensemble learning system as described in this paper, incorporates nonstagnant attribute weighting as well as the soft-probability voting that assists in more accurate prediction of power grid stability. We came up with nine designed stability indicators of traditional electrical parameters and a new stability index, power balance ratio, and cross-parameter statistical indicators. The hybrid under suggestion is the hybridization of the Random Forest, XGBoost and Gradient Boosting algorithms by using the soft-voting approach that outperforms the classifiers. With the increased robustness, the 10,000 samples of power grids were experimentally tested and found that our Neural Network model offered the individual accuracy of 97.80 percent and the Hybrid Ensemble offered the individual accuracy of 94.15 percent. The framework can provide confidence-sensitive predictions and forecast visualization of performance on twelve measurement metrics of performance. This solution proposes the first confidence-based scoring system of power grid stability, which simplifies the risk-based decision making. The new approach to feature engineering methodology summarizes the temporalelectrical relationships that have never been researched in the historical literature of grid stability. The work is worthwhile in the smart power system observation field since it presents a collective set of structures that is more successful than the individual algorithms approach and as well, the work has quantifiable confidence measures in benchmarking the security of critical infrastructure.

title: A Comprehensive Precision Agriculture Framework: Automated Plant Disease Identification and Categorization Through Cutting Edge Deep Learning Frameworks
authors: Jangam Subbarayudu, D. Mahammad Rafi, Juttu Suresh, Sangareddy Sumalatha, Muttavarapu Anusha, Chaduvula Sarath Sainath Reddy
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: The outbreak of plant diseases represents one of the greatest menaces to the world agricultural productivity resulting in a global loss of crops that is estimated at 20-40 per annum. The conventional detection techniques based on visual inspection which is manual are time consuming and subjective. In the current paper, an optimized deep learning architecture to identify and detect plant diseases based on an ensemble of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) will be presented (ResNet-50, DenseNet-121, and EfficientNet-B4). The authors make the contribution in the formulated system of adaptive preprocessing and attention-based feature fusion, weighted ensemble voting, which is developed based on explicit mathematical optimization. Extensive experimental validation of 15 plant species and 38 disease classes on a dataset of 54, 267 images supports a high accuracy of 97.8% and a high precision of 97.3 and a high recall of 97.1, which is a 2.0 percent improvement on existing algorithms with inference times less than 150 ms on edge machines. Ablation studies map the individual contribution of the components in detail, and analysis at the level of the classes indicate a stable detection across the types of diseases. The efficiency of the computations in the framework (model size: 89 MB, energy consumption: about 2.1 kWh per 1000 inferences) allows the implementation in farming scenarios with limited room.

title: Cross-Domain Few-Shot Adaptation of Siamese Networks for Robot Initial Localization
authors: Zhi Song, Lei Zou, Chenxi Wang, Na Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Statistics, Modeling and Advanced Algorithms (ASMA)
abstract: This paper presents a cross-domain robot initial localization method based on Siamese neural networks with parameter-efficient adapters. Traditional methods such as Adaptive Monte Carlo Localization(AMCL) and Point-to-Line Iterative Closest Point (PL-ICP) struggle to achieve reliable initial pose estimation in dynamic environments. Our method performs a location matching by learning discriminative embeddings of LiDAR scan data. Lightweight adapter modules enable rapid cross-environment adaptation while preserving spatial reasoning capabilities. Experimental results demonstrate that the method achieves outstanding performance with only a small number of samples from the target environment. Compared to traditional methods, the proposed approach exhibits higher accuracy and stronger environmental adaptability.

title: Federated AI Framework for Distributed Smart Healthcare at the Edge
authors: A. Swapna, K. Deepa
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: The exponential growth of smart healthcare devices at the edge has brought new challenges to secure, efficient, and privacy-respecting data processing. This paper proposes a secure edge computing system based on Federated Long Short-Term Memory model learning named FedLSTM, which aims to support smart healthcare data analysis. The proposed system effectively trains models on each data at the edge nodes, where patient data stays local to meet the most important privacy requirements and comply with HIPAA and GDPR regulations. The system comes equipped with state-of-the-art security features to protect the updated model during end-to-end communication, thereby securing data integrity. The experiment performed on smart healthcare datasets validates high levels of accuracy at 98.71 % with lower latency and increased patient data privacy compared to traditional models. The proposed system tests to be scalable and robust at the heterogeneous edge networking aspect to support smart healthcare analytics. The integration of federated learning with edge computing provides a new system that lays foundational support to build secure, real-time, and smart healthcare networking to support critical patient services at the network's edges.

title: Development of a Compact Slotted Patch Antenna for Early Stage Breast Tumor Detection Using Microwave Technology
authors: Sayed Shifat Ahmed, Ajay Krishno Sarkar
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This research develops a compact slotted microstrip patch antenna that can be used in conjunction with microwave imaging techniques for early detection of breast tumors. It was developed on a low-cost FR-4 substrate and is specifically designed to work at an operational frequency of 1.9612 GHz; as such it will provide a high level of reflection and return loss (-41.27 dB) thereby minimizing loss in signal radiating from the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 9 4 - 1. 9 9 ~ G H z}$</tex> frequency band. At lower frequency levels, the narrow bandwidth provides enhanced penetration capability allowing for better discrimination between normal and malignant breast tissue due to the higher dielectric contrast. In addition, SAR analysis confirmed maximum value of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.623 \mathrm{W} / \text{kg}$</tex> is significantly less than the international exposure limits. Using a multi-layer breast phantom model, the simulation results demonstrate the feasibility of utilizing this antenna as part of a non-invasive, real time diagnostic system for breast cancer detection and localization analysis through variations of the scattering parameters by independent excitation of each element. Overall, this miniaturized antenna has demonstrated its potential for integration into diagnostic systems for the detection and monitoring of breast cancer through non-invasive and safe methods.

title: A Mean-Value Performability Assessment of IoMT Systems Based-on Stochastic Reward Net and Fuzzy Lz-transform Method
authors: Moraru Victor, Guțuleac Emilian, Alexei Anatol, Gribincea Dorin, Cuzmin Simion, Seniușin Anton
publish: 2026 International Conference on Development and Application Systems (DAS)
abstract: This paper presents a predictive approach for uncertainty mean-value performability modeling and assessments framework of Internet of Medical Things (IoMT) System (IoMTS) based-on the Stochastic Reward Nets with Fuzzy parameters (FSRNs) and Fuzzy Lz-transform method (FLz-TM). This framework allows the development of compact models and efficient evaluation of the availability and cost security impact in which is used a proactive defense time-based Moving Target Defense technique. The application of FSRN and FLz-TM considerably improve computational efficiency of performability analysis, avoiding the curse of IoMT system states dimensionality. A numerical case study for mean-value performability analysis of a particular IoMT is given to show the effectiveness of proposed approach.

title: Privacy-Aware Edge-Based Intelligent Video Analytics for Scalable Crowd Management in Smart Cities
authors: Deepa Dawadi, Uday Surendra Yandamuri, Sunil Kumar V, J. L. Aldo Stalin, R. Naveenkumar
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: In modern times, intelligent video analytics for crowd management in smart cities gained significant attention because of rapid urbanization, increasing density of population and growing demand for public safety and effective control of traffic. However, existing You Only Look Once with Long Short-Term Memory (YOLO-LSTM) approach struggled with restricted ability to analyze long-term crowd dynamics, poor scalability and privacy risks because of raw data transmission. To overcome these challenges, Privacy-Aware Edge-Attention Video Analytics for Smart City Crowd Management (PAEAVCM) which incorporates privacy-aware edge computing, attention-based long-term temporal modeling. Initially, video streams are captured from distributed surveillance cameras and preprocessed through resizing, normalization, data augmentation, denoised and anonymized to ensure data consistency. Then, these anonymized frames are analyzed through real-time CNN-based object detection approaches to identify pedestrians and vehicles with low latency. Further, detected objects are tracked across frames for constructing spatio-temporal features which illustrate crowd density, movement and dynamics of flow and analyzed through attention-enhanced temporal learning approach for capturing long-term crowd dynamics. Moreover, to maintain system adaptability by preserving privacy, federated learning is adopted to update system locally at edge devices which shares only encrypted parameters rather than raw data. Lastly, intelligent alert generation module transforms predictive insights in prioritized alerts timely decision-making and proactive crowd management. Therefore, proposed PAEAVCM framework achieved accuracy 98%, precision 97%, recall 98% and F1-score 97% surpasses existing YOLO-LSTM method and validates its efficiency for real-world smart city deployments.

title: Enhanced Feature Selection for Motor Imagery-Based Brain-Computer Interface: A Comparative Study of Hippopotamus Optimization Algorithm with Riemannian and CSP Techniques
authors: Satvika S S, Puspita Biswas, Vishnu Priya R, Jayapriya J, Vinay M
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: EEG-based motor imagery (MI) classification is a critical component of Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) systems for assistive and rehabilitation applications. However, the nonstationary and noisy nature of EEG signals poses significant challenges for achieving robust classification performance. This paper investigates three hybrid MI-BCI pipelines that integrate spatial feature extraction, machine learning classifiers, and metaheuristic-based feature selection. Common Spatial Pattern (CSP) and Riemannian Geometry are employed for feature extraction, while Support Vector Machine and Random Forest classifiers are optimized using the Hippopotamus Optimization (HO) algorithm. Experimental evaluation on a public EEG motor imagery dataset demonstrates that the CSP+SVM+HO pipeline achieves the best performance, with an accuracy of 95 % and an F1-score of 0.95. The Riemannian Geometry+SVM+HO pipeline achieves 92.5 % accuracy, whereas the Riemannian Geometry + Random Forest +HO pipeline yields 72.5 %. These results highlight the effectiveness of optimized spatial filtering and feature selection in enhancing MI-BCI classification and support the development of reliable BCI systems.

title: Automated Clinical Discharge Summary Generation
authors: Shiva Kumar Souta, Bysani Venkat Sai Sabarish, Dommeti Charan Sri Sai, Ganapathi Kishore, M. Rambabu
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Clinical discharge summaries are essential for ensuring continuity of care between inpatient and outpatient settings; however, their manual preparation is time-consuming, error-prone, and contributes to clinician workload and discharge delays. This paper presents SummarEaseAI, a human-in-the-loop framework that integrates a BioClinicalBERT-based clinical entity extraction module and a Flan-T5-based text generation module within a clinical review interface. The proposed system processes accumulated clinical notes, extracts clinically relevant entities, and generates structured draft discharge summaries that can be reviewed and edited by clinicians before finalization. Experiments conducted on fifteen synthetically generated patient cases show that the system produces coherent and well-structured summaries while correctly capturing major diagnoses, medications, and procedures. Qualitative evaluation indicates recurring limitations such as occasional omissions, redundant information, and hallucinated content. Overall, the results demonstrate the feasibility of SummarEaseAI as an assistive documentation tool and highlight the need for future validation using real clinical data and clinician-centred usability studies.

title: Parametric Analysis and Variety Classification of Rice Grains Using Machine Learning and Deep Learning Models
authors: Shruti Modi, Satish Pratapur
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: The identification of abstract -Rice variety is the essential part of the agricultural quality determination, food processing, and standardization of markets. The process of manual inspection is subjective and time consuming, thus resulting in unequal classification. The current paper can offer a solid structure of a parametric analysis and automatic classification of rice grain varieties based on machine learning and deep learning models. The method that has been offered combines geometric, color, and texture-based parametric attributes with conventional machine learning classifiers and a convolutional neural network to learn deep features. An integrated algorithm is expressed to conduct preprocessing, feature extraction, feature selection and classification. It has been experimentally proved that the proposed model has better performance with the hybrid CNN-SVM model having best performance with the highest accuracy of 98.21 against the performance of separate classifiers. The suggested system is effective, expandable and can be applied to the real-life agricultural use.

title: Machine Learning Meets XAI: Grad-CAM Visualization for Enhanced Lung and Colon Cancer Detection
authors: Md. Azim Uddin Nahid, Md Ali Azam, Avibrata Saha Ratul, Ifath Rahman Tushar, Taohidur Rahman, Abdul Kadar Muhammad Masum
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The killer cancers of the world are lung cancer and colon cancer while considering the few deviations in tissues by histopathological examinations it is necessary to find the way out in earlier stages if it can be found which is not not easy. This research systematically assesses the performance of four state-of-the-art models working with Deep Convolutional Neural Network (D-CNN), DenseNet121, ResNet152V2, InceptionV3, and SE-ResNet152-based systems for the automated analysis of histopathology of lung and colon cancer. To foster transparency and drive clinician trust, we combined it with Grad-CAM, a method for explainable AI (XAI), to identify important areas that impact the model's predictions, and to maintain the interpretability throughout the training and evaluation processes. Using the LC25000 dataset under constant experimental settings, we have shown that the performance of SE-ResNet152 is better than the other models, and the results achieved an area under the curve (AUC) of 0.996 and an outstanding accuracy of 99.37%. This enhanced performance is an indication of its ability to distinguish malignant from benign tissues in spite of complex histological differences. Our proposed technique is not only able to enhance automated cancer diagnosis in a novel way, but also gives an interpretable and reliable framework that can be used in real-world clinical contexts.

title: Implementation and Testing of B-Dot Algorithm on CubeSat 2U Nanosatellite Prototype
authors: Andrei Spatari, Vladimir Melnic, Maxim Kapusteanski, Alexei Martîniuc
publish: 2026 International Conference on Development and Application Systems (DAS)
abstract: This paper presents the design and testing of an attitude determination and control system (ADCS) for a 2U CubeSat prototype, using magnetorquers and the B-dot algorithm. The choice of this solution is motivated by the low power consumption and low mass, compared to reaction wheels. The proposed system integrates hardware components (STM32 microcontroller, MPU9250 sensor, communication and data storage modules) and a software architecture capable of implementing the B-dot algorithm for angular velocity reduction and satellite orientation. Experimental results show that the algorithm enables the stabilization and reorientation of the prototype, but with a relatively long response time, caused by the low magnetic moment of the coils. The study highlights the feasibility of the solution, but also the need to optimize the magnetorquers design to improve performance.

title: Experimental Evaluation of Planar Inductors for Application in Buck-Type DC-DC Converters
authors: Olimpian T. Paulet, Dorin O. Neacsu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Development and Application Systems (DAS)
abstract: This paper investigates the impact of using planar inductors within the functionality of a Buck converter, utilizing the EVAL_TDA38826_1VOUT evaluation platform. Two construction topologies for planar inductors are analyzed, the circular geometry topology and the rectangular geometry topology, both in forced continuous conduction mode at various switching frequencies. The experimental section examines the waveform shapes for the converter's output voltage and the switching node specific to the switching transistor. The study discusses the results obtained from testing the planar inductors under identical operating conditions and, finally, provides conclusions based on the results obtained.

title: Distributed consensus filtering against hybrid attack under FlexRay transmission strategy
authors: Junhua Du, Jiaxing Li, Xianglin Kong, Jun Hu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Statistics, Modeling and Advanced Algorithms (ASMA)
abstract: The construction of distributed consensus filtering algorithm issue is addressed for nonlinear systems with hybrid attack and FlexRay transmission strategy (FTS). For the sake of enhancing resource utilizing rate, the FTS is adopted in the transmission channel to regulate information communication, which is constructed by coping with static and dynamic protocols simultaneously. On account of shared network channel, hybrid attack, involving DoS attack and deception attack, is considered in the transmission process from the consideration of network security. On the basis of available observation information, a new two-step recursive filtering method is presented under constrained variance framework and the upper bound associated with the filtering error covariance is solved. In particular, we provide the theoretical analysis for the construction of filter gain via the upper bound minimization from error perspective and then the recursive implementation can be guaranteed. Finally, an experiment is used to illustrate the validity of the proposed algorithm.

title: Design and Analysis of a Broadband Corner-Modified Square Patch Antenna for Sub-6 GHz 5G Applications
authors: Prabhu. T, Suganya. E, Sekar. R
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: This paper describes the design and testing of a small, wideband microstrip antenna for 5 G applications below 6 GHz. The antenna's design is based on a square patch layout with a change in one of the corners. The antenna is built on a FR4 substrate that is 1.6 mm thick and has a relative permittivity <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\varepsilon_{r})$</tex> of 4.4. The antenna's overall size is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$45 \times 45 \text{mm}^{2}$</tex>. The radiating patch has changes to the corners and a slot that is tilted at 45° to improve the impedance bandwidth and current distribution.
