title: MST-YOLO: A Lightweight Multi-Scale Feature Fusion-Based YOLOv8 for Small-Target Detection in UAV Aerial Images
authors: Tianyi Hu, Zhifei Li
abstract: The scope of drone applications continues to expand. By analysing small objects in aerial images captured by drones, it is possible to rapidly obtain relevant data such as geographical information; consequently, there is an increasingly urgent practical need for small object detection technology for drones. However, small objects in UAV aerial images present challenges such as low pixel coverage, indistinct features, irregular spatial distribution and susceptibility to occlusion; moreover, the detection task demands a high degree of accuracy in object localisation.To address these issues, this paper proposes an engineered improvement to YOLOv8 for UAV small object detection, termed MST-YOLO.Firstly, by integrating the MicroRoute visual transformer based on the Projection Region Attention module, we perform multiple rounds of feature extraction and enhancement on feature maps of specific scales; secondly, by adopting the ScaleFuse module in the neck network, we combine the advantages of standard convolutions and deep separable convolutions to enhance feature fusion capabilities whilst reducing computational load; Finally, building upon MicroRoute and ScaleFuse, we introduce the TriBoostHead detection head to further improve the final detection accuracy of small objects. This detection head integrates three attention mechanisms, effectively enhancing the model’s scale awareness, spatial awareness and task awareness regarding small objects.MST-YOLO is better suited to the engineering requirements for detecting dense small objects such as vehicles and pedestrians in road traffic scenarios, and is well-suited to practical applications such as traffic patrols and road condition monitoring.Experimental results show that the proposed architecture gets a mAP@0.5 of 39.4% and a mAP@0.5:0.95 of 23.4% on the VisDrone2019 validation set, which are 6.3% and 4.2% better than YOLOv8n respectively. On TT100k dataset, mAP@0.5 and mAP@0.5:0.95 are 76.2% and 57.3%, which are 4% and 2.6% higher than YOLOv8n; On DOTAv1.0 dataset, mAP@0.5 and mAP@0.5:0.95 are improved by 3.5% and 2.2% compared with YOLOv8n. These results prove that MST-YOLO does better at finding small objects than other methods.The comprehensive experimental results demonstrate that MST-YOLO possesses superior detection performance and generalisation capabilities in aerial small-object detection tasks.

title: An End-to-End Deep Learning Pipeline for Automated Mandible Virtual Surgical Planning Using Real-World Clinical Data
authors: Nattapon Kamboonsri, Teejuta Sriwaranon, Natdanai Tantisereepatana, Chedtha Puncreobutr, Boonrat Lohwongwatana, Gregory B. Olson, Alessandro Tel, Massimo Robiony, Titipat Achakulvisut, Peerapon Vateekul
abstract: Virtual surgical planning (VSP) is essential step in mandible reconstruction, involving multiple tasks, such as structure segmentation, cutting-plane definition, and implant planning. While many studies have shown promising results on subtasks using specialized ML models, these models are typically developed in isolation. This fragmentation prevents their seamless integration into a unified, automated clinical pipeline. We present a fully automated VSP assistant integrated into 3D Slicer combining segmentation, reconstruction, and planning modules into a single clinical framework. The proposed pipeline was validated on real clinical datasets, including unhealthy mandible dataset containing mandible abnormalities, healthy mandible dataset with normal mandible, and fibula dataset of normal fibula. Quantitative evaluation using the dice similarity coefficient (DSC) yielded scores of 0.911 for mandible segmentation, 0.854 for defect segmentation, 0.908 for fibula segmentation, and 0.816 for mandible reconstruction. Planning module reduced average operation time by 57% while maintaining accuracy comparable to manual planning. End-to-end evaluation on real clinical cases demonstrated a dramatic reduction in the total time engineer spent completing tasks from average of 5 hours 21 minutes using the manual workflow to 29 minutes 38 seconds. Our results demonstrate that automated VSP offers a practical, clinically precise solution that reduces the time-intensive nature of VSP, bridging the gap between isolated ML research and real-world surgical practice.

title:
Effect and Mechanism of SiO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Polishing Slurry on Chemical Mechanical Polishing Technology in Semiconductor Manufacturing
authors: Ya Peng, Zuozuo Wu, Wei Zhou, Yangjian Li, Jianwei Cao, Shuai Yuan, Deren Yang
abstract: Chemical mechanical polishing (CMP), as the only route to obtain global planarization at present, is widely used in the manufacturing process of semiconductors. SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> polishing slurry finds extensive application in CMP, primarily due to the fact that SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> abrasives exhibit moderate hardness and exceptionally outstanding chemical and mechanical properties. Clarifying the effect of SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> polishing slurry on semiconductor materials and its underlying mechanism is of great significance for optimizing the polishing process. This study conducts a comprehensive literature review and in-depth analysis to systematically summarize the effects of SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> polishing slurry on semiconductor materials during CMP, and deeply reveals the corresponding action mechanisms. Specifically, it focuses on the influences of key components in SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> slurry, which including SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> abrasives, alkaline pH regulators, oxidants, and other additives, on the CMP performance of semiconductor materials. Through theoretical analysis, the research elaborates on the chemical reactions of each component at the molecular or atomic level and clarifies the micro-mechanisms of interaction between abrasives, chemical components, and semiconductor material surfaces during the polishing process. This review not only establishes a theoretical framework for understanding the relationship between SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> polishing slurry and IC semiconductor materials but also provides constructive references for optimizing the polishing process of large-sized silicon wafers.

title: The Effect of Advertisement Strategy on Business Performance Moderated by AI
authors: Jansen Wiyanto, Okky Rizkia Yustian
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Advertising, or commonly referred to as advertising, is a business strategy often used to promote a product or service offered. In addition to making the product well known in the community, another use is to make the business or brand name famous and make consumers aware of the business or brand name. The purpose of this study is to see the effect of advertising strategies moderated by AI on business performance, whether it has a positive impact or vice versa. The research method used is a quantitative technique using a questionnaire with Google Form platform. The target is retail companies engaged in the electronics sector such as PCs and laptops in Bandung with a population of 4,800 companies. Specific criteria for the sample are companies that have been operating for at least 3 years, still using advertising strategies for marketing, have experience in advertising strategies, use Purposive Sampling techniques to be able to take samples according to the specified criteria, for the data processing method using PLS SEM, the results obtained in general are many companies that use AI in their company's advertising strategy.

title: A Multimodal Fusion-Based Approach for Political Meme Classification in Bengali
authors: Siam Hossain, Soyad Adnan Hossain Pranto, Momtazul Arefin Labib
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Internet memes have emerged as an influential medium for conveying political opinions and shaping online discourse through a combination of visual content and text. Political memes are difficult to automatically recognize because of their multimodal nature, implicit semantics, and frequent usage of code-mixed language, especially in low-resource environments like Bangla. In order to analyze the performance of a variety of common image-only, text-only, and multimodal fusion approaches, we frame political meme identification as a binary classification problem in this study. In order to enable combined visual-textual modeling, a Bangla online dataset is gathered from internal sources and its textual content is retrieved using optical character recognition. CNNs are used to learn visual characteristics, while transformer-based language models are used to obtain textual representations. Multimodal fusion is then used to merge these modality-specific representations in order to extract complimentary information from both text and images. Multimodal models consistently outperform unimodal techniques, according to empirical findings. With an accuracy of 98 % and an F1-score of 0.99 on the balanced test set, the DenseNet-161 + XLM-RoBERTa fusion model performs the best among the assessed architectures. The findings offer empirical insights into multimodal modeling for political content analysis in low-resource language situations and emphasize the significance of combining textual and visual signals for accurate political meme categorization. As far as we are aware, this is the first structure-relative assessment of unimodal and multimodal models for Bangla political meme categorization, demonstrating the clear benefits of multimodal fusion in a setting with limited resources.

title: Accurate Temporal Prior Modeling for Learned Video Compression
authors: Dongjian Yang, Xiaopeng Fan, Debin Zhao
abstract: Recently, learned video compression (LVC) has achieved remarkable progress. Accurate temporal prior modeling is crucial for the rate-distortion (RD) performance of LVC. However, existing methods often suffer from imprecise temporal priors due to limited reconstruction ability of frame decoders and insufficient exploitation of temporal information from reconstructed frames and features. To address these issues, we first design an enhanced feature reconstructor (EFR) by integrating content-aware depthwise separable convolution (CADSC) that excels at local modeling with efficient linear attention duality (ELAD) that facilitates complete global modeling. Based on the EFR, an enhanced frame decoder (EFD) is constructed to improve transform capability, thereby generating high-quality reconstructed frames and features. Furthermore, to fully mine temporal information from these high-quality reconstructions, we propose a multi-type temporal prior mixer (MTPM). Specifically, the MTPM fuses short-term temporal information obtained via optical-flow-based feature warping with long-term temporal information captured by a proposed state-update-based method, thereby further improving the accuracy of temporal prior modeling. The temporal prior produced by the MTPM not only provides high-quality conditional guidance for frame encoding and decoding, but is also combined with the hyper prior, spatial prior, and the discrete latent representation of the previous frame to construct a diverse prior fusion entropy model (DPFEM), which further enhances entropy estimation accuracy. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms existing LVC models and achieves an average performance gain of 29.64% compared with the LDB configuration of the H.266/VVC reference software VTM20.2.

title: Improved Multi-Class Pneumonia Detection from Chest X-Rays Using a Swin Transformer Approach
authors: Abu Hanif Riad, Farhana Bente Islam, Md Abu Sufian, Md Daud Hossain Supto, Krishna Das
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Globally, pneumonia ranks as a leading health threat, contributing to significant morbidity and mortality, with chest radiographs serving as the frontline diagnostic tool. Manual interpretation is challenging, particularly when differentiating bacterial and viral infections with overlapping features. Prior convolutional models such as MobileNet, MobileNet+LSTM, and EfficientNetB0 achieved moderate results but were limited by weak feature representation and class imbalance. This study proposes an improved multi-class pneumonia detection framework using the Swin Transformer architecture. The dataset was divided into training, validation, and testing sets, ensuring that data from the same patient did not appear in more than one subset to avoid information leakage. Preprocessing involved resizing, normalization, and augmentation, while training incorporated focal loss, weighted sampling, MixUp, and CutMix. Optimization was performed with AdamW, exponential moving average, and cosine annealing scheduling. The model achieved 95.15% accuracy, with precision scores of 0.943,0.976, and 0.934 and F1-scores of 0.932,0.987, and 0.936 for bacterial, normal, and viral pneumonia. Compared to MobileNet baselines, the Swin Transformer delivered superior accuracy and more balanced outcomes across categories.

title: FedAdapt: Unsupervised Federated Domain Adaptation for Cross-Satellite Hyperspectral Image Classification_supp1-3702427.pdf
authors: Maoguo Gong
abstract: Multi-satellite hyperspectral image classification is increasingly expected to be performed on-orbit to reduce downlink and annotation costs. However, existing unsupervised domain adaptation methods typically rely on the closed-set assumption that source and target domains share the same label space, which is often violated in real multi-satellite deployments due to temporal land-cover changes and sensor upgrades. When the target domain contains previously unseen classes, directly aligning the two domains may induce negative transfer, making it difficult to learn a reliable decision boundary between known and unknown categories, especially under privacy constraints that prohibit raw data sharing. To address these challenges, we propose FedAdapt, an open-set unsupervised federated domain adaptation framework for cross-satellite hyperspectral image classification. FedAdapt first constructs class prototype vectors to induce a discriminative metric space for known classes and estimates an instance-level reliability weight for each target sample by its similarity to the prototypes. Based on this reliability, a relevance-driven weighting strategy is introduced to perform cautious adversarial domain alignment, emphasizing likely-known target samples while suppressing potential unknowns to mitigate negative transfer. In addition, an energy-based dynamic thresholding scheme is learned to adaptively separate known and unknown target samples without manual threshold tuning. Experimental results on multiple cross-scene HSI classification tasks demonstrate the effectiveness of our method.

title: Deep Transfer Learning for Date Palm Leaf Disease Classification Using DenseNet201
authors: Raisul islam Ove, Ramisa Anjum Oishi, Tofayet Sultan, Md. Ariful Islam
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Date palm cultivation is crucial to the agriculture and economy of numerous nations. However, leaf diseases threaten yield and quality, and manual detection methods are time-consuming and require expert knowledge. In this work, we introduce a deep learning-based method that uses the DenseNet201 architecture and transfer learning to classify date palm leaf diseases. The dataset was pre-processed with resizing and normalization, and data augmentation was applied to improve generalization. Two experimental setups were conducted: i) a baseline model with the pretrained DenseNet201, and ii) a finetuned model with a new classification head. A variety of criteria were used to train and assess the models, including accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, confusion matrix, and ROC-AUC curves. The fine-tuned DenseNet201 model performed better, according to the results, with an F1-score of 0.90 and a test accuracy of 91.2 %, outperforming the baseline version. The proposed framework demonstrates the effectiveness of transfer learning for agricultural image classification and provides a step toward automated, real-time plant disease detection systems.

title: Performance and Efficiency Analysis of Modern Filesystems on Consumer-Grade NVMe SSDs Under Real-World Mixed Workloads
authors: Sajeeb Khan, Sikder Moynul Hasan, Sagor Roy, Anowar Kabir
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: A filesystem is the core software that manages data storage and retrieval, determining the overall efficiency of I/O operations. Modern NVMe (Non-Volatile Memory Express) SSDs (Solid State Drives) are exceptionally fast, but for consumergrade hardware, the operating system often struggles to cope with this speed, becoming a bottleneck. Most existing research has focused on powerful servers and enterprise systems; performance behavior on devices with limited resources, like 8GB RAM laptops, has been largely neglected. In this research, we present the first comparative study of EXT4, XFS, BTRFS, ZFS, and NTFS on a single consumer-grade NVMe SSD focusing on CPU efficiency rather than raw throughput. We evaluated the performance and efficiency of five major filesystems-EXT4, XFS, BTRFS, ZFS, and NTFS- on a setup with standard Linux running on an AMD Ryzen 5 processor. Instead of measuring only data transfer speed, we focused on the efficiency gap, quantified by the hardware resource overhead required to achieve a specific throughput. In our tests, an important trade-off was observed: although ZFS provides advanced features, it utilizes up to 99% of the CPU (and up to 254% multi-core usage) in consumer laptops to maintain its advanced features, rendering it unsuitable for general mobile use. Conversely, for write operations, XFS delivers the best performance with minimal resource usage. For the mixed workloads typical of development environments, BTRFS proves to be a balanced option. We conclude that for general consumergrade hardware, the primary performance metric should be CPU efficiency-specifically, minimizing CPU overhead-rather than raw speed.

title: Predicting Brain Stroke Risk from Clinical Data using Optimized Machine Learning Models with Random Stochastic Hyperparameter Optimization
authors: Md. Mahinur Rashid, Md. Badhon Sikder, Shamim Kabir Kazim, Muhammad Minoar Hossain, Md. Sadiq Iqbal, Mohammad Mamun
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Stroke kills or permanently disables more people than almost any other neurological condition, and healthcare systems across the world are still struggling to manage its scale both in terms of acute treatment and the long-term care survivors require. Early and accurate prediction of brain stroke (BS) is essential for timely clinical intervention and improved patient outcomes. Over the past decade, ML has steadily proven itself useful in clinical settings not just for automation, but for picking up on subtle risk patterns in patient data that conventional statistical methods often miss. We tested five tree-based classifiers - GB, RF, XGB, DT and LGBM - chosen specifically because they handle the kind of mixed clinical data this problem involves, where some features are continuous and others are categorical or binary. We also built a 1D-CNN as a deep learning comparison point, mainly to test whether a convolution-based approach could add anything meaningful when the input is a flat row of patient attributes rather than a sequence or image. To enhance predictive performance and mitigate feature redundancy, Random Stochastic Hyperparameter Optimization (RSHO) is employed. Experimental results demonstrate that the optimized GB model outperforms all other models, achieving the highest prediction accuracy of 97.59%. To improve model transparency and clinical interpretability, To make the model's reasoning accessible to clinicians, we applied two interpretability tools: SHAP, which shows which features push a prediction toward or away from stroke risk across the dataset and LIME, which breaks down what drove any individual patient's result. The proposed framework effectively combines high predictive accuracy, optimized feature learning and interpretability into a unified system. Overall, this study highlights the effectiveness of optimized ensemble ML models and XAI techniques for reliable and transparent Brisk assessment and in doing so, we hope this work nudges clinical stroke assessment one step closer to something that is both algorithmically rigorous and practically usable in real hospital settings.

title: Optimal Energy Management of Residential Microgrids Using Metaheuristic Algorithms
authors: Tasnim Sarker Joyeeta, Md. Sohel Rana, Md. Mahmudul Hasan, Fariya Tabassum
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Renewable energy resources (RERs) have gained attention due to rising electricity demand and fossil fuel depletion, while their stochastic nature poses significant energy management challenges. This research focuses on the integration of hybrid renewable sources, wind and photovoltaic (PV), with energy storage systems connected to the main power grid. The primary challenge is the optimal energy dispatch among renewable sources, energy storage, and the utility grid to minimize daily electricity cost. Two metaheuristic optimization techniques, artificial bee colony (ABC) and particle swarm optimization (PSO), are applied to coordinated energy dispatch in a residential wind-PV-battery microgrid. These algorithms are implemented under identical operational conditions and time of use (ToU) electricity scheme to ensure a valid comparison. The results indicate that both approaches effectively reduce daily electricity costs while satisfying system constraints. However, the PSO based strategy outperforms the ABC approach, achieving cost savings of up to 4.74% and 9.66% under single peak and two peak ToU schemes, respectively.

title: An Explainable Bias-Aware Deep Learning Framework for Evaluating Ideological and Political Teaching Quality
authors: Yali Fan, Tao Zhang, Yangyang Xiong
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: From the past few years, accurate and consistent evaluation of ideological and political teaching quality has become progressively tough due to the subjective nature of student feedback, the variety of evaluation indicators, and the dynamic evolution of teaching practices. Traditional teaching quality evaluation models, significantly depend on black-box learning mechanisms and manual surveys, which often struggle from biased feedback influence, poor interpretability, and partial practical guidance for teaching development. To address these limitations, this research proposes an eXplainable Bias-Aware Entropy-Driven Teaching Quality Evaluation Framework (XBA-ETQE) for ideological and political education assessment. Initially, multi-source teaching and learning data are gathered from questionnaires, academic records, classroom participation, and virtual learning platforms. Then, the collected data was preprocessed by data cleaning and min-max normalization to confirm consistency. After that, a bias-aware feedback calibration layer is presented to detect emotionally driven and inconsistent student responses and allocate adaptive consistency weights, thereby enhancing objectivity. Subsequently, main educational features are analyzed utilizing a probabilistic learning model based on naïve bayes and maximum entropy optimization. To address the black-box challenge of traditional models, an explainable teaching quality layer is incorporated after prediction to deliver feature-level contributions and cause-effect explanations. Experimental results establish that the proposed XBA-ETQE attained greater evaluation reliability in terms of Teaching Learning Effectiveness (TLE) of 90.89 than Deep Learning for Teaching Quality Analysis (DLM-TQA).

title: Comprehensive Analysis of Diabetic Retinopathy Classification Using Deep Learning, Transformer Architectures and Hybrid Frameworks
authors: Pruthvi. P R, Natesh. M
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is a retinal disorder caused by hyperglycemia and is one of the leading causes of vision impairment. If it is not treated at an early stage, it can result in permanent vision loss. The conventional way of DR diagnosis involves manual testing, which is not considered effective due to the presence of variations and micro lesions in the retina, which is complicated to observe from human vision. Hence, Deep Learning, transformer architecture, and hybrid frameworks are developed effectively for identifying DR in its early stage. The existing deep learning models struggle with spatial locality bias, which leads to misclassification, the model does not identify the relationship between lesion and its distribution of vessels. This survey presents comprehensive analysis of DR classification models by tracing the evolution from standard CNN models that focus on local feature induction to ViT and hybrid framework, which utilizes attention mechanism for global contextual awareness. DL models excel in an effective feature extraction and identifies complex pathological structures such as microanuerysms, hemorrhages, and exudates accurately. Accordingly, the hybrid, DL, and transformer model helps in long range perspectives, which is not efficient for standard DL model. Transformer-based model helps capture subtle diagnostic features through the incorporation of attention mechanism, which is crucial for DR classification. Furthermore, various DR datasets such as APTOS, EyePACS, DIARETDB1, and Messidor are widely used and has been discussed. The DR classification models utilize performance metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-Score to validate the performance of the model.

title: AGFM-Net: Angle-Guided Feature Modulation Network for HRRP Target Recognition
authors: Peng Zhao, Fangyi Wang, Haipeng Wang
abstract: High-resolution range profiles (HRRPs) are indispensable in radar target recognition tasks due to their capability to characterize the scattering structure of targets along the range dimension. However, HRRP signals are highly sensitive to variations in observation angles, leading to abrupt changes and significant distribution shifts, which in turn degrade recognition performance. To address this issue, this paper proposes an angle-guided deep learning framework for robust HRRP recognition. Specifically, an angle-guided feature modulation (AGFM) mechanism is introduced to incorporate observation angle information into the feature learning process via channel-wise modulation parameters, enabling adaptive feature alignment under varying viewing conditions. Furthermore, convolutional operations and attention mechanisms are jointly employed to enhance feature representation capability. Experimental results on a six-class HRRP dataset demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms conventional CNN-based and hybrid models, achieving an accuracy improvement of approximately 3% in classification tasks.

title: Workload-Aware Optimization of Elderly Care Resource Allocation Using an Improved Genetic Algorithm
authors: Yufang Chen
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: In modern times, timely evolution of aging population has significantly increased demand for effective and fair elderly care resource allocation in home care services. However, existing MaxSAT optimization approach has illustrated efficiency in producing feasible caregiver schedules yet struggled with major challenges like static allocation and absence of long-term workload tracking that leads to unfair workload distribution and limited adaptability. To overcome these limitations, Improved Genetic Algorithm-based Elderly Care Resource Allocation framework (IGA-ECRA) is proposed. Primarily, data is collected from real-world elderly care and processed using cleaning, encoding and workload aggregation for capturing long-term caregiver effort. Then, allocation problem is formulated as multi-objective optimization task which balances feasibility, workload fairness and effectiveness of service. Further, enhanced GA with penalty-based fitness evaluation and reoptimization is applied for generating adaptive and fair caregiver- Elderly schedules. Finally, proposed IGAECRA framework effectively obtains fair and workload-aware caregiver allocation by maintaining feasible and adaptive scheduling in dynamic elderly care demand. Therefore, proposed IGA-ECRA framework workload fairness index (0.91) and maximum overtime per caregiver (9.3) which demonstrates its practical efficiency for real-world elderly care services.

title: High-Precision Estimation of Near Range Parameters for Distributed Airborne SAR System Based on Maximum Coherence Search
authors: Songtao Shangguan, Liying Xu, Xiaolan Qiu, Yao Chen, Mingqian Liu
abstract: Current distributed airborne SAR tomography systems are capable of multiple imaging in a single flight pass. Among its multiple imaging subsystems, there exist configurations where the radar transmitting and receiving stations are not collocated, and in such systems, the near range parameters often deviate from the preset values. The use of erroneous near range parameters leads to defocusing in the imaging results and a shift of image pixels along the range direction, thereby degrading the coherence among the images acquired by the distributed airborne SAR system. To address this issue, this paper proposes a novel high-precision estimation algorithm for the near range parameters of distributed airborne SAR tomography systems based on maximum coherence search. This algorithm enables highly accurate, adaptable, and automated estimation of the near range parameters—key imaging parameters for multi-station airborne distributed SAR tomography systems. Its potential for practical applications is demonstrated.

title: A High-Sensitivity Multilayer SPR Biosensor for Early Detection of Dengue Infection Using Plasma and Platelet Samples
authors: Khandoker Tauhid Imam, Nafis Nur, Dewan Mohammad Tausif
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Surface plasmon resonance (SPR) biosensors offer high sensitivity and real-time, label-free detection. This work proposes a multilayer SPR sensor with a ZBLAN/BiFeO <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${ }_{3}$</tex> /Ag/InN/WSe <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${ }_{2}$</tex> /analyte stack for early dengue diagnosis using plasma and platelet samples. A high-index ZBLAN prism and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{BiFeO}_{3}$</tex> perovskite improve light coupling and plasmon excitation. Sensor performance is evaluated using both the transfer matrix method (TMM) and the finite element method (FEM), and the close agreement between them validates the design. The optimized structure achieves a sensitivity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$267.27^{\circ} /$</tex> RIU, an FWHM of 4.98°, and a detection accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.20 \text{deg}^{-1}$</tex>. The penetration depth is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.1935 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>, and the peak electric field in the WSe <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${ }_{2}$</tex> layer is about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$280 \text{kV} / \mathrm{m}$</tex>, indicating strong field confinement near the sensing interface. Parametric studies also examine different prisms, oxides, and 2D materials, as well as the effect of additional layers. The results show that the proposed SPR biosensor is a strong candidate for precise, early dengue detection in future biomedical sensing applications.

title: A Hybrid Machine Learning Framework for Credit Card Fraud Detection Using SMOTE and Information Gain
authors: Khaled Mahmud Sujon, Adnan Shafi, Zubair Al Zami, Iftekhar Uddin Ahmed
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Credit card fraud detection remains a major challenge due to extreme class imbalance and the adaptive nature of fraudulent behavior. Although machine learning has advanced automated detection, prior studies often evaluate models in isolation or neglect the joint effect of data-level and featurelevel preprocessing. In this work, we present a hybrid framework that integrates the Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) with Information Gain-based feature selection, and systematically assess its impact across four diverse algorithms: Logistic Regression, Random Forest, K-Nearest Neighbors, and XGBoost. Experiments on a widely used imbalanced benchmark dataset demonstrate that combining SMOTE and feature selection markedly enhances recall for the minority (fraudulent) class, while maintaining competitive precision. Notably, XGBoost achieved the highest post-processing ROC-AUC of 0.9794. These findings underscore the importance of tailored preprocessing in fraud analytics and provide actionable insights for building more reliable and interpretable detection systems. By jointly analyzing oversampling and information-theoretic feature selection across both linear and non-linear classifiers, this study advances the methodological foundation for robust fraud detection.

title: A Data-Driven Approach to Type 2 Diabetes Prediction Using Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Ekramul Hasib, Sakib Rahman, Md.Shahriar Imtiaz, Sultanul Arifeen Hamim, Muhammad Firoz Mridha
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM) is a significant global public health challenge, requiring accurate, reliable, and timely risk-based diagnostic support. Enhancing predictive tools is essential to improve clinical outcomes and reduce long-term complications. In this study, a novel and well-characterized ensemble of classifiers and prediction framework is proposed to address early T2DM risk prediction by accounting for demographic, physiological, and biochemical factors. The framework incorporates feature engineering with advanced ensemble techniques, including Gradient Boosting, Random Forest, XGBoost, and stacking models, alongside explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) methods such as SHAP and LIME to ensure clinical interpretability.Experiments conducted on 1065 patient records using stratified sampling and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$k$</tex>-fold cross-validation demonstrate that the Gradient Boosting model achieves the highest prediction accuracy of 96.7 %, outperforming stacking ensembles and XGBoost with marginal performance differences. Key predictive features identified include glucose level, body mass index (BMI), and systolic blood pressure. The proposed model's high accuracy, transparency, and interpretability position it as an ethical AI-assisted decision-support tool for early T2DM screening in clinical practice. Future work aims to integrate multimodal clinical data and further enhance diagnostic performance.

title: AI-Integrated Hybrid Knowledge System for High-Reliability Corporate Strategy Planning and Competitive Intelligence
authors: K Srividya Subash Chand, Thaer Faisal Abdelrahim Qushtom, U Prasad, Ankur Bahl, P Deepthi, Senthil Kumar R
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Adaptive, predictive and understandable models are required in strategic planning of dynamic enterprise ecosystems. This paper suggests an AI-Based Hybrid Knowledge System that is aimed at improving corporate strategy development and competitive intelligence by integrating methods of advanced artificial intelligence. This system incorporates Multilayered Knowledge Graphs, Temporal Causal Discovery, Neuro-Fuzzy Reasoning, Quantum-Inspired Optimization, Contrastive Competitive Signal Detection and Generative Scenario Synthesis. Based on the actual data provided by financial reports, policy performance, market indicators and competitor actions, the system shows a great improvement of predictive accuracy, strategy strength and interpretability. The experimental outcomes confirm the superiority of the model: accuracy of causal discovery was 96.1, the optimization efficiency was increased by up to 10.8 to the traditional algorithms, and the model of competitive signal detection accuracy was 88.2 on average with the lead time of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 0} \boldsymbol{+}$</tex> days. According to the reports of the executives, strategic agility was up to 33.8 % more enhanced than the traditional BI tools. Besides providing excellent forecasts, the system enables explainable, resilient, and trusted decisions by stakeholders. The study is an important step in the intelligence of the enterprise strategy since it consolidated symbolic and neural methods into a hybrid and modular AI architecture. The approach develops new perspectives in dynamic scenario simulation, adaptive planning, and continuous decision calibration in high-stakes corporate contextual interests.

title:
1.36 kV Planar-gate Ga
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    CAVET with Nitrogen-Graded Multi-Implanted Current Blocking Layer
authors: Jiahao Yao, Hehe Gong, Gaofu Guo, Anjing Luo, Fang-Fang Ren, Shulin Gu, Rong Zhang, Yuhao Zhang, Xiaodong Zhang, Jiandong Ye
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: Vertical Ga<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> power transistors have attracted significant interest for high-voltage applications; however, their performance is often compromised by process-induced defects, as conventional Ga<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> transistors fabrication typically relies on plasma mesa and trench etching, which introduces sidewall damage, leakage paths, and premature breakdown. In this work, we report a normally-off planar-gate Ga<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> CAVET fabricated using a fully ion-implanted and etch-free process flow, effectively eliminating plasma-induced damage and leading to a highly stable threshold voltage with ultra-low hysteresis. Furthermore, a nitrogen-graded current blocking layer (CBL) is realized through multi-step nitrogen ion implantations, forming a tailored vertical doping profile that efficiently suppresses off-state leakage and improves breakdown voltage (BV). As a result, the device achieves a high BV of 1.36 kV, an on-resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">on</inf>) of 9.12 mΩ•cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, and a low hysteresis voltage of 116 mV. This work demonstrates that combining damage-free processing with graded CBL engineering provides an effective pathway toward high-performance and reliable vertical Ga<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> power transistors.

title: Isolation Method for Reducing Current Noise of Hump-free MOSFET with Extended Active Region in Current Mirror
authors: Kyuyeop Lee, Kyunghan Kim, Dawon Jeong, Jaehyun Yoo, Yonghee Park, Jaehoon Jeong, Yonghoon Kim
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: This paper proposes a new isolation method that solves unique issue in current mirror MOSFET with extended active region. For ultimate goal of minimizing current mismatch, current mirror circuit requires low threshold voltage mismatch (VtMM), leading to adoption of hump-free design to reduce threshold voltage variation between transistors through mitigation of hump effect. While extended active region can create hump-free MOSFETs, it leaves said MOSFETs more susceptible to electrical interference through above the substrate, necessitating more robust isolation performance. A new simulation approach using 2D multi-domain MOSCAP is applied which circumvented use of time-intensive 3D simulation of large scale TCAD, which allowed swift comparison between different isolation methods. Based on this new analysis approach, a novel isolation method with field blocking polysilicon is proposed and tested. Field blocking polysilicon effectively blocked electric field above the substrate, leading to 88.9% improvement in isolation performance relative to conventional method.

title: Socio-Spatial Modeling of Offline Rumor Diffusion in Hyper-Local Urban Networks
authors: Mehedi Hasan, S.M. Taohidul Islam, Md. Belal Hossen, Abdullah Al Jaid Jim, Md. Tanvir Amin, Dipon Roy
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Physical ‘Adda’ networks in the Global South significantly accelerate misinformation, yet these offline dynamics are rarely captured by contemporary computational models. Addressing this limitation, this paper presents a Socio-Spatial Graph framework for modeling rumor diffusion in Barishal, Bangladesh. Through the superimposition of ‘Small-World’ social overlays on real-world street topology, we quantify an ‘Offline Multiplier Effect’: physical hubs were found to increase viral reach by approximately 157 % against baseline models. Further integration of local topological features into a Graph Neural Network (GNN), excluding global centrality metrics to prevent data leakage. This approach yielded a 71.04 % accuracy in predicting high-risk zones. This methodology provides a robust predictive tool for analyzing misinformation spread within dense urban environments.

title: Martian Mineral Formation Analysis Based on Martian Mineral Corpus and Knowledge Graph
authors: Zhe Wang, Tian Ke, Weihao Shen, Yinhe Liu, Yanfei Zhong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Martian minerals are key indicators of the planet’s geological evolution and potential habitability. Their formation mechanisms record critical evidence of early aqueous activity, surface geochemistry, and geological activities. However, analyzing these mechanisms requires integrating heterogeneous data from spectroscopy, geomorphology, and stratigraphy. Existing studies still rely heavily on manual interpretation of heterogeneous data, resulting in inconsistent conclusions and a time-consuming reasoning process. To address this challenge, we build the first unified Martian Mineral Corpus and Martian Mineral Knowledge Graph (MMKG). The corpus integrates global Mars data products and scientific literature, while the MMKG unifies mineral-related concepts with their semantic and spatiotemporal relations, providing comprehensive data and knowledge support for reasoning. On this basis, RBG<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> is proposed as an intelligent question answering (QA) framework designed for Martian mineral analysis. RBG<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> performs knowledge graph path retrieval under dual constraints: geological vectors distilled from the unified corpus guide multi-hop exploration, and geological knowledge-consistency validates candidate chains. This yields graph paths that are both context-aligned and causally plausible. Leveraging these paths as the backbone, RBG<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> integrates geological context from the corpus and the knowledge graph to perform unified reasoning. Experimental results show that RBG<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> outperforms existing methods on Martian mineral question answering. For mineral formation mechanisms, RBG<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> covers over 90% of key points of experts’ analysis on average and produces reasoning that is both interpretable and traceable, achieving the first reliable and intelligent Martian mineral analysis. Code has been made available at: https://github.com/guoluqingfeng10086/MMQA.

title: AppleNet-B1: Explainable Auto-Reweighted EfficientNet for Apple Disease Detection
authors: Md. Samiul Alom, Ahsan Al Rafee, Abrar Rakin, Ferdaus Anam Jibon, Nazmul Hasan Shouvo, A. H. M. Kamal, Md Istiak Ahamed
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Apple diseases reduce yield and quality on many farms, and early detection is still difficult in real field settings. This work introduces Adaptive AppleNet-B1, an auto-reweighted variant of EfficientNet-B1 designed to handle class imbalance and varied visual conditions in Manalagi apples. The model uses a two-stage training scheme with dynamic fine-tuning and automatic layer reweighting, which allows it to adapt to subtle texture and color changes across the six classes: Healthy, Anthracnose, Black Pox, Black Rot, Codling Moth, and Powdery Mildew. Data were collected from orchards in Poncokusumo, Indonesia, and expanded with controlled augmentation to stabilize learning. Adaptive AppleNet-B1 reached an accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{9 8. 9 4 \%}$</tex>, a macro precision of 98.87 %, a macro recall of 98.87 %, and a ROC-AUC of 99.96 %. It also gained Cohen's kappa of 0.99, showing strong agreement between true and predicted labels. These results outperform MobileNetV3-Large, ResNet50, DenseNet121, and a custom CNN baseline. The explainability study, built with Grad-CAM, shows that the model focuses on disease marks that match field symptoms, which improves clinical trust and practical use. In general, the system offers a fast and reliable tool for farm-level diagnosis and supports growers with a clear visual reasoning behind each prediction.

authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)

title: Weather State Classification Using Quantum Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Sujoy Kumar Ray, Ashis Kumar Mandal, Oishik Biswas, M. Jahirul Islam, Omar Shehab
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Weather forecasting is a major challenge because of the unpredictable nature of atmospheric systems and the large number of interacting variables. Although traditional machine learning models have been used for weather prediction, they cannot capture the complex, nonlinear dynamics of weather. Recently, Quantum Machine Learning (QML) shows ability to improve computational speed and pattern recognition. Its practical application in weather prediction, however, is not well exploited. This work methodically analyzes QML algorithms for predicting weather states and compares their predictive performance with that of classical machine learning. We assembled extensive hourly meteorological data for Sylhet City, Bangladesh (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$24.9172^{\circ} \mathrm{N}, 91.8319^{\circ}$</tex> E) over 185 days from Microsoft and IBM weather sites, totaling 4,504 data points. The dataset combines the main atmospheric parameters as temperature, real feel temperature, humidity, precipitation probability, visibility, dew point, and air pressure, from Microsoft and IBM weather sites. Four QML algorithms, including Quantum Random Forest (QRF), Quantum Decision Tree (QDT), Quantum Support Vector Machine (QSVM), and Quantum Neural Network (QNN) are trained and tested for five weather state classification, and compared to their classical counterparts named SVM, Random Forest, Decision Tree, and Neural Network. All quantum experiments were run on Qiskit simulators due to current hardware limitations. Results show that classical models performed slightly better than quantum models, and the difference was not very large.

title: A Physics-Based CatBoost Model for Thyroid Cancer Recurrence Prediction
authors: Sumon Ray, Proloy Kumar Mondal, Md Mahbub Hossain
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The prediction of recurrence in differentiated thyroid cancer (DTC) is an essential task for effective post-operative monitoring. To improve prediction performance, a predictive machine learning (ML) model is developed in this paper that combines Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) with a CatBoost classifier. PSO is used to jointly optimize feature selection and CatBoost hyperparameters, thereby increasing efficiency. As a baseline, the CatBoost model using all 16 features without feature selection or hyperparameter tuning achieved 96.10% accuracy with 100% precision, 86% recall, and 93% F1-score. In contrast, using only 6 selected features, the PSO-optimized CatBoost model achieved superior diagnostic performance, with an accuracy of 97.40%. The model attained 100% precision, 90.91% recall and 95.24% F1 Score for the Yes class, while for the No class it achieved 96.49% precision, 100% recall, and 98.21% F1-score. The entire dataset is divided into 80% for training and 20% for testing. The optimized model was evaluated on the entire dataset using 5-fold cross-validation and confusion matrices have been drawn to check the efficiency of the model on classification tasks. SHAP analysis identified the Response as the most critical feature. This finding supports timely and accurate detection of DTC recurrence using a reduced set of features.

title: Machine Learning-Based Classification for High-Efficiency Perovskite Solar Cell Material Screening
authors: Iqra Ashfaq, Hassan Saeed, Zaki Uddin, Aaisha Abid, Noor Fatima
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robotics and Automation in Industry (ICRAI)
abstract: Perovskite solar cells (PSCs) have achieved power conversion efficiencies (PCE) exceeding 25%, yet the vast combinatorial space of material compositions makes experimental optimization prohibitively expensive. This study presents a machine learning classification framework for predicting highefficiency PSC devices from material composition alone, enabling computational pre-screening before experimental synthesis. We analyze 5,909 experimentally validated devices extracted from the Perovskite Database, comparing nine classification algorithms spanning linear models, ensemble methods, instance-based learning, kernel methods, and neural networks. Our best-performing model, RandomForest, obtains a receiver operating characteristic area under the curve (ROC-AUC) of 0.870, accuracy of 81.3%, and recall of 74.6% for predicting whether a material combination is capable of achieving PCE 15%. Analysis of feature importance indicates that perovskite composition is the $\mathbf{2 0. 1 {\%}}$ contributor to classification accuracy, followed by electron transport layer (ETL) at 13.9% and hole transport layer (HTL) at $\mathbf{1 4. 9 {\%}}$. It is important to note that the performance of labelencoded material features (6 dimensions) is comparable to the performance of expanded chemical feature sets (17 dimensions), indicating that the raw material identifiers implicitly contain stoichiometric information. Statistical analysis indicates that SnO2-based ETL (51.1% success rate) and CuSCN-based.

title: BE-KNN: An Efficient Extension of k-Nearest Neighbors via Stable and Deterministic Ensemble Learning
authors: Kazi Sakib Hasan, Mohammed Montasir Abdullah
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The k-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) algorithm is widely applied in machine learning due to its simplicity and versatility. However, its reliance on distance calculations leads to scalability challenges, sensitivity to noise, and high computational costs when applied to large datasets. Ensemble approaches have been proposed to enhance robustness, yet they typically introduce randomness and increase runtime complexity. To address these limitations, we propose Batch Ensemble k-Nearest Neighbors (BE-KNN), a deterministic variant that partitions training data into batches, applies KNN within each batch, and aggregates predictions via majority voting. This design preserves reproducibility, reduces computational burden, and produces an ensemble-like effect without requiring multiple KNN models. We evaluated BE-KNN against standard KNN and stochastic ensemble KNN using 30 synthetic datasets and four real-world benchmarks (Wine, Iris, Breast Cancer, and Digits), with the F1-score as the primary evaluation metric. Results demonstrate that while standard KNN and stochastic ensembles achieve slightly higher accuracy on synthetic datasets, BE-KNN provides a significant reduction in execution time, up to 12 times faster than stochastic ensembles while maintaining competitive predictive performance. On real-world datasets, BE-KNN outperformed standard KNN in mean F1-score and demonstrated more stable performance across diverse tasks. These findings position BE-KNN as an efficient and robust alternative for real-time and resource-constrained applications.

title: Deep Learning-Based Chicken Breed Identification Using Convolutional Neural Network and Vision Transformer
authors: Md Farhan Uddin Rifat, Mohammad Arman Hossain, Abu Sifaet Chowdhury, Muhammad Mubinur Rahman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: In agriculture and business, correct identification of chicken breeds is a key factor in optimizing farming, informing selective breeding schemes, and ensuring species-specific attention. This research proposes a computer vision system using fine-tuned pre-trained deep learning models, including convolutional neural networks (CNNs) such as EfficientNetB0, ResNet-50, and vision transformers such as ViT-Tiny and DeiT-Tiny, to distinguish different breeds of chickens. The deep learning models were trained on a locally collected image dataset. The dataset consists of images of five breeds of chicken. The images were taken in different lighting conditions and backgrounds. The highest test accuracy of 98.75% was achieved using a fine-tuned ViT-Tiny model, followed by the EfficientNetB0 model with a test accuracy of 97.50%. This research demonstrates the practical application of this system by implementing the best models in a simple and user-friendly web application that allows industry experts and farmers to quickly identify chicken breeds using only a smartphone with internet connection.

title: An Unsupervised Band Selection Method Based on Sparse Graph Autoencoder Representation Learning
authors: Xuefei Wang, Yufei Liu, Lei Zhang, Fangzhou Chen, Han Yang, Cong Zhang, Shaowei Jiang
abstract: Hyperspectral images (HSIs) contain a large number of continuous spectral bands and can provide rich information about land cover, but they also suffer from issues such as data redundancy. Therefore, this paper proposes a band selection method (SGARL-BS) based on sparse graph autoencoder representation learning. The method first extracts normalized band features in the original band space and constructs a sparse band graph by combining locally scaled adaptive similarity with sparse k-nearest-neighbor constraints. Based on this, a multi-layer graph convolutional network is employed to learn band node embeddings. The model is trained using a cross-layer edge decoder and an edge reconstruction loss based on positive and negative edge sampling to preserve the local topological structure within the band graph. Finally, K-means clustering is performed in the embedding space to partition the spectral features, and the spectral feature closest to the cluster center is selected from each cluster as a representative prototype to form the final spectral subset. Comparative experiments were conducted on two commonly used hyperspectral datasets, and the results demonstrate that our proposed method achieves better performance than other comparison methods in classification tasks.

title: Trading Policy Optimization Using Deep Reinforcement Learning for the Dhaka Stock Exchange
authors: Md. Rafid Tasnim Nabil, Fahim Ahmed Patwary, Ekram Hussain, Md. Shahriar Mahbub
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The paper introduces a portfolio management strategy that uses a meta-learning approach to adaptively integrate the performance of multiple deep reinforcement learning (DRL)-based stock trading agents. Instead of relying solely on the decision of a single agent or using heuristic selection, the proposed strategy uses a meta-learning approach that considers the recently performed policies. Three actor-critic agents A2C, DDPG, and PPO are separately trained as expert learners. The neural network architecture that includes recurrent and attention layers is designed to assign the combination parameters. These parameters are required to adaptively weigh the portfolio allocation decisions of the three agents. The proposed approach is applied to the Dhaka Stock Exchange, an emergent stock market with the quickest stock price movement in the global industry. The experimental results indicate that the MAM-DRL (Meta-Adaptive Mixture of Deep Reinforcement Learning) approach significantly outperformed the standalone DRL agents and the non-adaptive ensemble approach in terms of risk-adjusted return.

title: A Comprehensive Overview of NLP Techniques for Hate Speech Detection in Bangla Texts
authors: Tajbin Jahan, Tahiya Ahmed Chowdhury, Md. Shamihul Islam Khan Limon, Md. Mushtaq Shahriyar Rafee, Md. Mahfujul Hasan
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: In Bangladesh, especially on YouTube, hate speech against people for their religious, ethnic, gender, disability and social identities is getting more and more common. Such content can be harmful to social polarization and public discourse, and more than ever, there is a need for an automated detection system that is unique to the Bangla language. This study aims to compare several models for Bangla hate speech detection from a manually labeled dataset consisting of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 8, 7 5 9}$</tex> YouTube comments. The evaluation is carried out within a common experimental setup for traditional machine learning models, deep learning architectures and the transformer-based models such as Random Forest, Neural Network, CNN, LSTM and variants of BanglaBERT. These results show that the models based on transformers demonstrated stable performance although not as good as Random Forest, which had the highest classification accuracy (88%). The results highlight the significance of proper benchmarking and dataset-specific elements when designing Hate Speech Detection systems for Bangla.

title: Layer-Weighted Contrastive Learning for Unpaired UAV Infrared-to-Visible Translation
authors: Jiaqi Liu, Minchao Luo, Shenfu Zhang, Rui Zhao, Xiangchao Meng
abstract: Infrared-to-visible image translation is a critical task for enhancing the interpretability of thermal imagery captured by UAV platforms, yet it remains challenging due to the substantial modality gap and the inherent noise characteristics of infrared sensors. Existing unpaired translation methods typically enforce feature alignment uniformly across network layers, neglecting the hierarchical nature of deep representations—where shallow layers capture local textures and deeper layers encode global semantics. This uniform treatment often results in semantic ambiguity or structural distortions in the generated visible images. In this paper, we propose Layer-Weighted Siamese Contrastive Unpaired Translation (LW-SiamCUT), a novel framework that explicitly models layer-wise feature importance to achieve robust cross-modal alignment. At the core of our method is a layer-wise weighted contrastive learning strategy that adaptively emphasizes high-level semantic features while preserving essential low-level spatial details, thereby improving both global scene coherence and fine-grained object localization. To further enhance feature reliability, we incorporate a Gated Spatial Denoising Module (GSDM) that suppresses sensor-induced noise and background interference during feature propagation. A lightweight structural regularization term is also introduced to maintain geometric consistency in the translated outputs. On the AVIID dataset, LW-SiamCUT comprehensively outperforms existing unpaired translation methods. Ablation experiments verify the respective contributions of gated denoising, layer-weighted contrastive loss, and structural regularization. In the downstream semantic segmentation task, the translated visible images achieve a higher mIoU. The experimental results demonstrate that layer-wise feature reweighting is a concise and effective mechanism, which is well-suited for UAV infrared-to-visible image translation.

title: AroBurn: An Attention-Driven Transfer Learning Model for Burn Severity Grading
authors: MD. Talha Bin Kobir, Shyla Afroge, Dina Shanjida, Md. Morakib Hossain, Mst. Maysha Khanom Moon, Md. Mushfiqur Rahman
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Accurate and timely assessment of burn severity is critical for clinical applications where visual inspection may be prone to human error. Recently, advancements in deep learning and computer vision techniques have opened new opportunities for automated burn assessment systems through image classification models. In this work, an automated system for classifying burn degrees has been proposed through transfer learning-based convolutional networks, with a primary focus on an improved version of the EfficientNetB0 network along with a new burnaware spatial attention module. The proposed network is trained on a dataset of 1,221 burn images, each of which is classified into three different burn severity levels. In addition, a rigorous data augmentation process including random horizontal flipping, rotation, and zooming, along with contrast adjustment, has also been included for improved model performance. Various state-of-the-art models, including VGG16, VGG19, InceptionV3, MobileNetV2, and ResNet50, along with AroBurn, models were tested for comparative assessment through standardized performance evaluation metrics. In this study, the model with an added attention module outperforms all other models with an accuracy of 85.54 % and an F1-score of 89.16 %. By efficiently focusing on burn areas that are clinically relevant, the burn-aware attention module increases the accuracy of feature extraction and classification. Adaptive learning techniques and class weighting additionally enhanced model performance. This study shows that AroBurn offers a dependable and scalable solution for clinical applications by greatly increasing the accuracy of burn severity classification over conventional convolutional network models.

title: Towards Physically Realizable Adversarial Attenuation Patch against SAR Object Detection
authors: Yiming Zhang, Weibo Qin, Feng Wang
abstract: Deep neural networks have demonstrated excellent performance in SAR object detection tasks, but remain susceptible to adversarial attacks. Existing SAR-specific attack methods can effectively deceive detectors while introducing noticeable perturbations to the digital domain, neglecting physical implementation constraints for attacking SAR object detection models. In this paper, a novel adversarial attack named Adversarial Attenuation Patch (AAP) is proposed, which employs an energy-constrained optimization strategy coupled with an attenuation-based deployment framework to achieve a seamless balance between attack effectiveness and stealthiness. AAP exhibits strong potential for physical realization by aligning with signal-level electronic jamming. Experimental results show that AAP effectively degrades detection performance while preserving high imperceptibility and shows favorable transferability across different models. This study provides a new perspective for physically realizable adversarial attacks against SAR object detection. The source code is made available at https://github.com/boremycin/SAAP.

title: Techno-Economic and Performance Analysis of a Photovoltaic-Based Electric Vehicle Battery Swap Charging Station
authors: Nayemul Bhuiyan, Mehedi Hasan Rasel, Saiful Islam Arif, Md Ashaduzzaman Niloy, Md Ashraful Islam, M M Naushad Ali
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The rapid growth of electric vehicle (EV) adoption has intensified the demand for sustainable and efficient charging infrastructure. Conventional grid-based charging stations face challenges such as long charging times, grid congestion, high operating costs, and environmental concerns. Battery swapping stations provide a faster alternative, but their high energy demand requires a reliable and clean power source. This study presents a techno-economic and performance analysis of a photovoltaic (PV)-powered EV battery swapping station designed for three-wheeler vehicles in Kanchon, Narayanganj, Bangladesh. The proposed system integrates a 236 kWp grid-connected solar canopy with a battery swapping infrastructure and an energy management system (EMS). System modeling and performance evaluation were conducted using PVsyst software, incorporating site-specific solar irradiance, meteorological data, and realistic load demand profiles. Results indicate an annual energy yield of 298.6 MWh, with a specific output of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1264 \text{kWh} / \text{kWp} /$</tex> year and a performance ratio of 78.09 %. Reliability assessment shows a P90 annual energy production of 278.6 MWh, confirming consistent system performance. Techno-economic analysis yields a levelized cost of energy (LCOE) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.19 \text{USD} / \text{kWh}$</tex> and a simple payback period of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5. 1}$</tex> years. Environmental assessment demonstrates a potential reduction of approximately 5,232 tons of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex> emissions over the system's lifetime. The findings highlight that PV-powered battery swapping stations are technically feasible, economically viable, and environmentally beneficial, offering a scalable solution for sustainable EV infrastructure in densely populated urban regions.

title: Efficient Sentiment Analysis Framework Optimized for Edge Implementation
authors: Pallavi B, Srinivasan, Ramachandra A C, H. S. Jagadeesh, Viswanatha V
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Extraction of emotional meaning of spoken and written data has gained popularity in commercial analytics, social media monitoring and sentiment analysis as well as in the fields of healthcare. Although there have been advances in the field of deep learning, there are still cases where modern approaches are affected by multilingual flexibility, real-time processing, and the ability to deploy them on edge devices with scarce resources. To overcome these, we proposed an “Efficient Sentiment Analysis Framework Optimized to Edge Implementation” system which provides real-time speech-totext conversion in eight languages with multilinguality by integrating Whisper Turbo V3 with a 1B-parameter LLAMA model trained with support of Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRa). When run on the NVIDIA Jetson Nano, the framework can have better accuracy, F1-score, and very less latency with text inputs and speech inputs. Adaptation to LoRa and 4-bit quantization in the GGUF format reduces the trainable parameters by more than 90 percent without loss of accuracy. An RMSE of 0.15 indicates greater performance compared to such baseline models as BERT and RoBERTa. As a result, the offered system is efficient in linking sophisticated transformer topology with realistic edge deployment, which provides the scalable and energy-efficient method of multilingual sentiment analysis combining edge deployment with advanced transformer structures.

title: Multi-Temporal Cloud Removal for Remote Sensing Images Based on Spatial-Temporal Convolutional Self-Attention
authors: Rui Sun, Ziqi Zeng, Junjie Xu, Yu Shi
abstract: The cloud cover in the optical remote sensing images will obscure the ground information to varying degrees, which causes the blurring and loss of the surface observation information and greatly affects the imaging quality of remote sensing images. Existing cloud removal methods mainly focus on spatial restoration from single-temporal images and insufficiently exploit complementary information from multi-temporal observations, which limits reconstruction quality under complex cloud conditions. In this article, we propose a multi-temporal cloud removal network based on spatial-temporal self-attention. A 3D convolutional residual block is introduced to capture dynamic changes across multi-temporal images and enhance temporal correlation learning. An adaptive dynamic weight fusion module is designed to adaptively aggregate temporal features and improve the utilization of complementary information from different temporal phases. In addition, a ConvTransformer is constructed to combine global contextual modeling with local structural perception, thereby improving the recovery of fine-grained details. Experimental results on the SEN2_MTC_Old dataset show that the proposed method achieves 27.800 dB PSNR and 0.842 SSIM with only 4.87M parameters, outperforming CTGAN, STGAN, and MCGAN. These results demonstrate that the proposed network can effectively exploit complementary multi-temporal information while maintaining a lightweight structure.

title: IoT-Based Smart Irrigation System for Water Optimization Using a Hybrid RF-LSTM Predictive Model
authors: Yomna. Gamal, Lobna A. Said, Ahmed Soltan, Ahmed H. Madian
abstract: Sustainable agriculture depends on effective water management, especially in arid areas like Egypt's Nile Delta. This paper describes a complete smart irrigation system that combines a high-performance hybrid machine learning model for predictive soil moisture analysis with an inexpensive Internet of Things (IoT) sensor network. The hardware utilizes a sensor node topology communicating via Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE). Our proposed solution is a hybrid system based on the Random Forest (RF) and the Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks. The hybrid system, after being trained on 20 years of agroweather data, achieves a 16% improvement over the Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of the LSTM approach. The hybrid system achieves an estimated 23% water-optimization potential.

title: Hybrid CNN-BiLSTM with Attention Mechanism and Ensemble Learning for Multi-Symptom Disease Prediction: A Comparative Analysis
authors: D. Sungeetha
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Proper prediction of disease based on symptomatic presentation is a major issue in medical diagnosis especially in situations where resources are limited such as in contexts where advanced diagnostic facilities are not readily available. This research will suggest a new hybrid deep learning framework, which is a combination networks to predict multiple diseases on the basis of multi-symptom patient data. The suggested model of hybrid CNN-BiLSTM got a high accuracy of 99.39% with precision of 99.46, recall of 99.39 and F1-score of 99.40. K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) recorded the highest accuracy of 99.49 percent but the proposed model performed better than the conventional ones such as Support Vector Machines (93.29 percent), logistic regression (92.28 percent), and Naive Bayes (85.37 percent). The ensemble top five algorithm did 99.39% accuracy. The deep learning hybrid technique using attention mechanism has a strong contribution to the prediction accuracy in diseases and retains the interpretability in terms of attention weights. The high performance of the model in a wide range of disease classes evidences its viability in the real-life medical application especially in the cases of initial screening and triage.

title: Multi-Scale BiFPN Networks for Tiny Crop Pest Detection with Temporal Infestation Forecasting
authors: Papri Saha, Firoz Ahmed
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Pest management in agriculture requires a system that can detect and predict outbreaks within agricultural settings. This study introduces a composite model consisting of a BiFPN-based EfficientNet-B4 detector and temporal forecasting models to detect tiny cotton pests and predict their population. Multi-scale feature fusion on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$896 \times 896$</tex> images is used in the detection module to achieve <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$92.7 \% \text{mAP} {@} 0.5$</tex> on the PestNU dataset of aphids and whiteflies occupying less than 0.5 % of the image area. In our case of population forecasting, various models are tested involving population counts derived from detection and environmental factors (temperature, humidity, and rainfall). We demonstrate that the ability to forecast whiteflies gives the best performance in the Random Forest (RMSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=2.22$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}=\mathbf{8 2. 1 \%}$</tex>) and aphids in transformer architecture (RMSE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=\mathbf{0. 0 7}$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}=23.9 \%$</tex>, accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=75.9 \%$</tex>), which can be attributed to the different population dynamics of the species. The integrated system facilitates the connection between reactive detection and proactive prediction and allows for accurate pest management in agricultural environments.
